text:

text:
I am filing a complaint regarding significant delays and mismanagement of my VA home loan appraisal process, which has caused financial harm and prevented closing. 

My closing was originally scheduled for XX/XX/year>, after going under contract on XX/XX/year>. The appraisal was ordered on XX/XX/year>, and the appraiser inspected the property on XX/XX/year>. 

Despite having nearly four weeks before closing, the appraisal was not completed in a timely manner. On XX/XX/year>, the lender spoke with the appraiser and provided guidance, which was not followed. As the closing date approached, the lender did not take effective action to resolve the delays or escalate the issue. 

On XX/XX/year> ( the scheduled closing date ), I was informed that the appraiser was still having issues properly classifying the property. At that point, the lender had not resolved the issue and had allowed the appraisal process to fall behind schedule without enforcing deadlines or pursuing reassignment. 

On XX/XX/year>, a VA ticket was submitted regarding the appraisal. On XX/XX/year>, I provided additional information to assist with property classification, and the lender indicated they could proceed. However, no meaningful progress occurred. 

On XX/XX/year>, I was informed that the VA had contacted the appraiser and gave a 24-hour deadline to respond. Despite this, the appraiser remained unresponsive, and the lender continued to wait rather than taking alternative action. 

On XX/XX/year>, I reached out again due to lack of communication. The lender confirmed they were still waiting on the appraiser. Multiple days passed without updates or resolution. 

On XX/XX/year>, the buyers agent also contacted the lender for an update as the extended purchase agreement deadline was expiring, but no timely response was provided. 

On XX/XX/year>, the appraisal report was finally deliveredover 30 days after inspectionand contained multiple issues, including errors and a valuation below the contract price. I initiated a Reconsideration of Value ( ROV ) ; however, the VA can not proceed with the ROV until the lender issues the Notice of Value ( NOV ). 

Despite being advised that the NOV would be completed within 48 hours, the lender has again returned to the same appraiser for additional clarification. As of XX/XX/year>, I have been informed that the appraiser is not responding, and the process remains stalled. 

These delays have resulted in direct financial harm. My rate lock has already required an extension, increasing my discount fee from {$220.00} to {$710.00}. The new lock expiration is XX/XX/year>, which is only a short extension period and does not reasonably allow enough time to close, especially with a holiday occurring during that timeframe. 

I requested a longer extension ( such as a standard 30-day extension ), but was informed that this was not an option. Given the ongoing delays outside of my control, I am concerned that additional extensions will be required, resulting in further increased costs. 

The lender has failed to effectively manage the appraisal process, enforce timelines, or take decisive action such as reassignment when it became clear the appraiser was not performing. 


This situation has been ongoing for over a month and continues to delay closing without resolution.
label: account_closure

text:
I am disputing a XXXX late payment reported by American Express for XX/XX/year>. Prior to any delinquency, I proactively contacted American Express and formally requested enrollment in a payment plan or hardship program for my Platinum card. American Express incorrectly closed the account and reported a XXXX delinquency. This sequence of events constitutes both procedural unfairness and an inaccurate adverse reporting. 

Under American Express 's own customer agreements and applicable federal guidelines, creditors are encouraged to work with consumers experiencing financial hardship. American Express 's refusal to engage with my payment plan request while simultaneously reporting a delinquency and closing the account is harmful and potentially actionable. 

Legal Basis for Dispute FCRA 1681s-2 ( b ) : American Express , as a furnisher, must report accurate information. A delinquency resulting from the denial of a requested payment plan especially where the consumer proactively sought resolution warrants correction.

FCRA 1681i : I have the right to dispute this information and request a reinvestigation.

ECOA ( 15 U.S.C. 1691 ) : Creditors may not engage in practices that are unfair or deceptive with respect to credit extensions and account management. 
CFPB Supervisory Guidance : The CFPB has emphasized that creditors should offer loss-mitigation options and payment plans before resorting to adverse actions, particularly for consumers who proactively reach out.
label: account_closure
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

Legal Basis for Dispute : FCRA 1681s-2 ( b ) : American Express , as a furnisher, must report accurate information. A delinquency resulting from the denial of a requested payment plan especially where the consumer proactively sought resolution warrants correction. 
FCRA 1681i : I have the right to dispute this information and request a reinvestigation. 
ECOA ( 15 U.S.C. 1691 ) : Creditors may not engage in practices that are unfair or deceptive with respect to credit extensions and account management. 
CFPB Supervisory Guidance : The CFPB has emphasized that creditors should offer loss-mitigation options and payment plans before resorting to adverse actions, particularly for consumers who proactively reach out.

text:
Near the end of last year, I was in the process of securing a VA mortgage loan from LoanUnited.com , LLC. The loan estimate I received on XXXX XXXX, XXXX, contained the following information : - Loan Amount : {$470000.00} - Interest Rate : 5.625 % - Monthly Payment : {$2700.00} - Total Closing Costs : {$6300.00} ( including as relevant ) : o Origination charges ( points ) : {$1100.00} o Lenders title insurance : {$750.00} Up to this point and thereafter, I supplied any and all requested information in a timely manner, ensuring no delays on my end as I had an outstanding construction loan accruing interest daily that I wanted to pay off with the mortgage as soon as possible. 

Despite my repeated efforts to move this process along, I did not receive the Closing Disclosure from until XXXX XXXX, XXXX a few days after the loan estimates initial stated expiration date. However, this Closing Disclosure extended the rate lock through XXXX XXXX, XXXX. 

This Closing Disclosure contained the following information : - Loan Amount : {$470000.00} - Interest Rate : 5.625 % - Monthly Payment : {$2700.00} - Total Closing Costs : {$12000.00} ( including as relevant ) : o Origination charges ( points ) : {$6900.00} o Lenders title insurance : {$2000.00} In other words, the company more than doubled the closing costs on the loan, including adding almost {$6000.00} in points and more than doubling the cost for title insurance. There was no explanation given for these changes. Rates had not increased ; my credit score was and remained high ; nothing about the transaction changed. 

The documentation provided by the company documenting the changed circumstances provides no actual documented change in circumstances ; only changes in the fees they intended to charge. 

Making these changes in the closing disclosure, a point in the process that when time is very critical, places borrowers in a position where many likely feel that they have no choice but to proceed with the new, financially burdensome fees. 

Because of these changes and the refusal of LoanUnited.com , LLC to honor ( or even speak with me about ) the original terms, I ultimately obtained my mortgage through a different company, closing on XXXX XXXX, XXXX. 

Had LoanUnited.com , LLC honored its loan estimate, as it is required to do when it extended the lock, I would have closed on XXXX XXXX, XXXX. 

The difference in closing dates cost me {$3100.00} as interest on my construction loan accrued at {$100.00} a day for those XXXX  days.
label: account_closure

This email did not originate from a XXXX email address and was indistinguishable from a phishing attempt. The Internal Revenue Service publicly and explicitly warns consumers that it never initiates contact via email and that unsolicited emails requesting IRS-related action should be treated as potential fraud. I followed this standard consumer fraud protection guidance and did not engage with the unverified email. 

I regularly receive phishing emails and text messages impersonating financial institutions. My XXXX account has previously experienced fraudulent activity. Given this history I had heightened and well-founded reason to treat unverified communications appearing to be from or related to XXXX with extreme caution. XXXX chose to use unverifiable third party communication methods to reach an account with a known fraud history, which placed an unreasonable burden on me as the consumer. 

The only direct outreach I received from XXXX was a voicemail left on XX/XX/year>, from a phone number with no caller ID. I had no means of verifying this call originated from XXXX  Financial Services rather than a fraudulent party. As a standard consumer fraud protection practice consistent with FTC guidance I do not answer calls from unknown or no caller ID numbers. I did not receive any physical mail from XXXX at any point regarding this verification request, the deadline, or the potential consequences of non-response.

GROUNDS FOR COMPLAINT 1. FAILURE TO PROVIDE PHYSICAL ADVERSE ACTION NOTICE I was never provided a physical adverse action notice as required under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, informing me of the reason for closure, the verification source used, or my consumer rights. Email through a third party sender does not satisfy this federal requirement.

2. UNVERIFIABLE COMMUNICATION METHODS Every communication XXXX  used in this process was unverifiable. The email came from a third party sender via XXXX. The voicemail came from a no caller ID number. I was never given any direct, verifiable communication from XXXX itself. Their verification process was indistinguishable from fraud by design. 

3. ACCOUNT HAS PRIOR FRAUD HISTORY This account previously experienced fraudulent activity. XXXX resolved that incident without requiring IRS income verification. They then initiated a sensitive IRS verification process years later through the least secure and least verifiable communication methods available, despite knowing this account had a fraud history. That is an unreasonable standard to hold me to.

4. NO PRECEDENT IN MY CREDIT HISTORY No other financial institution I have a credit relationship with has ever required IRS income verification. This was an unprecedented request I had no reason to anticipate, making it even more reasonable that I treated it with suspicion.

5. ACCOUNT WAS CURRENT WITH NO FINANCIAL RISK SIGNALS My automatic payment cleared the same month the account was closed. There was no delinquency, no missed payment, no returned payment, and no financial risk signal of any kind on this account at the time of closure. XXXX closed a current, paying, five year account over an unverified email and a no caller ID voicemail. 

6. DISPROPORTIONATE CREDIT IMPACT This is my oldest credit account. Its closure causes significant and disproportionate harm to my credit profile, including immediate impact to my credit utilization ratio and long term impact to my length of credit history all as a consequence of not responding to communications I had no reasonable way to verify as legitimate. 

I was a loyal, long standing customer in good standing who was given no verifiable or reasonable opportunity to respond to an unprecedented request delivered through communication methods indistinguishable from fraud. I have also filed a complaint with the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  regarding this matter.
label: account_closure
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

Their failure to do so violates : Regulation Z, 12 C.F.R. 1026.5 ( a ) ( 1 ), which states : The creditor shall make the disclosures required by this subpart clearly and conspicuously.

It also violates the Truth in Lending Act and Regulation Z requirements for clear disclosure of any terms or changes affecting the consumers rights and obligations, especially penalties such as permanent account closure.

This conduct further constitutes a deceptive act or practice under the Consumer Financial Protection Act, 12 U.S.C. 5536 ( a ) ( 1 ), which makes it unlawful to engage in any unfair, deceptive, or abusive act or practice.

After the closure, I was forced to agree to a payment plan just to prevent the account from going to collections. Because this entire situation was caused by Discovers own ambiguous and misleading email ( which constituted a change in terms ), there is no reason for me to be fighting off collections. I request an immediate hold on any collection activity.

I further request that XXXX : Reinstate my account to its prior status Remove all negative collection-related reporting Re-age the account fairly, taking this situation into consideration Set up a new, fair payment plan based solely on the amount I owe ( not elevated payments required to keep it out of collections ) Ensure all future communications regarding payments or account closure are clear, detailed, and non-ambiguous
label: account_closure
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
FORMAL COMPLAINT AGAINST AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK Submitted to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Date : XX/XX/XXXX Existing CFPB Complaint Reference : # XXXX I am submitting this as a comprehensive formal complaint against American Express National Bank incorporating all developments in this matter through XX/XX/XXXX. This complaint supersedes and expands upon my prior filing and updates. 

I. OVERVIEW I am XXXX XXXX, a cardmember of American Express since XXXX nearly twenty years. My account, a XXXX XXXX American Express Card ending in XXXX, carried a {$27000.00} credit limit with a balance of exactly {$4000.00} at the time of cancellation a 14.4 % utilization rate. My FICO score as of XX/XX/XXXX was XXXX, confirmed in American Express 's own cancellation notice. I had no missed payments and no delinquency at any point in my nearly twenty-year history with this company. 

American Express has engaged in a pattern of conduct that is retaliatory, deceptive, and in violation of multiple federal consumer protection statutes. The specific violations are detailed below.

II. 
AMERICAN EXPRESS NEVER DELIVERED WRITTEN NOTICE TO ME American Express claims to have sent a letter on XX/XX/XXXX initiating a financial review and requesting documents. I never received this letter. No certified mail was used. No tracking number exists. No delivery confirmation was obtained. The first time I saw this letter was when American Express attached it to their XX/XX/XXXX CFPB response more than 30 days after it was allegedly sent and seven days after my account had already been cancelled. 

To date as of XX/XX/XXXX American Express has never sent me a single piece of physical correspondence via postal mail regarding any of the matters they allege in their CFPB response. Every document they have cited was either sent via standard mail with no proof of delivery or disclosed for the first time in their regulatory response after adverse action had already been taken. 

American Express also claims to have extended the document submission deadline from XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX. This extension was never communicated to me in any form. I can not be held to a deadline I was never informed of, for a letter I never received.

III. THE ONLY CONTACT I RECEIVED AND AMERICAN EXPRESS 'S RESPONSE TO IT The sole communication I received from American Express regarding this review prior to the account cancellation was a single phone call on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX XXXX. 

At the outset of that call within the first two minutes I clearly stated that I did not wish to speak by phone. I disclosed that I was experiencing a profound personal bereavement : the recent loss of a child through XXXX and the death of my eldest child 's father. I explained that due to these losses I did not have the mental or emotional capacity to manage this matter by telephone and formally requested that all further communication be provided to me in writing. The representative acknowledged this request. 

American Express sent no written communication following that call. 

Less than 18 hours later, at XXXX XXXX  on XX/XX/XXXX, my account was cancelled. 

American Express 's XX/XX/XXXX CFPB response states only that call recordings showed " correct information was provided. '' It does not acknowledge my bereavement disclosure. It does not address my explicit request for written communication. It does not explain why, after acknowledging that request, American Express sent nothing in writing and instead cancelled my account the following morning. American Express disregarded the death of my child and the death of my eldest child 's father entirely. The emotional distress this conduct has caused layered on top of already devastating losses is significant and ongoing. 

IV. RETALIATION FOR EXERCISING FEDERAL CONSUMER RIGHTS I exercised my right under federal law to file complaints with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau and the Better Business Bureau. Following the filing of those complaints, American Express : Refused to reinstate my account Refused to answer my substantive questions about the basis for the account review Refused to provide the contractual language authorizing their document demand Refused to explain the inconsistency in which IRS transcript verification was offered and then rescinded in the same call Issued a form response through the Chairman 's office characterizing all of my documentation requests as requests for proprietary information Cancelled my nearly twenty-year account The sequence of events complaints filed, account cancelled constitutes retaliation against a consumer for exercising federally protected rights. American Express closed my account adversely because I filed regulatory complaints and because I requested written communication rather than submitting to a phone-only process while grieving. 

V. AMERICAN EXPRESS REFUSED MY PROPOSED ALTERNATIVE VERIFICATION During my communications with American Express, I proactively proposed that American Express obtain my tax records directly as an alternative verification method. This would have been significantly less burdensome than producing years of bank statements, particularly given my personal circumstances at the time. 

American Express refused this accommodation and refused to acknowledge my proposal. 

Furthermore, the bank statement request as originally framed was itself unreasonable. American Express specifically requested records from the account I use to make payments to American Express one of several accounts I hold. The request was not limited or specific. Producing years of comprehensive bank statements from the account used to pay American Express, while grieving XXXX deaths, constituted an undue burden that American Express made no effort to accommodate. 

VI. AMERICAN EXPRESS HAS BLOCKED ME FROM MAKING PAYMENTS Following the cancellation of my account, American Express rendered my account completely inaccessible. My cards were declined. My XXXX XXXX was removed. My online portal reflected a payment amount and minimum payment due of zero. I have an outstanding balance of exactly {$4000.00} on this account and was given no instructions on how, when, or to whom to make payment. 

American Express created a situation in which I was obligated to pay a balance on an account they made it impossible to access. They have blocked me from making payments while simultaneously holding me responsible for a balance on a cancelled account.

VII. AMERICAN EXPRESS HAS DELIBERATELY REPORTED DEROGATORY INFORMATION TO THE CREDIT BUREAUS Following cancellation, American Express reported adverse information to the consumer credit bureaus. My credit score has dropped significantly as a direct result a measurable, concrete, and ongoing harm. Prior to American Express 's actions, my credit score was exceptional. I hold over {$100000.00} in credit lines across other credit cards, none of which reflect any delinquency or adverse history. 

The derogatory reporting by American Express is the direct consequence of their own conduct : cancelling an account in good standing based on a letter they can not prove I received, blocking me from making payments, and failing to provide proper written notice at any stage of this process. Any adverse credit reporting flowing from these circumstances is itself improper and I am formally disputing it.

VIII. INACCURATE INFORMATION SUBMITTED TO THE CFPB In their XX/XX/XXXX CFPB response, American Express referenced my account balance inaccurately. My balance at the time of cancellation was exactly {$4000.00}. American Express 's response cited an imprecise figure. This matters because the accurate figure {$4000.00} against a {$27000.00} credit limit represents a 14.4 % utilization rate, which does not support any legitimate credit risk argument and should be accurately reflected in the regulatory record.

IX. FICO SCORE AND CREDIT PROFILE DO NOT SUPPORT THIS ACTION American Express 's own documentation confirms : XXXX score of XXXX as of XX/XX/XXXX an excellent score No missed payments No delinquency {$4000.00} balance on a {$27000.00} limit 14.4 % utilization Nearly twenty years of account history Over {$100000.00} in total credit lines across other accounts with no adverse history No legitimate credit risk basis for this cancellation has been articulated. American Express has not explained how any of these metrics justify the adverse action taken.

X. ADDITIONAL REPORTING AGENCIES THE WORK NUMBER AND XXXX American Express 's cancellation notice references XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX  XXXX XXXX ) and XXXX XXXX XXXX as sources used in their credit decision. These are employment and income verification databases. I have not been informed of what information these reports contained or how they were used in this decision. Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act I am entitled to this information and I am formally requesting it through this complaint.

XI. VIOLATIONS ALLEGED Based on the foregoing, I am alleging the following violations : Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( ECOA ) / Regulation B : Failure to provide a legally sufficient adverse action notice ; failure to state specific reasons for adverse action in the notice itself Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) : Failure to disclose specific information obtained from consumer reporting agencies used in the adverse action ; improper adverse reporting Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ( UDAAP ) : Retaliating against a consumer for filing regulatory complaints ; cancelling an account based on non-delivery of a letter sent without proof of delivery ; blocking payment access while holding consumer responsible for a balance ; disregarding a bereavement hardship disclosure Regulation B / ECOA : Failure to consider or offer reasonable alternatives to document submission ; refusal to accommodate a consumer 's proposed alternative verification method XII. RELIEF REQUESTED I am requesting that the CFPB : 1. Require American Express to produce proof of delivery certified mail tracking and delivery confirmation for the XX/XX/XXXX letter 2. Require American Express to provide written payment instructions for the {$4000.00} outstanding balance immediately 3. Require American Express to disclose all information obtained from XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX and used in this decision 4. Require American Express to correct all adverse credit bureau reporting resulting from this matter 5. Require American Express to provide a legally compliant Adverse Action Notice stating specific reasons for cancellation 6. Investigate whether American Express 's conduct constitutes retaliation against a consumer for exercising federal complaint rights 7. Investigate American Express 's failure to accommodate a consumer 's bereavement hardship disclosure and alternative verification request 8. Require American Express to reinstate the account or provide a full accounting of why reinstatement is denied given the documented facts This complaint and all supporting correspondence are being submitted simultaneously to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency. 

Respectfully submitted, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, PA XXXX CFPB Complaint # XXXX XXXX  Complaint # XXXX Date : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX

text:
I am submitting this complaint against loanDepot ( NMLS # XXXX, Loan Consultant XXXX XXXX, XXXX @ loandepot.com ; supervised by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX @ loandepot.com ) regarding Loan File # XXXX, for the purchase of XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX, FL XXXX. 
Contract was executed on XX/XX/XXXX. The property is a condominium, and the sufficiency of the HOA 's financial reserves is a fundamental underwriting requirement under conventional lending guidelines. Despite this, loanDepot failed to conduct a timely review of the association 's financial condition at the outset of the process. 
Based on XXXX XXXX 's professional guidance, I paid approximately {$1300.00} out-of-pocket for the appraisal, inspections, and HOA document release fees all required simultaneously before the association 's financial viability had been properly assessed. I also followed XXXX XXXX 's advice to pay down part of my auto loan balance in order to strengthen my loan profile, which further reduced the funds available for my home purchase. 
The following is a documented timeline of events : XX/XX/XXXX : Contract executed. I was advised to proceed with the appraisal based on the condominium 's budget available at that time. 
XX/XX/XXXX : Seller 's representative delivered condominium documents. 
XX/XX/XXXX : Additional HOA documentation was requested and paid for in order to obtain the association 's financial information. 
XX/XX/XXXX : A 30-day closing extension was requested due to delays in processing. 
XX/XX/XXXX : The lender advised that the association 's reserve position would require additional funds to proceed a critical concern raised only at this late stage, nearly 5 weeks after contract execution. 
XX/XX/XXXX : I was informed that the loan could not proceed due to insufficient HOA reserves and lack of a current SIRS ( Structural Integrity Reserve Study ). 
XX/XX/XXXX : I raised concerns about the out-of-pocket expenses incurred based on XXXX XXXX 's guidance. The response I received was dismissive and unprofessional. 
XX/XX/XXXX : I was asked to provide additional documentation. On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX confirmed in writing : " Basically this deal is dead, no reserves or current SIRS, there is nothing that can be done in her case. '' XX/XX/XXXX : The seller formally canceled the contract. 
The core failure is clear : loanDepot did not identify the association 's insufficient reserves and lack of SIRS compliance at the beginning of the underwriting process a fundamental step that should have been completed before advising me to incur any expenses. Had this review been performed promptly, I would not have paid {$1300.00} in fees, would not have reduced my auto loan balance on the lender 's advice, and would not have spent approximately 5 months under contract on an unviable property. 
I formally complained to XXXX XXXX XXXX, Area XXXX and supervisor of XXXX XXXX. In his written response dated XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX acknowledged that the tone and manner in which my concerns were addressed did not meet professional standards, and he apologized for this. However, he denied reimbursement, stating that appraisal, inspection, and HOA fees are standard third-party costs required upfront. 
I respectfully request : 1. A formal investigation into loanDepot 's handling of this loan file. 
2. An assessment of whether loanDepot failed its duty of care by not verifying the HOA 's financial condition including reserve adequacy and SIRS compliance prior to advising me to incur costs. 
3. Reimbursement of {$1300.00} in out-of-pocket expenses directly caused by loanDepot 's failure to conduct timely underwriting due diligence.

text:
I am filing this complaint against Autopay ( autopay.com ) regarding deceptive lending practices during an auto loan refinancing transaction. 

I was initially presented with a prequalification offer of 4.80 % APR for 72 months at {$400.00} per month on a loan amount of {$25000.00} on XX/XX/year>. Based on this offer, I authorized Autopay to perform a hard credit inquiry on my account. 

Following the hard credit pull, Autopay significantly changed the terms of the offer without explanation on XX/XX/year>. The new offer presented was 7.49 % APR for 75 months at {$450.00} per month an increase of 2.69 percentage points from the prequalified rate. The refinance would have provided no financial benefit whatsoever. 

When I questioned the change in rate, the Autopay representative used high-pressure sales tactics, stating that 7.49 % was the lowest rate I would qualify for and pressuring me to accept the offer immediately or else any future inquiries about a refinance loan from any other companies would be declined immediately. When I requested that loan documents be sent to me for review prior to making a decision, Autopay refused to provide any written documentation. 

From my basic understanding, refusal to provide loan documents for review prior to signing may constitute a violation of the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ), which requires clear written disclosure of loan terms before a consumer enters into a credit agreement. 

I am requesting that the CFPB investigate Autopay 's prequalification and rate change practices, as this pattern of presenting favorable rates to obtain hard credit inquiries and then delivering significantly worse terms appears designed to pressure consumers into accepting unfavorable loan agreements. 

I have not signed any agreement with Autopay and have rejected their offer.
label: account_closure
source_sub_issue: Changes in terms mid-deal or after closing

text:
On XX/XX/year>, I sent approximately {$20000.00} through PayPal for the purchase of a XXXX XXXX. During the transaction process, the seller asked me to confirm receipt with PayPal, and at the time I relied on the sellers representations and believed the transaction would be completed as promised. However, the item was ultimately not delivered, and the seller was unable to provide proof of delivery. 

I filed a dispute with PayPal regarding the transaction, and PayPal investigated and ruled in my favor. 

Shortly afterward, PayPal permanently limited my long-standing account based on alleged links between my account and the sellers account. PayPal referenced internal indicators such as related card information, address details, IP information, tax information, and account activity patterns. 

I repeatedly explained that I did not intentionally coordinate with the seller in any improper manner and that I was ultimately the buyer who suffered the financial loss and transaction issues in this matter. During executive-level review correspondence, a PayPal representative acknowledged that they did not believe the events surrounding the transaction were intentional on my part and acknowledged that I no longer have any relationship or transactions with the seller.

Despite multiple reviews, including escalation through PayPals Global Customer Complaints & Advocacy team, PayPal permanently banned my account and refused reinstatement. I requested clarification regarding the alleged linkage findings and requested reconsideration or conditional reinstatement, including willingness to comply with enhanced verification or restrictions. However, PayPal did not provide meaningful detail regarding the evidence being relied upon or a practical opportunity to challenge the findings.

I respectfully request : 1. A fair reconsideration of the permanent limitation decision ; 2. Greater transparency regarding the basis for the account linkage findings ; 3. Consideration of conditional or restricted reinstatement given my long account history and the acknowledgment that my conduct was not believed to be intentional misconduct.

I also believe the outcome is disproportionate and unfair given my approximately 14-year history with PayPal without prior issues or policy violations. After many years of responsibly using the platform, the first time I needed to rely on PayPals buyer protection system following a failed high-value transaction, my account was permanently limited despite PayPal acknowledging that my conduct was not believed to be intentional misconduct.

At no point during my many years as a PayPal customer had my account previously been restricted, flagged, or associated with any comparable issue. Despite the severe and permanent nature of this action, I was not provided with a meaningful opportunity to review or challenge the specific information being relied upon before losing access to my account permanently.

In addition to personal transactions, I have also used my PayPal account over the years in connection with alot of charitable and community-related activities without prior issues, complaints, or policy concerns. This is another reason why the permanent limitation has been particularly difficult and unexpected after many years of responsible account usage.

I have attached the relevant correspondence with PayPals Global Customer Complaints & Advocacy team.

Pay pal response Hello XXXX, Thank you again for taking the time to speak with me and to share your perspective. Ive made sure that the request you had put in for reconsideration, along with your disagreement with the findings, has been formally noted on your account record.

I also want to say that I truly understand how frustrating and discouraging this situation must feel, especially given your experience with the transaction. While our internal indicators do show a strong link between your account and the seller, I hear what youve shared, that you felt taken advantage of by your friend and that youve since ended any transactions and relationships with them. Im sorry if my earlier explanations in my email didnt fully reflect that, as I intended to acknowledge that you had a past relationship with the seller, what occurred during this transaction I believed was not intentional, and that you no longer have any association with that person.

After carefully reviewing everything a second time, including the full history of your relationship, recent events, the details of this transaction, the associated loss, and prior account history, we werent able to reach a level of comfort to overcome the potential future risk. I know this isnt the outcome you were hoping for, and Im sorry for the disappointment this causes.

Thank you, \XXXX\nGlobal Customer Complaints & Advocacy\nPayPal
label: account_closure
source_product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service

text: New Rez acquired my loan from Caliber Home Loans back in XXXX XXXX. At the time we were finishing up a construction project to convert a single family home into 2 condo units. Caliber was the lender and when they got acquired by New Rez, New Rez took over the loan. Once New Rez took over the loan, our project was already complete but the mortgage was not separated to accommodate 2 units. In XXXX of XXXX, I started the process to engage the partial release department at New Rez in order to request a partial release of the property. I spent weeks going back and forth on documents and finally got to a good spot where they had everything they needed. I informed New Rez in XXXX that the homes would be going up for sale and requested that we expedite the process before a buyer was identified. I followed up at least weekly via email and called the main customer service number as well to stay on top of requests from them. On XX/XX/XXXX, we had a buyer with a signed contract for one of the units and I immediately sent and email to the partial release dept and they acknowledged receipt on XX/XX/XXXX. I've been following up weekly for an update and kept hearing that all documentation was submitted and to wait further instructions. I also informed them that XX/XX/XXXX was the closing date and we really needed to have this resolved by then. Call after call, email after email, and nothing. I would get a response about once a week saying they are working on it and there is no escalation path for me to get any help. It is now XX/XX/XXXX and I'm at risk of losing my buyer because New Rez is now saying that they don't have all the documentation, like the sales contract, which I have proof of sending and an email from them acknowledging receipt. I sent an email back to them with those emails attached but of course nothing. They are telling me I can't close because it's going to take another 6-8 weeks for their folks to approve which makes no sense because they have had all documentation since XXXX. There is no accountability here and as a consumer this is completely unacceptable. I have read other comments online from others who have had similar experiences with them. I don't understand how they are able to do business this way. No follow up to their customers, claiming they need documentation that I've already sent, and now leaving me with potential loss of a buyer who really wants the property. I have never had a situation like this from any other lender I've dealt with. What's most shocking is there is no escalation path or human to talk to that can help me. This amounts to a failure on New Rez 's part to do a timely process for partial release, especially since this has been going on since XX/XX/year>. This is going to cause me material financial harm and potential damages from failing to close.

text:
Truist is now forcing their HELOC customers to sign an adhesion contract in order to get their HELOC loan. This adhesion contract is called " Waiver of Barrower 's Rights ''. This is the exact language of the contract : This document acknowledges the right to accelerate the debt and power of attorney given in the mortgage or security instrument to mortgagee to sell the property by nonjudicial foreclosure upon default by barrower without any judicial hearing and without any notice other than such notice as is required to be given pursuant to XXXX  XXXX of XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX of the Official Code of Georgia Annotated ; waives any and all rights which borrower may have under the fifth and fourteenth amendments to the Constitution of the United States, the various provisions of the Georgia Constitution, or any other applicable law to notice and to judicial hearings prior to the exercise by mortgagee, except such notice as sis specifically required to be provided in this form ; acknowledges that borrower has read this mortgage or security instrument and specifically this form and any and all questions regarding the legal effect of the borrower and borrower has been afforded an opportunity to consult counsel of borrower 's choice prior to executing this mortgage or security instrument ; acknowledges that all waiver of the rights of borrower have been made knowingly, intentionally, and willingly by borrower as part of a bargained for loan transaction ; and agrees the provisions of this form are incorporated into and made a part of the mortgage or security instrument.

The borrower was given a stack of documents at the closing and because the borrower is an attorney, the contract was not signed. The Truist agent stated that in order to get a HELOC loan from Truist, every borrower much forgo their constitutional protections. When the agent was asked, did anyone question this last part of the contract, the agent stated people just sign the paper work and this was the first time someone objected.

No person should be forced to waive their constitutional rights to due process just to get a loan. As Truist is already notorious for their numerous gross errors in their banking, being able to sell people 's homes by forcing foreclosure without notice, just because Truist claims there may be a late payment, is just gross. This contract further is against public policy because to get a home equity loan, the bank has the right to take the house and make the homeowner homeless even if the error was made by Truist.

One must also note that in XXXX and XXXX, the Department of Justice won judgments against Truist for deceitful processes. Truist should be investigated, yet again, for making and hiding these unconscionable adhesion contracts in their mortgage contracts.
label: account_closure
source_sub_product: Home equity loan or line of credit (HELOC)
source_sub_issue: Changes in loan terms during or after closing

After 13 years of excellent payment history, my account was closed without proper notice, as well as the issuer 's refusal to reinstate my account or provide a new credit card. This is a particular hardship for me because I depend on this travel rewards card for my frequent travel on XXXX XXXX Lines.

My XXXX bank checking account was compromised and closed while I was overseas. This led to a returned payment because the account was no longer active. 

Another occasion I provided the updated new bank account information to a representative of Bank of America but the information must have been recorded incorrectly resulting in a second returned payment. 

What is especially concerning is that my credit card account was closed without any prior notice. I only discovered the closure when my card was declined during a purchase. I immediately contacted Bank of America by phone and was informed that my account had been closed, but I could not be given a reason. I was told I would receive a letter explaining the details of the closure. 

Approximately 10 days later I received a letter stating my account had been closed due to returned payments. This delayed notification deprived me of the opportunity to address or correct an issue before such severe action was taken. 

Despite my long-standing history of responsible account management, my credit card was closed without my knowledge. I made multiple attempts to explain the circumstances, but my concerns were dismissed without fair consideration, and Bank of America refused to reopen my account or offer me a replace credit card. I was told the account is closed and that's final. 

When I applied for a new Bank of America XXXX XXXX XXXX Credit Card, I was told by email that the request was denied. She had NO record of my two written communications sent by XXXX mail immediately after I learned of the closure. This explanation directly contradicts my documented efforts to resolve the issue within this unknown time frame. 

I believe this situation reflects unfair and inconsistent treatment, particularly given my 13-year history of excellent account management. In addition, my credit score is excellent -- in the 800s.

I respectfully request an executive review from CFPB to investigate this matter to assist in reaching a fair resolution. Specifically, I am requesting the issuance of a new account.

text:
I am writing to file a formal complaint regarding my experience while attempting to close my Wells Fargo account. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I visited a Wells Fargo branch to cash a check. During that visit, a teller asked if I was interested in opening an account. Although I was initially unsure, I ultimately decided to open an account. 
Shortly afterward, I decided that I preferred to continue banking exclusively with my credit union and no longer wished to maintain a relationship with Wells Fargo. Before attempting to close the account, I called Wells Fargo Customer Service to ask about the process. I was informed that I could either close the account over the phone and receive a check by mail or visit a local branch with my driver 's license and close the account in person. 
Based on that information, I visited a Wells Fargo branch at approximately XXXXXXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX to close my account. I informed the teller that I wished to close the account. The teller requested my driver 's license, verified the last four digits of my Social Security number, and sent a verification code to my phone. I also completed a withdrawal slip.

Everything appeared to be proceeding normally. The teller retrieved the funds and informed me that the final step was for a manager to review the transaction. When the manager arrived, she informed me that she could not close the account at that time because she needed to contact another department. No additional explanation was provided. She asked me to take a seat in the lobby and estimated that the process would take approximately 20 minutes.

While waiting, I became concerned because this was not the process that had been explained to me by customer service. As time passed, I began to feel that I was being unfairly scrutinized and called customer service to discuss my concerns.

The manager later called me back to a window and informed me that she would be handling the transaction. She requested my driver 's license again. I explained that I had already provided my identification and completed the verification process with the first teller. Nevertheless, I was required to repeat the entire security process, including completing additional paperwork.

The manager stated that the issue was related to security concerns and informed me that my signature needed to match the signature on my driver 's license. My original signature was correct. However, by the time I was asked to sign again, I was feeling emotional and frustrated by the situation and scribbled my signature. Once the issue was explained, I signed again using my normal signature as it appears on my driver 's license.

During the interaction, the manager made a comment to the teller indicating that she expected to receive a call regarding the situation. I also overheard portions of conversations taking place in the back office area, although I was unable to hear everything that was said.

This entire experience left me feeling embarrassed, humiliated, and as though I was being treated like a criminal while attempting to access my own money and close my account. While I understand and respect the need for security procedures, I do not believe the situation was handled with adequate communication, transparency, or professionalism.

The lack of explanation, the repeated verification process, and the manner in which the situation was handled caused me significant emotional distress. I felt profiled and unfairly treated during what should have been a straightforward account closure.

I respectfully request that Wells Fargo review this incident, explain why additional verification was required after I had already been verified by the first teller, and address my concerns regarding the way I was treated throughout the process.

This was the worst banking experience I have ever had, and I hope Wells Fargo takes steps to ensure that other customers are treated with greater respect and provided with clear communication when issues arise. 


Wells Fargo XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, NV XXXX Person Who Was Helping Me, XXXX
label: account_closure

text:
I am filing this complaint regarding Citi 's decision to close my credit card account despite more than three years of perfect payment history and zero late payments. 

On XX/XX/year>, fraudulent activity occurred on my XXXX bank account. As a result of the fraud investigation, XXXX froze the account to protect me from further unauthorized activity. 

On XX/XX/year>, I received notice from Citi that a scheduled payment of approximately {$1700.00} had been returned because the bank account was frozen. This was not due to insufficient funds or an inability to pay. On the same day, I worked with XXXX to resolve the fraud issue and unfreeze the account. 

Also on XX/XX/year>, I submitted the requested replacement payment to Citi for approximately {$1700.00}. At the time, I was unaware that my XXXX bank account information had been removed from Citi 's payment portal. 

On XX/XX/year>, I received a second notice from Citi stating that the replacement payment had failed. I immediately contacted XXXX, and XXXX informed me that they had not denied any payment requests. I then contacted Citi and spent approximately 1 hour and 29 minutes on the phone investigating the issue. During that call, I learned that the payment had not been attempted from my XXXX account but instead from an old bank account that I did not normally use. 

After identifying the issue, I immediately made a full payment of approximately {$2400.00} on XX/XX/year>. 

On XX/XX/year>, the payment successfully cleared both my bank account and my Citi credit card account. 

Despite the successful payment and my immediate efforts to resolve the situation, Citi closed my account on XX/XX/year>. 

Following the closure, I repeatedly attempted to work with Citi to resolve the matter. I made multiple calls to Citi customer service and was repeatedly advised that my account would be reopened after review. During these conversations, I was told various timelines ranging from 48 hours to 15 days and was repeatedly led to believe that reinstatement was expected once the review process was completed.

I also attempted to contact Citi 's Executive Response Team directly and left voicemail messages on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/year>. I never received a return call. 

On XX/XX/year>, I had multiple conversations with Citi representatives, including calls lasting approximately 44 minutes, 13 minutes, and 25 minutes. I also received an incoming call from Citi on XX/XX/XXXX lasting approximately 7 minutes during which I was told I needed to wait 48 hours for the review process. On XX/XX/year>, I received another incoming call from Citi and was advised that the matter had been transferred to the Executive Response Team. After that point, standard Citi representatives informed me that they could no longer assist me because the Executive Response Team had control of the case.

Despite my repeated attempts to communicate with Citi and despite being told that my account would likely be reopened, I never received any meaningful follow-up from the Executive Response Team and my account remained closed.

At the time of closure, I had maintained more than three years of perfect payment history with Citi and had never made a late payment. I was not experiencing financial hardship and was not attempting to avoid payment. In every instance, I acted immediately to correct the issue and made all requested payments.

Citi is currently reporting the account as " closed by credit grantor. '' Although Citi is not reporting any late payments, the closure has negatively impacted my credit profile and has caused financial harm.

Most recently, I applied to refinance my automobile loan from approximately 8.7 % interest to approximately 4 % interest while maintaining a similar remaining loan term. The refinance application was denied, preventing me from obtaining financing that would have saved me more than {$1000.00} in interest. I am also planning to refinance my home in the future and am concerned that Citi 's actions may continue to negatively impact my ability to obtain favorable financing.

I do not dispute that the payments were returned. However, I believe Citi failed to adequately consider the unique circumstances involved, including a fraud-related bank account freeze, my immediate efforts to resolve the issue, the payment being routed through an old bank account, my reliance on information provided by Citi representatives , and my otherwise exemplary payment history.

I respectfully request : 1. A full executive review of my account and the circumstances surrounding the returned payments.

2. A review of all call recordings and account notes related to this matter.

3. Reconsideration of the account closure based on my three years of perfect payment history and immediate corrective actions.

4. A written explanation of why my account was closed despite successful payment and repeated assurances that the account would be reviewed for reopening.

5. Any corrective action Citi determines is appropriate after completing its review.

I am seeking a fair review of this matter and a reasonable resolution based on the complete facts and circumstances.
label: account_closure
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
I am submitting this complaint because Bank of America continues to deny multiple disputes and fraud claims without conducting what appears to be a fair and reasonable investigation. 

Regarding my fraud claims, my phone, debit card, identification, and cash were stolen. I promptly reported the unauthorized activity after discovering it and later filed a police report. I provided Bank of America with the police report and supporting documentation showing that I was the victim of theft and that I did not conduct the disputed transactions. 

The claims are : * Claim ID XXXX Unauthorized ATM withdrawals totaling {$4700.00} * Claim ID XXXX Unauthorized transactions totaling {$2200.00} Despite the police report and supporting evidence, Bank of America denied my claims, reopened them, and then denied them again only days later using the exact same reasoning. The bank has not explained what additional investigation was performed after reopening the claims or how my police report and evidence were evaluated. 

I was informed that the ATM claim was denied based on account history, prior merchant relationships, mobile banking activity, and other account information. However, I was also informed that ATM surveillance footage was not reviewed. Bank of America denied approximately {$4700.00} in disputed ATM withdrawals without reviewing evidence that could potentially identify the individual who conducted the transactions. 

In addition, I was told by a Bank of America representative that I was a scammer and that the bank had records showing scams dating back years. I found these statements offensive, unprofessional, and prejudicial. Rather than focusing on the facts and evidence I provided, it appears conclusions were reached before a complete investigation was conducted. 

I am also requesting CFPB review of Bank of Americas handling of my plastic surgery-related disputes, which have been ongoing for many months. Despite providing medical records, imaging results, surgical records, and other supporting evidence, I have received no meaningful resolution. 

The disputed amounts include : * {$6900.00} XXXX  XXXX dispute * {$3000.00} XXXX  XXXX dispute * {$2500.00} XXXX  XXXX dispute These disputes total {$12000.00} and have been pending or denied despite substantial supporting documentation. text:
* * Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Complaint Against CitiBank * * I am filing this complaint against CitiBank regarding the closure of my accounts, the withholding of my funds, and the banks handling of multiple wire transfers, including a {$250000.00} outgoing wire recall initiated by the bank and a {$680000.00} incoming wire deposit that has been placed in question. 

CitiBank has closed one of my business checking accounts and two personal checking accounts. The bank has provided little to no meaningful explanation for these actions and has failed to communicate clearly regarding the status of my funds.

Following the account closures, CitiBank informed me that my funds would not be released for up to sixty ( 60 ) days and that remaining balances would be mailed to my home address by check. No justification has been provided for this extended delay, nor has the bank provided any meaningful explanation for why I am being denied timely access to my own funds.

The funds being withheld represent money I rely upon for personal living expenses and financial obligations, including housing, transportation, and other essential costs. The delay in releasing these funds has caused significant financial hardship and uncertainty.

In addition, CitiBank initiated the recall of a {$250000.00} wire transfer that had been sent from my business account for a legitimate investment opportunity. I did not request, authorize, or initiate the recall of this wire transfer. CitiBank froze my accounts and took action to recall the wire on its own initiative.

The {$250000.00} consisted of legitimate business funds intended for a lawful investment transaction. CitiBanks actions have prevented me from completing that investment and may have caused substantial financial damages and lost business opportunities.

Despite the significant amount of money involved, CitiBank has provided no meaningful information regarding the status of these funds. The bank has failed to explain where the funds are currently being held, whether the funds have been successfully recovered, whether they remain restricted, or when they will be returned to me. I have repeatedly sought clarification but have received no meaningful answers.

Additionally, a separate incoming business wire in the amount of {$680000.00} was deposited into my business account. I spoke with my CitiBank account manager in XXXX XXXX, California, XXXX XXXX, who informed me that it was too late for the sender of this incoming wire to recall those funds. 

If that is the case, I do not understand why my legitimately received wire funds are being frozen, and why all of my accounts have been closed. This contradiction has not been explained to me by CitiBank, despite my repeated requests for clarification. 

CitiBanks lack of transparency regarding my closed accounts, the outgoing {$250000.00} wire recall, and the incoming {$680000.00} wire deposit has caused extreme financial distress and uncertainty. While I understand that financial institutions may conduct reviews or investigations when necessary, consumers should not be deprived of access to their funds without clear explanations, meaningful communication, or a reasonable timeline for resolution.

I respectfully request that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau investigate this matter and require CitiBank to : 1. Provide a detailed explanation for the closure of my business and personal accounts.

2. Explain the basis for withholding my funds for up to sixty ( 60 ) days.

3. Provide a complete accounting of all funds currently being held.

4. Provide a full explanation of the {$250000.00} wire transfer recall initiated by CitiBank.

5. Confirm whether the {$250000.00} in wire funds has been recovered and identify where those funds are currently being held.

6. Provide a full explanation for the handling and restriction of the {$680000.00} incoming wire transfer.

7. Explain why account funds are being frozen despite confirmation from my CitiBank account manager, XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX, California, that the incoming wire recall period had already expired for the sender. 
8. Provide a firm and immediate timeline for the release and return of all funds belonging to me.

9. Release any funds not legally required to remain restricted without further delay.

I also request that CitiBank provide an immediate written accounting of the location and status of the {$250000.00} wire transfer, identify the reason for initiating the recall, confirm whether the funds have been recovered, and provide a definitive timeline for returning those funds to me.

The complete restriction of my personal and business accounts, combined with the handling of multiple large wire transfers, has caused substantial financial hardship, disruption to my business operations, and significant emotional XXXX  I am seeking immediate assistance in obtaining answers and restoring access to my funds as quickly as possible. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

text:
To : U.S. Bank Executive Consumer Relations / Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Subject : Formal Dispute : Unfair Forfeiture of {$2300.00} in Earned Rewards Due to Sudden Account Termination Loyal Customer Since : XXXX Impacted Credit Cards : Personal Cards ending in XXXX & XXXX | Business Card ending in XXXX 1. Executive Summary I am writing to formally dispute the unfair and predatory forfeiture of my earned cash-equivalent assets following a sudden, unprompted decision by U.S. Bank to terminate our banking relationship. The bank immediately seized a combined total of XXXX personal rewards points ( valued at {$2200.00} ) and {$93.00} in business rewards points. U.S. Bank confiscated a grand total of {$2300.00} from me without providing a reasonable 30-day wind-down period to redeem them.

2. Background of Customer Relationship I have been a deeply loyal, high-value customer with U.S. Bank since XXXX. Over this 15-year relationship, I trusted U.S. Bank with my entire financial footprint, maintaining a checking account, savings account, multiple Certificates of Deposit ( CDs ), and multiple credit cards. I spent significant capital to accumulate these points in good faith, strictly adhering to all terms of service.

3. Chronology of Event and Financial Damage The Shutdown : I recently received an abrupt notice dated XX/XX/XXXX stating that U.S. Bank was cutting off its banking relationship with me effective on XX/XX/XXXX. ( Please find attached copy of the letter ) No window of time was provided to transition my proprietary rewards or offboard my accounts.

Asset Seizure : Upon closing my credit cards, U.S. Bank wiped out XXXX points on my personal card ending in XXXX, XXXX points on my personal card ending in XXXX, and {$93.00} in points on my business card ending in XXXX. 

Customer Service Runaround : On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted customer support to request a courtesy check or point reinstatement. Representatives bounced me between multiple departments, offering zero resolution and claiming the rewards were " forfeited and not guaranteed. '' 4. The Core Dispute & Regulatory Argument While credit card issuers maintain the right to close accounts at their discretion, invoking a standard " discretionary closure clause '' to permanently confiscate over {$2300.00} in consumer-earned assets without notice constitutes an Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Act or Practice ( UDAAP ) under the Consumer Financial Protection Act of XXXX. 
Substantial Injury : Wiping out {$2300.00} in accrued financial value causes me substantial monetary injury.

Not Reasonably Avoidable : Because U.S. Bank terminated the accounts suddenly and without warning, I had zero opportunity to avoid this injury by redeeming my points.

Lack of Countervailing Benefit : There is no consumer or competitive benefit to the financial marketplace that justifies a bank seizing XXXX XXXX XXXX of earned consumer rewards during a unilateral shutdown.

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding serious account handling errors, lack of reasonable accommodation for a medically incapacitated member, improper account closures, credit reporting harm, and extremely distressing actions taken by Navy Federal Credit Union. I was an existing member of Navy Federal Credit Union and maintained a checking account, savings account, and credit card. In an effort to help manage family finances, I attempted to add my mother as a joint owner on my existing accounts. I followed the instructions provided through Navy Federals online banking system to complete this request. Approximately 34 days later, my mother received a welcome email indicating that Navy Federal had opened separate, individual accounts in her name, which was never requested or authorized by either of us. Our intent was only to add her as a joint owner to my existing accountsnot to create new standalone accounts. Although Navy Federal eventually placed my mother as a joint owner on my account, the unauthorized individual account remained open. Shortly thereafter, my mother obtained a {$14000.00} loan from Navy Federal. This loan was intended specifically to assist with my financial situation. However, Navy Federal deposited the loan proceeds into her individual account, not the joint account we shared. At the time this occurred, my mother was undergoing treatment for XXXX XXXX and was hospitalized for XXXX. Because she wanted me to have access to the loan funds as intended, she began transferring money from her individual account into our joint account. After approximately {$5000.00} was transferred, Navy Federal abruptly froze and closed her individual account without explanation. When we contacted Navy Federal, we were told that my mother would need to appear in person at a branch to resolve the issue. We repeatedly explained that this was impossible due to her active XXXX treatment and hospitalization. Despite being informed of her serious medical condition, Navy Federal refused to offer any reasonable accommodation.

Navy Federal then proceeded to : Close my mothers individual account with the remaining loan funds still inside Close our joint account Restrict all access to account information and balances Leave my credit card balance inaccessible, with no ability to make payments or even view what was owed As a result of Navy Federals actions, both my mother and I were placed in financial limbo with no access to funds, no way to make payments, and no visibility into our obligations. Despite this situation being entirely caused by Navy Federals account errors and closures, they began reporting negative information to the credit bureaus for both of us, causing significant damage to our credit reports. Most distressingly, Navy Federal escalated this matter by involving a social worker, who came to my residence looking for my mother. After I informed them that she was hospitalized and undergoing XXXX, Navy Federal ( or its agent ) proceeded to track her down at the hospital, subjecting her to extreme emotional distress during an already critical medical situation.

At no point did Navy Federal demonstrate compassion, offer reasonable medical accommodation, or take responsibility for their initial account setup errors. This situation has caused severe financial harm, credit damage, and emotional distress to a seriously ill individual and her family.
label: account_closure

text:
I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding the handling of my Mattress Firm credit account issued through Synchrony Bank. I am extremely disappointed in how this matter was handled and the consequences that resulted from misinformation provided by your representatives. 
This situation involves a transaction processing error between two Mattress Firm store locations, followed by incorrect guidance from Synchrony customer service, which ultimately led to the closure of my account and the loss of my promotional 0 % interest financing, despite my account being in good standing and my payment history being on time. 

In XX/XX/year>, I attempted to make a purchase at the Mattress Firm location in XXXX, Indiana for a mattress for my granddaughter. My son was physically present in the store while I attempted to make payment over the phone using my Mattress Firm credit card. 

The transaction did not go through. I was told by the store that I would need to go to a physical store location with my card to complete the purchase. 

A representative then asked which store I would visit and stated that the request would be sent there. 

Because I live in Illinois, I went to the Mattress Firm store in XXXX, Illinois and completed the purchase in person the same day. 

During this transaction, the store representative struggled multiple times to process my payment because he did not know how to properly run the Mattress Firm credit card. He repeatedly attempted to run it as a Visa/Mastercard transaction , and had to call for assistance before the payment was finally processed. 

Based on this experience, I believe the same processing error likely occurred when I initially attempted the purchase over the phone at the XXXX location, which explains why that transaction did not complete. 

When my statement generated, I noticed : Four pending transactions from XXXX, Illinois One completed transaction from XXXX, Indiana This was extremely confusing because I never completed a purchase in XXXX. My only completed purchase occurred in XXXX, Illinois when I physically went into the store with my card. 

Naturally, this raised concerns that I had been incorrectly charged. 

I contacted Synchrony customer service to explain the situation and was instructed by your representative to file a dispute. 

Following the instructions provided by your representative, I filed the dispute. 

Later, I received notice that the dispute had been declined. 

When I called again, I spoke with a supervisor who informed me that I would need to contact the store directly to resolve the issue. At that time, the supervisor removed most of the pending transactions from my account, however one pending transaction for {$270.00} still remains. 

Eventually, I reached a supervisor at the XXXX Mattress Firm location, who was the only person able to clearly explain what had actually occurred. 

She informed me that : The XXXX store took credit for the purchase because the transaction request was transferred to the XXXX XXXX The pending XXXX transactions were temporary authorization holds that would fall off Only one finalized transaction should remain This explanation made complete sense and clarified the entire situation. 

However, this information should have been clearly explained by Synchrony customer service before instructing me to file a dispute. 

Despite my account being in good standing and my payment history being on time, my account has now been closed due to risk management. 

As a result of this closure : My available credit history and limit have been negatively impacted My monthly payment has increased My 0 % promotional interest financing has been removed I am now being charged interest on a balance that was previously under a promotional financing agreement This outcome is completely unjust, particularly because the dispute that appears to have triggered the closure was filed only because your representative instructed me to do so. 

Had the transaction processing been properly explained from the beginning, no dispute would have ever been filed. 

The handling of this matter raises serious concerns regarding compliance with federal consumer protection laws, including : Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) Consumers have the right to dispute billing errors without fear of retaliation or punitive account actions when acting in good faith.

Truth in Lending Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq. ) Creditors must clearly disclose and honor the terms of promotional financing agreements.

Unfair or Deceptive Acts or Practices ( UDAP ) Providing incorrect guidance to consumers that leads to adverse account action may constitute unfair servicing practices.

In this case, I acted in good faith and followed the instructions provided by your representatives, and I should not be penalized for doing so.

Additionally, there is still one pending transaction for {$270.00} that has not been removed, further demonstrating the confusion surrounding the transaction processing.

I have included screenshots of the pending transactions for your review.

Anyone reviewing these charges would reasonably find the activity confusing, which is exactly why I contacted customer service in the first place.

In light of the circumstances described above, I respectfully ask that Synchrony review this matter and consider the following actions to resolve the situation fairly : 1. Reopening my Mattress Firm Synchrony credit account 2. Restoring the original promotional 0 % interest financing associated with the purchase 3. Removing the remaining pending transaction from my account 4. Correcting any negative credit reporting that may have resulted from this situation 5. Reviewing the guidance provided by customer service representatives to ensure customers are given accurate information when seeking assistance I would also greatly appreciate the opportunity to speak with a member of the Synchrony Executive Office so that this matter can be discussed and resolved appropriately.

It is my understanding that this situation resulted from a combination of transaction processing between store locations and the instructions I received when contacting customer service for assistance. At every step, I acted in good faith and followed the guidance provided to me.

I have always maintained my account responsibly and in good standing, which is why this outcome has been particularly disappointing. I truly hope that after reviewing the circumstances, Synchrony will agree that this situation warrants reconsideration.

Thank you for taking the time to review my concerns. I appreciate your attention to this matter and look forward to your response.
label: account_closure

text:
I applied for and closed a residential construction loan with First Interstate Bank in XX/XX/XXXX for a new home build in Idaho. What followed was not a routine construction dispute. The loan process, draw administration, disclosure handling, and document controls appear to have broken down in ways that exposed me to improper disbursements, inaccurate loan records, undisclosed conflicts, and mechanics lien exposure. 

At closing on XXXX XXXX XXXX, the loan documents classified approximately {$55000.00} in excavation and foundation-related construction costs as soft costs. These were hard construction expenses, not ordinary administrative or closing costs. That classification appears to have allowed funds to be advanced without the controls that should have applied to construction draws. From that funding sequence, {$19000.00} was later paid to XXXX XXXX by Check # XXXX. Records also indicate that XXXX XXXX was in a registration lapse period during part of the relevant work timeline. 

A second major concern is that First Interstate loan officer XXXX XXXX ( XXXX # XXXX ) appears to have performed dual roles on this file. Records identify her as the loan XXXX, while email evidence shows she also handled processor-type functions, including loan conditions and credit-rescore directives. That overlap appears to have compromised basic segregation-of-duties safeguards on a file where funds were routed to a subcontractor owned by her spouse, XXXX XXXX of XXXX XXXX XXXX The same record also shows that the construction contract used to support the loan was executed on XX/XX/XXXX, while the contractor entity, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, did not legally exist until XX/XX/XXXX. 

The draw process then continued in a way that caused further harm. On XX/XX/XXXX, First Interstate funded a draw of {$98000.00}, including {$70000.00} specifically allocated to the lumber package and {$23000.00} for framing, siding, and labor. Despite that, Builders XXXX remained unpaid for months, later asserted an unpaid balance in the {$48000.00} {$49000.00} range, and recorded a lien against the property. At the same time, forensic reconstruction reflects transfers totaling at least {$15000.00} from the contractors primary operating account into XXXX secondary accounts, along with tax payments from that same account. That sequence raises serious concern that loan proceeds earmarked for construction materials and labor were not promptly or properly applied to the vendor obligations they were supposed to satisfy. 

There were also serious disclosure and document-integrity problems. XXXX personnel appear to have withheld federally required closing and disclosure documents and facilitated electronic document handling that did not match what the borrowers believed they were signing. The available records further reflect that DocuSign XXXX logged signatures from an IP address that conflicted with the borrowers physical location and carrier profile at the time. In addition, the DocuSign audit history later recorded that on XX/XX/XXXX, documents were PURGED from account - Requested by sending account during active litigation. A consumer should be able to rely on a bank and its settlement and title partners to preserve accurate loan records, provide required disclosures, and maintain a trustworthy audit trail. 

I also believe I was deprived of meaningful notice and the protections that should accompany a construction-loan closing. The record indicates that escrow and title personnel withheld key federally mandated documents tied to disclosure timing and rescission-related protections, and that material aspects of the loan and draw records were misclassified, altered, or later destroyed. These failures left me exposed to contractor nonpayment, mechanics liens, and a loan file that does not appear to reflect a transparent or properly controlled lending process.

This complaint is not about dissatisfaction with construction quality alone. It concerns a federally regulated mortgage and construction-loan process that appears to have involved : ( 1 ) misclassification of draw categories, ( 2 ) conflicted loan administration, ( 3 ) funding tied to a contractor entity that did not yet legally exist when the contract was signed, ( 4 ) failure to ensure loan proceeds were applied consistently with their stated draw purposes, ( 5 ) withholding of required loan and escrow disclosures, and ( 6 ) later destruction or purge of audit-trail documents relevant to the loan.
label: account_closure
