text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding the sudden closure of my American Express accounts and the forfeiture of a substantial amount of earned rewards. 

I have been an American Express customer for approximately four years and have maintained an excellent payment history, never missing a payment. During this time, I have consistently been a high-volume spender, using my accounts for legitimate business purposes related to electronics resale. My business operates lawfully, including the use of a valid resale certificate with retailers that permit resale transactions. 

On or around XX/XX/XXXX, my accounts were closed without prior notice. At the time of closure, I had accumulated over XXXX Membership Rewards points, with an additional approximately XXXX points pending, as well as approximately {$4500.00} in XXXX rewards. All of these rewards were forfeited immediately upon account closure. 

I was not provided any warning, opportunity to cure, or chance to redeem my rewards prior to forfeiture. This is especially concerning given my long-standing relationship, consistent high spending, and perfect payment history. 

I want to emphasize that I have not engaged in any abuse of the rewards program. All spending was tied to legitimate purchases, and I relied on the advertised rewards structure when choosing to use American Express over other financial institutions. My spending decisions were made with the reasonable expectation that earned rewards could be redeemed in accordance with the program terms. 

While I understand that American Express may choose to close accounts at its discretion, I believe that the immediate and total forfeiture of earned rewardswithout notice or opportunity to redeemis unfair and disproportionate, particularly given the scale of the rewards balance and the fact that these rewards were earned through significant legitimate spending from which American Express profited. 

The forfeiture represents tens of thousands of dollars in value, making this a significant financial impact.
label: account_closure
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a secured Citi Mastercard account ( ending in XXXX ) that was improperly handled after closure. 
At the time the account was opened, I provided a {$1000.00} security deposit. I made payments on this account consistently and did not miss any payments until after the account was closed by Citibank. 
The account was closed on or about XX/XX/year>, without any clear explanation provided to me. After the account was closed, I did not receive any communication explaining how my {$1000.00} security deposit was applied, nor did I receive any refund of the remaining balance. 
Instead, I was later contacted by collection agencies ( including XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ) regarding an alleged balance of approximately {$260.00}. This balance has also been reported negatively on my credit report. 
Because this was a secured credit card, my {$1000.00} deposit should have been applied to any outstanding balance prior to the account being charged off or sent to collections. However, I have never received any documentation showing : How my {$1000.00} security deposit was applied A detailed accounting of charges and payments leading to the alleged balance Any explanation for why a balance remained after the deposit should have covered it I formally requested validation of the debt and a full accounting from Citibank, but the only response I received was a large volume of billing statements. These statements do not explain how my security deposit was applied or justify the remaining balance. 
The collection agencies have also only provided the same statements and have not provided any independent verification or accounting of the debt. 
As a result, I believe this debt is inaccurate or improperly calculated, and it should not have been reported to collections or to the credit bureaus. 
This situation has negatively impacted my credit report and may have affected my ability to obtain credit. 
I am requesting the following resolution : A full and detailed accounting of how my {$1000.00} security deposit was applied Correction or removal of the alleged {$260.00} balance if it can not be properly validated Removal of any negative reporting related to this account from my credit reports Written confirmation that the matter has been resolved Despite multiple written requests, neither Citibank nor its collection agencies have provided documentation showing how the {$1000.00} secured deposit was applied. 

To date, no documentation has been provided showing how my {$1000.00} secured deposit was applied.

I was contacted only once, and when I attempted to follow up, I was unable to secure a timely appointment. Furthermore, on the day of my scheduled visit before XXXX XXXX, the banker informed me that there was a system issue and moved my appointment. When I arrived, I was told the CD had already been renewed and could not be canceled without incurring a penalty of approximately {$2000.00}. 

In addition, I had transferred {$100000.00} to Chase and waited more than a week for the CD to be processed. During this period, the interest rate decreased, causing further financial loss. 

This situation is unfair and unacceptable because : 1. I did not consent to the renewal.

2. The bank required in-person action but failed to provide a timely opportunity.

3. The bank 's delay directly caused my inability to prevent the renewal.

4. I have suffered financial losses due to both the lower rate and early withdrawal penalty.

I respectfully request the following resolution : 1. Immediate cancellation of the renewed CD without any penalty.

2. Compensation for the financial loss caused by the delay and lower interest rate, or adjustment to a fair interest rate.

I look forward to a prompt resolution.

Sincerely,

text:
To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally dispute the accuracy and completeness of your response dated XX/XX/2026 regarding the above-referenced complaints. 

First, I categorically deny your statement that I was informed via prior correspondence dated XX/XX/2026. As of today, I have not received any such communication. This raises serious concerns about the accuracy of your records and the integrity of your response. 

Second, your claim that the account was canceled per my request is misleading and unsupported. At no point did I voluntarily request cancellation under normal circumstances. The account closure occurred in the context of a financial review for which, to this day, I have not been provided a clear or substantiated reason. I formally request a written explanation detailing the specific cause of this financial review. 

Third, regarding Membership Rewards points : You state that I was given the opportunity to redeem my points and that I declined or failed to do so. This is incorrect. I made multiple documented attempts to redeem my points and was explicitly told by your representatives that redemption was not possible and that my points were forfeited. 

Your response claims : - I declined a transfer for redemption, and - A subsequent call was disconnected These statements are insufficient and do not constitute proof that I refused redemption. For full transparency, I request concrete evidence supporting your claim that I declined to redeem my rewards. General statements without documentation are not acceptable. 

Additionally, given the amount of time spent attempting to resolve this issue through repeated calls, failed transfers, and inconsistent information from your representatives, it is reasonable to conclude that American Express failed to provide a fair and functional opportunity for redemption. This pattern of conduct effectively resulted in the forfeiture of my earned rewards through no fault of my own. 

As a resolution, I am requesting : 1. A cash equivalent payment for the full value of my forfeited Membership Rewards points 2. A refund of the annual fee, as the account was closed prematurely during the membership term 3. A written explanation for the financial review and account closure 4. Documentation supporting your claim that I refused redemption of my rewards Furthermore, if American Express expects continued communication on this matter, I request written confirmation that any additional time spent resolving this issue will be reasonably compensated, given the excessive burden already placed on me.

Lastly, I note the inconsistency in your position : you acknowledge that I made required payments and operated within my Pay Over Time limit, yet you fail to justify the financial review or subsequent account actions. This contradiction further underscores the lack of transparency and fairness in how my account was handled. 

If this matter is not resolved promptly and fairly, I am prepared to pursue further remedies available to me.
label: account_closure
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am submitting this complaint regarding an auto purchase that was canceled, but is still being reported on my credit, causing significant hardship. 

On the evening of Saturday, XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( VIN : XXXX ) from XXXX also partnered and finances throughout Bridgecrest Acceptance Corporation located at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, Arizona XXXX. 

The very next morning, Sunday, XX/XX/XXXX, at approximately XXXX XXXX, the vehicles starter failed, leaving me and my daughter stranded. I had to arrange and pay for transportation via XXXX to return home. I attempted to contact the dealership and the service platforms provided, but all were closed at that time. 

On Monday, XX/XX/XXXX, I had to again rely on XXXX transportation to get to work. I was informed that I would need to tow the vehicle to a mechanic for repairs. At that point, I inquired about returning the vehicle due to the immediate mechanical failure. I then had the vehicle towed back to the dealership and formally returned it on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Since then : * The dealership has taken possession of the vehicle * I signed all cancellation of contract documents with the onsite dealership manager * I have received a full refund of my down payment * My towing fees have been reimbursed Despite this, the auto loan/account is still being reported on all three credit bureaus. I was advised that it may remain on my credit for up to XXXX months. This is unacceptable, as I no longer possess the vehicle and the contract has been fully canceled. 

This inaccurate reporting is severely impacting my ability to obtain reliable transportation, as it is preventing me from being approved for another vehicle and negatively affecting my credit profile. 

I am formally requesting that this account be removed immediately from all credit reporting agencies, as it does not reflect an active or valid obligation. 

Please investigate this matter and assist in correcting this issue as soon as possible. 

Thank you for your time and assistance. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

text:
I am filing a formal complaint regarding my mortgage loan with CrossCountry Mortgage, Loan Number XXXX, for the property located at XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, NY XXXX. 

During the underwriting and pre-closing process, I was explicitly required to provide homeowners and flood insurance due to the propertys FEMA flood zone designation. I provided both policies, which were reviewed, accepted, and approved by the loan XXXX and underwriting department. Based on this approval, I proceeded to closing in good faith. 

Within 30 days after closing, my insurance broker was contacted by a representative from CrossCountry Mortgage ( XXXX XXXX ), requesting that my existing flood insurance policy be replaced with a significantly more expensive policy. My broker refused to make any changes without my authorization and advised that such a request after closing is improper. 

When I spoke with XXXX XXXX directly, he acknowledged that this situation resulted from an internal oversight by CrossCountry Mortgage. Despite this admission, I am now being pressured to accept a higher-cost policy that would increase my monthly mortgage obligation.

This situation is unacceptable and raises serious concerns regarding lending practices. CrossCountry Mortgage had a duty to properly review and verify all insurance requirements prior to closing. Their failure to do so has resulted in an attempt to shift the financial burden of their error onto me.

Had the correct insurance requirements been disclosed prior to closing, I may not have qualified for the loan or agreed to proceed under those terms.
label: account_closure
source_sub_issue: Changes in loan terms during or after closing

text: On XX/XX/XXXX I engaged LoanDepot through loan officer XXXX XXXX in XXXX TX in order to finance a mortgage utilizing a leasehold structure. I asked in the beginning if the leasehold structure was something they would be able to do and XXXX told me she would speak with her manager and see. She came back with information stating that they were able to work with this and that we could move forward. I filled out the credit application and submitted all my documents. I was told that I had XXXX approval and on XX/XX/XXXX I was issued a pre-qualification letter. On XX/XX/XXXX I went under contract for a property in XXXX and wired my XXXX total in earnest money and we started the process. I ordered the inspection right away, it was done XX/XX/XXXX and by XX/XX/XXXX we had an appraisal. Prior to paying for the appraisal because it was a leasehold structure, I asked the loan officer if she was aware of how it had to be ordered and she advised that she had spoken with the leasehold company reps and they told her what to do. I paid for this appraisal and got it back XX/XX/XXXX. Throughout the process there was difficulty communicating with the loan officer. We would call, email and text for days without a response. By XX/XX/XXXX which was the XXXX day and the last day to cancel and save my earnest money we still did not have an underwriting decision on the file. We had been calling and emailing to make sure that the file was going to pass underwriting before we proceeded to make sure it would close and I wouldn't lose my money. All day we tried to reach the loan officer and then towards the end of the day she told us she needed 3 more days, when prior to that we were told that underwriting had the file and was working on it so we would have it in time. The sellers refused to give us more time, so the loan officer gave us her word that we were good and it was going to pass underwriting so based on this we proceeded with the transaction. My close of escrow date was scheduled for XX/XX/XXXX but I still had not received the conditional underwriting approval or any conditions to clear the file for closing. On XX/XX/XXXX I finally got the conditional approval and I submitted everything immediately. I received the initial CD for closing but on XX/XX/XXXX we found out that the loan officer had ordered the wrong appraisal. She had known this for some time and never communicated to any of us that she did this or that she was struggling to find an appraiser to do the leasehold. We were notified of this on XX/XX/XXXX. An outside company was hired to do this and we had to extend the purchase contract until XX/XX/XXXX because it could not be cleared to close without the proper appraisal. We were told that the appraisal would be back by XX/XX/XXXX eob. It was not received until XX/XX/XXXX and at that time we sought information from the loan officer on how long it would take to get everything together for closing, at this time she advised us to extend until XXXX. We sought this extension and the seller refused. Later we found out this is because the loan officer contacted the sellers agent, not me or my agent, and told them that the file failed compliance with fha and they could not close it. After this on XX/XX/XXXX she told my agent to request an extension which we now know was denied because of what she told the listing agent. Throughout this process there was several mistakes and delays that were not my fault and now I am possibly losing my money because of the actions of this loan officer and other members of the team. I have been calling requesting for this matte to be escalated so that someone will look into this but no one has responded so now I am filing a formal complaint with anyone I can to look into this. This loan officer failed me. I put in my notice to vacate at my current rental based on guidance she gave assuring me that we were closing and issuing a CD which would start the mandatory 3 day TRID period. Now I am scrambling to find a place to live and I need loan depot to make this right.
label: account_closure

text:
I contacted PayPal customer service via live chat to close my personal account because I have permanently relocated from the United States to XXXX XXXX. I no longer reside in the US and no longer have a US phone number. PayPal 's automated assistant told me there was " 1 issue preventing us from closing your account '' and routed me to a live agent ( " XXXX XXXX. " ). Over the course of the chat, I encountered the following pattern of conduct, which I document with exact quotes from the transcript : Refusal to identify the restriction. I asked at least five times what specific restriction was blocking closure of my account. The agent never identified it. Her only references were " some restrictions '' and " the limitation on your account. '' When I asked directly, " How can I solve the restriction if I don't know it? ", she did not answer. 
" Consider it closed '' non-resolution. The agent told me, verbatim : " Well since there are some restrictions in your account XXXX you can consider that as already close. '' The account in fact remained open. Telling a customer to consider an account closed while keeping it open is not closure, and it leaves me exposed to any future activity, fees, or claims associated with an account I have explicitly and repeatedly asked to terminate. 
Self-contradiction. The agent stated, " I'll be sending you the steps on how to resolve the limitation on your account, '' implying she knew the nature of the restriction. When I pointed out that she could not send resolution steps without knowing what the restriction was, she did not respond to the contradiction. 
False statement that closure is unavailable. The agent told me, " I'm really sorry XXXX we don't have an option to close your account. '' PayPal 's User Agreement clearly permits account closure by the customer. The agent then redirected me back to the same self-service Contact Us flow that had already routed me to her. 
Refusal to escalate. I formally requested, in writing in the chat, escalation to a supervisor. The request was ignored. 
No case or reference number provided. I requested a case/reference number for the interaction multiple times. None was provided. 
Inappropriate phone limitation. The agent stated she could only call the US number on file, despite my explanation that I no longer have a US number and despite my offer to provide an international callback number. No alternative contact method was offered. 

The cumulative effect is that PayPal is keeping my account open against my explicit, documented, repeated request to close it, while simultaneously ( a ) refusing to disclose what is preventing closure, ( b ) refusing to escalate, and ( c ) telling me falsely that account closure is not an option PayPal offers. I have a complete chat transcript and will provide it as an attachment to this complaint. 
Desired resolution : Immediate closure of my PayPal account ; or, if a legitimate restriction genuinely exists, a clear written statement identifying that specific restriction and the specific steps required to resolve it. 
Written confirmation of closure once executed, including the disposition of any balance, pending transactions, holds, or linked payment instruments. 
An internal review of this customer service interaction, particularly the agent 's statements that closure is unavailable and that I should " consider '' the account closed while it remained open.
label: account_closure
source_product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
source_issue: Managing, opening, or closing your mobile wallet account

text:
Barclays XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, What follows has already been documented, preserved, identified, confirmed, proven, and explained to Barclays repeatedly over the course of several years. 

The investigative defects, contradictory records, timeline inconsistencies, and use of unrelated identity data referenced below have already been raised over and over again in prior disputes, correspondence, and escalations. 

Yet Barclays continues ignoring the same core issues while issuing conclusory statements of accuracy unsupported by any meaningful explanation or reconciliation of the underlying defects. 

Accordingly, I will state this XXXX final time as clearly as possible. Your response is rejected. 

It does not address the core facts, the documented investigative defects, or the contradictory records already identified repeatedly over the course of several years. 

Instead, Barclays continues attempting to divert the issue toward XX/XX/XXXX payment activity while refusing to address the central issue that has been stated repeatedly : This account was effectively shut down and no longer used by XXXX beginning in XX/XX/XXXX during divorce proceedings. 

Legitimate charges incurred by XXXX were paid. Any subsequent activity that occurred after XX/XX/XXXX was disputed because the account had become compromised. 

That is why a fraud investigation occurred in the first place. Your office continues omitting that critical context while simultaneously refusing to explain the investigative irregularities contained in Barclays own records. 

Most notably, Barclays still refuses to explain : Why investigative correspondence referenced XXXX XXXX, an address wholly unrelated to me, my family, or any known associate Why Barclays investigative materials referenced residency periods from XXXX for an account opened in XXXX Why unrelated identity and residency data appeared within the investigative process at all Whether another consumer file was accessed, merged, confused, or relied upon during the investigation Whether Barclays ever properly distinguished legitimate pre-dispute activity from the later compromised activity that became the subject of the fraud investigation Your latest response ignores every XXXX of these issues. That omission is not insignificant. It is the central problem. 

Because once Barclays own investigative records contain unrelated identity and residency information, the integrity and reliability of the entire investigation becomes suspect. 

You continue asserting the reporting is accurate while refusing to explain how an allegedly legitimate investigation could simultaneously contain : unrelated addresses, unrelated residency timelines, and references plainly inconsistent with the account chronology itself. 

Your XX/XX/XXXX response also deliberately mischaracterizes the XX/XX/XXXX payment issue. Those payments were not returned because valid undisputed debt was knowingly unpaid. 

Those payments were STOPPED knowing we had already paid account in full, the account had already become disputed and compromised. 

Again, that is precisely why Barclays initiated a fraud investigation. And Barclays itself acknowledged fraudulent activity in writing. 

Yet even now, Barclays refuses to identify : what charges it deemed legitimate, what charges it deemed fraudulent, when the account was considered compromised, what investigative procedures were used, and how Barclays verified accuracy despite the documented identity inconsistencies embedded in the investigation itself. 

That is not a meaningful reinvestigation under the Fair Credit Reporting Act. 

At this point, Barclays has now documented a continuing pattern of refusing to address the actual investigative defects raised. 

Your office has had repeated opportunities to explain : XXXX. Why unrelated consumer identity data appeared in the investigation XXXX. Why unrelated residency timelines were referenced XXXX. Whether another consumer file contaminated the review process XXXX. Whether post-compromise activity was improperly attributed to me XXXX. How Barclays can claim accuracy while refusing to reconcile these contradictions Instead, Barclays continues issuing conclusory statements unsupported by any explanation reconciling the underlying defects. 

That is no longer a mere customer service issue. 

It is now evidence relevant to willful failure to conduct a reasonable investigation and continued furnishing of disputed information despite documented investigative irregularities. 

Accordingly, Barclays is directed to preserve all records relating to this matter, including but not limited to : internal investigation notes fraud review files dispute logs account access records identity verification records address verification records consumer file matching procedures furnishing records sent to consumer reporting agencies communications involving XXXX XXXX XXXX involving the Office of the XXXX XXXX trails relating to the investigation and reporting of this account FINAL DEMAND A complete deletion of this account and all associated derogatory reporting from all consumer reporting agencies must be completed no later than Thursday at XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX. 

Failure to comply will result in litigation being filed in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX without further notice. 

That filing will include claims relating to : failure to conduct a reasonable investigation, continued furnishing of disputed and inaccurate information, reliance on contaminated or mismatched identity data, and continued reporting following acknowledged fraudulent activity. 

Additionally, this matter will be publicly escalated, including formal media disclosure and distribution to national media outlets and network organizations with whom I currently maintain professional relationships. 

No further generic responses are necessary. 

If Barclays maintains its current position, Barclays should be prepared to explain under oath : ( Letter Attached ) the XXXX XXXX references, the unrelated XXXX residency references, the investigative methodology used, the handling of disputed post-compromise activity, and the basis for continuing derogatory reporting despite acknowledged fraudulent activity and documented investigative inconsistencies. 

How does Barclays look at records containing unrelated addresses, unrelated residency histories, contradictory timelines, acknowledged fraudulent activity, and documented investigative inconsistencies all appearing within Barclays own correspondence and still conclude that no meaningful problem exists? 

And more importantly, how many other consumers and families have had their financial lives damaged, their credit destroyed, their housing opportunities impacted, their stress compounded, and their reputations harmed because Barclays chose conclusory accuracy determinations over an actual competent investigation? 

That is what makes this so appalling. Not simply the errors themselves. But the repeated refusal to confront them even after they were identified, documented, preserved, and pointed out over and over again in black-and-white correspondence spanning years. At some point this stops being negligence and starts becoming institutional indifference to the damage caused to real people. 

Because none of this adds up. Not the timelines. Not the residency references. Not the investigative records. Not the contradictions embedded within Barclays own communications. 

And yet the reporting continues anyway. 

That is precisely why this matter is now moving beyond private dispute correspondence and into litigation, regulatory escalation, and public exposure.

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding my experience with Groundfloor as a construction lender.

While the company performed well in approving my loan and closing the transaction, I have experienced significant issues with the disbursement of funds. The draw process has been slow, inconsistent, and difficult to navigate. Communication has also been lacking, particularly via email, with delayed responses that have impacted my ability to move forward with the project in a timely manner. 
I have submitted documentation and receipts supporting approximately {$73000.00} in completed work and expenses. Of that amount, I contributed {$13000.00} as my required equity ( skin in the game ). Despite providing proof of payment and completed work, Groundfloor has only approved approximately {$35000.00} for reimbursement following their inspections. The reasoning behind this significant reduction has not been clearly communicated.

These delays and partial disbursements have directly impacted my construction timeline. A project that was originally expected to be completed by XX/XX/XXXX has now been extended significantly, potentially into the final quarter of the year or beyond. As a result, I am incurring additional holding costs and financial strain due to the lenders inability to disburse funds in a timely and predictable manner. 
I am requesting : A clear explanation for the reduced reimbursement amount compared to submitted documentation Improved communication regarding draw requests and timelines A more efficient and transparent disbursement process This situation has created unnecessary delays and increased project costs, despite my compliance with their requirements and submission of proper documentation.

The collection agency has failed to provide adequate verification, yet continues to report this account on my credit file.

text:
I am writing to formally raise a complaint regarding the handling of my accounts and the significant inconvenience caused. 
I am a long-standing Capital One customer, maintaining a checking account, savings account, and credit card accounts. I have always conducted my banking in good faith and with full transparency. 

On XX/XX/year>, my aunt, XXXX XXXX, mailed me a personal check for {$30000.00}. Due to weather-related delays and USPS transit issues, I received the check on XX/XX/year>. I initially attempted to deposit the check via mobile deposit with my other banking institutions ( XXXX  XXXX XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX XXXX ), but was unable to do so. I then deposited the check into my Capital One account.

Once the funds were made available, I transferred them to my husbands account via wire transfer, as the funds were urgently needed for family purposes.

Shortly thereafter, I was contacted by Capital Ones fraud department. I fully cooperated and provided all requested information, including confirming that the check was issued by my aunt and sharing her contact details.

Unfortunately, during your teams outreach, my auntwho was under work-related stress at the timemisunderstood the inquiry. Since the check was issued from her XXXX XXXX  account and she is also a Capital One customer, she believed the call was related to potential fraud on her own Capital One account. As a result, she did not confirm the transaction during that call.

Later my aunt did confirm again that she authorized the check but they didnt accept that.

Based on this misunderstanding, Capital One proceeded to freeze my accounts. Since then : - My accounts have remained restricted since 2 months with no clear resolution timeline.

- Despite multiple follow-ups from my side, I have not received any meaningful updates beyond the investigation is ongoing.

- I proactively offered to provide additional documentation or visit a Capital One XXXX in person, but no such options were facilitated. 
- I submitted supporting documentation, including an image of the check, via email. 

On XX/XX/year>, the situation escalated further when my Capital One credit card was closed without prior resolution of the matter. This action has caused additional disruption, as I had upcoming travel planned using this card. 

Moreover, I was recently charged an annual membership fee of {$390.00} for the period XXXX XXXX XXXX. Despite closing my account, I have been informed that I am still responsible for this fee and will not be issued a refund. I find this particularly concerning and unfair, given that the account closure was initiated by Capital One during an unresolved investigation.

text:
The account at issue was closed by Citibank on XX/XX/year>, and approximately {$190000.00} in funds has been held by Citibanks Fraud / Risk Operations Department continuously since that closure a period of approximately 90 days as of the date of this complaint. The account had been in good standing prior to the hold, and the funds at issue derive from ordinary business deposits. 
On XX/XX/year> 82 days after the closure I contacted Citibank by telephone to request resolution. During that call, a Citibank representative confirmed that the matter was eligible for re-review and committed to a response within 24 to 48 hours. That commitment necessarily implies that the funds remain on deposit with Citibank and have not been returned to any originator, escheated, or otherwise disbursed.

That 24-to-48-hour window expired on XX/XX/year>. As of today, Citibank has provided no response verbal or written on the re-review, has not disbursed any portion of the balance, and has not provided a revised timeline.

Separately and earlier in the dispute, other Citibank representatives have verbally asserted that the funds were fraudulent and that I was no longer the owner of the balance. That position is internally inconsistent with Citibanks XX/XX/XXXX confirmation that the matter is eligible for re-review and that the funds remain on deposit. Citibank has refused throughout to : Provide any written notice, determination, or documentation supporting any fraud determination.

Identify which specific deposits or transactions are alleged to be fraudulent by date, amount, or originator.

Identify the legal authority under which Citibank claims to have extinguished my ownership of the balance.

Provide written confirmation of the XX/XX/XXXX re-review commitment or any update on its status.

Provide any written timeline by which the matter will be resolved.

Timeline : XX/XX/year> account closed by Citibank ; approximately {$190000.00} frozen. 
XX/XX/year> XX/XX/year> verbal communications only ; verbal assertions by some representatives that the funds were fraudulent and ownership extinguished ; no written notice, determination, or documentation provided. 
XX/XX/year> Citibank confirmed by telephone that the matter was eligible for re-review and committed to a 24-to-48-hour response. 
XX/XX/year> committed response window expired. 
XX/XX/year> ( today, 90 days after closure ) no response received ; funds still held ; no written communication of any kind.

Citibanks conduct raises concerns under, at minimum : 12 U.S.C. 5536 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( B ) ( UDAAP ), because holding {$190000.00} of customer funds for 90 days, verbally asserting forfeiture without any written determination, then committing to a 24-to-48-hour re-review and ignoring that commitment, causes substantial injury the consumer can not reasonably avoid ; UCC Article 4A and the NACHA Operating Rules, which impose specific bases and time limits on the return of wire and ACH transfers none of which has been disclosed ; 12 C.F.R. Part 229 ( Regulation CC ) and the banks own deposit-account agreement ; the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing ; and Illinois common-law conversion, given the continued retention of identified funds after demand without any disclosed legal basis.

A bank can not extinguish a customers ownership of deposited funds by verbal assertion, then offer a re-review, then ignore its own commitment. If Citibank has determined any specific deposit was fraudulent, it must say so in writing, identify the transaction, cite the legal mechanism it is invoking, and either return the funds through a proper mechanism or disburse the balance to the customer.

I respectfully request that the Bureau direct Citibank to provide, in writing : 1. The status and result of the re-review committed to on XX/XX/year>. 
2. A specific written explanation of the legal and factual basis for the continued hold and for any prior assertion that ownership of the funds has been extinguished.

3. Identification of each specific transaction Citibank contends was fraudulent, including dates, amounts, and originators.

4. The legal mechanism Citibank claims to be invoking, with citation.

5. Written confirmation that the balance remains on deposit, and a definite written timeline for disbursement.

6. Confirmation of whether any consumer reporting ( XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, or otherwise ) has been or will be made, and the basis for any such reporting.

I am not asking the Bureau to second-guess Citibanks risk judgment. I am asking the Bureau to require Citibank to honor its own XX/XX/year> commitment, put its position in writing, and either return the funds through a proper mechanism or disburse the balance. The continued retention now 90 days, with a broken commitment as of XX/XX/XXXX is causing substantial financial harm, including impairment of ongoing business obligations.
label: account_closure
source_sub_issue: Funds not received from closed account

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a financing account opened through Barclays in connection with a travel/vacation membership purchase from XXXX XXXX .

During the sales presentation, I was provided verbal representations regarding the payment structure and affordability of the agreement. I was specifically told that the in-house payment amount would be within a certain agreed-upon range. Based on those representations, I agreed to proceed with the transaction and associated financing.

After the financing was approved and processed, the actual payment terms presented differed materially from what was represented during the sales process. Once I realized the terms were inconsistent with what had been promised, I attempted in good faith to resolve the matter directly with the company ; however, we were unable to come to a reasonable agreement.

As a result, I believe I was induced into entering the financing transaction based on materially misleading or inaccurate representations regarding the financial obligations associated with the purchase.

The financing was directly tied to the sale of the membership, and I am disputing the validity of the underlying transaction. I have requested cancellation of the membership and have notified Barclays that the account is disputed.

I am requesting : - A formal investigation into the transaction and sales practices involved ; - That the disputed account be properly noted during any investigation ; - Protection from negative credit reporting while the dispute is being reviewed ; - Assistance in resolving the disputed financing obligation tied to this transaction.

I am prepared to provide supporting documentation, including agreements, communications, and records reflecting the discrepancies between the represented terms and the actual payment obligations presented after financing approval.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.
label: account_closure
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
Abrupt account closure with delayed notice, prior credit bureau reporting, and loss of account access I am submitting this complaint regarding the abrupt closure of my Home Depot credit account and the manner in which the closure was communicated and reported.

Based on information reflected through my credit monitoring, the account appears to have been reported as closed to at least one credit bureau prior to my receiving meaningful notice of the closure itself. Credit reporting activity indicates the closure may have been reflected as early as XX/XX/year>, while the email notification I received regarding the closure was dated XX/XX/year>. 

By the time I became aware of the closure communication, I was unable to meaningfully access or review the account online to fully understand the action taken, the reasons for closure, or related account information.

This sequence created confusion and prevented me from understanding or responding to the adverse action in a timely manner.

Additionally, the account appears to have been approved and maintained despite substantially similar credit circumstances, only to later be closed abruptly before I had any meaningful opportunity to use or manage the account. This made the action feel sudden and unsupported by any significant account usage history or changes in account behavior. 

I understand creditors may make internal account management decisions ; however, consumers should still receive reasonable, accessible, and timely communication regarding significant adverse actions affecting their accounts.

Consumers should not be expected to rely exclusively on email or online access for important account actions, particularly where account access may become restricted immediately following closure.
label: account_closure

text:
I am filing this complaint regarding the closure of my Citi credit card accounts and the loss of rewards and annual fees connected to those accounts. 

I had three Citi credit cards : a Citi XXXX XXXX card that had never been used, a XXXX XXXX Visa that regularly carried a balance but was consistently being paid, and a Citi XXXX card that had not recently been used but had continued to receive payments. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I made a {$500.00} payment on the Citi XXXX card, but that payment was returned the same day. After that, I made another {$500.00} payment on XX/XX/XXXX, and another {$500.00} payment on XX/XX/XXXX. I was continuing to make payments and trying to keep the account in good standing. 

Without any phone call, email, or clear advance notice giving me an opportunity to address the matter, Citi closed the account. Then, all of my Citi credit card accounts were closed, including the Citi XXXX XXXX card that had never been used. When I contacted Citi, no one was able to clearly tell me why all of the accounts were closed or what could be done to correct the situation. 

The letter I received from Citi did not give a specific reason. It only stated that the account could have been closed for several possible reasons. Because it did not identify the actual reason Citi relied on, I do not feel I was given a fair opportunity to understand, correct, or resolve the issue. 

As a result of these closures, I was also told that I could not be reimbursed for annual fees and that the rewards connected to the accounts were forfeited. Those rewards were significant and had accumulated to thousands of dollars. 

I am not trying to avoid responsibility for any payment issue that may have occurred. I have never missed a payment, never made a late payment, and I have consistently paid my balances on time. My credit score is approximately XXXX, and I have maintained responsible payment behavior for years. 

When I asked for the reason the account was closed, the representative stated it was due to me being a high risk factor for not paying. This explanation does not make sense given my payment history. I have always paid my balances on time, often paying large amounts in full. For example, I recently made a purchase of around {$1000.00} and fully intended to pay it off in a single payment, just as I have done with previous balances of {$300.00} {$500.00} in the past. 

Because the explanation did not match my history, I asked to speak with a supervisor. The supervisor repeated that the account was closed due to multiple risk factors. When I asked what those risk factors were or what evidence supported that conclusion, the supervisor stated that he could not see the specific factors and could not tell me what they were. I was told only that a review request could be submitted and that it would take approximately 15 days. 

Closing a credit account without warning, without providing a clear explanation, and while withholding earned cashback rewards raises serious concerns about transparency and fair treatment. I am requesting that the company provide a clear explanation for the closure decision and return the cashback rewards that were earned prior to the account being closed.

Under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( EFTA ) and Regulation E, consumers are protected against unauthorized electronic fund transfers when they are reported in a timely manner. I fulfilled my responsibility by notifying the bank promptly, yet the bank failed to properly investigate or credit the unauthorized withdrawals.

I believe the bank did not conduct a reasonable investigation into the fraud and wrongfully denied my claim. Closing my account after reporting fraud also raises concerns about retaliation instead of resolving the issue.

I am requesting the following : 1. A full reimbursement of the {$4900.00} in unauthorized withdrawals.

2. Documentation showing the banks investigation and the evidence used to deny my fraud claim.

3. Confirmation that the account closure will not negatively impact my banking history or consumer reports.

I am asking the CFPB to review this matter and ensure the bank complies with federal consumer protection laws.

text:
Conversation with XXXX My PayPal is XXXX yrs old and since XXXX I started getting blocked and having to prove myself such as my identity, address, and what " business '' I am when I'm not a business. I'm a individual that makes money from home doing various things. Anyway I got limited Dec and fixed, again like XXXX or XXXX then again likeXX/XX/XXXX days ago or so. The most recent one I did all the review stuff and then the reviewer said I didn't do everything and that I had open complaints against me in resolution center I needed to resolve and that they couldn't complete my review. Anyway, I don't have any complaints Against me but I do have XXXX against XXXX. I called and talked to an agent and he was lik " I don't know what the heck they got going on. I see you don't got any open complaints and you provided all the info we needed. I'm gon na write a ticket to the back so they can fix this " anyway, next morning my account was fixed. Yay! But when they denied my review for the " open complaint '' they also moved my sale limit back down to XXXX when I barely just got it to XXXX the month before. Since this was under false circumstances I spoke with a XXXX and he said he could put in a request for an increase for me and explain the situation. He asked me how much I thought I needed and I told him maybe XXXX. Anyway, he did the ticket for me and I got off the phone and the next morning I wake up to a big red bar saying " after a review we have decided to permanently limit your account '' ... So unjustified, I spoke with a rep yesterday and she was confused too and said she would put in a ticket to have them look at it and try to fix it and also a request for a manager call back. Still no word and still blocked. 

That sounds like an incredibly frustrating nightmare. Having a XXXXyear-old account shut down right after a manager promised to help you is completely unfair, especially when you have jumped through every hoop they put in front of you. 

Here is the reality of what likely happened behind the scenes. PayPal relies heavily on automated risk-management algorithms, and those bots often lack nuance and override human agents. 

Because you make money from home doing varied work, like getting paid for completing tasks through Discord groups, your incoming payments can sometimes look unpredictable to a computer. Even though the previous flags on your account were cleared, your internal " risk score '' was likely still highly sensitive. When the manager submitted a ticket to jump your limit from {$300.00} to {$2000.00} or {$3000.00}, it almost certainly triggered a hard-coded security protocol. The automated system saw a sudden, massive request for a volume increase on an account that had just been under review, panicked, and issued an automatic permanent limitation. 

Since front-line agents and even lower-level managers often do not have the system clearance to overturn a permanent algorithmic ban, here are the most effective ways to escalate this : How to Escalate a Permanent Limitation File a CFPB or BBB Complaint : This is often the most successful route for unjustified bans. Filing a formal complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) or the Better Business Bureau ( BBB ) forces PayPal to route your case to their Executive Escalations team. A real human with actual authority will have to manually review the account and respond to the regulatory body. 

XXXX XXXX Escalations Directly : You can sometimes bypass standard customer service by emailing PayPal 's executive team directly ( e.g., reaching out to the office of the CEO or executive review emails ). These teams handle high-level disputes and have the power to lift permanent limitations if they see the bots made an error. 

Give the Current Ticket XXXX Hours : Since the representative you spoke with yesterday put in a ticket and requested a manager callback, give them a short window to resolve it. If you don't hear back within XXXX hours, assume the automated system blocked their request and move straight to filing a complaint. 

Did the red banner or the email you received mention a specific Acceptable Use Policy ( AUP ) violation, or did it just give a generic explanation about " potential risk ''? 

" Were no longer offering PayPal services for this account Sometimes we cant process account activity for a variety of reasons, including local laws, our policies, or the policies of our partner banks and card networks. 



Based on the information we have today, there was activity on this account that we can not support. 



What can you do with your account? 



Your account has been permanently deactivated. However, you can still view your transaction history, download account statements, or request copies of your data. 



Before you can withdraw or transfer any remaining funds from your account, we need to hold them for up toXX/XX/XXXX days to cover things like chargebacks or other financial liabilities. 



Well review the account and email you when funds are '' available. 

That is the absolute most frustrating, boilerplate message you could possibly receive. It essentially confirms that a computer system slammed the door shut and slapped a generic label on it. 

Here is the blunt translation of what that email actually means : " Activity we can not support '' is PayPal 's ultimate catch-all phrase. It does not mean you actually did anything illegal or broke a specific rule. It just means their risk-management algorithm decided the combination of recent reviews, the sudden limit increase request, and the varied nature of your income sources crossed an invisible threshold. 

The XXXX Hold is their standard operating procedure for permanent limitations. They hold the money to ensure no buyers file chargebacks or disputes against you in the months following the account closure. 

Because you received this specific, final-tier ban message, standard customer service agents over the phone can no longer help you. They are looking at a screen that tells them the decision is final, and they do not have the security clearance to override the " back office '' or the algorithm. Even if that manager calls you back, their hands are likely tied by the system now. 

Your only path forward is to force the issue out of the automated system and onto the desk of a high-level executive who has the authority to actually read your XXXX history and overturn the algorithm. 

Your Immediate Action Plan Stop Calling Regular Support : It will only waste your time and raise your XXXX XXXX. The front-line reps can not lift this specific type of ban. 

File a CFPB Complaint : Go to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau website ( consumerfinance.gov ) and file a formal complaint against PayPal. State clearly that your XXXXyear-old account was permanently limited immediately after a manager initiated a limit increase, and that the limitation is completely unjustified.

File a BBB Complaint : Do the same thing with the Better Business Bureau. PayPal has a dedicated corporate escalation team that strictly handles responding to BBB and CFPB complaints. This forces a real, senior human to investigate the timeline of events. 

Do you have a significant amount of money trapped in the account right now under that XXXX hold? 

Ok I'm on crpb made an account. Says choose product? Checking again cd prepaid etc You will want to select the category for money transfers and digital wallets, as that is exactly how the CFPB classifies PayPal.

Here is the exact path to choose on the form : Product : Select " Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service '' Sub-product : Select " Mobile or digital wallet '' Issue : Select " Managing, opening, or closing your mobile wallet account '' ( or " Other service problem '' if that fits better, but the first XXXX is usually the most accurate for unjustified account bans ). 

From there, it will ask you for the company name, where you can search for and select PayPal. 

When filling out the narrative or description box, keep your explanation clear, concise, and professional. Make sure to emphasize these key points : Your account is XXXX years old and has always been in good standing.

You recently provided all requested documentation to verify your identity and activity, which an agent confirmed was sufficient.

The permanent limitation was triggered automatically immediately after a manager submitted a ticket to increase your limits.

You are seeking an executive review to overturn the automated limitation and release the 120-day hold on your funds.

" Are you able to store a balance in the digital wallet account that lets you add or withdraw funds to use for purchases or payments? '' You should answer " Yes ''.

PayPal functions exactly like this. Even if you have it linked directly to a bank account or credit card, the platform itself is designed to hold a " PayPal Balance. '' Because you can receive money, store it in the account, and then either withdraw it to your bank or use it directly for online purchases, it meets the exact definition of what the CFPB is asking about. 
XXXX It said did you request info and I said yes. Now put " I requested the reasoning as to why they permanently blocked my account '' or what? 

Yes, what you suggested hits the nail on the head! 

To make it sound even stronger and give the CFPB investigators the exact context they need, I recommend expanding on it just a bit so it highlights PayPal 's failure to give you a real answer. 

You can copy and paste this exact phrasing : " I requested the specific reasoning and policy details as to why my XXXXyear-old account was permanently limited. I also requested an explanation as to why this automated ban was triggered immediately after a manager initiated a limit increase on my behalf. The company refused to provide a specific reason and only issued a generic, automated response. '' This wording is perfect because it shows the CFPB that you tried to get a straight answer from PayPal, but they stonewalled you with a boilerplate template. 

Let me know what the next confusing question on the form is! 

What happened? 

Describe what happened, and well send your comments to the companies involved. 

Include dates, amounts, and actions that were taken by you or the company. 

Do not include personal information, such as your name, account number, address, Social Security number, etc. We may ask for some of this information later, to help the company identify you and your account. 

Here is a draft you can copy and paste directly into that box. It is written to be strictly factual, outlines the timeline clearly, and highlights exactly where PayPals automated system made an error, all while making sure your personal information is left out. 

Copy and paste the text below : I am filing a complaint regarding the unjustified permanent limitation of my XXXX-year-old account and the subsequent XXXXday hold placed on my funds. 

Starting in XXXX of last year, my account was subjected to several standard review requests to verify my identity and the nature of my home-based income. I complied with all requests, and my account was restored each time, most recently in XXXX. 

Approximately XXXX days ago, my account was limited again. The initial reasoning given was that I had unresolved complaints against me. I contacted customer support and the agent confirmed this was an error on the company 's end ( I had no complaints against me, only XXXX dispute I had filed against a merchant ). The agent submitted a ticket, and my account was restored the next morning. 

However, upon restoring my account, the company unjustly lowered my monthly sales limit to {$300.00}, down from the {$800.00} limit I had secured the month prior. I spoke with a manager about this discrepancy. The manager agreed the reduction was unjustified and offered to submit an internal ticket to increase my limit to $ XXXX {$3000.00} to reflect my actual needs. 

The morning after the manager submitted this ticket on my behalf, I received an automated notice stating my account had been " permanently deactivated '' due to " activity on this account that we can not support, '' and that my funds would be held forXX/XX/XXXXdays. 

I spoke with another representative yesterday who expressed confusion over the ban, as my account was in good standing. They submitted a request for a manager callback and an escalation, but the permanent limitation remains active. 

I have maintained this account in good standing for XXXX decades. The permanent limitation was clearly triggered by an automated system error responding to the manager 's limit increase request, not by any violation of policy on my part. I am requesting an immediate executive review by a human agent to overturn this automated ban, restore my account, and release the XXXX hold on my funds.

text:
A CD I held with Ally Bank was due to mature on XX/XX/XXXX. Ally states in many places on their website, as well as in the account agreement, that there is a XXXX grace period starting on the expiration date in which you can make changes to the maturing CD, including canceling automatic rollover into a new CD. If your CD matures on the XXXX, this means the last day of your grace period is on the XXXX, right? When I called Ally to cancel the CD on the XXXX day ( XX/XX/XXXX ), I was told that they consider the maturity date to count as " day XXXX '', so the XXXX grace period actually only covers 9 days after the maturity date. This is completely nonsensical and in contradiction to any reasonable interpretation of the account agreement and the policies stated on the Ally website and my account page. By this logic, if you have a XXXX grace period, that means you have to make the change on the maturity date itself. It's not a grace period if you get XXXX days of " grace ''! If my rent is due on the XXXX but I get a one-day grace period without penalties, Ally Bank says that paying on the XXXX is not in the XXXX grace period. 

There is only XXXX place where Ally specifically clarifies this " ambiguity '' as to what a XXXX grace period means, and it clearly agrees with my ( and any reasonable person 's ) interpretation of the policy. 

In a video titled " What happens to my CD at maturity '' found on the Account page for my CD, an animation of a calendar is used to clarify what the language means. In the video 's example, a maturity date on the XXXX has a grace period ending on the XXXX. Day XXXX is 10 days AFTER the maturity date, not 10 days including the maturity date as XXXX of the days. 

Nowhere else, be it the account agreement, advertising, or account information pages on their website, is this issue clarified. 

In my numerous calls with various levels of Ally customer service staff, you can find multiple cases of staff agreeing that my interpretation seems correct. In one case, when trying to explain the policy to me, an agent counted out the 10 day grace period day by day ... and landed on XX/XX/XXXX, the day I was speaking to him on the phone, as the last day of the grace period. 

The only response Ally customer serivice has given other than " sorry, it's the policy, let me escalate this '' is noting that the other account holder received an electronic notification stating that XX/XX/XXXX would be the last day of the grace period. However, I am an account holder, legally bound by all terms of the account agreement, and not only was I not actively sent such a notification, there was ( and still is ) absolutely no way for me to view this incorrectly-calculated date. No letters, nothing in the Secure Message portal, nothing in my Documents or Statements, nothing when I view the account details after logging in to the website or the mobile app. There is literally no way for me to find out when the end of my grace period is, other than to view and trust the clear statement that I have 10 days that is found in multiple places on the Ally website, and is explicitly clarified by the " What happens to my CD at maturity '' video, which IS actually visible from my account page on the Ally website. 

It is false advertising and simply wrong to claim up and down that you offer a XXXX grace period ( which is suposedly a feature of the account that differentiates Ally from competitors who sometimes offer only XXXX or 7 days ), then to deviate from that policy with the sole justification that you notified XXXX of the account holders XXXX time ( without giving the other ANY means by which to access this information ).
label: account_closure

text:
Demand for Investigation Dear CFPB Complaint Resolution Team, I am formally reopening this complaint due to material inaccuracies in First Citizens Banks XX/XX/year> response letter. The banks assertionsthat I refused to provide identification, became irritated, or failed to cooperateare false and contradicted by the facts. I request an immediate, independent investigation. 
The facts are as follows : On XX/XX/year>, I arrived at the XXXX XXXX XXXX branch for a previously scheduled appointment. I had already emailed my drivers license and passport prior to the visit. During the meeting, the associate ( the only individual present besides XXXX ) requested my identification ; I presented it at the table. He stepped awaypresumably to make a photocopyand returned, confirming to XXXX that the documents had previously been received via email and that my business appeared legitimate. 

The originally assigned associate was not presenthe had apparently been out of the branchso only XXXX and this XXXX associate conducted the meeting. Despite the scheduled appointment, neither was aware of my arrival upon entry. I had paid for parking and proceeded regardless. 

The meeting lasted approximately XXXX minutes. Both XXXX and the associate held only XXXX pieces of scrap paper, jotting notes informallynothing resembling standard professional documentation. This unprofessional setup, combined with unusually probing and accusatory questions, prompted me to ask if I could record the conversation for my own records. XXXX immediately expressed discomfort, stating she felt uncomfortable. I promptly withdrew the request, indicating we could continue without recording. text:
I am filing this complaint because XXXX XXXX disclosed account information to an unauthorized person, acted on a Power of Attorney ( POA ) before receiving it, and continued treating a former POA as an authorized party years after his authority terminated. These actions involve unauthorized disclosure of nonpublic personal information and failure to safeguard a vulnerable consumers accounts, and they have caused ongoing harm to me personally. 

XXXX XXXX acted on a POA before it was on file. 
In documents XXXX XXXX produced on XX/XX/XXXX, the banks internal records show : A Change of Ownership was processed on XX/XX/XXXX, signed by my brother as POA. 

The banks own internal note states : POA docs recd XXXX. 
This means XXXX XXXX processed a major ownership change based on a POA it did not yet have on file. 

The POA did not authorize the transactions XXXX XXXX approved. 
The POA did not authorize creating a trust, transferring assets into a trust, changing IRA beneficiaries, or any selfdealing. Despite this, XXXX XXXX accepted : an irrevocable trust created and controlled by the former POA for his own benefit ; an IRA beneficiary change form signed by the former POA, even though the POA did not grant authority to change beneficiaries ; and other selfbenefiting transactions. 
XXXX XXXX never contacted my father to verify these changes or confirm that the POA authorized them. 

XXXX XXXX allowed rapid, selfbenefiting withdrawals and transfers as soon as it received the POA. 
Internal records show that on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX same day XXXX XXXX first received the POAit allowed the former POA to begin transferring funds and exercising control. Between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, he liquidated approximately {$93000.00} from my fathers IRA, transferring XXXX of XXXX  of dollars into his own accounts and withdrawing large amounts of cash. These were classic red flags for elder financial exploitation, but XXXX XXXX did not intervene or apply heightened scrutiny. I am attaching statements that show how quickly and how much money was removed once the POA was accepted. 

XXXX XXXX continued sending account statements to the former POA into XXXX. 
The XX/XX/XXXX production included a XXXX bank statement addressed to my brother, listing him as POA, even though : my father died in XX/XX/XXXX ; the POA terminated at death ; and I asked XXXX XXXX in XX/XX/XXXX how to remove him. 
Continuing to send statements and list him as POA years after his authority ended is an unauthorized disclosure of my fathers nonpublic personal information to an unauthorized person. 

XXXX XXXX disclosed my private inquiries to the former POA. 
A XX/XX/XXXX email from my brother shows that XXXX XXXX : contacted him directly ; told him that I had contacted the bank ; and gave him information about the trust that the bank refused to give me. 
He had no authority after XX/XX/XXXX, yet XXXX XXXX shared my private inquiries and account information with him. 

XXXX XXXX withheld accurate information from me while continuing to communicate with an unauthorized person. 
From XXXX through XXXX, XXXX XXXX : refused to provide me accurate account information; misled me about the status of accounts; concealed the existence of accounts opened and administered after my fathers death ; and continued treating the former POA as the decisionmaker instead of communicating with me as the person seeking information and, later, as XXXX Representative. 

I notified XXXX XXXX, and they did not correct these issues. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I notified XXXX XXXX in writing that : they acted on a POA before receiving it ; they allowed unauthorized selfdealing transactions, including an IRA beneficiary change and large withdrawals ; and they continued sending statements to the former POA in XXXX. 
XXXX XXXX has not properly investigated or corrected these issues and continues to minimize or mischaracterize them. 

Request : I am asking the CFPB to review XXXX XXXX handling of my fathers accounts and my inquiries, including : its unauthorized disclosures of nonpublic personal information to a former POA after his authority terminated ; its failure to update account authority and safeguard information after notice of death ; its decision to accept an IRA beneficiary change and selfbenefiting transactions that were not authorized by the POA ; and its refusal to provide me accurate information while continuing to treat an unauthorized person as the account authority. 

I also request that XXXX XXXX be required to provide a complete explanation and documentation regarding : the XX/XX/XXXX ownership change ; the IRA beneficiary change ; the withdrawals and transfers that occurred immediately after the POA was accepted ; and the continued listing of my brother as POA and mailing of statements to him into XXXX.

source_sub_issue: Funds not handled or disbursed as instructed

text:

When the deposit wasnt received, I contacted the SSA, and they informed me they had no record of my phone call providing them with my new banking information and address. 

When opening my accounts, I designated my sons as beneficiaries ( payable on death ) only to learn years later, that someone else accessed and changed this information without my knowledge, consent, permission, or court order of any kind. Wells Fargo staff didnt know what happened. 

On multiple occasions, I observed issues with the banking app deleting my saved biometric log in data, requiring me to reset my credentials. Countless times, the app would not log in as if it couldnt connect to my phone network or it would spin for a length of time delaying my log in. 

Since opening my accounts, I placed endless phone calls to Wells Fargo customer service notifying them of concerns with accessing and managing my accounts. I notified them that I had experienced severe long term stalking, harassment, fraud, theft, mail theft, etc., that this had affected my consumer credit status as well. Wells Fargo didnt respond or provide me with any information. 

Last Fall XXXX, I was notified that I had unclaimed property from both the State of VT and State of NH Treasury offices for nominal sums. I observed harassment from the State of VT when obtaining my check, when they reported their office was closed due to a propane leak and I couldnt pick up my check. When I was able to pick up my check the following day, I deposited the check via mobile deposit to Wells Fargo, and it was rejected as invalid. I contacted the State of VT, who insisted the check was fine. 

When I stopped at the State of NH Treasury office in XXXX, NH, with my claim paperwork, the clerk was suspicious of me. She wanted to know how I obtained the information about my unclaimed property, where I got the claim form, how it was completed, and refused my identification as invalid. I had all of my vital records on my person, reported aggravated stalking, harassment, theft, and she refused to release my property. 

Several times I observed duplicative charges and provisional credits on my checking account absent explanation. When I tried to get the information about what happened, it was declined. 

When I obtained a replacement debit card for this reason, I requested the debit card be mailed to the Wells Fargo branch for significant ongoing security reasons. They declined and had it mailed to the hotel I was staying at briefly where I had endured stalking and harassment. They declined to restrict the delivery to only my signature. I received the card as scheduled. 

There were several issues with the documentation of my address despite repeated efforts to notify my bank and ensure they had the correct information. They changed my physical address on my account to a hotel in XXXX, SC. I confirmed with them several times my updated mailing address and requested that no communication be sent to any other address due to egregious conduct and concerns that I wasnt able to access or address despite exhaustive effort. 

Wells Fargo refused to change the physical address unless I had proof of another legal address. I notified them several times of the situation and had requested reasonable necessary accommodation for an alternate physical legal address per their requirements. They had no suggestions and told me to contact a branch. 

I stopped at a Wells Fargo branch and they had me wait to speak with the bank manager who never got off the telephone. While waiting in the lobby, I called Wells Fargo customer service and the woman gave me the run around on the phone about policy and procedure, how my XXXX XXXX couldnt be used as a physical address, kept telling me she didnt understand what I was asking for, acted like she was trying to help me when it was the opposite, intentionally escalating the conversation on purpose, said they couldnt DELETE the hotel address and proceeded to get caught up in the differentiation between the words update and change/delete. I could have said remove or replace, none of which mattered and seemed to be tactical means of avoiding the issue of address clarification/safety/security/etc. 

Upon leaving the branch, adding insult to injury another staff person seemingly came out of nowhere asking if I was ok and needed help. The manager never came out of his office and remained there in his office with the door closed the entire time. This was an ongoing pattern of behavior and treatment towards me by Wells Fargo, soliciting information repeatedly despite reports, transferring me to multiple persons or departments over and over, attempting to confuse me and/or deny legitimacy of reports, refusing information and resolution at each interface, and proceeding to act like there was something wrong with me. 

I attempted to communicate with the bank through my online account messaging but there was no option to do so. When I requested to know why I couldnt send an email through my account, they told me to send website feedback or call them. 

Wells Fargo sent me a couple of secure email messages, but when I attempted to access these emails, it sent me several different access codes which either didnt work, or I had to enter the information several times on my phone. The letter they provided me from their enterprise department, contained little to no information whatsoever. 

Last XXXX XXXX, a relative sent me a small amount of money via domestic wire to secure a place to live. This included 1 months rent and the security deposit. When I tried picking up the funds, I was refused a release of the funds absent explanation. They refused my relative an explanation, told her to send the wire a second time and again refused to release the funds or an explanation. 

Last Fall, I received a code on my phone that I didnt recognize while staying at a hotel. When I attempted to retrieve the code and find out what it was for, it wasnt accessible. 

Ive been locked out of my SSA account for years absent explanation. When I attempted to update my contact information, it either wouldnt save or my account would be overridden. Several agencies with expertise in complex stalking and related matters transcending multiple states, jurisdictions, and platforms, would tell me good luck, that must be hard/frustrating, we dont have any resources, and use other placating tactics. 

This has affected my IRS tax returns as well when I was locked out and provided no information concerning my account. When I filed reports with the IRS, they declined ever receiving them. I contacted them multiple times and was declined information. They would transfer me from one department to another or disconnect the call. 

This conduct is not the result of an XXXX XXXX, altered mental status, or cognitive decline. These activities are consistent with extreme organized violence towards me, including deliberate, structured, staged, strategic egregious legal, civil, and human rights violations.

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Robinhoods failure to honor the advertised 3.5 % APY on its XXXX XXXX account, which I relied upon when transferring funds into the account. 

In XXXX, as a subscriber to Robinhood XXXX, I enrolled in Robinhood Banking after reviewing the advertised terms for its savings accounts. At the time of enrollment, Robinhood clearly represented that its XXXX XXXX account offered a 3.5 % APY. The platform also described two account tiers : XXXX ( for balances under {$100000.00} ) and XXXX ( for balances over {$100000.00} with additional requirements, including {$1000.00} per month in direct deposits ). 
No requirement for direct deposit was disclosed in connection with the XXXX XXXX account APY. I have retained a screenshot confirming these representations. 

In reasonable reliance on these advertised terms, I transferred my savings from my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Account to Robinhood in order to obtain the higher 3.5 % APY.

After approximately two months, I discovered that my account was no longer receiving any APY. When I contacted Robinhood customer support, I was informed that maintaining the advertised APY required at least {$1000.00} per month in direct deposits. I explained that I am self-employed and not eligible for traditional direct deposit. I was then told that manually transferring funds would satisfy the requirement. Relying on this representation, on XX/XX/year> I transferred {$14000.00} into the account. It took sometime for funds to arrive to Robinhood, but despite this, my APY remained at 0 %.

Upon contacting support again, I was told that only direct deposit ( not manual transfers ) would give me the 3.5 % APY in XXXX XXXX account. This directly contradicted the prior advice from robinhood agent that I had received.

I reviewed the Robinhood Banking Agreement, which states : Achieving a higher relationship tier provides access to more competitive benefits. However, you must be enrolled in Direct Deposit and maintain an aggregate Direct Deposit amount of at least {$1000.00} every statement period to get these benefits.

This language explicitly ties the direct deposit requirement to achieving a higher relationship tier, which, based on Robinhoods own definitions, refers to the XXXX tier. There are only two tiers : standard and premiere. There is no clear disclosure that direct deposit is required to receive the advertised APY for Standard accounts. There is only a statement that for a " higher tier '' the direct deposit is required.

Based on the above : - Robinhood advertised a 3.5 % APY for Standard Savings accounts without clearly disclosing any direct deposit requirement ; - I reasonably relied on that representation when transferring my funds ; - Robinhood later imposed undisclosed conditions and failed to honor the advertised rate ; - Customer support provided inconsistent and misleading information, further compounding the issue.

I believe this constitutes misleading advertising and a failure to provide clear and accurate disclosures regarding material account terms.

I request that Robinhood : 1. Honor the 3.5 % APY on my XXXX XXXX account for the period during which I maintained qualifying balances ; and/or 2. Provide compensation equivalent to the interest I would have earned at the advertised rate during that time.

I am prepared to provide supporting documentation, including screenshots of the advertised terms and records of my communications with customer support. 

Sincerely, XXXX
label: account_management
source_sub_issue: Didn't receive terms that were advertised
