text:
I am filing this complaint as a follow-up to a previously closed complaint [ XXXX ] against Wells Fargo Bank. I am refiling because Wells Fargo 's official CFPB response contained a material factual error that invalidates their denial, and because my original filing was brief and unaccompanied by documentation. I am now providing the full picture with supporting attachments.

I. WHAT HAPPENED THE FRAUD In XX/XX/XXXX, I was targeted by a sophisticated multi-layered employment fraud scheme. I was referred by a known contact to " XXXX XXXX, '' presented as a legitimate employer ( XXXX XXXX  ) seeking an executive assistant immediately. I submitted my resume, completed a job application, and communicated via email and text throughout what appeared to be a standard professional hiring process. 
XXXX XXXX informed me he would be sending two cashier 's checks digitally to fund my home office setup, representing this as standard onboarding practice. I deposited both checks through Wells Fargo 's mobile deposit application. Wells Fargo accepted both deposits and made the funds available with no hold, no flag, and no warning. I relied on my bank 's acceptance of those deposits as confirmation of their legitimacy a reasonable expectation for any consumer.

Based on the available funds and direct instructions from my purported employer, I made three payments to " XXXX XXXX, '' represented as a company-partnered supplier : XX/XX/XXXX {$400.00} via XXXX XX/XX/XXXX {$430.00} via XXXX XXXX ( debit card ) XX/XX/XXXX {$400.00} via XXXX XXXX ( debit card ) Total : {$1200.00} These were not discretionary purchases. They were payments made under explicit fraudulent instruction, funded entirely by deposits Wells Fargo had accepted and cleared.

Two days after the deposits, Wells Fargo closed my checking account of nearly 30 years citing " altered checks '' with no prior warning, no hold notice, and no guidance. At that point, {$1200.00} in payments had already been made because Wells Fargo had made those funds available without detection. The supplies I paid for were never delivered. All parties XXXX XXXX, the referring contact, and XXXX XXXX ceased all communication. Every identity involved was fabricated.

II. WELLS FARGO 'S ROLE IN THIS LOSS Wells Fargo 's mobile deposit system accepted and cleared two altered cashier 's checks without detection and made those funds available to me. Had Wells Fargo 's system flagged or placed an extended hold on those instruments, I would not have made a single payment. The bank 's failure to detect altered instruments at the point of deposit was a direct and proximate contributing cause of my total financial loss. This is not addressed anywhere in Wells Fargo 's response.

III. THE DISPUTE PROCESS FAILED ME I filed a dispute on XX/XX/XXXX. The claim was opened and closed the same calendar day with no outreach to me, no review of my submitted documentation, and no written denial explanation. On XX/XX/XXXX, I spent the majority of the day on calls with at least eight Wells Fargo representatives, each of whom confirmed the claim was still open. It closed immediately following those calls, just after XXXX PST. I also submitted comprehensive documentation email correspondence, text message screenshots, and payment records none of which was reviewed before closure.

A same-day closure without investigation, without provisional credit, and without written denial explanation does not satisfy the requirements of a reasonable investigation under Regulation E, 12 CFR Part 1005.11. Wells Fargo subsequently conducted an ECMO review in response to my escalation, which also failed to correct the errors identified below.

IV. WELLS FARGO 'S CFPB RESPONSE CONTAINS A MATERIAL FACTUAL ERROR This is the most critical issue in this refiled complaint.

Wells Fargo 's official CFPB response references " two XXXX XXXX transfers totaling {$830.00}. '' A Wells Fargo claims representative also stated repeatedly by phone on XX/XX/XXXX around XXXX PST that the transactions could not be reversed because they were made via " XXXX XXXX. '' This is factually incorrect. 
I have attached transaction screenshots that clearly show both payments were processed directly from my Wells Fargo debit card not from an XXXX XXXX account. XXXX XXXX  and debit card transactions are governed by different dispute frameworks, different chargeback rights, and different regulatory standards under Regulation E. If Wells Fargo investigated and denied these transactions under an XXXX XXXX classification, they investigated the wrong transaction type entirely. A denial built on a misclassified transaction is not a valid denial it is evidence that no proper investigation occurred.

I am requesting that the CFPB note this factual error as part of Wells Fargo 's official response record and require Wells Fargo to reinvestigate under the correct transaction classification.

V. WELLS FARGO 'S " NO FRAUD INDICATORS '' FINDING IS UNSUPPORTED Wells Fargo 's response states their investigation found " no indicators present confirming fraud. '' I submitted email correspondence, text message screenshots, and payment records documenting every stage of the fraudulent scheme fabricated employment, fraudulent cashier 's checks accepted by Wells Fargo 's own system, explicit payment instructions, promised delivery that never occurred, and complete disappearance of all parties.

Wells Fargo 's response does not identify what evidence they reviewed, what investigative standard was applied, or why fabricated professional identities, altered financial instruments cleared by their own bank, and a vanishing supplier do not constitute fraud indicators. A conclusion of " no fraud '' that does not engage the submitted evidence is not an investigation it is a predetermined outcome.

VI. WELLS FARGO DIRECTED ME TO CONTACT THE MERCHANT MULTIPLE TIMES Wells Fargo 's response and their Executive Office representative advised me to " resolve this with the merchant directly. '' This advice confirms that Wells Fargo either did not review my submitted documentation or chose to ignore it entirely. There is no legitimate merchant to contact. Every party involved the employer, the referral, and the supplier was a fabricated identity operating as part of a coordinated fraud scheme. Directing a documented fraud victim to contact a fictitious scammer is not a resolution. It is a deflection that further demonstrates the inadequacy of Wells Fargo 's investigation.

VII. THE AUTHORIZATION DEFENSE DOES NOT APPLY Wells Fargo 's position is that I authorized these transactions and therefore bear full liability. Authorization requires informed and voluntary consent. My consent was obtained entirely through fabricated employment, false professional identities, and fraudulent financial instruments. There was no legitimate transaction every element was manufactured to deceive me. Authorization induced by fraud is not valid authorization. The CFPB 's own guidance recognizes that " authorized '' does not mean " voluntary and informed '' in the context of scam-induced payments. Wells Fargo has not addressed this distinction in any of their responses.

VIII. SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION ATTACHED -Wells Fargo 's official CFPB response referencing XXXX  XXXX misclassification  -Debit card transaction screenshots rebutting the XXXX XXXX classification -Email correspondence documenting the fraudulent hiring process and payment instructions -Text message screenshots documenting payment instructions and delivery promises XXXX and XXXX XXXX  payment confirmations showing dates, amounts, and recipient Message documenting promised check reissuance after account closure Active complaint reference numbers : FBI IC3 [ XXXX ], FTC [ XXXX ] , OCC [ filed XX/XX/XXXX , awaiting case # ], California DFPI [ XXXX ].

I have been a Wells Fargo customer for nearly 30 years with no prior fraud history. I am requesting a thorough, lawful reinvestigation and the reimbursement I am entitled to under federal consumer protection standards.
label: account_management

text:
This complaint concerns a custodial/minor account opened in my name while I was a minor that Wells Fargo states was controlled by a custodian. Wells Fargo confirmed in writing that I owned the funds in the account while the custodian controlled the account. The account contained substantial funds with deposits totaling approximately {$360000.00}. 

After reaching the age of majority  on XX/XX/XXXX, I repeatedly sought assistance regarding the account and requested closure of the account, including appearing with a custodian. My requests were denied, and I was never informed of any required process, documentation, or steps necessary to complete the closure. 

At no time after I reached the age of majority did Wells Fargo proactively notify me of any alleged requirements necessary to obtain control of, close, or transition the account. During a XX/XX/XXXXXXXX   recorded call, Executive Office representative XXXX stated that she could see college accounts opened for me after I reached the age of majority, but that she could not see any documentation showing funds from the custodial account being transferred into those accounts. 

Additionally, on XX/XX/XXXX, a Wells Fargo banker informed me that after XXXX  XXXX, I should have received online access, a debit card, and control of the account. This directly conflicts with later statements from Wells Fargo representatives claiming I had no authority at all regarding the account. 

Despite Wells Fargo repeatedly referencing XXXX  XXXX and the age of majority during recorded conversations, the custodial account remained active and under custodial control until it was closed in XX/XX/XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, during a recorded call, Executive Office representative XXXX stated that although I did not have transactional authority, I did have authority to close the account after reaching the age of majority if I appeared with a custodian. XXXX further explained for the first time that the process would have required closing the custodial account, opening a new account, and transferring the funds into the new account. 

That explanation raises significant questions regarding how the custodian was later able to close the account and transfer the funds without my participation if Wells Fargos stated process required my participation to open a new account and receive the transferred funds.

However, Wells Fargo later reversed course in letters dated XX/XX/XXXXXXXX  and XX/XX/XXXX, and during an XX/XX/XXXXXXXX  recorded call with Executive Office representative XXXX, by stating that I had no authority at all and that all matters and documentation must be addressed solely with the custodian.

My requests were for clarification and documentation regarding the account structure, authority, policies, procedures, handling of the account, and the basis for the denial of my requests.

During one branch interaction, branch manager XXXX informed me that the funds were my fathers money, which directly conflicted with Wells Fargos later written statement confirming that I owned the funds in the account. 

My inquiries into the handling of the account were further prompted after I received correspondence regarding a life insurance benefit check issued payable to me in the amount of {$280000.00} that had been deposited into the custodial account. The correspondence advised that if I disputed the endorsement or handling of the check, the matter would need to be investigated through the bank. After attempting to seek guidance through local law enforcement, I was also advised that the matter would need to be addressed with the bank. This led me to pursue clarification from Wells Fargo regarding the handling, authority structure, and administration of the account. 

Wells Fargo has provided materially inconsistent explanations regarding this account through multiple employees and representatives, even though the account was allegedly closed in XXXX and its status should not have changed. I have now received different and conflicting explanations from multiple Wells Fargo employees regarding my authority, account access, required procedures, and account handling. These inconsistencies raise significant concerns regarding Wells Fargos administration, documentation, oversight, and explanation of this custodial account and the guidance provided to me after reaching the age of majority. 

Additionally, Wells Fargos repeated references to age XXXX and the age of majority during recorded conversations have raised significant questions regarding when custodianship over this account was supposed to end, what authority the custodian was permitted to retain after I reached the age of majority, and whether the custodian should have continued possessing transactional authority over the account after I reached the age of majority, including the ability to continue withdrawing, depositing, handling, and ultimately closing the account without my participation. 

Based on Wells Fargos descriptions of the account as custodial in nature and its repeated references to the age of majority, I am requesting clarification as to whether the account was governed under a XXXX-type structure and, if so, whether Wells Fargo properly applied, explained, and administered the requirements and authority associated with that structure after I reached the age of majority. 

At this point, Wells Fargos responses demonstrate inconsistent explanations of authority, failure to explain applicable requirements, refusal to clarify account structure and policies, unsupported refusal of documentation, unexplained denial of my requests, unexplained closure of the account by the custodian without my participation, and failure to provide a consistent and policy-based explanation regarding the handling and closure of an account in my name containing substantial funds.

text:
Subject : Complaint Against Wells Fargo for Wrongfully Withholding {$12000.00} Deposited Into Business Account I am submitting this complaint regarding Wells Fargo 's refusal to release {$12000.00} deposited into a newly opened business account, despite evidence that the check cleared the maker 's account and despite my repeated efforts to resolve the matter. 
On XX/XX/year>, I opened a Wells Fargo business checking account. At approximately XXXX XXXX., I deposited a check in the amount of {$12000.00} through Wells Fargo 's mobile deposit system. 
On XX/XX/year>, the check cleared the maker 's account at XXXX XXXX. Later that same day, I received an email from Wells Fargo stating that the deposited funds were being held and that my account was being closed. A letter dated XX/XX/year>, also stated that Wells Fargo had reviewed the deposit and decided to close the account effective XX/XX/year>. 
On XX/XX/year>, I contacted my local Wells Fargo branch and spoke with XXXX XXXX and Branch Manager XXXX. I was informed that Wells Fargo 's Fraud Department believed the deposited check was fraudulent. I explained that the check was legitimate and was asked to provide proof. I subsequently sent a copy of the cleared check. 
On XX/XX/year>, I emailed XXXX XXXX and advised that I had contacted the maker of the check. The maker confirmed that the check had cleared their account on XX/XX/year>. I attached a copy of the cleared check to the email. 
On XX/XX/year>, I called the branch and was told that Wells Fargo 's Fraud Department continued to claim that the check was fraudulent. I was asked to bring in the original check because the Fraud Department wanted to inspect it. I delivered the original check to the branch that same evening. 
On XX/XX/year>, I spoke again with branch personnel. I was told that despite Wells Fargo having possession of the original check, the Fraud Department still believed the item was fraudulent and would not release the funds. I was also told that once the account was closed, the {$12000.00} would be returned to me via cashier 's check. 
Also on XX/XX/year>, Wells Fargo sent a letter extending the account closure date to XX/XX/year>, stating that the deposited item required further investigation. 
On XX/XX/year>, Wells Fargo sent a letter stating that it would not return the {$12000.00} because I allegedly had no right to the funds. Upon calling the Fraud Department using the number provided in the letter, I was told that Wells Fargo would only speak with the bank on which the check was drawn and would no longer discuss the matter with me. 
On XX/XX/year>, Wells Fargo mailed me a cashier 's check for only {$100.00}, representing the remaining balance in the account, while continuing to withhold the {$12000.00} deposit. 
XXXX On XX/XX/year>, I visited the Wells Fargo branch to cash the {$100.00} cashier 's check. Branch Manager XXXX informed me that the bank on which the check was drawn would need to recover the funds directly from Wells Fargo. She further stated that Wells Fargo had attempted to contact the issuing bank multiple times, but that the issuing bank would not verify the check. 
On XX/XX/year>, a Wells Fargo representative contacted me and stated that the maker of the check or the issuing bank would have to contact Wells Fargo directly to claim the funds. 
On XX/XX/year>, a Wells Fargo Rep called earlier today. Wells Fargo stated that the maker of the check and its bank must recall the check by calling Wells Fargo Fraud Loss Mitigation Department @ XXXX to recover the funds. 
Wells Fargo 's position is contradictory and has left me without access to funds that were successfully paid by the maker and debited from the maker 's account at XXXX XXXX. The check cleared the issuing bank on XX/XX/year>, yet Wells Fargo continues to retain the funds while refusing to provide a clear explanation, refusing to release the money to me, and refusing to identify any legal basis for its claim that I am not entitled to the funds. 
As of the date of this complaint, Wells Fargo has retained {$12000.00} belonging to me and/or the maker of the check for more than a month after the account closure process began. The bank has repeatedly changed its explanation regarding the status of the funds and has provided no meaningful avenue for resolving the matter. 
I respectfully request that the CFPB investigate Wells Fargo 's actions and require Wells Fargo to : XXXX. 
XXXX. 
XXXX. 
XXXX. 
XXXX. 
Provide a detailed written explanation and legal basis for withholding the {$12000.00}. 
Identify the current status and location of the funds. 
Release the funds to the lawful owner without further delay. 
Provide all records relating to the fraud investigation and determination. 
Compensate me for any damages or losses resulting from Wells Fargo 's wrongful withholding of these funds. 
I have attached supporting documentation, including copies of Wells Fargo 's letters, email correspondence, proof that the check cleared the maker 's account at XXXX XXXX, and evidence that I provided the original check to Wells Fargo.

text:
b'I XXXX XXXX XXXX) was the victim of a fraud using my debit card and my deposit accounts at USAA Bank.  In all the scammers succeeded in getting XXXX (and later an additional XXXX from my accounts.  I reported the fraud to USAA within 15 minutes from discovery(per my XXXX  bill call log), but despite the fact that the EFTA expressly limits my maximum liability to $XXXX  when reported within 2 business days (which this clearly was) USAA has refused to refund my money.  The detailed facts follow:
On XXXX XXXX while at work on a construction site I received a telephone call on my cellphone identified by caller id as USAA.  The caller who identified himself as XXXX  from USAA fraud protection services informed me that they had detected fraudulent activity on my account and that they were taking steps to stop the fraud and protect me.  He seemed very genuine and knowledgeable, so I initially assumed he was genuine.  The call continued for about 45 minutes (for much of which time he placed me on repeated holds while he said he was working on it and taking to a supervisor).  When he said they were changing my password I began becoming more suspicions and while on one of the many holds I hung up the call.  I then called USAA directly.  On calling USAA I told them that I suspected that I had been scammed and they transferred me to their fraud department.  They confirmed the that call from XXXX  was fake and said they immediately froze all my accounts, disabled my debit card, had me reset my password and said they would investigate.   After resetting my password and while still on the call with USAA I signed on to my account.  They went through my recent transactions and they discovered several fraudulent transactions that I had not made, had not yet been posted to my online account and all of which I was completely unaware existed.  They included the following items:
1.	A $XXXX  transfer from USAA savings to my USAA checking which was posted to my online account that day XXXX.(USAA Bank Statement XXXX ACH dispute credit of a payment of $XXXX  that I had made a month prior to my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX account, shows disputed XXXX ACH dispute credit of a payment of $XXXX  that I had made that month to my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX account, shows disputed on XXXX ACH dispute provisional credit of a payment of $XXXX  that I had made two months prior to my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX account, shows disputed XXXX transfer to XXXX XXXX  of XXXX that XXXX XXXX apparently sent to +XXXX XXXX  transfer to XXXX XXXX  of $XXXX  that XXXX XXXX apparently sent to +XXXX XXXX have never heard of and have no idea of what the above phone is and certainly did not authorize the transfers.
The net effect of the above fraudulent transactions when combined with the existing balance in my account was sufficient to allow the fraudsters to transfer the $XXXX  to XXXX XXXX.
As directed by USAA following our call, I filed a fraud complaint with my local police department, the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Patrol Sargent XXXX XXXX.  The report number is XXXXItems 2, 3, and 4 showed on my bank account posting date XXXX (see bank statement).  I did not initiate the ACH disputes of the payments to XXXX XXXX and told USAA they were fraudulent and should have been cancelled and reversed.  However, USAA did nothing to stop them and the effect of allowing the ACH disputes and credits to go through increased the apparent balance in my account and allowed the subsequent transfers of the amounts to XXXX XXXX.  XXXX XXXX has since contacted me concerning the reversed payments, I advised that they had be falsely done by scammers and have sent replacement payments from my later paychecks to avoid my accounts becoming delinquent.  Adding insult to injury, by letter dated XXXX USAA has now advised me that they were reversing the item 4 temporary credit of $XXXX  and charged my account thus causing me to lose that amount again since they had allowed those funds to be transferred with item 5.  USAA gave the nonsensical explanation Because the transaction processed as intended, the provisional credit that was posted to your account for the disputed amount will be debited from your account on XXXX XXXX XXXX totally ignoring the fact that the transaction was the result of fraud and that I had not caused it.

text:
I am writing regarding the fraudulent Bank of America account associated with my name. I previously disputed this account and provided a valid police report documenting that the account activity was unauthorized and the result of fraud/identity theft. 
Despite submitting the police report and supporting documentation, Bank of America has failed to remove or block the fraudulent account and has not provided adequate justification for maintaining it. This failure is causing ongoing harm. 
Please be advised that under federal law : 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2 ( FCRA Identity Theft Block ) requires furnishers and credit reporting agencies to block the reporting of information resulting from identity theft once a police report or identity theft report is provided. 
15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ) requires furnishers, including banks, to conduct a reasonable investigation and correct or delete inaccurate or fraudulent information after receiving a dispute.

15 U.S.C. 1693 et seq. ( Electronic Fund Transfer Act ) and 12 C.F.R. 1005 ( Regulation E ) require financial institutions to investigate unauthorized transactions and limit consumer liability for fraud. 
I have complied with my obligations by promptly disputing the account and providing a police report. Bank of Americas continued failure to remove or properly resolve this fraudulent account may constitute violations of the Fair Credit Reporting Act and the Electronic Fund Transfer Act. 
I am requesting the following : Immediate removal and closure of the fraudulent account Written confirmation of the results of your investigation Correction of any reporting related to this account If this matter is not resolved promptly, I am prepared to pursue further regulatory and legal remedies, including escalation to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency and additional action under applicable federal law. 
Please respond in writing. 
XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX

text:
Bank : Citizens Bank XXXX : XXXX, XXXX XXXX Product : Checking account / Debit card Issue Type : Unauthorized transaction / Bank error / Overdraft fees What happened : I am filing a complaint regarding improper handling of a debit card fraud incident by Citizens Bank in XXXX, XXXX  XXXX. 
In XX/XX/year>, I attempted to make a small purchase with XXXX XXXX for approximately {$6.00}. Instead, the merchant attempted to charge my debit card for {$130.00}. I immediately received a fraud alert, reported the transaction as fraud, and my debit card was locked. 
When I later went into the Citizens Bank branch in XXXX to make a routine deposit/withdrawal, the teller informed me that my card was locked due to fraud and asked what happened. After I explained the fraud situation, the teller told me that instead of issuing a replacement card, she would simply unlock my debit card so I would not have to travel to XXXX XXXX to obtain a new one. Relying on the bank employees guidance, the card was unlocked. 
Several weeks later, XXXX XXXX charged my debit card again for {$48.00}. I did not authorize this charge. This transaction caused my account to become overdrawn. 
When I raised the issue, the bank had me sign a non-fraud dispute form ; however, this charge occurred only because the bank unlocked a card that had already been reported as compromised. I did not knowingly or intentionally authorize this transaction. 

This situation resulted from a bank error and failure to follow proper fraud-prevention procedures. Once fraud was reported, the debit card should have remained locked and a replacement card with a new number should have been issued. Unlocking the compromised card exposed my account to further unauthorized charges. 
Under Regulation E, this transaction qualifies as an unauthorized electronic fund transfer. Signing a non-fraud dispute form does not waive my consumer protections, especially when the charge would not have occurred but for the banks actions. 
As a result of the banks decision, I incurred an unauthorized charge and overdraft.
label: card_dispute

text:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased event tickets totaling {$1300.00} using my American Express Delta XXXX XXXX for a XXXX XXXX XXXX yacht event scheduled for XX/XX/year>. 

The event promoter hosted two separate yacht events occurring simultaneously on the same date and time. Upon realizing the ticket purchased was for the incorrect yacht, I contacted the merchant on XX/XX/year>. During that call, I was advised to purchase the correct tickets and was told the original tickets would be refunded. Relying on that representation, I immediately purchased replacement tickets totaling {$1400.00}. 

The merchant later refused to issue a refund for the original {$1300.00} transaction and classified it as a no-show, despite it being impossible to attend two simultaneous events. 

I filed a dispute with American Express under services not rendered. The dispute was resolved in the merchants favor. I requested reconsideration ( Dispute References XXXX and XXXX ), but American Express maintained the denial , stating that written confirmation of a refund was required. 

My complaint is not based solely on a refund promise. The core issue is merchant misrepresentation and impossibility of performance. The original service could not be rendered because the merchant directed me to purchase alternate tickets for a concurrent event. Even absent a written refund confirmation, it is factually impossible for a consumer to attend two simultaneous events. 

I believe American Express improperly categorized and evaluated this dispute under a refund/no-show framework rather than under services not rendered or merchant misrepresentation. I am requesting regulatory review of American Expresss dispute handling and a reversal of the {$1300.00} charge.

text:
I am filing this complaint regarding unauthorized charges on my credit card account issued by JPMorgan Chase. Over an extended period of time, fraudulent charges were made on my account that I did not authorize. Some of these transactions appear to have been processed through digital wallets such as XXXX XXXX. While I acknowledge that I initially missed some of the charges due to being busy and not closely reviewing my statements, I began reporting the fraudulent activity and contacting Chase around XXXX XXXX of last year. At that time, I informed the bank that the charges were unauthorized and requested that the fraud be investigated. During those calls I specifically asked that any digital wallets associated with my card, including XXXX XXXX / XXXX XXXX, be removed or disabled. Despite these requests, the fraudulent activity continued after I reported it. I was never informed that digital wallet tokens could continue updating or being re-provisioned automatically, and I had no knowledge that this type of activity could continue even after reporting fraud and requesting the wallets be removed. During discussions with Chase representatives, I was also informed that there were as many as 180 digital wallets associated with my card at one time, which is clearly inconsistent with normal consumer use. Despite the unusual nature of this activity and my repeated fraud reports, Chase has denied all of the fraud claims and with their only explanation being that most of them came from my wife an authorized user. However, they are giving me no clarity on all of these wallets that were created or why the activity continued after I reported the fraud. text:
Subject : Complaint Reporting of Unverified Debt / Lawsuit Dismissed / FCRA Violations Complaint Statement I am submitting this formal complaint regarding inaccurate and unverified credit reporting by Midland Credit Management , Inc, Petland and Capital one., and the continued reporting of an alleged debt that was the subject of a civil lawsuit which has now been dismissed. 

Midland Credit Management filed a lawsuit against me in the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, Florida, Case No. XXXX. 
The Plaintiff voluntarily dismissed the case pursuant to Florida Rule of Civil Procedure 1.420, without proving the debt, without presenting admissible evidence, and without obtaining judgment.

Because the Plaintiff dismissed the case, the alleged debt remains unverified and unsupported. 

Despite this, the account continues to be reported on my credit reports. 

Violations of Federal Law Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) Under 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ), consumer reporting agencies must ensure maximum possible accuracy.

Under 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ), furnishers must conduct a reasonable investigation after dispute.

Under 15 U.S.C. 1681i, credit bureaus must delete information that can not be verified. 
A debt that could not be proven in court can not be considered verified. 
CFPB Regulation F Debt Collection Rules Under 12 CFR 1006, a debt collector must possess sufficient documentation before pursuing collection or litigation.

Midland initiated litigation but dismissed the case without proof, showing lack of sufficient documentation.

Continuing to report the debt after dismissal is deceptive and unfair.

FTC Act Unfair and Deceptive Practices Reporting a debt that can not be proven in court may constitute an unfair or deceptive practice under 15 U.S.C. 45.

Consumers should not be harmed by credit reporting based on unsupported claims. 


Attachments Notice of Voluntary Dismissal Court case information Credit report showing account
label: card_dispute

text:
I am filing this complaint regarding Citi 's repeated denial of my billing dispute ( Case XXXX ) for a charge of {$790.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Citi 's denial letter dated XX/XX/year> appears to rely primarily on the merchant 's refusal to issue a credit rather than evaluating the substance of my dispute. I have followed up multiple times on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/year> with no meaningful response or reconsideration. 

My dispute has two distinct grounds : 1. Forged / Unrecognized Signature on Merchant Documentation The merchant submitted paperwork to Citi that either does not contain my signature, or contains a signature I do not recognize as mine and that was never presented to me at the time of purchase. This documentation was not shown to me during the transaction and appears to have been fabricated or altered after the fact. I explicitly notified Citi of this in writing and offered to provide a signature sample for handwriting comparison. Citi did not pursue this. 

2. Material Misrepresentation and Deceptive Sales Practices The purchase was made in a high-pressure, tourist-facing retail environment. The merchant made verbal representations about the quality and authenticity of XXXX XXXX  that were material to my decision to purchase. The documentation provided contains no verifiable information about fiber composition, authenticity standards, or quality certification. Comparable XXXX products are widely available online for {$3000.00}. I was charged {$790.00}. 

Citi has not engaged with either of these arguments. Under the Fair Credit Billing Act and Visa dispute rules, authorization of a charge does not override material misrepresentation, nor does it validate documentation that the cardholder did not sign or agree to. The merchant 's refusal to issue a refund is not a valid basis to close a dispute Citi has an independent obligation to investigate under the FCBA. 

I am requesting that the CFPB require Citi to conduct a proper review of both the signature authenticity issue and the misrepresentation claim, and reverse this charge.
label: card_dispute

I only discovered that my account was closed when my card was declined on XX/XX/XXXX. I then contacted PayPal Credit customer service on XX/XX/XXXX, and was informed by an agent that my account had been closed due to dishonored payments. 

The agent stated that the dishonored payments occurred on XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. However, I was never notified of these failed payments. I have no way of knowing how I was supposed to become aware of these issues, as there were no alerts, messages, or visible notices in my PayPal account, and I did not receive any email communication regarding these specific payment failures. 

I have carefully reviewed my email, including spam and all folders, and did not receive any notification that my payments had failed or that my account would be closed. I was later told by an agent that closure notifications are only sent via email, which I did not receive. 

After speaking with a PayPal Credit agent again, I was only then able to see a closed account status through the Synchrony website. Prior to this, my PayPal Credit account was not visible in my PayPal account, and there were no clear notices or warnings about the closure. This raises concerns about the lack of transparency and the difficulty for consumers to access critical account information in a timely manner. 

Because I was unaware of any issue, I was not given a fair opportunity to correct the situation before my account was closed. Additionally, all of my accumulated rewards points were forfeited without any warning. 

I am also concerned about the potential negative impact this account closure may have on my credit history and FICO score. I have made efforts to stay current on my payments, and I am worried that this situation, which I was not properly notified about, could unfairly affect my credit standing. 

I find it unreasonable that my account was closed without clear notice, without any account freeze or warning period, and that my rewards were removed despite my intent to make payments and maintain the account in good standing. 

I am requesting a full review of my account closure, clarification of the failed payment attempts, confirmation of whether any negative credit reporting has been made, and consideration for reinstating my rewards or providing an appropriate resolution.

I am filing this complaint because Citi has repeatedly denied my billing dispute ( Case XXXX ) for a charge of {$870.00} from XXXX XXXX in XXXX, XXXX, without adequately evaluating the substance of my claim. The basis of my dispute is material misrepresentation and deceptive sales practices not buyer 's remorse or price dissatisfaction. 

While traveling in XXXX, I was brought to XXXX  XXXX and subjected to a high-pressure sales environment in which the merchant made verbal representations about the quality, authenticity, and value of a pendant ( silver setting with pink star XXXX XXXX that were material to my decision to purchase. I was pressured into signing paperwork and providing my passport before I had adequate opportunity to review the documentation. 

After leaving the store and reviewing the paperwork, I discovered it contained no objective or verifiable information regarding gem quality or grading, metal purity or certification, or any independent valuation basis. Comparable items a silver pendant with pink star sapphire are widely available on platforms like XXXX for under {$100.00}. I paid {$870.00}. 

When I contacted the merchant the following day to request a refund, they refused and instead attempted to upsell me on additional items, further demonstrating the predatory nature of the transaction. 
I filed a written dispute with Citi in XX/XX/XXXX, including supporting documentation. Citi denied the dispute citing the merchant 's invoices, signed paperwork, and a passport copy. I submitted a detailed written rebuttal on XX/XX/XXXX, explaining that authorization does not equal informed consent when material information is misrepresented or omitted, and that under Visa dispute rules a transaction can be authorized yet still invalid where consent was obtained through deceptive sales practices. Citi has not engaged with this argument and continues to uphold the denial based solely on the merchant 's refusal to issue a credit. 

The merchant 's refusal to refund is not a valid basis to close a FCBA dispute. Citi has an independent obligation under the Fair Credit Billing Act to investigate the merits of my claim. They have failed to do so. 

I am requesting the CFPB require Citi to conduct a proper review of this dispute under misrepresentation and deceptive sales practice standards, and to reverse the {$870.00} charge.

text:
I am filing this complaint against Citibank for refusing to investigate an unauthorized duplicate charge, despite my having documentary evidence that the charge is fraudulent, and after Citibank 's own representatives instructed me to delay filing. 
Background Prior Related Dispute ( Same Vendor/Location ) I previously filed a CFPB complaint regarding charges from XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, XXXX a merchant operating in the same commercial complex ( XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX as the merchant in this complaint. Citibank issued conditional credits on those disputes and subsequently reversed them. I am noting this because both disputes involve the same merchant building and represent a pattern of unauthorized charges from that location. 
Current Dispute XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX charges from XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX IN posted to my Citibank account : {$330.00} legitimate charge {$860.00} unauthorized, no goods or services received I have XXXX documents that together prove the {$860.00} charge is fraudulent : Original vendor receipt ( Order No. XXXX, dated XX/XX/XXXX ) confirms XXXX item purchased : XXXX handmade/hand-knotted wool carpet, XXXX, XXXX  design, for XXXX XXXX, payment marked " Paid in Full '' XXXX shipping receipt confirms delivery of one shipment valued at {$330.00}, consistent with the XXXX purchase price at prevailing exchange rates Citibank statement shows both charges on the same date from the same merchant The XXXX receipt conclusively establishes that only one item was shipped and delivered. There is no second item, no second shipment, and no authorization for the {$860.00} charge. 
Citibank Instructed Me to Wait Before Disputing When I first identified the {$860.00} discrepancy, I contacted Citibank and was advised by their representatives to wait until the item was delivered before filing a dispute, as this involved international shipping. I complied with Citibank 's instructions. The item arrived in late XXXX XX/XX/XXXX. I contacted Citibank promptly after delivery to file the dispute. 
Citibank is now refusing to investigate, citing their internal dispute window a window that only lapsed because I followed Citibank 's own instructions to wait. Allowing Citibank to use a deadline against me that their own representatives caused me to miss is fundamentally unjust and constitutes an improper denial. 

Legal Basis Equitable Tolling The dispute window must be tolled from the date Citibank instructed me to delay filing. A creditor can not direct a consumer to take no action and then weaponize that inaction as grounds for denial. 
TILA 1643 ( 15 U.S.C. 1643 ) The {$860.00} was never authorized. Unauthorized charge protections under TILA are not subject to the same rigid 60-day window that applies to billing errors under the FCBA.

text:
On XX/XX/year>, I discovered that Self had assessed a {$25.00} annual fee on my secured credit card account about a year after opening, even though I never received the physical card, never received tracking information, never activated the card, and never received notice that an annual fee would be imposed. 

This is not just a dispute over a {$25.00} charge. The core issue is that Self attempted to impose a fee on a card I never received or used, without clear notice, proof of delivery, proof of activation, or any transparent account history showing why the fee was allegedly valid. Self also appeared prepared to use that disputed fee as a basis to reduce, offset, or delay return of my security deposit, which is my money. 

On XX/XX/year>, I sent Self a written dispute demanding removal of the fee, reversal of any related interest, penalties, negative coding, collection activity, or credit reporting, return of my security deposit or credit balance, and production of documentary proof if Self contended the fee was valid. That written dispute expressly invoked billing-error type concerns and requested documentary clarification. Self has not provided a substantive response.

T

his matter raises Regulation Z concerns. Und er 12 C.F.R. section 1026.13, a consumer may dispute billing errors and request additional clarification and documentary evidence. Under 12 C.F.R. section 1026.11, once a credit balance exists and a written request is made, the remaining credit balance is required to be refunded within seven business days. To the extent Self failed to provide or deliver periodic statements reflecting the fee or balance, that also raises concerns under 12 C.F.R. sections 1026.5 and 1026.7 regarding required periodic statements. The call handling made the problem worse. Before I was able to speak with a manager, a Self representative acted dismissively and suggested that I might not get my money back. The representative first told me the fee could not be waived. I then stated three separate times that I
wanted the account canceled. Instead of processing that request directly, the representative attempted to keep the account open and twice tried to steer the conversation toward waiving the fee and sending me a new card that I had never received in the first place. Rather than clearly addressing my dispute and cancellation request, the representative appeared to redirect the interaction into a scripted retention process. During that same exchange, the representative made a remark to the effect of " ahh there it is, '' which I found distasteful, unprofessional, and harmful in the middle of a live dispute over my money. 

From my perspective, this reflects a broader transparency and servicing problem. Self did not provide the card, did not provide notice, did not provide clear documentary support, did not provide a meaningful correction path after written notice, and did not handle the complaint in a professional or transparent way. I want the company to explain how it could assess an annual fee on a card never received or activated, why I had to repeat my cancellation request three times, why the interaction shifted into retention efforts instead of straightforward dispute resolution, and why Self failed to correct the issue after written notice despite the Regulation Z concerns identified above.
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
I am submitting this complaint as a follow-up to a prior CFPB complaint regarding American Express XXXX which remains unresolved. 

Despite my previous complaint and the companys response, American Express continues to report my account as delinquent, even though my inability to make payments was directly caused by their own system failures, payment processing errors, and account restrictions. 

This is not a case of simple non-payment. 

From XXXX through XX/XX/year>, I made repeated and documented attempts to make timely payments. However, due to multiple system issues, including : * incorrect bank account handling, * AutoPay failures, * account holds, * invalid or blocked payment methods, * and inconsistent system responses, I was effectively prevented from completing payments. 

At the same time, American Express continued to apply fees, interest, and negative reporting. 

The company has provided multiple inconsistent explanations for the same events, including : * use of a closed account, * AutoPay not properly set, * cancellations on the bank attached to AutoPay, * Check Truncation Return, * State law prohibits PPD. 

These explanations are contradictory and indicate a failure to properly investigate and resolve the issue. 

Despite acknowledging some issues by issuing partial credits, American Express has refused to : * correct or remove negative credit reporting, * refund related interest charges, * or provide a clear and verifiable technical explanation. 

Reporting my account as delinquent under these circumstances is inaccurate and unfair, as the inability to pay was directly caused by the companys own system failures and restrictions. 

Additionally, after the CFPB complaint process, the company failed to provide a meaningful resolution or engage in a reasonable settlement process. 

As a good-faith effort to resolve this matter, I have offered to settle the account based on the balance of {$680.00} ( prior to the system failures ), by paying 20 % of that amount as a full and final settlement, conditioned upon correction of credit reporting and account closure. 

This offer has not been properly addressed. 

I am requesting : * immediate correction or removal of any negative credit reporting related to this account, * a full investigation of the system failures and payment processing errors, * refund of all interest and improper charges, * and proper resolution of this matter based on the documented evidence. 

This complaint is being submitted due to the companys failure to resolve the issue after the prior CFPB complaint and continued inaccurate reporting.

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding unauthorized transactions on my Nordstrom charge account and the handling of my fraud dispute.

On XX/XX/year>XXXX  ( posted XX/XX/year>XXXX  ), the following transactions were made on my account without my knowledge or authorization : {$63.00}, {$430.00}, {$600.00}, {$360.00}, {$11.00}, and {$330.00}, totaling {$1800.00}. I became aware of these charges after receiving a credit alert and immediately took action by contacting customer service to report the suspected fraud. 

During my initial call, I attempted to file a fraud claim ; however, the representative incorrectly processed the claim. As a result, I was required to call again to properly report the unauthorized transactions. During my second attempt, I was informed that the fraud claim was filed and that I would receive a letter in the mail regarding the appropriate determinations of the filed claim. 

Subsequently, I received two separate letters, both dated XX/XX/year>. One letter stated that a temporary credit had been applied to my account while the matter was under review. The second letter, however, stated that the transactions were determined to be valid and that I had benefitted from them. These contradictory determinations, issued on the same date, raise serious concerns about the integrity and accuracy of the investigation process. 

I dispute these findings. I did not make, authorize, or benefit from any of the transactions listed above. Furthermore, I have not used this account for a significant period of time prior to these charges. The merchant location ( s ) involved should have surveillance footage and transaction records that could verify the identity of the individual who conducted these purchases.

I request a detailed explanation of how the determination was made that these transactions are valid, including what evidence was reviewed and how it supports the conclusion that I authorized or benefited from these charges. To date, no such proof has been provided. 

Under applicable consumer protection laws, I should not be held liable for unauthorized charges, particularly in the absence of a reasonable and well-documented investigation. The handling of my claim appears to be deficient and inconsistent. 

As a resolution, I request that : 1. All unauthorized charges totaling {$1800.00} be permanently removed from my account.

2. Any associated interest or fees be reversed.

3. A full and transparent explanation of the investigation findings be provided.

4. My account be corrected to reflect that these charges were fraudulent. 

I appreciate your prompt attention to this matter and expect a timely and fair resolution.

text:
Subject : Escalated Complaint XXXX XXXX Failure to Properly Investigate Disputed Digital Transactions I am submitting an escalated complaint against Capital One regarding their handling of multiple disputes involving transactions made with a XXXX  ( XXXX ) merchant. 

Capital One denied my disputes on the basis that the digital goods were received. However, this conclusion is fundamentally flawed and ignores the actual nature of the transaction and the service provided. 

Shortly after these purchases, the merchant permanently banned my account and revoked all access to the digital goods and services I paid for. As a result, I no longer have possession, access, or any ability to use the items purchased. In practical and economic terms, this is no different from not receiving the goods or services at all. 

Capital One failed to evaluate this critical fact. Their investigation appears limited to confirming initial delivery, without assessing whether the merchant fulfilled their obligation to provide continued access to the purchased digital content. This reflects a serious deficiency in their dispute resolution process, particularly for digital transactions where continued access is essential to the value of the purchase. 

By ignoring the merchants unilateral revocation of access, Capital One has effectively allowed the merchant to retain payment while depriving me of the benefit of my purchase. This outcome is inconsistent with basic consumer protection principles and raises concerns about unjust enrichment. 

I respectfully request : XXXX. A full and independent reinvestigation of all disputed transactions XXXX. Consideration of the merchants account ban as a failure to provide the purchased service XXXX. Immediate reversal of the charges if the merchant can not demonstrate that I retain access to what I paid for XXXX. A detailed explanation of the policies, evidence, and reasoning used in denying my disputes If Capital One maintains its position, I request that they clearly explain how a transaction can be considered fulfilled when the consumer is permanently denied access to the purchased goods. 

This situation sets a dangerous precedent where merchants can revoke access after payment without consequence, leaving consumers without protection. 

I expect a timely and fair resolution.
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
Re : Formal Complaint Against Macys Credit Services and Failure to Remove Fraudulent Charges Despite Written Confirmation of Resolution Dear Sir or Madam, I am submitting this formal complaint regarding Macys Credit Services for their failure to remove a clearly fraudulent charge from my newly opened Macys credit card account, despite issuing written confirmation that the matter had been resolved in my favor. Macys has provided contradictory statements, failed to honor its own investigation results, and refused to provide documentation supporting their reversal. I am requesting the CFPBs assistance in compelling Macys to correct my account and provide the evidence I have repeatedly requested. 

Background of the Issue On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a mattress at the Macys store located in XXXX XXXX, XXXX, XXXX. Macys was offering a XXXX no-interest financing promotion and an additional {$100.00} discount for opening a new Macys credit card. I accepted the offer and opened the account. 
At the time of the incident described below, I had not yet received the physical Macys credit card. 

Fraudulent Charge On XX/XX/XXXX, a charge of {$520.00} appeared on my Macys credit account for childrens merchandise including XXXX sweatshirts, pajamas, socks, chocolates, and similar items. I did not make this purchase, nor did I authorize anyone else to do so. 
I immediately contacted Macys to report the fraudulent activity. 

Macys Written Confirmation of Resolution XXXX XX/XX/XXXX, Macys sent me a letter acknowledging receipt of my fraud claim and stating that they would investigate the matter. ( Attached as Exhibit A ) On XX/XX/XXXX, Macys sent a second letter stating that the investigation was complete and that the disputed charge along with any related interest had been removed from my account. ( Attached as Exhibit B ) Contradictory Reversal by Macys On XX/XX/XXXX, I logged into my Macys online account and discovered that the fraudulent charge had not been removed, contrary to the written confirmation I received. 
I contacted Macys Customer Service and was informed that an internal investigation dated XX/XX/XXXX concluded that the purchase was legitimate directly contradicting the XX/XX/XXXX letter stating the opposite. 
When I informed the representative that I had written proof of the resolution, they were unable to explain the discrepancy. 


Failure to Provide requested Evidence I have repeatedly requested : - The video surveillance footage of the individual making the purchase - A copy of the signed receipt for the transaction - Any documentation Macys relied upon to determine the purchase was legitimate and Macys has not provided any of this information.

Requested Relief I respectfully request that the CFPB assist in compelling Macys to : 1. Permanently remove the fraudulent {$520.00} charge and all related interest or fees.

2. Correct my account history to reflect that I was not responsible for this transaction.

3. Provide copies of all evidence used in their investigation, including surveillance footage, signed receipts, and internal notes.

4. Explain in writing why their XX/XX/XXXX letter contradicts their subsequent statements. 

Conclusion I have acted promptly and in good faith at every stage. Macys has issued conflicting statements, failed to honor its own written determination, and refused to provide documentation supporting its reversal. This situation has caused significant stress and has undermined my confidence in Macys handling of consumer credit accounts. 

I appreciate the CFPBs assistance in resolving this matter.
label: card_dispute

text:
I am submitting this complaint against Zwicker & Associates, P.C., acting on behalf of XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX, regarding continued collection and litigation activity on a disputed debt without adequate substantiation of personal liability.

Zwicker is attempting to collect on an alleged XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  credit card account purportedly associated with a business entity. I dispute any personal liability for this obligation in its entirety. I did not open, authorize, or personally guarantee the alleged account, and I did not receive any personal benefit from it.

I provided written notice disputing the debt and requested validation. Despite being on clear notice that liability is disputed, Zwicker has continued collection activity and has maintained the position that I am personally responsible. Zwicker asserts that it previously provided validation in the form of billing statements and account terms. However, those materials do not establish that I am personally liable for the alleged obligation. They do not include competent evidence of a valid, executed personal guaranty or other documentation linking me individually to the debt.

Continuing to represent that I am personally liable, without possessing or providing documentation sufficient to substantiate that claim, is misleading and improper. It places the burden on me to disprove liability while Zwicker proceeds as though the obligation has already been established.

In addition, Zwicker has continued collection and litigation activity despite the unresolved dispute regarding authorization and liability. Using the legal process to pursue a disputed and unsubstantiated obligation raises serious concerns regarding the accuracy, integrity, and fairness of their collection practices.

This conduct has caused harm, including damage to my credit profile, reputational harm, and the expenditure of substantial time and effort to dispute and respond to claims that have not been properly supported.
label: card_dispute
source_sub_issue: Didn't receive enough information to verify debt

text:
b'Complaint Against American Express  Repeated Credit Limit Reductions Causing Consumer Harm

I am submitting this complaint regarding a pattern of repeated credit limit reductions by American Express that has directly harmed my credit profile and ability to responsibly manage my debt.

American Express has repeatedly reduced my credit limits, often immediately following material payments, and in several instances lowered my limits to or below my existing balances. These actions have artificially increased my credit utilization, including instances where my accounts were reported as over-limit despite good-faith payments made to improve my standing.

As a result, my credit profile has been negatively impacted not due to increased borrowing, but due to issuer-initiated limit reductions. These actions have created a self-reinforcing cycle in which my efforts to reduce balances are negated by subsequent limit decreases.

Despite making consistent and, at times, substantial payments toward my balances, American Express has repeatedly reduced my credit limitsoften immediately following those payments. In several instances, these reductions brought my credit limit down to, or even below, my existing balance.

Impact of These Actions

As a result of these actions, I have experienced:

- Artificially elevated credit utilization ratios
- Instances where my utilization exceeded 100% due solely to limit reductions
- Negative impacts to my credit score and overall credit profile
- Reduced access to alternative credit and refinancing options
- Over-limit conditions despite making good-faith payments

American Express has indicated that these actions are based on high utilization. However, by repeatedly lowering my limits to match or fall below my balances, they are creating a self-reinforcing cycle in which my utilization remains elevated regardless of my payment behavior.

Additionally, these limit reductions have often occurred with little or no meaningful advance notice, and in some cases within days of making payments intended to improve my account standing.

I believe this pattern of conduct may constitute an unfair practice, as it penalizes repayment behavior, distorts credit reporting outcomes, and undermines a consumers ability to improve their financial standing despite making good-faith efforts to reduce debt.

Additional Concern Regarding Notice and Payment-Based Reviews

Historically, American Express provided advance notice that my credit limit could be reduced unless a payment was made. In some instances, I did not make the requested payment, and I understand that a resulting limit reduction may follow.

However, more recent actions have differed in two important ways:

- Credit limits have been reduced without any advance notice
- Credit limits have been reduced shortly after I made material payments intended to improve my account standing

This shift has made it difficult to manage my accounts effectively, as I no longer have clear visibility into how my actionsparticularly making paymentsimpact the likelihood of adverse account changes.

Account History  Representative Examples
1. Delta American Express Card

Original Credit Limit: $XXXX XXXX: Notice received indicating limit reduction unless $XXXX payment madeXXXX XXXX payment madeXXXX  Limit reduced to $XXXX XXXXBalance reduced to $XXXX: Notice of review; requested $XXXX payment within 3 daysXXXX: Limit reduced to $XXXX (near balance level)XXXX XXXXBalance reduced to $XXXX: Limit reduced to $XXXX (no prior notice)XXXX XXXX XXXX payment madeXXXX: Limit reduced to $XXXX (no prior notice)
Result: Balance of $XXXX exceeded new limit, placing the account in an over-limit condition despite payment
2. American Express XXXX  Card

Original Credit Limit: $XXXX XXXXBalance: $XXXX: Limit reduced to $XXXX (no prior notice received)XXXX XXXX: Notice of review; requested $XXXX paymentXXXX Balance reduced to $XXXX XXXX XXXX payment madeXXXX: Limit reduced to $XXXX (no prior notice)
Result: Significant limit reduction following payment, materially increasing utilization

Pattern of Behavior

Across both accounts, the pattern is consistent:

- I make payments to reduce balances
- American Express reduces my credit limit shortly thereafter
- My utilization remains elevated or increases as a result
- This elevated utilization is then cited as justification for further reductions

In addition, the manner in which these actions are taken has changed over time. Historically, American Express provided advance notice that a payment could help avoid a credit limit reduction. More recently, however, credit limits have been reduced without meaningful advance notice and, in some cases, shortly after I made material payments intended to improve my account standing.

This inconsistency has made it difficult to effectively manage my accounts, as there is no longer a clear or reliable relationship between my payment behavior and the likelihood of adverse account actions.

As a result, this creates a cycle where it becomes materially more difficult to improve my credit profile despite making good-faith efforts to pay down balances.

Supporting documentation is included as exhibits demonstrating the sequence of material payments followed by credit limit reductions and the resulting increase in utilization and over-limit conditions.'
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:

I did not receive any formal communicationeither by mail or emailexplaining the reason for these closures. As a consumer, I understand that financial institutions may close accounts at their discretion ; however, I believe I am entitled to clear communication regarding such actions, especially when they may affect my creditworthiness and financial standing. 

I am requesting the following : A written explanation detailing the specific reason ( XXXX ) for the closure of both accounts.

text:

text:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I paid {$1400.00} to XXXX XXXX XXXX for professional private investigative services. The merchant failed to deliver services as contracted- they possessed critical investigative findings ( a death record for the subject I hired them to locate ) but never communicated this information to me. I only discovered this when the subject 's widow contacted me directly. 

I filed a chargeback dispute with Capital One in XX/XX/XXXX and provided extensive documentation proving service failure : - The merchant 's own report showing " DATES OF DEATH : XX/XX/XXXX '' in a clearly labeled section - Email and text message correspondence showing the merchant ignored multiple requests for refund - Evidence that my assigned contact was unlicensed and unaware of findings in her own report - Admission from merchant 's supervisor that findings were never communicated Capital One requested " Proof of Return/Cancellation '' and closed my dispute on XX/XX/XXXX when I could not provide this documentation. 

THIS IS THE WRONG DISPUTE CATEGORY. This was not a return of physical goods- this was a SERVICE DISPUTE for services not delivered as described.

Capital One misclassified my dispute and applied requirements for product returns to a professional services failure case. When I spoke with a supervisor to explain this was a services dispute ( not a return ), they refused to reopen under the correct category.

The correct dispute reason should have been " Services Not as Described '' or " Defective Services '' - NOT " Return/Cancellation. '' I provided overwhelming evidence that services were paid for but not properly delivered, the merchant had critical information and failed to communicate it, and the merchant ignored contractual obligations and multiple refund requests.

Capital One closed the dispute on a technicality that doesn't apply to service disputes, effectively denying me the fraud protection I'm entitled to as a cardholder. I have been a customer for over 10 years and cancelled my account due to this mishandling.

I am requesting that Capital One reopen my dispute under the correct category and review the evidence I provided showing the merchant 's failure to deliver contracted services.
label: card_dispute

text:
I am disputing a charge on my Rooms To Go Synchrony credit card for a XXXX XXXX king-size XXXX XX/XX/year> at the XXXX XXXX XXXX FL XXXX XXXX delivered XXXX  XX/XX/year>. The mattress does not meet my reasonable expectations and was not as described. 
Although labeled king-size, it is too cramped for my family of fivewe can not fit comfortably, and the support level causes discomfort and poor sleep for everyone. Nothing is defective, but it simply does not match what I expected based on the showroom display and product descriptions. The mattress has remained completely unused beyond initial testing and is still in its original condition ( see attached photo showing it on the floor with plastic wrap intact and the XXXX XXXX tag visible ). 
Rooms To Go authorized return of the rest of the bedroom set but refuses a refund on the mattress, insisting only on an exchange after a XXXX day wait. I do not want to continue any business with them after this experience, especially after being treated rudely and dismissively by the XXXX XXXX store manager, who was insulting when I tried to resolve the issue. 
They claim their no-refund policy is valid because it is printed on the back of the receipt. However, under XXXX  XXXX XXXX, a store with a no-refund policy must post a conspicuous sign at the point of sale ( near the register ) before purchase. I do not recall any such sign being posted. The statute clearly states that failure to exhibit the sign means a refund policy exists, and the store must provide a full refund within 7 days for unused merchandise in original condition with proof of purchase. The receipt ( given after payment ) does not satisfy this requirement. 
I filed a dispute with Synchrony over a month ago. It was initially denied because the retailer only offers an exchange. I have been waiting for resolution and have not yet paid the separate undisputed {$330.00} sofa balance from XXXX years ago, as I am awaiting the outcome of this dispute to keep the account properly managed. I have demonstrated good faith throughout by keeping the mattress unused and attempting multiple resolutions ( store, corporate, XXXX, and Synchrony ). 
I request that Synchrony reverse the charge and issue a full credit/refund for the mattress under the Fair Credit Billing Act, considering the Florida law violation and the mismatch in expectations. Attached is a photo of the unused mattress. 
Thank you for your assistance.

text:
I am filing a complaint against LVNV Funding LLC and its servicer, Resurgent Capital Services regarding failure to validate an alleged debt. 

On XXXX XXXX XXXX, I formally requested validation of the debt they claim I owe. As of today, they have not provided any documentation or evidence to verify that this debt is accurate, that I am the correct consumer, or that they have the legal right to collect.

Despite my request, the account continues to be reported on my credit report and/or collection activity may still be ongoing. This is a violation of my rights under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), which requires debt collectors to cease collection efforts until proper validation is provided. 

I am requesting that LVNV Funding LLC and Resurgent Capital Services : 1. Provide proper validation of the alleged debt, including original creditor information, account documentation, and proof of their authority to collect ; or 2. Immediately cease collection activity and remove the account from my credit report if they can not validate the debt. 

I also request that this matter be investigated and that appropriate action be taken to ensure compliance with federal law. 

Thank you.
label: card_dispute
source_issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed

text:
I am filing this complaint regarding an unauthorized charge on my PayPal Credit account serviced by Synchrony Bank. 

On XX/XX/year> at approximately XXXX XXXX, an unauthorized transaction in the amount of {$760.00} from XXXX XXXX was processed on my account. 

Immediately after receiving notice of this suspicious transaction, I contacted the company and reported fraudulent activity. I spoke with a representative who canceled/locked my card and opened a fraud claim. 

Before the larger fraudulent transaction, there was also a smaller authorization/test charge for {$0.00} marked VOIDED at the same time. This strongly suggests my account information was being tested before the larger unauthorized purchase was completed. 

Despite reporting the fraud immediately while the charge was pending, the company still allowed the {$760.00} charge to post to my account. 

Days later, I received an email stating they had attempted to call me for more information. However, I received no missed calls, no voicemail, and no evidence of any attempted call. 

I also received generic responses stating they could not locate the transaction, even though it appears on my account statement. 

Additionally, when I click on the transaction details within their system, I receive an error message stating : Sorry, we couldn't retrieve your data. Please try again later. 

I acted quickly and responsibly to prevent loss, yet the company failed to stop the charge and failed to properly investigate the dispute. 

I am requesting : 1. Immediate reversal of the unauthorized {$760.00} charge 2. Removal of any related interest or fees 3. Full fraud investigation 4. Written explanation of why the charge was allowed to post after being immediately reported 5. Correction of any negative reporting related to this matter This account is in my name and associated with my company, XXXX XXXX, XXXX. I use this credit account for legitimate business purposes and I am responsible for paying all charges made on it. There is no reason or benefit for me to fabricate a fraudulent claim regarding a purchase that I would ultimately be obligated to pay.

text:
This is a follow-up to CFPB complaint # XXXX which was closed without resolving the issue. 
I am disputing the allocation of a {$3500.00} payment in relation to a posted cash advance transaction. 
I dispute the explanation provided regarding payment allocation. 
You state that payments are applied to the highest interest rate balance first, but that this did not occur because the cash advance was considered unbilled. This explanation is inconsistent with the actual account activity. 
The cash advance transaction had already posted to my account at the time my {$3500.00} payment was made. In addition, cash advances begin accruing interest immediately from the transaction date. This was therefore a posted, interest-accruing balance carrying the highest APR on the account. 
Given that no minimum payment was due at the time ( as no statement had yet been generated ), the full payment should have been treated as an excess payment. Under the federal payment allocation requirements of the Credit CARD Act and Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.53 ), excess payments must be applied to balances with the highest APR first.

Your explanation does not address how a posted, interest-accruing balance with the highest APR could be excluded from this requirement solely because it had not yet appeared on a billing statement. This raises serious concerns that the payment allocation applied in this case is inconsistent with federal requirements. 
Please provide a detailed, transaction-level breakdown of how my payment was allocated, along with the specific legal or contractual basis for excluding this balance from highest-APR allocation under these circumstances. I request correction of the allocation and reversal of any resulting interest or fees.
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
I opened a Synchrony Financial account in XX/XX/XXXX and made XXXX promotional purchases : {$2600.00} on XX/XX/XXXX ; {$2600.00} on XX/XX/XXXX ; and {$1200.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. I had funds available for each purchase but chose to finance because XXXX merchant partners, XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX and XXXX, promoted XXXX  financing at point of sale and assured me that multiple promotions could be consolidated on XXXX card. 

I enrolled in autopay and reasonably believed my payments were being applied toward these promotional balances. What was not disclosed is that Synchrony retains sole control over how payments are allocated across promotional balances, with no visibility or override available to the consumer. 

The minimum payment Synchrony required was insufficient to pay off any promotional balance within its promotional term. Despite this, my statements showed the account in good standing and did not clearly warn that making the required payment would still result in deferred interest charges. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, the XX/XX/XXXX promotion expired. Approximately {$1100.00} in backdated interest was added at once, and the balance began accruing interest at 35 % APR. I was not notified of this event. From that point forward, Synchrony allocated XXXX payments to that balance, allowing it to compound at XXXX XXXX while I continued making every required monthly payment. 

The XX/XX/XXXX promotional balance has also received XXXX dollars across more than a year of consecutive payments and is now approaching the same expiration outcome by the same allocation design. I only discovered this issue because XXXX XXXX flagged a XXXX XXXX APR on my account. That discovery led me to learn that another promotional balance expiring in XX/XX/XXXX had likewise received no payment allocation and was on track for the same result. 

My current statement projects XXXX  years to repay this account at the minimum payment, despite these purchases being sold as XXXX financing offers. At the minimum payment, the projected cost exceeds {$23000.00} on approximately {$6500.00} of goods, not including additional deferred interest that would have been added had I not discovered this issue. 

XXXX  offers promotional expiration alerts, but this feature is optin, defaults to XXXX days notice, and I was never informed it existed or enrolled. This outcome was not caused by missed payments or inaction on my part but was the predictable result of XXXX undisclosed payment allocation practices combined with a required payment amount that could not satisfy promotional terms.
label: card_dispute

text:
On my Bank of America XXXX XXXX credit card ending in XXXX, I have an earned balance of XXXX rewards points, which holds a cash equivalent value of {$940.00}. Bank of America unilaterally closed this account on XX/XX/year>. I have paid my balance in full every month and have absolutely no history of adverse actions, missed payments, or misconduct on this account. Bank of America is now refusing to pay out the earned rewards.

According to the CFPB 's Circular 2024-07, this is a UDAAP violation. The Circular explicitly identifies the following as an unfair practice : 'Revoking previously earned rewards based on policies that tie revocation to actions that are not within the consumers control and do not constitute fraud or misconduct by the consumer, like an issuer unilaterally closing an account. ' Furthermore, Bank of America is retaining a {$95.00} annual fee. Because they unilaterally terminated the 12-month service agreement 69 days early without cause, I am owed a pro-rated refund of {$17.00}. Retaining an upfront fee for services the bank refuses to provide constitutes an additional unfair and deceptive practice under the same UDAAP standards, as it causes a substantial, unavoidable injury to the consumer.
label: card_dispute
