text:
The following complaint is not a duplicate and it presents new information against Wells Fargo. 

I am filing this complaint against Wells Fargo regarding their ongoing failure to resolve a documented accounting discrepancy and their subsequent obstruction of the dispute resolution process. 
XXXX. Failure to Provide Access to Billing Records ( Regulation Z Violation ) Despite Wells Fargos stated policy that " Credit card statements are available online for up to 2 years, '' the bank has removed my account statements and transaction history for the year 2024 from my online portal. This occurred while I was actively engaged in a billing dispute. Under the Truth in Lending Act ( Regulation Z ), specifically 12 CFR 1026.5 ( b ) ( 2 ), creditors are required to provide periodic statements. By removing access to these digital records during a legal dispute, Wells Fargo is hindering my ability to verify accounting and is in violation of federal consumer protection standards. 
XXXX. Refusal to Provide Critical Reconciliation Data There is a specific discrepancy involving a {$6000.00} transfer from XXXX  XXXX XXXX. XXXX  XXXX XXXX has provided letters documenting the successful transfer, yet Wells Fargo has failed to credit my account or provide a corresponding accounting of the funds. 
To resolve this, I requested XXXX standard banking data points : The XXXX Federal Reserve XXXX XXXX ID XXXX 
The XXXX " XXXX '' XXXX Logs. 
The XXXX " Unapplied Funds '' ( XXXX ) XXXX. 
Wells Fargos counsel has labeled these standard requests for " receipts '' as " irrelevant, objectionable, and unnecessary. '' This refusal to provide technical proof of the transaction 's status prevents a factual reconciliation of the account. 
XXXX. text:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a painting from XXXX for {$440.00}. The item was advertised explicitly as a " 100 % hand-painted ONE-OF-A-KIND ORIGINAL painting - not a print, or recreation artwork. '' What I received was a printed reproduction with surface paint applied - a product the art market classifies as a hand-embellished print, not an original painting. The merchant 's own follow-up emails confirmed the item was printed first and then painted over, directly contradicting the listing. Furthermore, shipping records reveal the item was shipped from XXXX, XXXX - inconsistent with the representation of a hand-crafted original painting and indicative of mass production. 
Upon receiving the item, I contacted the merchant on XX/XX/XXXX requesting cancellation and a full refund. The merchant did not respond substantively. Having received no resolution from the merchant, I filed a dispute with Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX. 
Following the dispute filing, the merchant responded and agreed in writing on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX to accept the return and issue a full refund " as soon as the package is delivered. '' I returned the item per the merchant 's instructions using their prepaid label. USPS confirmed delivery to the merchant on XX/XX/XXXX. No refund was ever issued. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank closed the investigation and reversed my conditional credit. Their letter states : " The merchandise is still in your possession. '' This statement is demonstrably false. The merchant received the returned item on XX/XX/XXXX - 28 days before Citibank issued that determination. The merchant made a false representation to Citibank to defeat the chargeback. Citibank accepted that representation without reviewing the USPS return evidence I had submitted.

I have since obtained the merchant 's evidence package submitted to Citibank. It consists solely of proof of original delivery of the item to me. The merchant submitted no evidence whatsoever addressing the subsequent return. My USPS return tracking confirmation - showing delivery back to the merchant on XX/XX/XXXX - directly and completely counters the merchant 's only argument. Citibank closed the investigation without reconciling these two pieces of evidence.

The result : the merchant holds both the returned painting and the {$440.00} simultaneously. This is not a disputed outcome- it is unjust enrichment enabled by Citibank accepting the merchant 's incomplete evidence over documented cardholder evidence.

I submitted the complete email chain, USPS tracking confirmation, and original product listing to Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX and again on XX/XX/XXXX. The dispute was closed three days after my second submission with no acknowledgment that the evidence was reviewed. Four successive denial letters dated XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX each cite different procedural reasons expired timeframes, no recourse, and finally a demand for a " credit slip. '' A credit slip is issued by the merchant upon processing a refund. The merchant has refused to issue one. Requiring me to produce a document that can only exist if the dispute is already resolved is not an evidentiary standard- it is a denial mechanism.

Citibank 's investigation was initiated, conducted, and closed on a false factual premise. Under 15 U.S.C. 1666 and Regulation Z, Citibank is required to conduct a reasonable investigation of a billing error. Closing a dispute in favor of a merchant whose only submitted evidence proves original delivery - while ignoring USPS confirmation that the item was returned 28 days prior to the determination- does not meet that standard.
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
I am filing this complaint against Way2Go regarding unauthorized transactions involving my government benefit card account and the companys repeated failure to properly investigate, resolve, and reimburse stolen funds removed from my account without my authorization.

Unauthorized transactions were made from my Way2Go child support benefit card account without my knowledge or permission. After discovering the fraud, I immediately contacted Way2Go and disputed the unauthorized transactions. During this process, I also discovered that unauthorized changes had been made to my account information, including my address, email address, and phone number.

I experienced ongoing issues accessing my account and obtaining account information. A replacement card that was sent to me by certified mail was later deactivated upon receipt, causing additional hardship and preventing access to my funds. I repeatedly contacted Way2Go regarding the fraudulent activity, disputed transactions, account security concerns, and requests for investigation records and supporting documentation.

Despite my repeated fraud reports and disputes, Way2Go repeatedly denied and closed my claims without adequately resolving the unauthorized activity or financial harm caused. I requested investigation records, transaction information, and documentation related to the fraud investigation multiple times. However, the company failed to timely provide necessary records, which interfered with my ability to fully pursue and complete the matter earlier through law enforcement channels.

I filed police reports regarding the unauthorized transactions and fraudulent activity connected to my account. I was later informed that additional investigative steps, including subpoenas, may be necessary and could take additional time. Despite this, Way2Go has continued to deny reimbursement for the stolen government benefit funds and has failed to provide a meaningful resolution regarding the unauthorized access to my account.

Due to the security failures and lack of confidence in Way2Gos handling of my account and fraud disputes, I no longer use the Way2Go card to receive my child support benefits. I changed my payment method to direct deposit into my personal bank account to protect my funds from further unauthorized activity.

This situation has caused significant financial hardship, emotional distress, stress, and loss of access to critical child support funds intended to support my household and children.

I am requesting that Way2Go : 1. Reopen my fraud claim and conduct a proper investigation into the unauthorized transactions and unauthorized account changes, 2. Reimburse the stolen government benefit funds removed from my account without my authorization, 3. Provide all investigation records, transaction records, and account access information related to this matter, 4. Provide a full explanation of how unauthorized access and unauthorized account changes were allowed to occur on my account despite my fraud reports and disputes, 5. Review the handling and denial of my prior claims, which were repeatedly closed without adequately resolving the fraud and financial harm caused.

text:
Subject : Formal Dispute of Improper Post-Settlement Fees, XXXX XXXX, and Collection Activity on Closed American Express Account To Whom It May Concern : I am formally disputing the entire remaining balance currently being claimed by American Express. I believe I am being subjected to unfair and deceptive billing practices involving post-settlement fees, residual interest, interest on late fees, and continued collection activity on an account that had already been settled and closed. 

An American Express account associated with my XXXX XXXX membership was opened. I did not personally open this account ; however, after reviewing the charges, I was able to verify that the XXXX XXXX membership charges themselves were legitimate charges associated with my XXXX account. In an effort to resolve the matter promptly and in good faith, I contacted American Express, negotiated a settlement, paid the agreed settlement amount, and requested that the account be closed. 

The account was used by XXXX XXXX to automatically bill recurring membership charges. The original balance on the account was approximately {$150.00}. 

Account history is as follows : XX/XX/XXXX : Original balance approximately {$150.00}. 

XX/XX/XXXX : American Express added approximately {$39.00} in late fees and interest charges. 

XX/XX/XXXX : An additional XXXX XXXX membership charge of approximately {$35.00} was automatically billed to the account by XXXX XXXX. American Express then continued adding late fees, interest charges, and interest on those late fees. 

XX/XX/XXXX : Another automatic XXXX XXXX membership charge of approximately {$35.00} was generated through XXXX XXXX. Additional late fees, interest charges, and interest on late fees continued accruing. 

To fully resolve the matter, I negotiated a settlement directly with American Express. On XX/XX/XXXX, I paid the agreed settlement amount of {$170.00} in full and again requested that the account be closed. The payment of {$170.00} exceeded the original balance of {$150.00} by approximately {$26.00}. 

The account was subsequently closed. However, despite the settlement payment and closure of the account, American Express continued assessing additional late fees, residual interest, interest charges, and interest on those fees after the account had already been settled and closed. 

American Express later informed me that because a XX/XX/XXXX payment due date had already been generated before the settlement payment was processed on XX/XX/XXXX, the system automatically generated additional late fees and interest charges. However, the current balance now being claimed consists solely of post-settlement late fees, residual interest, and interest on those fees that accrued between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX on an account that had already been fully settled and closed. 

No new purchases, transactions, or authorized charges were made after the XX/XX/XXXX settlement payment. 

I was traveling during part of this time period and only became aware of the continuing charges after returning home and receiving an additional bill for approximately {$88.00}. Based on the statements and explanations provided, this amount appears to consist entirely of post-settlement late fees, residual interest, and interest assessed on those fees. 

Despite repeatedly requesting my account number, complete account records, and a full accounting of all charges, fees, and interest, I have yet to receive this information from American Express. I explained to American Express representatives that I never received the physical credit card and therefore do not have the card number available to provide. Instead of providing the requested documentation and account records, I was informed by an American Express representative that they would be happy to send me another credit card. I refused this offer because I do not want another credit card and had already requested that the account be closed. 

I am attaching the billing statement currently in my possession in support of this dispute. 

I dispute the validity of these post-settlement charges in their entirety. I did not authorize continued post-closure billing, ongoing late fees, residual interest accrual, or interest charges after the settlement payment was made and the account was closed. 

It is unreasonable and improper for American Express to continue generating fees and compounding interest on an account that had already been settled and closed. Continuing to assess residual interest and late fees under these circumstances appears unfair, deceptive, and inconsistent with the reasonable understanding of a consumer who negotiated and paid a settlement specifically intended to fully resolve the account. 

Accordingly, I demand the following : XXXX removal and reversal of all fees, residual interest, late fees, and interest charges assessed after the XX/XX/XXXX settlement payment. 
Written confirmation that the account is permanently closed with a XXXX balance. 
XXXX cessation of any additional interest accrual, late fees, or collection activity related to these disputed charges. 
Written confirmation that the account has been marked as formally disputed during the investigation of this matter. 
Removal and correction of any negative credit reporting associated with these disputed post-settlement charges. 
A complete accounting ledger showing every charge, fee, interest posting, adjustment, and transaction applied after XX/XX/XXXX. 
Confirmation that all recurring XXXX XXXX billing associated with this account has been permanently stopped and disconnected from the closed account. 

Please consider this a formal written dispute of the remaining balance. I request that American Express investigate this matter thoroughly and respond in writing. 

I am also submitting complaints regarding these billing practices to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ), California Department XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX ( XXXX ), Federal Trade Commission ( FTC ), California Attorney XXXX XXXX, and other applicable consumer protection agencies. 

Sincerely,
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

I am a cardholder of the American Express Delta SkyMiles Reserve Card, a premium credit card with a high annual fee that includes an annual companion certificate as a core advertised benefit. 

For the years XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX, Delta Air Lines has confirmed that companion certificates were issued. However, Delta has also stated they are unable to provide proof of delivery or confirm that I ever had access to these certificates. These certificates never appeared in my Delta account and were never accessible for use.

American Express has stated that due to internal system changes, they no longer have visibility into these historical records and can not verify whether these benefits were actually delivered or accessible. Despite this, they continue to assert that the benefit was fulfilled based solely on internal issuance. 

For the most recent certificates in XXXX and XXXX, I received clear email notifications and the certificates are visible and accessible in my Delta account wallet. This demonstrates the standard delivery process and confirms that when the benefit is properly delivered, I am able to receive and access it without issue. 

In contrast, for XXXX through XXXX : I received no email notifications No certificates appeared in my Delta wallet I had no ability to access or use the benefit Importantly, I have not changed my contact information, account details, or notification preferences at any point since opening the card. There is no reasonable explanation for why the benefit would be properly delivered in later years but not during the earlier period unless there was a failure in delivery. 

During those years, I actively traveled with XXXX and paid for flights using my own funds and/or SkyMilesexpenses I would not have incurred had these certificates been properly delivered and accessible. 

This represents a clear failure to provide a core benefit of a premium product. Neither American Express nor Delta can provide any evidence that I ever received or had access to these certificates, yet they continue to rely on a technical issued status to deny responsibility.

I have made multiple attempts to resolve this directly, but have been met with deflection between the two companies and reliance on incomplete or unavailable records. 

Additional Notes : This issue spans multiple consecutive years ( XXXX ), indicating a systemic breakdown rather than an isolated incident. The contrast with properly delivered certificates in XXXX and XXXX further supports that these earlier benefits were never successfully delivered.

text:
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, CA XXXX Reference No. : XXXX Wells Fargo Bank Claim Assistance XXXX XXXX. XXXX XXXX XXXX, NC XXXX To : Federal Trade Commission and Wells Fargo Bank Claims Department XXXX XXXX Formal Appeal and Request to Reopen Disputed Transaction Claim No. XXXX Dear Sir or Madam : I, XXXX XXXX, hereby submit this formal appeal regarding Wells Fargo Banks denial of the disputed transaction claim referenced above. I respectfully request that Wells Fargo immediately reopen and reinvestigate this matter pursuant to the protections afforded under the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ), the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), Regulation Z, and all other applicable federal consumer protection statutes and regulations. 
On or about XX/XX/year>, the following transaction was charged to my account : XXXX  {$130.00} The merchant represented that AI marketing software and related services would be delivered and functional as advertised. However, the promised software, functionality, and associated services were not delivered in accordance with the merchants representations, promotional materials, or advertised claims. Consequently, I did not receive the benefit of the goods and/or services purchased. 
Pursuant to the Fair Credit Billing Act and Regulation Z, credit card issuers are legally obligated to investigate billing disputes involving goods or services that were either not accepted by the consumer or not delivered as agreed. Specifically, 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ( a ) provides protections where a transaction reflects goods or services that were not delivered in the manner represented to the consumer. 
The disputed transaction constitutes a billing error because the merchant failed to provide the AI marketing software and related services as promised. Accordingly, Wells Fargo has a legal obligation to conduct a reasonable and good-faith investigation into this disputed transaction. 
Additionally, pursuant to 12 C.F.R. 1026.12 ( c ), consumers are entitled to assert claims and defenses against a card issuer arising from transactions paid by credit card where the consumer has made a good-faith effort to resolve the dispute directly with the merchant. The disputed amount exceeds the statutory threshold required under applicable federal law. 
Federal law further requires that, upon receipt of a timely written billing dispute notice, the card issuer must : Acknowledge the dispute within XXXX ( XXXX ) days ; Conduct a reasonable and thorough investigation ; Resolve the matter within XXXX billing cycles, not to exceed XXXX ( XXXX ) days ; and Credit the consumers account if the billing error is substantiated. 
Because the products and services were not delivered as advertised, represented, or agreed upon, I respectfully request that Wells Fargo : Immediately reopen Claim No. XXXX ; Conduct a renewed, comprehensive, and good-faith investigation into the disputed transaction ; Reverse and credit the disputed amount of {$130.00} to my account ; and Provide written confirmation regarding the status and outcome of the reopened investigation. 


This correspondence is submitted in good faith and in support of my rights under applicable federal consumer protection laws and regulations. Nothing contained herein shall be construed as a waiver of any rights, claims, remedies, or protections available to me under federal or state law. 
Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I respectfully request written confirmation acknowledging receipt of this appeal and advising me of the next steps in the investigation process. 
Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

text:
b'I am filing this complaint against Johnson Mark LLC ("Johnson Mark"), a Utah debt-collection law firm operating from XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, Utah XXXX. Johnson Mark is a "debt collector" under 15 U.S.C.  1692a(6) and XXXX XXXX XXXX, 514 U.S. 291 (1995). Johnson Mark is attempting to collect an alleged XXXX XXXX credit card debt of XXXX (Reference Number XXXX, account ending in XXXX). I dispute the alleged debt in its entirety and have not at any time acknowledged that I owe it.

This complaint documents seven distinct violations of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act, 15 U.S.C.  1692 et seq., and the Fair Credit Reporting Act, 15 U.S.C.  1681 et seq. The conduct is continuing and has remained uncured across more than 70 days of written notice and three certified-mail dispute escalations.

TIMELINE

 XX/XX/XXXX: Johnson Mark mailed a demand letter (XXXX XXXX) claiming XXXX owed to XXXX XXXX. The back page of the letter is blank  no Notice of Important Consumer Rights was included.

 XX/XX/XXXX: I mailed a written dispute and 10-demand validation request to Johnson Mark by U.S.P.S. Certified Mail (tracking XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX). I sent a copy to XXXX XXXX XXXX (tracking 9XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX).

 XX/XX/XXXX: Johnson Mark mailed a "validation response." It consisted of a one-paragraph form letter, a one-page Account Detail printout, and a single XXXX XXXX billing statement. Zero of my ten validation demands were substantively answered.

 XX/XX/XXXX: XXXX XXXX mailed four letters bearing Case Number XXXX. One of those letters expressly confirmed that no paper original credit application exists for the alleged account  the account was opened via internet or telephone in XXXX.

 XX/XX/XXXX: XXXX XXXX mailed a "Credit Bureau Resolution" letter purporting to confirm reinvestigation under 15 U.S.C.  1681s-2(b). The letter contained literal unreplaced template placeholders: angle-bracket fields surrounding the charge-off date, the reporting date, the balance descriptor, and the balance amount.

 XX/XX/XXXX: I mailed an 11-demand insufficiency response to Johnson Mark by certified mail (tracking XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX), with a copy to XXXX XXXX (tracking XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX).

 XX/XX/XXXX: I am sending a 15-demand continuing dispute and renewed demand for validation to Johnson Mark today by U.S. Postal Service Certified Mail and concurrently by email to Johnson Mark's published consumer-correspondence email address. The full letter is attached.

VIOLATION 1  Section 1692g(a)(2) failure to identify the current creditor

Johnson Mark's XX/XX/XXXXdemand letter and its XX/XX/XXXX validation cover letter both reference "our client" without unambiguously identifying the creditor to whom the alleged debt is currently owed in the cover letter itself. 15 U.S.C.  1692g(a)(2) requires identification of "the name of the creditor to whom the debt is owed." A reasonable consumer should not have to triangulate the identity of the principal creditor from an attached printout. This deficiency is material and has been outstanding for more than 70 days notwithstanding three written demands.

VIOLATION 2  Section 1692e(2)(A) and Section 1692f(1) material contradiction on the XXXX credit

Johnson Mark's XX/XX/XXXX demand letter states "XXXX" in "Paid/Credited" amounts, reducing the claimed balance from XXXX to XXXX Johnson Mark's own XX/XX/XXXXvalidation Account Detail printout states "XXXX" in payments and credits. Two Johnson Mark documents directly contradict each other on a material element of the alleged amount due. The firm has been demanded in writing to reconcile this contradiction on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX  three times. It has not done so. A debt collector's communication of an amount that is internally contradicted by its own validation file is a false representation of the character or amount of a debt under  1692e(2)(A) and an attempt to collect an amount not authorized by the agreement creating the debt or permitted by law under  1692f(1).

VIOLATION 3  Section 1692e(5)/(7) and Section 1692e(10) "RESTRICT SUIT" misrepresentation

Johnson Mark'sXX/XX/XXXX validation Account Detail printout  the document Johnson Mark itself produced as validation  contains the notation: "XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX), N.A. (RESTRICT SUIT)." This is XXXX XXXX's own internal flag against initiating litigation on the account. Despite Johnson Mark's own document showing this restriction, Johnson Mark has not explained the notation, has not confirmed whether litigation on this account is or is not authorized by XXXX XXXX, and has not produced any written authorization from XXXX XXXX that would override the on-its-face litigation restriction. Threatening or initiating litigation that the principal has internally restricted, while concealing the restriction from the consumer, is a deceptive means to collect under  1692e(10) and a threat to take action that cannot legally be taken under  1692e(5).

VIOLATION 4  Section 1692e(10) missing consumer rights notice / selective compliance

Johnson Mark's XX/XX/XXXX demand letter (Ref XXXX) has a blank back page. No Notice of Important Consumer Rights or equivalent disclosure was included. A contemporaneous Johnson Mark demand letter dated XX/XX/XXXX, in a related Atteberry-family matter, did include a printed consumer rights notice on the back page. A second contemporaneous Johnson Mark demand letter dated XX/XX/XXXX to my husband in another matter also had a blank back page (matching mine). The same firm, in the same time frame, selectively includes or omits the consumer rights disclosure across letters issued to consumers. That is not a printing accident. It is a policy choice that materially affects consumer awareness of rights and constitutes a deceptive omission under  1692e(10) and a Regulation F validation-information defect under 12 C.F.R.  1006.34.

VIOLATION 5  Section 1692g(a) and 12 C.F.R.  1006.34 itemization defect

Johnson Mark's demand letter states XXXX in interest and XXXX  in fees on the alleged account. The XXXX XXXX billing statement that Johnson Mark itself enclosed with theXX/XX/XXXXvalidation response reflects a 28.99% APR, XXXX in interest charges for a single billing cycle, and XXXX in year-to-date interest. Under 12 C.F.R.  1006.34(c)(2)(iv) through (viii), Johnson Mark was required to identify an itemization date and provide the amounts and dates of any interest, fees, payments, and credits applied since the itemization date. The figure XXXX cannot be reconciled with the firm's own enclosed statement showing active interest accrual at 28.99% APR. This is a Regulation F itemization defect and a  1692g(a) validation-information defect.

VIOLATION 6  XXXX  attorney-review failure / Section 1692e(3)

15 U.S.C. 1692e(3) prohibits the false representation or implication that a communication is from an attorney. Under XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX (XXXX), and the line of authority requiring meaningful attorney review when a debt collection law firm holds itself out as such, Johnson Mark's letterhead-signed demand letters, validation responses, and any subsequent court filings must be supported by independent attorney review of the specific account. I have demanded  three times  documentation that an attorney admitted to the Utah State Bar independently reviewed the alleged account, the alleged balance, the authority to collect, and the supporting records before issuing each communication on firm letterhead. Johnson Mark has produced no such documentation. The firm cannot lawfully hold itself out as attorney debt collector while routinely producing form letters lacking individualized attorney review.

VIOLATION 7  Section 1692e(8) and Section 1681s-2(a)(3) disputed-status reporting

15 U.S.C.  1692e(8) prohibits communicating credit information that is known or should be known to be false, including the failure to communicate that a debt is disputed. 15 U.S.C.  1681s-2(a)(3) requires furnishers to report a debt as disputed once notified. I have demanded confirmation, with dates, that Johnson Mark has either reported the dispute to all consumer reporting agencies to which the alleged debt has been furnished or has caused XXXX XXXX  to do so. No such confirmation has been provided. XXXX XXXX  XX/XX/XXXX letter  containing literal unreplaced mail-merge placeholders surrounding the balance and reporting date  is direct evidence that XXXX XXXX reinvestigation under  1681s-2(b) was an automated template process with no human review. Johnson Mark's role in that defective reinvestigation, and in any disputed-status communication to credit reporting agencies, has not been disclosed despite written demand.

CONTINUING SECTION 1692g(b) VIOLATION

15 U.S.C.  1692g(b) requires a debt collector who receives a written dispute within thirty days of the initial communication to cease collection of the debt until validation is obtained and mailed to the consumer. I disputed the alleged debt in writing on XX/XX/XXXX  well within thirty days of theXX/XX/XXXX demand letter. Johnson Mark's XX/XX/XXXX form-letter response does not constitute validation under any reasonable construction of  1692g(b): it answers zero of ten specific demands and contains internal contradictions that themselves require explanation. Johnson Mark is therefore obligated to cease collection until adequate validation is provided. Any continued collection activity  including without limitation any further demand or settlement letters, any reporting or continued reporting to consumer reporting agencies without a disputed notation, any further interest or fee accrual, any service of summons or complaint, or any other affirmative attempt to collect  that occurs while the dispute remains open and unvalidated constitutes, on these facts, a separate  1692g(b) violation.

FULL ENUMERATION OF OPEN DEMANDS

The XX/XX/XXXX letter (attached) sets forth fifteen specific demands. Each remains unanswered. The violation sections above address the most significant categories; the complete demand list is reproduced here for the Bureau's record so that any compelled response addresses each demand by number:

1. Identification of the current creditor under 15 U.S.C.  1692g(a)(2).
2. Itemization information required by 12 C.F.R.  1006.34(c)(2)(iv) through (viii).
3. The original cardholder agreement bearing my signature (or a written confirmation that no signed agreement, paper or electronic, exists for the alleged account).
4. The written authorization or engagement document by which XXXX XXXX  has specifically authorized Johnson Mark to collect the alleged account ending in 1647.
5. A complete itemized payment history from origination XX/XX/XXXX) through the present.
6. Documentation reflecting the charge-off date, the charge-off balance (XXXX per XXXX XXXX XX/XX/XXXX letter), and any post-charge-off transactions or assignments.
7. Reconciliation of the XXXX contradiction between Johnson Mark's XX/XX/XXXX demand letter and Johnson Mark's XX/XX/XXXX Account Detail printout.
8. Explanation of the "RESTRICT SUIT" notation and confirmation whether litigation on this account has been authorized or restricted by XXXX XXXX.
9. The contractual provision and accounting authority under which interest has continued to accrue or may accrue after charge-off.
10. Documentation that an attorney admitted to the Utah State Bar independently reviewed the specific account, the alleged balance, the authority to collect, and the supporting records before signing each communication issued under firm letterhead. Heintz v. Jenkins, 514 U.S. 291 (1995).
11. Written confirmation, with dates, that Johnson Mark has reported the dispute to all consumer reporting agencies to which the alleged debt has been furnished or has caused XXXX XXXX to do so.
12. Reconciliation of XXXX XXXX's XX/XX/XXXX mail-merge failure letter and identification of Johnson Mark's role in any  1681s-2(b) reinvestigation of the alleged account.
13. Production of any IRS Form 1099-C or related cancellation-of-debt documentation, or written confirmation that none has been issued.
14. Explanation of the basis on which Johnson Mark selectively included a Notice of Important Consumer Rights on a contemporaneous XX/XX/XXXX letter to a related consumer in the XXXX family but omitted the same notice from the XX/XX/XXXX letter to me (Ref XXXX).
15. A complete, legible copy of the cardholder agreement XXXX XXXX contends governs the alleged account, including all amendments, change-in-terms notices, and addenda issued from origination (XX/XX/XXXX) through charge-off (XX/XX/XXXX).

PATTERN EVIDENCE  CROSS-CASE

Johnson Mark is simultaneously prosecuting collection conduct against my husband on a separate alleged XXXX XXXX account. He filed a CFPB complaint against Johnson Mark on XX/XX/XXXX (Complaint ID XXXX) documenting a parallel set of FDCPA violations on parallel facts. Same firm, same attorneys (XXXX XXXX, UT Bar XXXX XXXX; XXXX XXXX, UT Bar XXXX XXXX), same selective-compliance posture on consumer rights notices, same  1692g(a)(2) creditor-identification deficit, same form-letter "validation" that resolves nothing. The pattern is institutional, not isolated.'
label: card_dispute

text:
I am filing this complaint against Portfolio Recovery Associates , LLC regarding a credit card collection account that Portfolio Recovery Associates is reporting on my consumer credit file.

I sent Portfolio Recovery Associates a written request for validation of this alleged debt. In that request, I asked Portfolio Recovery Associates to provide documentation proving the validity of the debt, their ownership of the account, my legal obligation to pay, the complete balance calculation, and their authority to collect and furnish this account to the consumer reporting agencies.

Portfolio Recovery Associates has not provided any response or documentation validating this alleged debt. I have not received proof of ownership, a complete chain of title, a bill of sale or assignment connecting this specific account to Portfolio Recovery Associates, a signed agreement or contract establishing my legal obligation, a full payment history, or a complete itemization showing how the balance being reported was calculated.

Despite failing to provide validation, Portfolio Recovery Associates continues to report and update this account on my credit reports. That is the issue. Portfolio Recovery Associates is continuing to collect and furnish a disputed account without providing the documents needed to prove that the account is accurate, legally owed, properly owned, and properly reportable.

Under 15 U.S.C. 1692g ( b ), once a consumer disputes a debt in writing and requests verification, a debt collector must stop collection activity until the debt is verified and verification is mailed to the consumer. Portfolio Recovery Associates has not mailed me the validation requested, yet this account continues to be treated as collectible and continues appearing on my consumer reports.

Portfolio Recovery Associates continued reporting and collection activity also raises concerns under 15 U.S.C. 1692e, which prohibits false, deceptive, or misleading representations in connection with the collection of a debt. Continuing to represent that this debt is valid, owed, collectible, and accurately reportable without providing proof of ownership, itemization, and account-level documentation is misleading.

This also raises concerns under 15 U.S.C. 1692e ( 2 ) ( A ) because Portfolio Recovery Associates is representing the character, amount, or legal status of a debt that has not been properly verified. If Portfolio Recovery Associates can not produce proof showing that it owns this specific account, that the balance is accurate, and that I am legally obligated to pay, then the character, amount, and legal status of the account remain unsupported.

Portfolio Recovery Associates conduct also raises concerns under 15 U.S.C. 1692f ( 1 ) because a debt collector may not collect any amount unless that amount is expressly authorized by the agreement creating the debt or permitted by law. Portfolio Recovery Associates has not provided the agreement, itemization, payment history, or supporting documents showing that the amount being collected is authorized or properly calculated.

Because Portfolio Recovery Associates is furnishing this information to the credit reporting agencies, the company also has duties as a furnisher under the Fair Credit Reporting Act. After a dispute is made through the credit reporting agencies, a furnisher must conduct a reasonable investigation and correct, delete, or modify information that is inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable under 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ).

I recently mailed disputes regarding this account to the credit reporting agencies because I dispute the accuracy and completeness of the information Portfolio Recovery Associates is furnishing. Portfolio Recovery Associates has already failed to respond to my direct validation request, and if it continues verifying this account through the credit bureaus without producing competent documentation, that raises serious concerns about whether any real investigation is being conducted.

Portfolio Recovery Associates has not provided : Proof that Portfolio Recovery Associates owns this specific account ; A complete chain of title from the original creditor to Portfolio Recovery Associates ; A bill of sale or assignment identifying this specific account ; A signed agreement or contract establishing my legal obligation ; A complete payment history ; A full itemization showing how the balance was calculated ; Proof that the balance transferred accurately ; Proof of authority to collect ; Proof of authority to furnish this account to XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX.

Without these documents, Portfolio Recovery Associates can not reasonably claim that this account is accurate, collectible, owned by Portfolio Recovery Associates, or properly reportable.

The issue is not simply that I asked for validation. The issue is that Portfolio Recovery Associates is continuing to report and update a disputed collection account while failing to provide the basic documents needed to prove ownership, balance accuracy, legal obligation, and reporting authority.

A generic statement that the account is verified is not sufficient. I am requesting that Portfolio Recovery Associates provide actual account-level documentation proving the account, or delete the account from all consumer reporting agencies.
label: card_dispute
source_sub_issue: Didn't receive enough information to verify debt

text:
Company : Navy Federal Credit Union Product : Checking/Savings Account Issue : Problem with a purchase shown on your statement / Unauthorized transactions/ Fees What happened : From XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, I disputed unauthorized electronic transactions on my account with Navy Federal Credit Union . These transactions were investigated under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act and Regulation E.

Navy Federal failed to comply with Regulation E requirements during this process. Specifically, they refused to provide provisional credit while conducting an extended investigation that lasted approximately 90 days. Under federal law, if an institution can not complete its investigation within 10 business days, it must issue provisional credit while continuing the investigation.

Because Navy Federal did not provide provisional credit, I was forced to absorb the full financial impact of the disputed transactions during the investigation period. As a result, I incurred overdraft fees, advance fees, and interest charges, all directly tied to funds that were under dispute.

To avoid financial collapse and meet basic living needs, I had to reverse payments from my credit cards into my checking account during this period. This created additional financial strain and long-term consequences.

After approximately 90 days, the investigation concluded in my favor, and the disputed funds were returned. However, Navy Federal refused to refund any of the fees or interest that accrued during the investigation period, even though those charges were caused by their failure to follow Regulation E.

When I attempted to resolve the issue, I was told that the credit department would not coordinate with the dispute department, and therefore no relief would be provided. This reflects a systemic failure and an unwillingness to correct harm caused by their own noncompliance.

Why this is wrong : Navy Federals actions violate the Electronic Fund Transfer Act and Regulation E, which require : Timely investigation of disputes ; Issuance of provisional credit if an investigation exceeds 10 business days ; Protection of consumers from financial harm during the dispute process.

By refusing provisional credit and allowing fees and interest to accumulate on disputed funds, Navy Federal improperly shifted liability onto me and profited from funds that were ultimately determined not to be my responsibility.

What I want : I am requesting that Navy Federal Credit Union : Refund all overdraft fees, advance fees, and interest charges incurred between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX that resulted from the disputed transactions ; Provide a full accounting of all fees and interest assessed during this period ; Take corrective action to ensure future compliance with Regulation E requirements.

Additional notes ( optional field in CFPB form ) : This situation caused significant financial hardship over a three-month period. I was forced into unsustainable financial measures solely because Navy Federal did not follow federal law governing electronic fund transfer disputes. The fact that the investigation was ultimately resolved in my favor confirms that I should not have borne any of these costs.

Additionally, these are all secured credit cards which navy federal has a hold on XXXX dollars in my savings account that they prevent my access to. I have requested several times that they use those funds to pay the cards however they refuse to. To date my credit is being destroyed and fees are stacking up along with interest because they wont use the funds that are on hold specifically for the purpose of paying the credit cards.
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
To whom it may concern, I am submitting this complaint regarding Capital Ones handling of my fraud dispute involving a charge from XXXX in the amount of {$1200.00} posted on XX/XX/year>. 

Capital One concluded that the transaction was valid and belongs to me, yet failed to provide any meaningful explanation or supporting evidence for that determination. In their written response, the company explicitly stated that they were unable to provide any documentation. 

I find this extremely concerning. 

A financial institution should not be able to deny a fraud claim involving a significant amount of money while refusing or failing to provide the documentation, investigative findings, or evidence used to reach that conclusion. The response I received was largely generic language regarding interest charges and cardmember agreements, rather than a detailed explanation of the actual fraud investigation. 

At this time, I still dispute the transaction as unauthorized and continue to question the adequacy and transparency of Capital Ones investigation. 

I respectfully request that Capital One provide : * The basis for determining the transaction was authorized. 
* Whether the transaction involved chip insertion, tap-to-pay, manual entry, or any PIN verification. 
* Any receipts, merchant records, or investigative documentation relied upon. 
* Any evidence connecting me personally to the transaction. 
* A detailed explanation of how the conclusion was reached. 

A consumer should be allowed to understand the evidence used against them when a fraud claim is denied, especially when the institution claims certainty while simultaneously stating that no documentation can be provided. 

I am requesting a proper reinvestigation of this matter and a more transparent explanation of Capital Ones findings. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

text:
I am filing this complaint against American Express for repeatedly and wrongfully denying my billing dispute despite clear photographic evidence that I received the wrong item. 

On XX/XX/year>, I purchased one pair of XXXX XXXX XXXX sneakers in Size XXXX from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) for {$130.00}. The merchant 's own invoice confirms I ordered Size XXXX, XXXX XXXX. 

What I received were two MISMATCHED shoes one size XXXX and one size XXXX. This is not a matter of dissatisfaction. I did not receive the product I ordered. I have photographs clearly showing the two different sized shoes. 

I have disputed this charge multiple times with American Express ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX, Transaction ID : XXXX ). Each time, American Express has denied my dispute with the same response : that I made the purchase, that it was delivered, and that I agreed to the merchant 's refund policy. Not once has American Express addressed the actual issue that I received mismatched shoes, not the matching pair of Size XXXX sneakers I ordered. 

The merchant 's own chargeback response to American Express lists the dispute reason as " Not as described '' which is precisely correct. Yet American Express sided with the merchant anyway, ignoring my photographic evidence entirely. 

The merchant 's own refund policy states they will " make it right '' if a customer receives the wrong item. They have not done so. This appears to be a XXXX dropship operation that routinely fulfills orders incorrectly and relies on the chargeback process to avoid accountability. 

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), I am entitled to a chargeback for goods that are not as described. American Express is failing to uphold this right. 

I am XXXX year American Express cardholder who has spent hundreds of thousands of dollars with this card. I am requesting that the CFPB require American Express to : 1. Reopen this dispute and properly review my photographic evidence 2. Issue a full credit of {$130.00} to my account 3. Provide a written explanation addressing the mismatched shoes specifically I have also sent a formal written escalation letter to American Express via certified mail. 

Account ending : XXXX Dispute Reference : XXXX Transaction ID : XXXX Transaction Date : XX/XX/year> Amount : {$130.00}
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
I am filing this complaint against Spring Oaks Capital LLC regarding inaccurate and unverifiable debt collection reporting on my consumer credit file. 

The company is reporting a collection account in the amount of {$610.00} and claims ownership of an alleged debt. However, they are not the original creditor and have refused to provide competent evidence that they legally own, purchased, or were assigned this debt. 

Despite my requests, the company has failed to provide : A signed original contract or agreement Chain of title proving lawful ownership/assignment Complete accounting records Documentation bearing my signature Proof of authority to collect The continued reporting and collection of this account without proper verification appears to violate the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), including furnishing inaccurate or misleading information and attempting to collect a debt without validating ownership. 

Additionally, the account appears to be reported in a misleading manner regarding dates, status, and ongoing balance updates despite insufficient verification. 

I request that the CFPB investigate : Whether the company has lawful ownership of the alleged debt Whether the reporting complies with FCRA requirements Whether the company can produce competent evidence of contractual liability Whether inaccurate or misleading information is being furnished to consumer reporting agencies If the company can not provide proper validation and lawful proof of ownership, I request immediate deletion of this account from all credit reporting agencies and cessation of collection activity. 

Thank you for your assistance and investigation into this matter.
label: card_dispute
source_issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed

text:
I am formally escalating a complaint regarding a deferred interest financing account issued by Synchrony Bank related to a {$13000.00} purchase at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 
Despite making payments consistently and in good faith since XX/XX/year>, Synchrony has applied approximately {$4500.00} in retroactive interest after the promotional periodan outcome that is both disproportionate and the direct result of inadequate disclosure and lack of meaningful customer notification.

Let me be clear : this is not a case of non-payment or neglect. This is a case where a customer acted responsibly, yet was subjected to a severe financial penalty due to how this product is structured and communicated.

I am submitting a formal complaint regarding a deferred interest promotional financing account issued by Synchrony Bank in connection with a retail purchase at XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX  XXXX. 
My household made all payments in good faith and with the intent of complying fully with the promotional terms. However, several issues arose that ultimately resulted in a severe and unexpected financial penalty : Key issues that require immediate attention : Failure of clear disclosure : The promotion was presented as 0 %, but the critical distinction of deferred interestand its retroactive applicationwas not communicated in a way that a reasonable consumer would fully understand. Once the {$4500.00} was billed to the account a reasonable business would have been a notification via text or email of this severe penalty and failure to pay the full balance. Failure to do so impacted the monthly payments significantly and created additional penalties unbeknownst to me. Given the digital world it seems reasonable to send notifications for significant events. As a user I don't have a need to log in, search for and review each monthly statement if I have a target payoff date and am making the required fixed monthly payment as there is no activity on the account.

Absence of expiration notice : Synchrony did not provide any proactive, prominent reminder ( email, text, or otherwise ) that the promotional deadline was approaching, despite the significant financial consequences.

Payment structure misalignment : Minimum payments were set at levels that did not meaningfully amortize the balance, increasing the likelihood of triggering retroactive interest even for diligent customers.

Punitive outcome despite positive payment history : All payments were made on time. The triggering issue was a minor underpayment due to incremental chargesyet the result was a {$4500.00} penalty and a sudden spike in required payments. This dramatically increased the balance and caused a spike in the minimum payment. This penalty was imposed despite a history of consistent, on-time payments and good faith effort to meet the terms of the agreement.

Your customer service channels have refused to offer any accommodation, citing strict adherence to contract terms. While I understand the position that the contract is the contract, that does not resolve the underlying issue : the structure and communication of this product create outcomes that are fundamentally unfair to consumers acting in good faith. Electronic statements while convenient for the lender are an additional effort for me, the user to log in, search for and review each monthly statement like I would a paper statement that I have to open and file. Monthly payments were fixed and there was no new activity on the account so no need to make the additional effort, however I would review closely if prompted that a significant event occurred ...

Given the circumstances, I am requesting that Synchrony Bank : Waive or substantially reduce the {$4500.00} in retroactive interest charges ; or Recalculate the account balance using a non-retroactive interest method ; or Offer a reasonable settlement or adjustment reflecting my consistent payment history and the lack of clear, proactive communication regarding the promotional deadline.

I believe this situation reflects broader concerns about the transparency and fairness of deferred interest financing products, particularly when paired with insufficient reminders and payment structures that do not align with consumer expectations.

If Synchrony chooses not to resolve this matter in good faith, I am prepared to continue escalation, including : Ongoing engagement with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Filing additional complaints with consumer protection and state regulatory bodies Sharing this experience through appropriate public and consumer advocacy channels I would strongly prefer to resolve this directly and amicably. However, the current positionoffering no flexibility despite clear mitigating factorsis not acceptable.

Please respond with a substantive resolution, not a restatement of policy. I respectfully request that this complaint be reviewed and that Synchrony Bank provide a reasonable and good-faith resolution.
label: card_dispute
source_issue: Advertising and marketing, including promotional offers
source_sub_issue: Confusing or misleading advertising about the credit card

text:
I booked a hotel through Capital One, specifically, Capital One XXXX. 

Capital One XXXX failed to honor prepaid hotel booking, failed to re-accommodate me, and failed to follow up twice Complaint body : On XX/XX/XXXX, I arrived at XXXX XXXX by XXXX by XXXX in New Jersey for a prepaid hotel reservation booked through Capital One XXXX for {$130.00}. I made this reservation on XX/XX/XXXX. When I arrived, the hotel could not locate or honor my reservation and told me there was no room available for me. They told me to call capital one XXXX  and that they were overbooked. 

I called Capital One XXXX from the hotel and spent over an hour trying to get help. Capital One XXXX was unable to resolve the issue or provide replacement lodging. 

Because I needed a room that night before a business meeting, I physically found and paid for a second XXXX XXXX myself for {$260.00} using my Capital One card. Only after I had already left and checked into the replacement hotel did XXXX XXXX claim XXXX original reservation had been found. They claimed that I was ineligible for a refund for the first hotel room. That later discovery did not fix the problem because the reservation was not usable when I arrived and needed the room. 

Capital One also failed to follow up twice. First, the original Capital One XXXX agent told me I would receive follow-up after the incident, but I did not receive a meaningful resolution. Second, I spoke with a Capital One XXXX on Thursday, XX/XX/XXXX, and was again promised follow-up. It is now Thursday, XX/XX/XXXX, and I still have not received any substantive response only an automated email. 

This is unacceptable. I was left without a usable prepaid room after midnight, Capital One XXXX failed to re-accommodate me in real time, I had to secure my own replacement lodging, I lost roughly two to three hours dealing with this issue, and I did not get to sleep until around XXXX XXXX. before a business meeting. The issue is no longer just the failed hotel booking ; it is Capital One XXXX  repeated failure to take ownership after leaving a customer without usable lodging after midnight.

My requested resolution is : ( 1 ) refund of the original {$130.00} booking, ( 2 ) reimbursement or statement credit for the {$260.00} replacement hotel I had to secure myself, ( 3 ) XXXX Capital One miles or equivalent statement/travel credit for the overnight disruption, failed re-accommodation, poor support, and two missed follow-ups, and ( 4 ) direct outreach from a Capital One corporate/customer advocacy representative, not another general travel-support agent.

I had also been considering expanding my relationship with Capital One through targeted checking and savings offers, but this unresolved experience has made me reconsider whether Capital One can support me reliably as a broader customer.

text:
Synchrony Bank ( CareCredit ) reduced my credit limit from {$8000.00} to {$1000.00} without providing any prior notice. This reduction materially harmed my credit utilization ratio and lowered my credit score, which had been XXXX before this action. The card is maintained specifically for veterinary emergencies, so low usage is expected and consistent with the products intended purpose as a medical-use line of credit. They made it a general use credit card after I opened my account, but that's simply not what I use it for. 

I received no adverse action notice, no email, no letter, and no secure message. When I contacted customer service, I was told the reduction was due to inactivity, but I was given no opportunity to take affirmative action to prevent this. The supervisor also stated that the company has to make money, which does not explain why an unused credit limit would need to be reduced. 

On XX/XX/year>, I mailed a formal written complaint to Synchrony, including identifying account information and a request for review. I have received no acknowledgment or response. Synchrony has removed secure messaging from the CareCredit website, and phone support can not escalate beyond a call-center supervisor, leaving me with no way to resolve this directly. 

I am requesting : - Restoration of my prior {$8000.00} credit limit or a clear explanation of what documentation is required.

- An explanation for the lack of notice before taking an adverse action.

- Clarification of Synchronys policies regarding credit-limit reductions on medical-use accounts.

Attached is a copy of the letter I sent.
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

text:
Between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, multiple Apple Card transactions occurred under documented coercion and extortion, including explicit threats involving my family. This has been supported by law enforcement involvement, including a police investigation and FBI awareness due to the interstate nature of the activity. 

I reported these transactions beginning XX/XX/XXXX and repeatedly provided supporting evidence, including documentation of threats, a detailed timeline, and corroborating materials. The evidentiary record demonstrates a continuous pattern of coercion, a complete absence of voluntary or uncoerced consent, and no retained benefit from the transactions. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, Goldman Sachs legal/dispute department received and signed for a certified mail package containing the full evidentiary record, including documentation of coercion, law enforcement involvement, and supporting exhibits. This placed the Bank on formal notice of the applicable legal framework and the complete factual record. 

Despite this, Goldman Sachs has continued to evaluate the matter under a fraud or billing error framework rather than determining whether valid authorization existed under Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.12 ). Following confirmed receipt of the full evidentiary package, the Bank has issued multiple additional denials that do not address coercion, do not evaluate authorization under duress, and do not identify any evidence supporting valid authorization. 

In total, the Bank has now issued repeated determinations on this matterboth before and after formal noticewithout applying the correct legal standard. These determinations consistently disregard the same evidentiary record, which remains unchanged and fully supported. 

Additionally, the transactions at issue represent a clear and abrupt deviation from XXXX years of prior account history, including rapid, repeated purchases of round-dollar gift cards at unfamiliar merchants. The Banks conclusion that there was no suspicious or fraudulent activity does not reconcile with this pattern and is not supported by the account history. 

The Bank has also failed to provide requested information, including a transaction-level breakdown of the account, the methodology used to calculate the balance and available credit, and the specific evidence relied upon to conclude that authorization was valid. No explanation has been provided addressing the documented coercion or how it was evaluated under Regulation Z. 

This reflects a continued failure to conduct a reasonable investigation, a failure to evaluate authorization under the correct legal standard, and a pattern of non-responsive determinations issued after formal notice and receipt of complete supporting evidence. 

The required legal inquiry is not whether a traditional billing error occurred, whether the merchant delivered goods, or whether the account showed signs of takeover. The required inquiry under Regulation Z, 12 CFR 1026.12 ( b ), is whether the charges were authorized by the cardholder. Authorization requires voluntary, uncoerced consent. Where use is obtained through coercion, threats, or duress, any apparent participation is not valid authorization.

Attached communications demonstrate ongoing coercion and threats during the disputed timeframe. While certain messages may reference compliance with demands, these statements were made under duress and in response to continued threats. The overall communication record reflects a lack of voluntary, uncoerced consent and repeated attempts to stop the harm, not genuine authorization. These communications must be evaluated in their full context as part of a coercion pattern, not isolated statements, as required when determining authorization under Regulation Z
label: card_dispute
source_sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card

Complaint Against Citibank Fraud Claim Mishandling & Billing on Fraudulent Transaction I am filing this complaint regarding Citibanks handling of a fraudulent balance transfer transaction and the subsequent mismanagement of my fraud claim. 

On XX/XX/year>, I used a Citibank promotional balance transfer check in the amount of {$2400.00}, intended to pay my XXXX account. I later confirmed with Citibank that the check was processed on XX/XX/year>. However, the payment never reached my XXXX account. 

After multiple calls and extensive effort, I requested and received a copy of the check on XX/XX/year>. The check was fraudulently made payable to an unauthorized individual ( XXXX XXXX ) and deposited into a XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX account. This was not authorized by me under any circumstance. 

I immediately contacted Citibank and was told by a representative on XX/XX/XXXX that a fraud dispute had been opened, the check would be canceled, and I would be refunded the full amount ( {$2400.00} ) plus the {$120.00} balance transfer fee. I was also told I would not be responsible for the charge. 

However, I have since learned ( as of XX/XX/year> ) from a Citibank Security Operations representative that the dispute was never actually submitted on XX/XX/XXXX, despite what I was told. It was only submitted on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXXover two weeks later. 

During this time XXXX XXXX  I made over XXXX phone calls attempting to resolve this issue XXXX  Citibank repeatedly failed to provide accurate information- I was misled about the status of my fraud dispute - No provisional ( temporary ) credit was applied to my account- I continued to be billed for the fraudulent amount XXXX  My account is now marked past due, and I am being asked to make payments on a fraudulent charge I have also filed a police report regarding this fraud ( Report XXXX XXXXXXXX ). 

Citibanks failure to properly and timely investigate this fraud, combined with providing false information about the dispute status, has caused significant stress and potential harm to my credit. 

I am requesting the following resolution : XXXX. Immediate refund of the full fraudulent amount ( {$2400.00} ) and associated fees ( {$120.00} ) XXXX. Immediate application of a provisional credit while the investigation is ongoing XXXX. Removal of all late fees, past-due status, and any negative credit reporting related to this issue XXXX. Written confirmation that I am not responsible for this fraudulent transaction XXXX. A full review of Citibanks handling of my case, including why I was misinformed about the dispute being filed This situation has been ongoing for over two months, and Citibank has failed to follow proper procedures for handling fraud claims. I am requesting immediate intervention and resolution. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

text:
Subject : Dispute of Inaccurate Late Payment and Reinsertion of Accounts Without Proper Notification Dear MOHELA Dispute Department, I am writing to formally dispute the reporting of a late payment for XX/XX/XXXX on the following accounts associated with my credit file : XX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX These accounts have previously been reinserted into my credit report without proper notification, which is a direct violation of 15 U.S. Code 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) ( B ) of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ). This law clearly states that a furnisher or credit bureau must provide written notice within 5 business days of any reinsertion of previously deleted information. I did not receive any such notification, which infringes upon my rights as a consumer.

Furthermore, I have attempted to resolve this issue multiple times by contacting your office. However, the average wait time exceeds two hours, which has made it extremely difficult to exercise my right to dispute inaccurate or outdated information. I have made a good-faith effort to address this concern, yet I have received no resolution.

According to the FCRA 15 U.S. Code 1681c, negative information such as late payments are only allowed to remain on a consumers credit report for up to seven years from the date of delinquency. The XX/XX/XXXX late payment and associated accounts listed above now exceed or are approaching the reporting time limit and should be permanently removed from my credit report. They must not be reinserted again under any circumstances. 

In addition, the Metro 2 reporting format used for furnishing data to credit bureaus must be complete, consistent, and in compliance with FCRA and CDIA guidelines. I have identified inconsistencies in the payment history and reporting dates associated with these accounts, which further confirms that the information being reported is inaccurate and should be removed. Incomplete or inconsistent data violates the standard Metro 2 format and fails to meet the legal requirement under 15 U.S. Code 1681s-2 ( b ) that data furnishers must conduct proper investigations and report accurate information.

Given the legal grounds outlined above, I am requesting the immediate and permanent deletion of the XX/XX/XXXX late payment and all associated accounts listed from my credit file. I also request that you send written confirmation of this removal once completed. These inaccuracies are negatively affecting my credit profile and creating unnecessary barriers to rebuilding my financial standing. 


Thank you for your prompt attention to this urgent matter.
label: credit_reporting
source_product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports

text:
I reviewed my consumer credit report and discovered information that is inaccurate, incomplete, and misleading. 

I never provided written authorization or consent permitting this information to be furnished or maintained on my consumer report.

The reporting of this information lacks a demonstrated permissible purpose as required under 15 USC 1681b. Lawful authority to report is a prerequisite to any claim of accuracy, verification, or investigation.

The reporting of this information fails to meet the requirement of maximum possible accuracy under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, including 15 USC 1681e ( b ).

Additionally, the information reported includes data that is excluded from consumer reports under 15 USC 1681a ( 2 ) ( B ), as it reflects personal experiences rather than permissible credit reporting information.

Consumer reporting agencies have a duty to ensure fairness, accuracy, relevancy, and proper utilization of consumer information. This agency failed to adopt or follow reasonable procedures to ensure the accuracy and lawful reporting of this information.

Accuracy can not be established where the underlying disclosure itself is not supported by lawful authorization.

This complaint does not constitute an admission of liability, ownership, or responsibility for the reported account. 

The absence of a demonstrated permissible purpose renders continued reporting improper regardless of any claim of accuracy.
label: credit_reporting
source_product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports

text: I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding unauthorized accounts that persist on my credit report despite my repeated efforts to have them removed. These accounts are causing significant damage to my financial reputation and overall well-being. It is evident that the inclusion of these fraudulent items violates 15 USC 1681b regarding permissible purposes, as the credit bureaus have no legitimate reason to report them without my explicit consent. Please refer to the attached file detailing these items I am disputing. The Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S. Code 1681 ) says ( 1 ) The banking system is dependent upon fair and accurate credit reporting. Inaccurate credit reports directly impair the efficiency of the banking system, and unfair credit reporting methods undermine the public confidence, which is essential to the continued functioning of the banking system. So whenever there is a violation under the FCRA, it impairs the efficiency of the banking system. This is a violation of my privacy with my consumer report, since I control what is listed and what is not. In the event that any accounts are reported without my written consent, that is considered identity theft.

text:
To : XXXX Auto  Subject : Formal Dispute of Inaccurate Credit Reporting Repossession Account To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally dispute the accuracy and completeness of the repossession account that your company is furnishing to consumer reporting agencies. The tradeline currently reflects a repossession and a remaining deficiency balance ; however, the reporting does not include any payment history or delinquency timeline necessary to verify the accuracy of the information being reported. 

Despite the absence of this essential information, the account continues to be updated monthly and is reflected as recently updated in XX/XX/year>. Without a complete payment history, it is impossible to verify the alleged delinquency timeline or determine the accuracy of the reported balance. 

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ), furnishers of information to consumer reporting agencies are required to provide information that is accurate and complete. Additionally, under 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ), once a dispute has been raised, the furnisher must conduct a reasonable investigation and verify the accuracy of the information being reported. 

The current reporting also appears incomplete because the original creditor information is not displayed, and there is no documentation supporting the calculation of the reported deficiency balance following repossession. 

To properly verify the accuracy of this account, I am requesting that XXXX Auto provide the following documentation : 1. The complete payment history from account origination through the alleged default and repossession.

2. The Date of First Delinquency ( DOFD ) as defined under 15 U.S.C. 1681c ( c ).

3. A copy of the original signed retail installment contract or loan agreement.

4. Documentation of the repossession, including the notice of repossession and the date the vehicle was repossessed.

5. Documentation showing the sale of the vehicle and the amount received.

6. A detailed itemized breakdown explaining how the reported deficiency balance was calculated.

7. Documentation verifying the information furnished to consumer reporting agencies. 

If XXXX Auto is unable to provide documentation verifying the accuracy and completeness of this account, the continued reporting of this information may constitute inaccurate or incomplete reporting in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act. 

Therefore, I request that XXXX Auto either : ( 1 ) Provide the documentation requested above verifying the accuracy of the account, or ( 2 ) Immediately request deletion of this tradeline from all consumer reporting agencies if the account can not be properly verified. 

I look forward to your prompt response and resolution of this matter. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
label: credit_reporting

text:
In XX/XX/year>, XXXX vehicles were financed through XXXX XXXX XXXX after a dealership facilitated the purchase. During the financing process, XXXX applicants initially participated in the credit application. The dealership later instructed that the financing proceed under XXXX applicant only due to a stronger credit profile. The dealership confirmed that the final loan documentation and financing would be issued under the single approved borrower. 

Despite this instruction, the dealership incorrectly submitted the vehicle registration paperwork to the Department of Motor Vehicles using a fabricated hybrid name that combined elements of both applicants names. The registration was submitted under the name XXXX XXXX XXXX. This name does not correspond to any legal person and does not match the identity of either applicant. 

As a direct result of this administrative error, the vehicles were registered with the DMV under a non-existent identity. Because the registration name did not match the legal identity of the borrower responsible for the loan, it became impossible to properly register the vehicles, renew license plates, or maintain legally compliant insurance tied to the vehicle registration. 

Beginning in XX/XX/year> and continuing through the present, repeated efforts were made to correct this error with the dealership. These efforts included multiple in-person visits to the dealership, direct communications with the sales representative, and communications with the dealership employee responsible for DMV registration XXXX. At least XXXX in-person attempts were made to resolve the issue. Despite these repeated attempts, the dealership failed to correct the registration error or submit corrected documentation to the DMV. 

Because the registration was issued under a fictitious name, the borrower was unable to legally register the vehicles in their name, obtain matching insurance documentation, or renew license plates once the dealer plates expired. The dealer plates expired approximately XXXX months ago. Driving the vehicles in this condition would expose the borrower to legal risks including traffic citations, vehicle impoundment, or enforcement action due to invalid registration and insurance mismatch. 

While this unresolved registration error prevented lawful operation of the vehicles, Ford Motor Credit continued demanding loan payments for vehicles that could not legally be driven due to the dealerships administrative mistake. 

The situation escalated further when repossession activity occurred even though the underlying issue preventing payment compliance originated from the dealerships documentation error and its failure to correct DMV registration records. 

This situation has created severe harm including : Inability to legally register vehicles with the DMV Inability to maintain compliant insurance matching the vehicle registration Exposure to legal risk from operating vehicles with invalid registration Repossession activity tied to a dealership administrative error Damage to credit reports and credit profile Note : In total, XXXX vehicles were financed through Ford Motor Credit as part of the same transaction. Due to the dealerships registration error, both vehicles were recorded with the DMV under a name that does not correspond to any real legal identity. Because of this mistake, I have been unable to properly register the vehicles, obtain insurance that matches the registration, or legally operate the vehicles on public roads. Under these circumstances, it is unreasonable and unfair for the finance company to apply payment pressure or pursue repossession when the vehicles can not be legally driven or properly registered due to an administrative error outside of my control. Expecting payment for vehicles that can not be lawfully used places the borrower in an impossible position and shifts the consequences of the dealerships mistake onto the consumer. 

The dealership and Ford Motor Credit were repeatedly notified of the registration error and its consequences. Despite multiple opportunities to correct the error, no corrective action was taken within a reasonable timeframe. 

A formal investigation is requested into the dealerships DMV registration practices and Ford Motor Credits handling of the account once the error was reported. Assistance is requested to correct the registration records, reverse any credit damage associated with the repossession, and address the financial harm created by this administrative failure.
label: credit_reporting
