This is a follow-up to CFPB Complaint No. XXXX ( against Equifax , filed XX/XX/XXXX ), which Equifax closed XXXX XX/XX/XXXX with " non-monetary relief '' that did not address any of my substantive disputes. 

PRIOR COMPLAINT RECAP In Complaint No. XXXX I documented six specific issues with two XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ) tradelines on my Equifax file : 1. Active re-aging of XXXX chargeoffs ( Equifax showed " most recent missed payment : 1 month ago '' on accounts charged off in late XXXX ). 
2. Corrupted payment history showing " on time '' months on charged-off accounts with unpaid balances of {$6900.00} and {$2000.00}.

3. Cross-bureau inconsistency : same furnisher reporting XXXX  negative months to XXXX  , XXXX to XXXX, XXXX to XXXX. 
4. Credit Card DOFD ( XX/XX/XXXX ) is based on an internal collections transfer the furnisher 's own representative confirmed I did not initiate ; he confirmed I was already delinquent before XX/XX/XXXX. 
5. Line of Credit DOFD of XX/XX/XXXX postdates the chargeoff ( XXXX  shows the chargeoff/closed date as XX/XX/XXXX ). DOFD can not occur after chargeoff. 
6. Equifax never provided the method of verification I formally requested under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ) on XX/XX/XXXX. 

EQUIFAX 'S XX/XX/XXXX RESPONSE Equifax stated the following fields were " updated '' on the XXXX tradelines : SCHEDULED PAYMENT, ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, ACCOUNT HISTORY, CHARGE OFF AMOUNT. They provided no explanation, did not delete the tradelines, did not correct the DOFD, did not address the re-aging, and did not provide the method of verification. 

WHAT THE NEW XX/XX/XXXX REPORT SHOWS I pulled a fresh Equifax report on XX/XX/XXXX ( the same day Equifax closed the prior complaint ). Side-by-side comparison to my XX/XX/XXXX report : - XXXX XXXX XXXX ( acct XXXX ) : still reporting as Charge-off, {$6900.00} balance, {$6900.00} past due, XXXX potentially negative months ( was XXXX -- decreased by XXXX because they removed one month from the grid ). 
- XXXX  Line of Credit ( acct XXXX ) : still reporting as Charge-off, {$2000.00} balance, {$2000.00} past due, XXXX potentially negative months ( was XXXX -- decreased by XXXX ). 
- " Most recent missed payment '' indicator on the FICO 8 score factors page : 0 MONTHS AGO. This is WORSE than my XX/XX/XXXX report ( which said " 1 month ago '' ). Equifax is now reporting that I missed a payment THIS MONTH on accounts that were charged off in XXXX. This is active, escalating re-aging. 
- " On time '' months still appearing in the payment history grid for charged-off accounts ( XX/XX/XXXX- XX/XX/XXXX now showing as on time / blank, even though both accounts remain in charged-off status with full unpaid balances ). The corrupted pattern was not fixed -- it was extended. 
- FICO 8 score : XXXX ( unchanged ). 
- Cross-bureau inconsistency unchanged : Equifax XXXX vs XXXX  XXXX vs XXXX  XXXX negative months for the same account from the same furnisher. - DOFDs unchanged. - MOV under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ) still not provided. THE RE-AGING IS NOT JUST WRONG -- IT IS GETTING WORSE The single most damaging item on my Equifax file is the
FICO score factor stating my most recent missed payment was 0 months ago. This is the primary reason my Equifax FICO 8 ( XXXX ) is below my XXXX  and XXXX  scores. Each month Equifax updates this indicator forward, the chargeoff is treated as a fresh delinquency. Under FCRA 605 ( c ), the reporting period runs from the original DOFD and can not be reset by ongoing reporting. Equifax 's furnishing logic is treating ongoing chargeoff status as new delinquency every cycle.

PARALLEL ACTIONS TAKEN In addition to this complaint, I have : - Filed CFPB complaints against XXXX ( XXXX ), XXXX  ( XXXX ), and the furnisher XXXX ( XXXX ) on XX/XX/XXXX.

- Preserving all evidence for FCRA 616 ( willful ) and 617 ( negligent ) civil action. 

This complaint is the second formal CFPB filing against Equifax on these accounts. The XX/XX/XXXX evidence demonstrates Equifax 's prior response was inadequate and that the underlying violations are continuing and worsening.

I have been a Citi cardholder for approximately XXXX years and maintained a perfect payment history prior to late XXXX. During that time, I experienced significant personal hardship, including XXXX deaths in my family and unexpected financial obligations because of that. 

Additionally, in early XXXX my bank account was compromised. Because of this, I put fraud monitoring on my account. With fraud monitoring, you're required to approve any ACH withdrawal that comes from your account. The issue stems from having to approve it by XXXX XXXX  Eastern Time, in which sometimes the time got away from me and I simply was late doing so. It was automatically rejected, causing XXXX of XXXX payment to become late. 

I proactively contacted Citibank and entered into a structured payment arrangement of {$930.00} per month and the card was cancelled. At the time the arrangement was established, I understood that this payment plan would bring the account current and maintain it in good standing. 

However, I later discovered in XX/XX/XXXX, after noticing Citi reporting my account late for four months now on my credit report, that an additional past-due balance was still required to be paid separately, and that the account had continued to be reported as late for multiple months ( XXXX through XXXX ). This requirement was not clearly communicated to me when the payment plan was established. The arrangement was set up through their customer service department, which I assume was an offshore call center, and there appears to have been a misunderstanding regarding what was required to bring the account fully current. As soon as I was informed of the additional past-due balance, I immediately paid it and brought the account into good standing. 

Despite my proactive efforts and immediate correction once the issue was clarified, Citibank has continued to report these late payments to the credit bureaus. These negative marks are the direct result of a breakdown in communication during a hardship arrangement that I entered into in good faith. 

I am requesting that Citibank review this situation and make a goodwill adjustment to remove the late payment notations associated with this period, as they do not accurately reflect my intent, long-term payment history, or my efforts to resolve the account responsibly. 

I am currently in the process of applying for a mortgage, and these late payment notations are having a significant negative impact on my credit profile and ability to qualify. The FCRA states that it's the consumer reporting agency 's responsibility to ensure that all information contained within consumer files is accurate and free from errors. This section imposes a high and often disregarded standard on consumer reporting agencies. See, e.g., XXXX v. Experian XXXX. XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, at * XXXX ( E.D XXXX XXXX XXXX. XXXX, XXXX ) ( breaking down the requirements of 1681e ( b ), and explaining that " assure ' means 'to make sure or certain : put beyond all doubt, '' ' [ maximum ' means the 'greatest in quantity or highest degree attainable [, ] ' and 'possible ' means something falling within the bounds of what may be done, occur or be conceived. text:
This complaint concerns inaccurate credit reporting and improper loan servicing by Aidvantage, including failure to process valid documentation and wrongful reporting of delinquency and default status over an extended period. 

The borrower was enrolled in school beginning XX/XX/XXXX, as confirmed by official documentation from the school. This qualifies the borrower for in-school deferment. Despite this, Aidvantage reported late payments beginning in XX/XX/XXXX and continued reporting delinquency each month through XX/XX/XXXX, ultimately placing the loans into default status. 

The borrower provided Aidvantage with the school enrollment letter in XX/XX/XXXX clearly showing the XX/XX/XXXX enrollment date. Aidvantage failed to process this documentation and failed to apply the appropriate in-school deferment. As a result, Aidvantage continued to report inaccurate late payments from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX and escalated the account into default, which is not valid given the borrowers verified enrollment status. 

Disputes were submitted to the credit bureaus on or about XX/XX/XXXX, along with supporting documentation. More than 45 days have passed without correction of the inaccurate reporting. Aidvantage has therefore failed to conduct a reasonable investigation and has continued furnishing inaccurate information after being placed on notice. 

This issue affects multiple tradelines ( approximately XXXX accounts ), all of which reflect inaccurate reporting tied to the same failure to apply the verified in-school deferment. 

A complaint has also been filed with XXXX XXXX XXXX, and the issue has been escalated directly with Aidvantage. Despite this, Aidvantage has failed to correct the reporting, failed to process the documentation, and has continued to report a default status in error. 

This constitutes : * Failure to conduct a reasonable investigation * Continued furnishing of inaccurate information * Failure to correct after notice and expiration of the investigation period * Improper loan servicing, including wrongful placement into default status Resolution requested : * Immediate removal of all inaccurate late payment reporting from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX across all affected accounts * Immediate reversal and removal of any default status applied in error * Application of in-school deferment effective XX/XX/XXXX * Correction of all affected tradelines and confirmation that accurate information has been furnished to all credit bureaus The borrower reserves the right to pursue additional remedies available under applicable consumer protection laws if this matter is not fully resolved.
label: credit_reporting

text:
CFPB Complaint Against Sunbit , Inc .

Company : Sunbit , Inc . 
Product : Installment Loan / Consumer Financing Issue : Incorrect account status information, inaccurate due dates, improper credit reporting, and failure to provide meaningful customer support Complaint Narrative : I am filing this complaint regarding Sunbit , Inc.s inaccurate communications, misleading online account information, improper credit reporting, and failure to provide adequate customer service concerning my account. 

Sunbit has repeatedly provided incorrect and conflicting information about my account status and contractual payment due dates. I possess screenshots and documentation supporting each of the issues described below, including copies of my loan contract, Sunbit email communications, screenshots of Sunbits online account portal, and records of my attempts to resolve this issue through customer care. 

Specifically : Inconsistent and incorrect account status dates On XX/XX/year>, I received an email from Sunbit earlier that morning stating my account was XXXX days past due. 
Despite this, Sunbit subsequently treated and/or reported my account as XXXX days past due, which directly contradicts their own sameday communication. 

I have screenshots of this email clearly showing the XXXXday pastdue designation. 
This premature classification materially misrepresents my delinquency status. 



Online portal does not reflect contractual due dates Sunbits online customer portal displays payment due dates that do not match the due dates stated in my loan contract. 

I have screenshots of the portal alongside copies of the contract showing that the portals information does not align with the agreedupon contractual terms. 

This discrepancy makes it impossible for a reasonable consumer to rely on Sunbits system to accurately determine payment obligations. 



Inaccurate credit reporting Because Sunbit characterized my account as 30 days delinquent before it had actually reached that status, inaccurate information was reported or prepared for reporting to consumer reporting agencies. 

Reporting a XXXXday delinquency when the account was still only XXXX  days past due is demonstrably inaccurate based on Sunbits own communications and records. 



Failure to maintain reasonable procedures to ensure accuracy The conflicting email communications, inaccurate portal information, and premature delinquency designation demonstrate a failure to maintain reasonable procedures designed to ensure the accuracy of information furnished to consumer reporting agencies. 

These failures are documented through screenshots of emails, the online portal, and the underlying contract. 



Failure to provide meaningful customer support or escalation I attempted to contact Sunbits customer care department to resolve these discrepancies and prevent inaccurate reporting. 

During my attempt to resolve the issue, I was informed that no supervisors were available, and I was not provided with a reasonable alternative escalation path. 

This failure to provide access to supervisory review prevented timely dispute resolution and allowed inaccurate account information to persist. 

Denying consumers the ability to escalate known inaccuracies contributes to unfair and deceptive practices and increases the likelihood of harm. 




Applicable Legal Violations Sunbits actions appear to violate the following federal consumer protection laws : Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) 15 U.S.C. 1681s2 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) A furnisher may not report information it knows or has reasonable cause to believe is inaccurate.

15 U.S.C. 1681s2 ( a ) ( 2 ) A furnisher must correct and update information that is inaccurate or incomplete.

15 U.S.C. 1681 ( b ) Consumer reporting information must meet the standard of maximum possible accuracy.

Reporting a XXXXday delinquency when the account had not yet reached that threshold violates this accuracy requirement.

Consumer Financial Protection Act ( UDAAP ) 12 U.S.C. 5531 and 5536 Prohibit unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts or practices. 
Providing misleading due dates, conflicting delinquency information, unreliable account portals, and denying reasonable escalation for dispute resolution constitutes deceptive and unfair conduct likely to mislead a reasonable consumer and cause harm
label: credit_reporting

text:
Thank you for the response provided by Toyota Financial Services. However, several important issues remain unresolved and portions of the response are inaccurate or incomplete.

1 Notice of Intent to Sell Vehicle XXXX states that a Notice of Our Plan to Sell Property was sent on XX/XX/XXXX. I never received this notice and therefore was never given a meaningful opportunity to redeem or reinstate the vehicle. I currently do not have access to my Toyota Financial account to verify what address this notice was allegedly sent to. I request proof of the address used, proof of mailing, and confirmation that the notice was sent to the correct address.

2 Statements Regarding Post-Repossession Payments Toyotas response states that the payments received after repossession were fully customer-controlled recurring payments. This explanation is incomplete and misleading.

I had automatic recurring payments enabled before the repossession occurred. In XX/XX/XXXX, Toyota suspended or restricted my account access and the recurring payment process was interrupted. The account functionality was later restored or reactivated in XX/XX/XXXX, after which four additional automatic withdrawals occurred.

This was not a situation where I intentionally continued making new voluntary payments after repossession with uninterrupted account access and control, as implied in the response. I request clarification regarding : when and why the account access was suspended, when and how the recurring payment functionality was restored, and why automatic withdrawals continued after repossession and during the charge-off process.

3 Incomplete Accounting and Remaining Discrepancies I continue to dispute the accuracy and completeness of the accounting provided. The response still does not adequately reconcile : the total amount paid over the life of the contract, the application of post-repossession payments, the repossession and sale-related charges, and the remaining deficiency balance.

Additionally, the vehicle sale amount appears significantly below reasonable market value for a XXXX XXXX XXXX, and no explanation or supporting documentation regarding the commercial reasonableness of the sale has been provided. 
XXXX XXXX XXXX Because these disputes remain unresolved, I continue to dispute the accuracy and completeness of the credit reporting associated with this account.

I respectfully request : proof of mailing and address used for all repossession and sale notices, a complete reconciled accounting of the account, clarification regarding account suspension/reactivation and recurring payment functionality after repossession, and review/correction of any inaccurate or misleading credit reporting.

Thank you.

text:
I contacted the company prior to any delinquency and requested hardship assistance. During those communications, I was informed by a representative, and later confirmed by a management-level representative, that I had been enrolled in a hardship program and that no negative reporting would occur while the program was in place. 

I relied on these representations in good faith. 

Despite this, the account was reported with multiple late payments, including 30, 60, and 90-day delinquencies. The account is also being reported as closed while continuing to reflect delinquency and a past-due balance, which is inconsistent with the hardship representations that were made. 

I did not receive any written documentation outlining the terms of the hardship program, including payment terms or reporting conditions. 

I disputed this matter, but the reporting was not corrected. 

Additionally, this reporting has already resulted in adverse action by another creditor, including a reduction in my credit limit due to delinquency and past-due status reflected in my credit report. 

Given the lack of documentation supporting the hardship terms that were communicated to me, and the inconsistencies in the reporting, I am requesting that the company either : Provide complete documentation substantiating the accuracy of the reporting, including hardship program terms and how delinquency was calculated under those terms. 
or Delete the account from my credit file if the information can not be verified as accurate and complete. 

I am also requesting that all relevant account records, including recorded calls and internal notes, be reviewed as part of this process.

text:
XXXX XXXX Subject : Request to Reopen CFPB Complaint XXXX XXXX Matter Not Resolved / False Reporting by Genesis Finance XXXX XXXX  I am formally requesting that CFPB reopen complaint XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX which was closed on XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX as resolved. This matter has not been resolved, and Genesis Finances response to CFPB was false, misleading, and materially inaccurate.

I strongly dispute Genesis Finances claim that my vehicle was Voluntarily Surrendered. The false designation of Voluntary Surrender is currently appearing on my credit reports and remains visible to creditors, lenders, and financial institutions. This inaccurate derogatory reporting falsely portrays me as a borrower who defaulted on financial obligations, causing ongoing and substantial harm to my creditworthiness, reputation, and ability to obtain future financing, This statement is completely false and does not reflect the facts of my case. 

The vehicle in question was a XXXX XXXX XXXXemon vehicle XXXX XXXX  and was returned as part of a negotiated XXXX XXXX Settlement Agreement XXXX XXXX On XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, I returned the vehicle to the dealership from which I had leased it, in the presence of Genesis Finance representatives. At that time, Genesis agents provided me with a settlement check for nearlXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX which I accepted pursuant to the settlement agreement. 

From that date forward, I was no longer the owner or possessor of the vehicle. 

On XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, I officially surrendered the license plates to the XXXX, and I possess documentation confirming this transaction. Therefore, it is factually impossible for Genesis Finance to claim that I voluntarily surrendered the vehicle in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, because I had already lawfully returned the vehicle and relinquished all ownership and possession nearly XXXX months earlier under a binding settlement agreement. 

Genesis Finances reporting is false, inaccurate, and highly damaging to my credit profile. 

Additionally, after regulatory intervention, I received an additional payment of XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, which I deposited into my bank account. This further confirms that this matter was being resolved through a settlement processnot through default, repossession, or voluntary surrender. 

I also wish to clarify that I was late on a payment only once, and that occurred solely because Genesis Finances case XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX explicitly instructed me not to make that payment during a XXXX-way telephone call in the presence of my son, who can verify this conversation. 

At no time did I voluntarily default on this lease, abandon the vehicle, or surrender it due to inability or unwillingness to pay. The return of the vehicle occurred solely because the vehicle was defective and qualified as a lemon vehicle, and the matter was resolved through a formal settlement process. 

I must also state unequivocally that Genesis Finances response to CFPB was XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX false and misleading XXXX XXXX 

The information Genesis Finance provided to CFPB does not accurately reflect the facts of my case. Furthermore, even the telephone number Genesis supplied as contact information was not a valid working number for their company, which raises serious concerns regarding the credibility, accuracy, and good faith of their response. 

I believe CFPB closed my complaint based on false, incomplete, or misleading information submitted by Genesis Finance. 

The damage caused by Genesis Finances false reporting has been devastating. Their inaccurate reporting of Voluntary Surrender has severely damaged my credit history and credit score, despite the fact that this vehicle was returned under a lawful settlement agreement involving a lemon vehicle claim.

text:
I reviewed my consumer credit report and discovered information that is inaccurate, incomplete, and misleading. 

I never provided written authorization or consent permitting this information to be furnished or maintained on my consumer report. 

The reporting of this information lacks a demonstrated permissible purpose as required under 15 USC 1681b. Lawful authority to report is a prerequisite to any claim of accuracy, verification, or investigation.

The reporting of this information fails to meet the requirement of maximum possible accuracy under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, including 15 USC 1681e ( b ).

Additionally, the information reported includes data that is excluded from consumer reports under 15 USC 1681a ( 2 ) ( B ), as it reflects personal experiences rather than permissible credit reporting information.

Consumer reporting agencies have a duty to ensure fairness, accuracy, relevancy, and proper utilization of consumer information. This agency failed to adopt or follow reasonable procedures to ensure the accuracy and lawful reporting of this information.

Accuracy can not be established where the underlying disclosure itself is not supported by lawful authorization. 

This complaint does not constitute an admission of liability, ownership, or responsibility for the reported account. 

The absence of a demonstrated permissible purpose renders continued reporting improper regardless of any claim of accuracy.
label: credit_reporting
source_product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports

text: The Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S. Code 1681 ) says ( 1 ) The banking system is dependent upon fair and accurate credit reporting. Inaccurate credit reports directly impair the efficiency of the banking system, and unfair credit reporting methods undermine the public confidence which is essential to the continued functioning of the banking system. So whenever there is a violation under the FCRA, it impairs the efficiency of the banking system. This is a violation of TILA US Code 1666B since I was never late. Consumer Reporting Agencies play a crucial role in gathering and assessing consumer credit information. However, they must exercise their responsibilities with fairness, impartiality, and respect for the consumers right to privacy. The banking system relies on accurate and fair credit reporting, and when these reporting methods are unfair, it undermines public trust, which is vital for the system 's stability. I never gave my explicit, written consent for these agencies to report my information, which is a violation of 15 U.S. Code 1681b ( 2 ). This violation has led to an alleged case of identity theft, breaching 15 U.S. Code 1681c-2. The accounts in question are not related to any transactions made by me. The Fair Debt Collection Practices Act was created to ensure that laws and procedures are in place to protect consumers from abusive, deceptive, and unfair practices by debt collectors. In this case, the company has violated 15 U.S. Code 1692f, which prohibits debt collectors from using unfair or unconscionable means to collect a debt. Specifically, they can not collect any amounts that arent explicitly authorized by the agreement or allowed by law. Additionally, the company has violated 15 U.S. Code 1692e, which prohibits the use of false, deceptive, or misleading representations in debt collection efforts. This includes using deceptive methods to collect a debt or gather consumer information. Moreover, they are forbidden from contacting consumers at inconvenient times or places, or engaging in behavior that harasses or abuses consumers. In this case, the company has harassed me by claiming I owe a debt that I do not recognize as my responsibility. They have also contacted me at inconvenient times and places, in violation of the law. I am filing this complaint because I disputed inaccurate or incomplete information on my consumer report with a consumer reporting agency. Under 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( Fair Credit Reporting Act 611 ), the agency is required to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation, free of charge, within 30 days of receiving my dispute, to determine whether the information is inaccurate. The agency must notify the data furnisher, review and verify the accuracy of the information, and delete or correct it if it can not be verified. The agency must also provide me with written results of the reinvestigation, including a copy of my corrected consumer report, and notify me of my right to add a statement of dispute. 

In my case, the agency did not comply with these requirements. It failed to complete a reasonable reinvestigation and/or did not correct or delete unverifiable information within 30 days. text:
On XX/XX/XXXX, my son, the primary borrower on this private student loan, filed XXXX XXXX bankruptcy. I remained the non filing co-signer on the account, and we live at the same address. Before this issue, the account had about XXXX years of perfect on time payment history. 

After the bankruptcy filing, Sallie Mae XXXX online portal access and canceled the existing autopay arrangement. At the same time, monthly statements and mailed notices stopped coming to our home. Neither my son nor I received clear notice or instructions explaining how payments were supposed to be made during that period. Online access was not restored until after discharge, and only after my son received a credit alert on XX/XX/XXXX and called customer service. That same day, a payment was made to bring the account current, and on XX/XX/XXXX, another payment was made to cover the XX/XX/XXXX payment. I did not begin receiving late notices until after online access had been restored and the delinquency had already been paid. Because we share the same address, it appears Sallie Mae may have stopped sending notices during the bankruptcy period, yet no explanation or alternative payment instructions were ever provided. As a result, the account fell behind without meaningful notice, and Sallie Mae later reported it as XXXX days late for XX/XX/XXXX. 

My son also had another student loan with a different servicer, XXXX, during the same period. That account was not handled in the same way and did not result in comparable late reporting, which makes XXXX XXXX handling of this account even harder to understand. 

Once access was restored and the delinquency became known, the account was promptly brought current and has remained in good standing since. In fact, after the XX/XX/XXXX payment brought the account current, the XX/XX/XXXX payment put the account XXXX month ahead. Even so, Sallie Mae refused to correct the XX/XX/XXXX late reporting. I disputed the matter, but the response did not address the central issue : this delinquency followed Sallie Maes own servicing actions, including disabling account access, canceling autopay, and stopping mailed communications, without giving either the borrower or the non filing co-signer a clear way to monitor the account and avoid delinquency. 

This has had a real impact on my life. On XX/XX/XXXX, I was forced to walk away from a home purchase because this derogatory mark affected my mortgage financing and created a serious risk that the transaction would not close. Since then, I have delayed my home search for about XXXX months while trying to fix this problem before moving forward. I have spent hours on the phone with Sallie Mae. I had my son write to its Customer Advocacy department. I sent multiple letters to Sallie Maes executive offices. I submitted disputes to all major credit bureaus.I also consulted with multiple law firms that handle FCRA matters to better understand my rights and how to address this dispute. Everything I have done has been an effort to limit the financial damage this reporting has caused. 

The harm has not been only financial. This has been deeply stressful. It has strained my relationship with my son and led to conflict within my family. At the same time, I am still living in housing that is not in livable condition, and my rent is expected to increase substantially. I understand this issue may take time to resolve, but the longer this reporting stays on our files, the more damage it causes. 

I respectfully ask for CFPBs help in requiring Sallie Mae to review this matter fairly and correct the XX/XX/XXXX late payment reporting. I am not asking for special treatment. I am asking Sallie Mae to address whether it is fair and accurate to continue reporting this delinquency when the missed payment followed Sallie Maes own servicing disruptions and lack of notice, rather than an ordinary failure or refusal to pay.

text:
I am filing this complaint regarding Rocket Mortgages refusal to consider a goodwill adjustment for a single late payment reported in XX/XX/XXXX on Loan # XXXX. 

I am not disputing the accuracy of the payment history. I acknowledge that the payment was received after the due date due to a one-time clerical error when scheduling payments from XXXX bank accounts. XXXX payments were intended to be made from separate accounts, but both were mistakenly scheduled from the same account, causing XXXX to be returned. Due to the holiday season, I was not immediately aware of the issue. As soon as I became aware on XX/XX/XXXX, I immediately corrected it by submitting a {$10000.00} payment to bring the account fully current and cover any associated fees. 

I have maintained excellent credit dating back to the 1990s and have never had a late payment on any obligation. I currently hold XXXX additional mortgages that have never once had a late payment. This is a single, isolated incident that does not reflect my financial behavior. 

I have made multiple attempts to resolve this directly with Rocket Mortgage, including formal written requests, a handwritten letter sent via UPS ( tracking number XXXX, delivered and signed for on XX/XX/XXXX ), and a formal Qualified Written Request. Rocket Mortgage acknowledged my strong payment history and confirmed that this was an honest mistake that I corrected immediately. 

However, their responses repeatedly deny my request by stating they are required under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) to report accurately. This does not address my actual request. I am not disputing accuracy I am requesting a discretionary goodwill adjustment. 

Rocket Mortgage also publishes guidance indicating that goodwill adjustments may be considered in appropriate circumstances. Despite clearly meeting the criteria of a one-time error with an otherwise flawless history, they have refused to apply discretion and have not provided a meaningful explanation as to why. 

This has caused significant financial harm, including reductions in credit limits and account closures by other creditors, despite my long-standing history of responsible credit use.
label: credit_reporting

text: I am filing this complaint to demand a full re-investigation of all inaccurate, unverifiable, and misleading information being reported on my credit reports by Equifax , Experian, and TransUnion. text:
Re : Notice of Improper Reinsertion of Deleted Student Loan Account I am writing to dispute the reinsertion of a previously deleted account on my credit report. 
The account in question is a student loan originally reported under XXXX and has now reappeared under Department of educationXXXX This account was previously disputed and deleted from my credit report. Its reappearance constitutes a reinsertion under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 5 ) ( B ) ). Under this law, a credit reporting agency may not reinsert a deleted item unless : 1. The furnisher certifies that the information is complete and accurate, and 2. I am notified in writing within 5 business days of the reinsertion.

To date, I have not received any notice of reinsertion as required by law.

Therefore, I am requesting the following : Immediate removal of this account from my credit report Documentation of the certification of accuracy provided by the furnisher Proof that proper reinsertion procedures were followed, including the required notification If you are unable to provide this documentation, the account must be deleted in accordance with the FCRA.

Please complete your investigation and provide written confirmation of the results, along with an updated copy of my credit report.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this
label: credit_reporting

text:
I am submitting this complaint against Equifax Information Services LLC regarding multiple hard inquiries appearing on my consumer report that I do not recognize and did not authorize. After reviewing my Equifax credit report on XX/XX/XXXX, I found the following disputed hard inquiries : XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ), XXXX  ( XX/XX/XXXX ), XXXX  ( XX/XX/XXXX ), XXXX  ( XX/XX/XXXX ), XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ), XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ), AN # XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ), XXXX XXXX  ( XX/XX/XXXX ), and XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ). 
These inquiries were made without my knowledge, consent, or authorization. Under 15 U.S.C. 1681b ( a ) ( 2 ), a hard inquiry may only be reported if there was a lawful permissible purpose. Under 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ), Equifax must follow reasonable procedures to assure maximum possible accuracy, and under 15 U.S.C. 1681i, Equifax must conduct a reasonable reinvestigation when disputed information is challenged.

I requested a real investigation, not a generic or automated response. For each inquiry, Equifax should be able to identify the company that accessed my file, the permissible purpose claimed, and the application, authorization, contract, or other documentary evidence supporting the inquiry. If Equifax can not provide competent documentation showing a valid permissible purpose, the inquiry must be deleted immediately.

These unauthorized hard inquiries are harming the accuracy and integrity of my credit file and may be negatively affecting my creditworthiness. I am asking the CFPB to require Equifax to conduct a manual reinvestigation, disclose the exact method of verification used for each inquiry, and delete every inquiry that can not be fully verified with documentary evidence.
label: credit_reporting
source_product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
source_sub_issue: Credit inquiries on your report that you don't recognize

text:
Complaint Against : American Credit Acceptance , LLC I am submitting this complaint regarding inaccurate credit reporting and an incomplete investigation by American Credit Acceptance , LLC.

I previously disputed this account ; however, the company responded with a generic explanation and failed to correct clear inaccuracies.

First, my credit report is currently reflecting a balance of approximately {$11000.00} on this account. However, documentation provided by American Credit Acceptance shows that the actual deficiency balance is {$7700.00}. This is a clear violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ), which requires furnishers to report accurate information. Reporting an inflated balance is misleading and harmful to my credit profile.

Second, the account is being reported as a repossession, when the vehicle was voluntarily surrendered. This distinction matters, and reporting it solely as a repossession is misleading and does not accurately reflect the account history.

Third, the company has failed to provide sufficient validation of the alleged debt. I have not received : * Verifiable proof of proper notice prior to the sale of the vehicle * Proof that the deficiency notice was properly mailed and delivered * Complete auction documentation supporting the sale price * A detailed and verified breakdown of all fees and charges applied The company claims it conducted an investigation, but its response relied solely on internal records rather than providing independent, verifiable documentation. This does not meet the standards required under the Fair Credit Reporting Act for a reasonable investigation.

Due to these issues, I am requesting the following : 1. Immediate correction of the inaccurate balance being reported 2. Correction of the account status to accurately reflect a voluntary surrender 3. Full validation of the debt with proper documentation 4. If the company can not provide complete and verifiable documentation, I am requesting deletion of this account from all credit reporting agencies I am seeking a fair resolution and expect the company to comply fully with federal law. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
label: credit_reporting
source_issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem

text:
I am a CareCredit cardholder ( Synchrony Bank, account ending XXXX ) writing to formally complain about servicing failures by Synchrony Bank that materially harmed my ability to manage my account and have caused significant, documented damage to my credit profile and financial life.

I am not disputing the underlying purchase, the disclosed deferred-interest terms, or risk-based credit limit decreases. I am specifically complaining about three issues : ( 1 ) Synchrony 's unified login platform migration in XXXX XXXX, which disrupted my online account access during a period in which I was making consistent payments and prevented me from continuing to pay through the channel I had been using ; ( 2 ) Synchrony 's continued assessment of late fees and interest at 32.99 % APR on the account after Synchrony itself closed the account in XX/XX/XXXX ; and ( 3 ) the cumulative impact of these servicing actions on my credit reporting and ability to access credit.

Account history : I opened the CareCredit account on XX/XX/XXXX with an initial purchase of {$2500.00} under an 18-month deferred-interest promotion expiring XX/XX/XXXX. The original credit limit was {$2500.00} [ see Attachment 01 ]. Throughout late XXXX and into XXXX, I made payments through Synchrony 's online portal at synchrony.com / carecredit.com.

My pre-migration payment pattern was consistent and online-portal-based : Payment Date Amount Channel XX/XX/XXXX {$67.00} Online portal XX/XX/XXXX {$130.00} Online portal XX/XX/XXXX {$190.00} Online portal last successful online payment [ Attachment 02 ] [ Payment gap during migration window no portal access ] XX/XX/XXXX {$500.00} Phone payment same day as account closure [ Attachment 03 ] The pre-migration payments were not only consistent but escalating - {$67.00}, then {$130.00}, then {$190.00} - which is not the profile of a customer headed toward default.

The migration that disrupted my access : In XXXX XXXX, Synchrony migrated its CareCredit and private-label credit card customers to a unified login platform on synchrony.com a " new sign-in experience. '' This migration is publicly documented and well-known to have caused widespread access problems for cardholders. A compiled exhibit of public third-party documentation is provided in Attachment 09, drawn from two sources : XXXX XXXX XXXX ( active and unresolved as of XX/XX/XXXX ), which states : " We have identified that users with Synchrony Bank Credit Cards and Care Credit receive a XXXX / XXXX error in XXXX XXXX for XXXX and in XXXX XXXX for XXXX. This is a known issue and has been escalated to our teams for investigation. At this time, we don't have an estimated resolution, but we're actively working on it. '' The alert has been formally reopened six times since XX/XX/XXXX five months of escalation with no resolution. ( XXXX ) Verbatim XXXX XXXX XXXX reviews of Synchrony 's own MySynchrony app, with reviewer names and dates spanning XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX covering the entire period of this complaint. Selected examples ( full screenshots in Attachment 09 ) : XXXX XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX, 54 helpful votes ) : " for the past 3 weeks, I haven't been able to access my account on their app anymore!!! I'm only allowed to pay as a guest now! So now I have to keep track which bill is due manually or they'll make me pay a late fee! '' XXXX XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : " Haven't been able to log in for months. This app is useless. '' A XXXX user ( XX/XX/XXXX, 27 helpful votes ) : " when I try to log into the app to check balances or pay a bill about 3 out of 4 times it will say we are having trouble connecting try again later. '' XXXX XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : " it hasn't opened for me in months. it used to work, then suddenly wouldn't log in. I've been trying weekly for 4-5 months now and it still won't work. '' XXXX XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : " On the previous app I was able to pull up and make payments with the one login for the 3 accounts. Now, it says it doesn't recognize my username or password. '' XXXX XXXX ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : " required re-register every time log in. '' The full text and additional context for each of these sources is provided in Attachment 09 for the reviewer 's reference.

My experience with Synchrony during this time : I did not receive - or did not recognize as actionable - any clear notification from Synchrony explaining that my login credentials would be invalidated, what I needed to do to maintain online access, or how to make payments through alternative channels during the transition. I have searched my email accounts and can not locate any clear, actionable migration communication from Synchrony or CareCredit. Synchrony had my mobile phone number from account opening [ Attachment 01 ], yet I received no SMS notification of the migration either.

My access was disrupted during this migration window. I was unable to log in to my CareCredit account through the channel I had been using to make my regular payments. I continued receiving paper statements and notices in the mail demanding payment, but the online portal- my established payment method- was inaccessible. During the disruption, I assumed, based on Synchrony 's prior conduct, that the portal access issue would be resolved through normal channels. I did not anticipate that the disruption would persist for months without resolution. The publicly documented systemic nature of the migration disruption [ Attachment 09 ] confirms that my experience was not unique - it was the documented experience of Synchrony cardholders across multiple platforms throughout this period. 

My XX/XX/XXXX phone payment demonstrates good faith and intent to pay. When I worked through Synchrony 's phone payment channel, I did not make a minimum payment. I made a {$500.00} payment, which was XXXX times larger than my largest previous payment [ Attachment XXXX ]. This was a good-faith catch-up payment by a customer trying to bring his account current after a servicer-caused disruption to his established payment method. 

Synchrony closed my account the same day I made the catch-up payment. According to my TransUnion credit report, Synchrony closed my account on XX/XX/XXXX - the same day my {$500.00} payment posted [ Attachment 06c ]. According to my XXXX  report, the closure was effective XX/XX/XXXX [ Attachment 06d ]. Synchrony chose to close the account rather than restore my online access or work with me to keep the account in good standing after I demonstrated my willingness and ability to pay.

Notably, Synchrony charged me a {$41.00} late fee on XX/XX/XXXX seven days BEFORE my {$500.00} catch-up payment [ Attachment 03 ] - meaning they knew the account was past due and chose to assess additional fees rather than reach out to help me cure or restore my access.

Post-closure conduct : Once Synchrony closed the account on XX/XX/XXXX, my reasonable understanding as a consumer was that the account was no longer active and that there was no longer a standard servicing relationship through which to make ongoing payments. Synchrony itself had unilaterally ended the active account relationship. I continued to receive paper statements demanding payment on the resulting balance, but Synchrony 's own action - closing the account on the same day I made a {$500.00} catch-up payment - communicated clearly that the standard active-account servicing channels I had used were no longer the appropriate path.

Despite closing the account and ending normal servicing, Synchrony continued to assess $ 41 monthly late fees and interest at 32.99 % APR on the balance for the next six months [ Attachment 07 - portal activity showing post-closure fees ]. Specifically, between XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX alone, Synchrony assessed {$120.00} in late fees and {$240.00} in interest charges on the closed-account balance. The balance grew from {$1900.00} in XX/XX/XXXX [ Attachment 03 ] to {$3200.00} by XX/XX/XXXX [ Attachment 05 ] - a {$1300.00} increase driven entirely by deferred-interest billing [ Attachment 04 - XX/XX/XXXX statement showing {$1300.00} deferred-interest charge ] and post-closure fees and interest, with XXXX new purchases. 




Synchrony 's decision to close the account : Rather than restore my online access or work with me after I demonstrated good faith with the {$500.00} payment- is the proximate cause of the six months of post-closure fees and interest accrual that followed. A consumer can not reasonably be expected to service a closed account through the same channels as an active account. Closing an account ends the active relationship; continuing to assess late fees and 32.99 % interest on the resulting balance for six months, on an account the consumer reasonably believes is no longer in active servicing, is fundamentally unfair.

Documented financial harm : The negative tradeline reported by Synchrony is now appearing on all three major credit bureaus showing 120+ days delinquent, 177 % utilization, and a balance of {$3200.00} against a {$1800.00} credit limit [ Attachment 06a - three-bureau summary ; Attachment 06b - payment history showing the cascade from XX/XX/XXXX forward ]. This single tradeline has caused the following documented harm : On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX  reduced my business credit line on my XXXX XXXX account from {$10000.00} to {$4300.00} a 57 % reduction - citing " Delinquency reported on accounts '' as the primary reason and " Balance owed on revolving accounts too high '' as a secondary reason [ Attachment 08 XXXX  adverse action notice ]. The Synchrony CareCredit tradeline is the dominant driver of both factors - it is the only seriously delinquent account on my credit report, and its 177 % utilization ( driven by Synchrony 's post-closure fee stacking ) is a major contributor to my elevated revolving balance metrics. XXXXXXXX XXXX adverse action also reduced employee credit lines connected to this account, extending the harm to my team. This is not a claim of speculative harm- this is a third-party financial institution 's adverse action notice attributing the credit reduction directly to the tradeline at issue. 

My XXXX  score, previously above XXXX, has been reduced substantially as a direct result of this single account. 




What I have already done to resolve this. 

I contacted Synchrony by phone and was offered a settlement of approximately {$1300.00} in exchange for resolving the account. The Synchrony representative declined to ( a ) update the tradeline to reflect a good-standing status of " Paid in Full, '' ( b ) reverse late fees and interest assessed during the migration access disruption period, or ( c ) consider deletion of the tradeline as part of settlement. The Synchrony representative specifically directed me to file a complaint with the CFPB if I wished to pursue tradeline deletion as part of resolution.
label: credit_reporting

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding the EDFINANCIAL account currently being reported on my credit file with a reported balance of {$3900.00}. I respectfully request a complete investigation into the accuracy, balance calculation, and payment reporting associated with this account under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ). After reviewing my credit report, I noticed discrepancies and potentially misleading reporting that may not accurately reflect the true status of this account.

Specifically, the account reflects a balance that exceeds the original highest balance reported, which raises concerns regarding the accuracy and calculation of the amount being furnished to the credit bureaus. I am requesting that EDFINANCIAL provide full verification of the debt, including the original signed agreement, complete payment history, itemized balance calculations, interest and fee breakdowns, and documentation proving the accuracy of all reported late or missed payments. If this information can not be fully verified, I request that the account and any associated derogatory payment history be corrected or removed immediately from all consumer reporting agencies pursuant to Sections 609 and 611 of the FCRA.

Additionally, I respectfully request goodwill consideration concerning any derogatory reporting tied to this account. Any missed payments occurred during a period of financial hardship, and I have continued making efforts to address my financial obligations responsibly. The continued reporting of negative payment history is causing substantial harm to my ability to rebuild my credit and move forward financially. I am asking for compassion and consideration in reviewing this account for the removal of derogatory marks and inaccurate reporting.

Please conduct a thorough investigation and provide written confirmation of the results, including any corrections, updates, or deletions made to my credit report.

text:
CFPB Complaint Auto Loan Validation & Inaccurate Credit Reporting I am filing this complaint against JPMCB AUTO it is furnishing auto loan information to the credit bureaus that I believe is inaccurate, incomplete, unverifiable, and not properly validated. 

Account # : XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX I have requested documentation supporting the companys legal authority to continue reporting this account, including the original signed agreement, full payment history, complete billing ledger, charge-off or repossession records if applicable, balance calculation, Date of First Delinquency, and any documentation showing ownership or assignment of the account. The company has not provided sufficient validation to support the accuracy of the information being furnished.

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, furnishers have a duty to report accurate information and may not furnish information they know or have reasonable cause to believe is inaccurate under 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ). If a dispute is received through a credit bureau, the furnisher must conduct a proper investigation and report accurate results under 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ). Credit reporting agencies are also required to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation of disputed information under 15 U.S.C. 1681i.

I am also requesting a review of the XXXX XXXX reporting fields connected to this account, including account status, payment rating, payment history profile, current balance, past-due balance, Date of First Delinquency, date closed, charge-off date, repossession status if applicable, special comment codes, and compliance condition codes. Any inconsistent, incomplete, outdated, or contradictory reporting should be corrected or deleted.

This inaccurate reporting is damaging my credit profile and affecting my ability to obtain fair credit terms, financing, housing, and other financial opportunities.
label: credit_reporting
source_sub_issue: Reporting company used your report improperly

text:
Good afternoon. I am writing to formally dispute the default status reported on my student loan, which has negatively affected my credit score. I understand the importance of staying current with financial obligations, and I would never intentionally default on a loan. However, in this case, I was never properly notified about the status of my student loan or the need to begin repayment. 

The communications and billing statements were sent to my former residencemy parents housewhich I have not lived at since graduating college. As a result, I was completely unaware that my account had entered default. I only became aware of the issue when I recently checked my credit report and saw the default had been reported. 

Had I received proper notification, I would have addressed the situation immediately. I am currently employed and fully capable of making payments. I am also eager to take steps toward resolving the matter responsibly. However, the default status is now significantly limiting my financial flexibility and damaging my credit standing, which further impairs my ability to resolve this issue efficiently. 

Given that the default occurred without proper notice or an opportunity to cure the delinquency, I respectfully request the removal or adjustment of the default from my credit report. I also ask that my account be considered for rehabilitation or other remedies that allow for correction without the long-term impact of a default that occurred under these unusual and unintended circumstances. 

Thank you for your time and understanding. I am committed to resolving this and welcome any assistance or options Mohela may offer. Please confirm receipt of this message and let me know what documentation or steps may be needed on my part to move forward.
label: credit_reporting

text:
I am submitting this as a new complaint and formal reinvestigation demand, as the previous case ( # XXXX ) was closed without resolution of the underlying issue. The inaccurate reporting remains on my credit file, and I do not accept the prior closure as a final determination. 

This letter serves as formal notice that the accounts reported by : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 

are fraudulent and do not belong to me. 

I did not open, authorize, apply for, or agree to these accounts. These accounts are the result of identity theft. 

Experian Fraud Determination I was formally informed by Experian that the XXXX and XXXX accounts were determined to be fraudulent. Experian recognized that the tradelines were the result of identity theft. 

Despite this, the accounts continue to be reported or handled improperly by other reporting agencies. 

The fact that XXXX major consumer reporting agency has already recognized these accounts as fraud demonstrates that the underlying data is unreliable and that continued reporting by others is unreasonable. 

FTC Identity Theft Report Filed I have filed an official Identity Theft Report with the Federal Trade Commission ( FTC ) documenting that these accounts were opened fraudulently using my personal identifying information. 

The FTC report was completed truthfully and under penalty of perjury. 

This is not a casual dispute. 
This is not a billing disagreement. 
This is a documented identity theft claim. 

Closure of Prior Case Was Procedural, Not Substantive The previous case ( # XXXX ) was closed, but the inaccurate reporting remains. Closing a complaint without correcting the underlying inaccuracy does not resolve the issue. 

The accounts still appear as derogatory collections and continue to : Lower my credit score Misrepresent my creditworthiness Influence lending decisions Cause ongoing financial harm Because the reporting persists, this matter remains unresolved. 

Demand for Method of Verification If the accounts are being maintained as valid, I am demanding full disclosure of the method of verification used, including : The specific documents reviewed Whether verification was conducted electronically through e-OSCAR The name and title of the individual who verified the account The date and system used for verification The chain of custody of the alleged account data Simply stating that the furnisher verified the account is insufficient. 

Verification of data is not verification of ownership. 

Demand for Contractual Proof If Nationwide or Aargon contend that the accounts are legitimate, they must produce competent proof of authorization, including : A signed contract bearing my wet signature ; or A verifiable electronic signature showing affirmative consent ; and Authentication logs tied to account opening ; and Application records initiated by me To date, no signed agreement or authenticated proof has been produced. 

Address association does not equal consent. 
Residency does not equal contract. 
Data presence does not equal authorization. 

Without contractual documentation, continued reporting is improper. 

Improper Reliance on Furnisher Statements Merely contacting the furnisher and accepting their statement that the account is valid does not constitute a reasonable reinvestigation in an identity theft case. 

When fraud is alleged and supported by an FTC affidavit, a reporting agency must do more than forward the dispute and accept the response at face value. 

The issue is ownership. 

If the furnisher can not prove I entered into a contractual relationship, the account must be deleted. 

Continued Reporting Causes Harm The continued presence of these fraudulent accounts has caused substantial harm, including : Credit score damage Increased interest rates Reduced credit access Emotional distress Time and expense disputing inaccurate reporting I should not suffer financial consequences for fraud committed by someone else. 

Failure to Block Fraudulent Information When a consumer submits an identity theft report, fraudulent accounts must be blocked from reporting unless the furnisher establishes that the information is correct and belongs to the consumer. 

No such proof has been provided. 

Therefore, continued reporting is improper. 

Formal Requests I am formally requesting : Immediate deletion and blocking of the XXXX XXXX XXXX tradeline. 

Immediate deletion and blocking of the XXXX XXXX tradeline. 

Written confirmation of deletion. 

Written confirmation that these accounts will not be reinserted. 

Full disclosure of the method of verification used. 

Copies of any signed agreements relied upon to maintain reporting. 

If signed contracts or authenticated proof can not be produced, deletion is required. 

This Is a Stall and Escalation Notice This letter serves as notice that the matter remains open and under active dispute. 

Any continued reporting without production of signed contractual proof will be considered reporting without proper validation. 

I am creating this new case because the prior closure did not correct the inaccuracy. 

The accounts remain fraudulent. 

The reporting remains harmful. 

The matter remains unresolved. 

Final Position I did not open these accounts. 
I did not authorize these accounts. 
Experian has already recognized them as fraud. 
I filed an FTC Identity Theft Report. 

If the furnishers can not produce competent proof of authorization, the accounts must be permanently deleted. 

Until that occurs, this dispute remains active.
label: credit_reporting
source_product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports

text:
Subject : 3 Fixes to Make Banking More Fair and User-Friendly Hi, I want to share a few ideas that I think could improve everyday banking for regular people. 

First, I think banks should provide an easy way for customers to make money directly within their app or website. Whether thats through better interest options, rewards for responsible usage, or tools that connect users to legitimate income opportunities, theres room to shift the focus from fees to financial growth. For example, banks could collaborate with businesses like hotels or food delivery platforms to offer vetted work-from-home dispatch roles or paid training programs. They could also help facilitate short-term, location-based gig opportunitieslike making it feasible for someone to travel to another city for a week, deliver food or do similar work, and reliably come out ahead financiallyby bundling earnings tools, temporary housing partnerships, and transparent cost/earnings estimates into one place.

Second, it shouldnt be possible for account balances to go negative. Overdraft fees disproportionately affect people who cant afford them, and the idea that a bank allows negative balances to exist then charges for it feels outdated and harmful. A system that simply declines transactions when funds arent available would be much safer and more transparent. 

Third, new checking account bonuses should be accessible to people without requiring large initial deposits or high balance thresholds. Right now, many of these offers are geared toward people who already have significant funds, which makes them feel exclusionary. Incentives should be structured in a way that also benefits people who are trying to get started or rebuild financially.

Im sure there are operational challenges behind all of this, but from a consumer perspective, these changes would make banking feel more fair, less punitive, and more aligned with helping people succeed. 

Thanks for your consideration! 

Respectfully,
label: fees_interest
source_issue: Problem caused by your funds being low

text:
This complaint is filed in response to XXXX XX/XX/year> response to my prior CFPB complaint dated XX/XX/year>, in which I requested written confirmation of three specific points before executing the Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release attached to XXXX XX/XX/year> letter to the California Department of Financial Protection and Innovation, File No. XXXX. Those three points were : One. That upon my execution and delivery of the Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release, the {$6900.00} already paid constitutes full and final satisfaction of the underlying judgment in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Case Number XXXX, consistent with Agreement item 2, and that no further balance of any kind is owed.

Two. That XXXX, as successor in interest to XXXX, will execute and file a Satisfaction of Judgment with the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX for the entire action and all causes of action within 20 days of receiving my signed agreement, consistent with Agreement item 3 and California Code of Civil Procedure Section 724.060, and that XXXX will provide me a file-stamped copy of the filed Satisfaction of Judgment.

Three. That upon XXXX performance of the above, all collection activity on this account will permanently cease, no further sum of any kind will be sought, no garnishment will be pursued, and no adverse credit reporting will occur or continue, consistent with the Mutual Release and Covenant Not to Sue provisions of the Settlement Agreement. 
In its XX/XX/year> response, XXXX declined to address any of these three points and stated only that the agreement already contemplates the entire proposal, Consumer only needs to sign and return it. That response is non-responsive.

XXXX  position on this account has shifted materially three times in three months. On XX/XX/year>, XXXX told the CFPB it would consider resuming the settlement. On XX/XX/year>, XXXX told the California DFPI through its Authorized Representative XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, that it will honor the offer. On XX/XX/year>, XXXX answer to three direct questions was to sign without further clarification. That pattern is precisely why written confirmation is required before execution.

The consequences of signing this agreement are not minor. It contains a California Civil Code Section 1542 waiver and a Mutual Release that permanently extinguish my right to bring any future claims related to this matter. Under California Code of Civil Procedure Sections 724.010, 724.050, and 724.060, a Satisfaction of Judgment carries specific statutory obligations and timelines. Executing a document of this nature based on XXXX  one-sentence response, after a record of shifting positions across two regulatory agencies, is not something I am prepared to do without clear, written answers on the record.

If XXXX position is that the agreement already provides full satisfaction, a Satisfaction of Judgment filing within 20 days, and permanent cessation of all collection activity and credit reporting, then XXXX should have no difficulty confirming those three points in writing in response to this complaint. I am prepared to execute the Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release immediately upon receipt of that written confirmation. XXXX  refusal to provide it is the only obstacle to resolution.

text:
I am filing this complaint regarding U.S. Bank 's systematic assessment of monthly service fees on a business checking account that was functionally unusable due to the bank 's own transfer restrictions. I opened a XXXX XXXX Checking Account on or about XX/XX/XXXX under my company : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, intending to take advantage of a promotional offer requiring a {$25000.00} deposit. I had these funds readily available in my linked XXXX  XXXX XXXX account. 

From the outset, U.S. Bank made funding the account impossible in any normal or routine manner : I was required to complete a manual PDF form just to deposit an initial {$300.00}. 

After linking my XXXX  XXXX XXXX account ( which contained well over {$25000.00} ), U.S. Bank blocked external ACH transfers.

I was informed by U.S. Bank employees that I could only fund the account through repeated small incremental transfersan impractical and burdensome process.

I reasonably assumed that after depositing the {$300.00}, the linked XXXX  XXXX XXXX account would allow the full {$25000.00} transfer. When this failed, I became frustrated and concluded the account was unusable for its intended purpose. I stepped away, reasonably believing my {$300.00} would remain safe until I could resolve the matter. 

U.S. Bank then assessed XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX monthly " dormancy '' fees * * against this account for over one year, systematically draining the entire {$300.00} balance and pushing the account to a negative balance. I received no noticeno mail, no email, no alertthat fees were accruing or that my balance was being depleted. 

When I discovered the negative balance, I immediately contacted U.S. Bank. They refunded only {$90.00} as a " courtesy '' ( actually only {$30.00} was refunded, not the {$90.00} they verbally claimed ), but refused to refund the remaining $ XXXX in fees. The bank 's own letter dated acknowledges the fees were charged and that they could reverse them, yet they have arbitrarily chosen not to fully reverse the wrongful charges. 

The bank 's advertising encouraged a {$25000.00} deposit with a cash bonus, while their systems made that deposit impossible. The bank then penalized me for inactivity on an account they made unusable. This is a bait-and-switch scheme that systematically traps consumers. 

Legal Violations A. Consumer Financial Protection Act ( CFPA ), 12 U.S.C. 5536 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( B ) U.S. Bank 's conduct constitutes an unfair, deceptive, or abusive act or practice ( UDAAP ) : Unfair Conduct : The bank knowingly imposed transfer restrictions that made the account unusable The bank assessed fees for inactivity while the account was unusable The bank provided no meaningful notice of accruing fees The bank 's $ XXXXmonth fee on a {$300.00} balance ( 10 % per month ) is disproportionate and causes substantial injury Deceptive Conduct : The bank advertised a bonus requiring a {$25000.00} deposit while making the required funding impossible The bank represented that linking an external account would enable transfers, then blocked them The bank 's letter acknowledges fees were " assessed correctly '' while also partially refunding themcontradictory representations The CFPB has previously fined U.S. Bank {$37.00} XXXX for similar misconductopening unauthorized accounts and engaging in deceptive sales practices. In that enforcement action, the CFPB found that U.S. Bank 's conduct caused " substantial injury to consumers in the form of fees, negative effects on credit reports, and the loss of control over their personal information ''.

B. New Hampshire Consumer Protection Act ( RSA 358-A:2 ) Under New Hampshire 's Consumer Protection Act, it is unlawful to use " any unfair or deceptive act or practice in the conduct of any trade or commerce ''. Specifically, the statute prohibits : Claiming that goods or services have certain characteristics or benefits when they do not ( the bank advertised a bonus offer while making the {$25000.00} deposit impossible ) Falsely representing sponsorship, approval, status, or affiliation Advertising goods or services with the intent not to sell them as advertised The bank 's bonus offer was made with the intent to attract deposits, yet their systems were designed to block those deposits. This is precisely the type of deceptive conduct the NH Consumer Protection Act was designed to address.

C. Truth in Savings Act ( 12 U.S.C. 4301 et seq. ) Banks are required to clearly and conspicuously disclose terms, fees, and charges. U.S. Bank 's fee structure $ XXXXmonth on a {$300.00} balancewas buried in fine print and not meaningfully disclosed. The bank 's $ XXXXmonth fee on a {$300.00} balance is 10 % per month120 % annuallyfar exceeding any reasonable service fee structure. 

The CFPB has noted that " dormancy fees, latency fees, or any other administrative fees or service charges that have the effect of reducing the total amount for which the holder may redeem '' are prohibited, and while this provision specifically addresses gift certificates, the same unfairness principles apply to bank dormancy fees. 

D. Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing Every contract includes an implied duty of good faith. U.S. Bank 's conductmaking the account unusable, charging fees for inactivity, and providing no noticeviolates this fundamental duty. The bank can not create the conditions that led to dormancy and then penalize the consumer for dormancy.

U.S. Bank 's History of Misconduct : XX/XX/XXXX : The CFPB fined U.S. Bank {$37.00} XXXX for opening fictitious accounts without customer consent from XXXX to XXXX. The CFPB found the bank illegally accessed credit reports and opened checking, savings, credit card, and line of credit accounts without permission. 

Pattern of Fees : A CFPB report found that U.S. Bank 's average annual fees per account were among the highest of examined institutions, with overdraft fees of {$36.00} at U.S. Bank compared to {$0.00} at other institutions.

Ongoing Consumer Complaints : The CFPB 's Consumer Complaint Database contains over 4,700 complaints against U.S. Bank. The bank has faced numerous complaints regarding account closures, fee disputes, and service issues.

I have received overdrafts which have been ordered so that the penalties are maximized. For example if there were 3 transactions, the bank would process the largest first, so the account was overdrawn, and then process the remaining 2 transactions, charging overdrafts on all three, whereas if they processed the two small charges first, they would have cleared, and then the large one would have overdrawn and resulted in 1 charge.

text:
I am filing a complaint regarding Wells Fargo 's handling of material changes to the monthly service fee requirements for my Everyday Checking account ending in XXXX. 

I have maintained this account since XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, I discovered that Wells Fargo had charged monthly service fees totaling {$70.00} as follows : * XX/XX/XXXX : {$10.00} * XX/XX/XXXX : {$15.00} * XX/XX/XXXX : {$15.00} * XX/XX/XXXX : {$15.00} * XX/XX/XXXX : {$15.00} I immediately contacted Wells Fargo on XX/XX/XXXX, to dispute these charges and request a refund. Wells Fargo denied my request through multiple complaint reviews, culminating in a final response dated XX/XX/XXXX, from its XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Wells Fargo stated that notice of the new fee requirements had been provided in account statements issued in XXXX, XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. 

My complaint is not that Wells Fargo provided no notice whatsoever. Rather, my concern is that the notice was not reasonably conspicuous given the significance of the change. The disclosure was placed in very small print near the bottom of page 2 or page 3 of a five-page account statement. Wells Fargo did not send a separate email notification, postal mail notice, secure online banking message, or any other prominent communication regarding the change. 

The change materially increased the requirements needed to avoid monthly service fees and ultimately resulted in recurring fees being assessed against my account. One would expect such a significant change to be communicated clearly and prominently. Instead, the disclosure was embedded within routine statement content where it could easily be overlooked. 

Wells Fargo 's position is that account statements alone constitute official notice of account changes. While this may satisfy its internal policies or account agreement, I believe the manner in which the disclosure was presented failed to provide meaningful notice to consumers and was not reasonably designed to draw attention to a material fee increase that could directly impact customers ' finances. 

Additionally, Wells Fargo further denied my refund request on the grounds that a separate XXXX XXXX  monthly service fee had been reimbursed in XX/XX/XXXX and that additional refunds could not be issued within a XXXX period. This prior reimbursement was unrelated to the fees at issue here and does not address the underlying concern that the fee changes were disclosed in a manner that was not reasonably conspicuous. I believe Wells Fargo improperly treated my complaint as a request for a routine courtesy refund rather than addressing whether customers received meaningful notice of a material fee change. 

I request Wells Fargo to reimburse the {$70.00} in monthly service fees charged to my account, and prevent the assessment of future monthly service fees resulting from this inadequately disclosed change unless and until clear and conspicuous notice is provided.
label: fees_interest
source_issue: Problem caused by your funds being low

I have repeatedly tried to explain my concerns to Wells Fargo representatives, but I feel that no one is listening or taking the time to properly review the issue. From my perspective, the way weekend transactions are being processed is unfair and is causing me to be charged in a way that feels like I am paying for transactions twice.

Transactions made over the weekend do not always post immediately, and when they are later processed, they can create overdraft situations that do not accurately reflect the available balance I saw at the time of purchase. As a result, I have been charged fees that I do not believe are justified. 

The transaction that resulted in the overdraft fee posted only recently, yet I was still charged a fee. When I contacted customer service to request a review and a refund, I was simply told that nothing could be done. I do not believe this is acceptable customer service, especially when I am raising legitimate concerns about how my account activity is being processed.

I am requesting a full review of my account, an explanation of how these weekend transactions are being processed, and a refund of the overdraft fee. I also ask that Wells Fargo seriously consider the concerns I am raising instead of dismissing them without a proper investigation.

I have been trying to resolve this issue in good faith, and I hope Wells Fargo will do the same.

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
label: fees_interest
