text:
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX. 
XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX, PA XXXX Nature of Complaint I am submitting this complaint regarding repeated debt collection activity, refusal to provide proper debt validation, continued credit reporting of disputed information, and the filing of litigation despite multiple identity theft disputes and formal requests for validation.

I repeatedly disputed the alleged account directly with the collector, through credit reporting agencies, and through correspondence directed to the collection attorneys. I also provided notice that the account was disputed due to identity theft concerns and lack of knowledge regarding the alleged debt.

I possess both a Federal Identity Theft Affidavit and a notarized affidavit disputing the alleged account.

Despite these disputes and requests for validation, the collector and its attorneys failed to provide competent evidence establishing : Original signed agreement or contract Complete chain of assignment or ownership Bill of sale identifying my specific account Original account-level records Admissible proof of balance calculations Competent evidence proving legal ownership of the alleged debt Instead of providing proper validation, I was served with a lawsuit.

This conduct appears to violate multiple provisions of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) and Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), including continued collection activity and litigation on disputed information without providing meaningful validation.

FDCPA Violations Alleged I believe XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and/or Pantenaude & Felix , A.P.C. violated the FDCPA by : Attempting to collect disputed debt without proper validation Continuing collection activity after receiving disputes Using litigation as a collection tactic despite unresolved disputes Misrepresenting the legal status or validity of the alleged debt Attempting to collect without sufficient documentation proving ownership and accuracy Relying on summaries or generic statements instead of admissible underlying records FCRA Violations Alleged I believe they also violated the FCRA by : Continuing to report disputed information without conducting a reasonable investigation Failing to properly mark the account as disputed Continuing collection and reporting activity after receiving identity theft notices and affidavits Furnishing information that could not be properly verified Requested Relief I request that the CFPB investigate XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and Pantenaude & Felix, A.P.C. regarding : Failure to provide proper debt validation Continued collection activity after disputes Litigation filed despite unresolved identity theft disputes Potential inaccurate credit reporting Potential violations of the FDCPA and FCRA I further request : Immediate cessation of collection activity Dismissal of litigation related to the disputed account Deletion of all related tradelines from all consumer reporting agencies Written confirmation that the account will no longer be collected or reported Preservation of all account-level and litigation-related records Please also note that I reserve all rights to pursue private civil remedies and additional complaints regarding these actions.

FORMAL NOTICE OF DISPUTE, DEMAND FOR DELETION, NOTICE OF FCRA VIOLATIONS, AND NOTICE OF INTENT TO FILE SUIT Account : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Date Opened : XX/XX/year> To Whom It May Concern : This correspondence serves as a formal legal notice regarding the inaccurate, misleading, incomplete, and unverifiable reporting of the above-referenced account currently appearing on my consumer credit reports. 

I previously disputed this account with your agency. Despite receiving notice of dispute, you continued reporting the tradeline without correcting the material inaccuracies and inconsistencies associated with the account. Your prior investigation results are disputed and rejected. 

The tradeline currently reports an alleged charge-off and approximately {$29000.00} written off, while simultaneously reporting incomplete and inconsistent account information, including but not limited to missing or blank balance fields, incomplete payment information, and materially misleading account reporting. 

Your continued reporting of this account raises serious concerns regarding violations of the : * Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), 15 U.S.C. 1681 et seq.

* 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ) Failure to maintain reasonable procedures to assure maximum possible accuracy.

* 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) Failure to conduct a lawful and reasonable reinvestigation after receiving notice of dispute.

* 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 6 ) - ( 7 ) Failure to provide adequate disclosure regarding the method of verification and investigation procedures used.

* Reporting materially misleading and incomplete information to third parties. 

The reporting of a charged-off account while critical account fields remain incomplete, inconsistent, blank, or unverifiable constitutes deceptive and misleading reporting and calls into question whether any reasonable reinvestigation occurred at all. 

Accordingly, I hereby demand the following : XXXX. Immediate deletion of this tradeline from my consumer reports ; XXXX. A complete reinvestigation of this account ; XXXX. Written disclosure of the method of verification used ; XXXX. Identification of every party, database, and document relied upon during your investigation ; XXXX. Copies of all documents allegedly verifying this tradeline, including : * Original signed contract ; * Complete payment history ; * Charge-off accounting records; * Date of first delinquency ; * Proof of ownership and authority to report ; * Metro 2 compliance documentation. 

Please also consider this letter a formal preservation-of-evidence notice. You are instructed to preserve all documents, communications, XXXX records, investigation notes, electronic records, and internal communications related to this account and all prior disputes. 

If this account is not permanently deleted within XXXX ( XXXX ) days of receipt of this notice, I am prepared to pursue all available legal remedies available to me under federal law, including filing complaints with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) and initiating litigation for negligent and/or willful noncompliance with the FCRA. 

Please be advised that litigation exposure for willful FCRA violations, reputational harm, emotional distress, denial of credit opportunities, and related damages may subject your agency and all liable parties to claims up to {$25000.00} or more, in addition to court costs, attorney fees, punitive damages, and all other relief permitted by law. 
Nothing in this correspondence shall be construed as an admission of liability or acknowledgment of the alleged debt.

I received a call Tuesday afternoon around XXXX from someone claiming to be from Truist Fraud department. They stated that I had a wire transfer setup to be sent internationally in the amount of XXXX to the country XXXX to a company XXXX XXXX. So, I told them that was fraud and needed to be stopped. He said I would need to login to the Truist one view bank Account to stop the transfer. I logged in to the account, and I did not give him any information. He said he wanted to confirm the amount that was in my bank and I did not say anything about how much was, but he said the exact amount like he already knew. He wanted me to confirm the last few transactions showing up in the balance, so I did. He told me he was going to send me a message in the inbox on my banking online notifications, and I never received anything so he said I would have to log in to Truist One View we portal. I told him we set up an account in XXXX, but we don't log in to that and I did not have the password to do that. The Truist one view is a separate web site for File Transfers to vendors that companies can use to transfer money. We don't pay our vendors that way because we use XXXX XXXX XXXX  to do so. He kept telling me to try to log in so I could stop the transfer. And I never could log in to Truist XXXX XXXX. So he said let me send a code from Truist and you read it off to me so I can get the transfer of alleged {$18000.00} stopped. And I did give him the code and that must be when the transfer was set up. I realized this may be fraud and immediately unplugged my computer and hung up then called the Truist phone number about 5 minutes of being on the phone with the hacker and asked if there was a transfer showing for XXXX and she said no. I was transferred to another person in Truist fraud department and told him everything that was going on. The first attempt got blocked by Truist and showed to be fraud and the second attempt went through. 
At approximately XXXX XXXX, we received an email from Truist ( header Truist Alerts XXXX Tue, XXXX, XXXX at XXXX To : XXXX ) stating a wire transfer has been scheduled Payment ID XXXX account ending in XXXX scheduled date XX/XX/XXXX Recipient XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  payment amount {$49000.00}. 
At approximately XXXX XXXX, we received an email from Truist ( header Truist Alerts XXXX Tue, XXXX, XXXX at XXXX To : XXXX ) stating a wire transfer has been scheduled Payment ID XXXX Account ending in XXXX Scheduled date XX/XX/XXXX Recipient XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX payment amount # XXXX. 
During this time, I am on the phone with Truist Fraud Department for approximately an hour. I dont recall the mans name but he assured me the transfer was stopped and a restriction would be on my bank account for outgoing transactions. text:
Discover Bank is furnishing inaccurate and misleading credit information by reporting current late payments and delinquency on a closed account that incurred unauthorized charges while I was incarcerated. Discover was formally notified of my incarceration and the fraud, yet continues to report the account as late rather than suppressing, correcting, or removing the derogatory information.yhe account was also sold to a collection agency which is also reporting to my credit as well This conduct violates the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and applicable furnisher duties, and reflects a failure to conduct a reasonable investigation after notice of fraud and identity-related misuse. 



Factual Background I was incarcerated during the period in which the disputed charges occurred. 
While incarcerated, I did not have access to the account, did not authorize any transactions, and could not have incurred the charges. 
The account was closed, yet Discover is reporting new late payments as current delinquencies, which is factually impossible absent authorized activity. 
I notified Discover Bank of my incarceration and that the charges were fraudulent. 
Despite this notice, Discover continued to furnish late payments to the credit reporting agencies rather than marking the account as disputed, suppressing reporting, or correcting the tradeline. 



Legal Violations 1. Fair Credit Reporting Act 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) & ( b ) Discover, as a furnisher, is prohibited from reporting information it knows or has reasonable cause to believe is inaccurate. Once notified of a dispute, Discover is required to : Conduct a reasonable investigation Review all relevant information Correct or delete inaccurate information Reporting current late payments arising from fraudulent charges incurred while the consumer was incarcerated is inherently inaccurate and misleading. 

2. Failure to Conduct a Reasonable Investigation ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ) ) After being notified of incarceration and fraud, Discover failed to reasonably investigate or correct the reporting. Continuing to report delinquency without addressing the impossibility of consumer authorization during incarceration is per se unreasonable. 

3. Materially Misleading Reporting ( Regulation V, 12 C.F.R. 1022 ) Even if Discover claims the balance exists, reporting current lates without accounting for fraud and incarceration is materially misleading, which is prohibited under Regulation V.

4. Identity Theft/ Fraud Protections ( 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2 ) Charges incurred while the consumer is incarcerated constitute identity-related fraud or unauthorized use. Discover failed to appropriately block or correct reporting after notice. 



Harm Caused Ongoing credit score suppression due to inaccurately reported current delinquencies Increased risk-based pricing, denial of credit, and financial harm Continued reputational harm due to false credit data Requested Resolution I respectfully request that the CFPB require Discover Bank to : 1. Immediately delete all late payments and derogatory marks associated with the disputed period 2. Remove or correct the tradeline to reflect fraud/unauthorized activity 3. Cease reporting any delinquency related to charges incurred during my incarceration 4. Provide written confirmation of corrections to all credit reporting agencies 5. Ensure compliance with FCRA furnisher obligations going forward
label: fraud_scam
source_issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed
source_sub_issue: Debt was result of identity theft

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a disputed debt currently being reported on my credit report in the amount of {$14000.00}, which has been placed with the collection agency SYNERGETIC COMMUNICATIONS. The alleged debt originates from a XXXX truck financed through XXXX XXXX XXXX, located in XXXX, MS . The vehicle involved was a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 
I dispute the accuracy and validity of this alleged balance because a substantial portion of the amount appears to have been generated from a XXXX invoice submitted by a third party without my knowledge or authorization. 
The truck was taken to a legitimate repair facility for diagnostic work. I personally received the original invoices from that repair shop, which consisted of : An initial diagnostic invoice totaling {$270.00} A final diagnostic invoice totaling {$930.00} Total invoice : {$1200.00} These invoices accurately reflect the diagnostic and repair work that was actually performed on the vehicle. 
However, my former XXXX XXXX XXXX later submitted an invoice to XXXX XXXX XXXX through a business he owns called XXXX XXXX XXXX totaling approximately {$6300.00}. Based on the documentation I obtained, it appears that portions of the legitimate XXXX XXXX invoice were copied and incorporated into an invoice issued by XXXX XXXX XXXX, despite the fact that this business did not perform the diagnostic or repair work reflected in the legitimate invoices. 
XXXX XXXX XXXX apparently paid the {$6300.00} invoice submitted by XXXX XXXX XXXX in order to recover the truck from the terminal where it was located. I was not aware of this transaction at the time it occurred. 
After discovering this situation, I contacted XXXX XXXX XXXX and informed them that the invoice they paid was fraudulent and that the legitimate repair invoices total only {$270.00} and {$930.00}. I advised them that I possess the actual invoices documenting the legitimate work performed. 
Despite being informed that the {$6300.00} invoice appears to be fraudulent and does not reflect work performed by XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX advised me that this was not their problem and that I would still be responsible for paying the full balance they claim is owed. 
XXXX XXXX XXXX subsequently placed the alleged balance into collections, and the amount of {$14000.00} is now being reported on my credit report by Synergetic Communication Inc. 
This situation raises serious concerns regarding compliance with federal consumer protection laws, including : XXXX. Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) and 1681s-2 ( b ) ) This law requires entities that furnish information to credit reporting agencies to provide information that is accurate and not materially misleading. A balance that includes charges derived from a fraudulent invoice may constitute inaccurate or misleading information being furnished to credit reporting agencies. 
XXXX. Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1692e and 1692f ) Debt collectors are prohibited from using false, deceptive, or misleading representations in connection with the collection of a debt and from attempting to collect amounts that are not expressly authorized by the agreement creating the debt or permitted by law. 
XXXX. Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ( UDAAP ) under the Consumer Financial Protection Act ( 12 U.S.C. 5531 ) Attempting to collect or report a balance that includes charges obtained through fraudulent misrepresentation by a third party may constitute an unfair or deceptive practice, particularly when the creditor has been notified of the dispute and supporting documentation. 
I notified XXXX XXXX Trucks of the fraudulent invoice and provided notice that I possessed documentation showing the legitimate repair invoices. Despite being informed of this dispute, the company refused to investigate or correct the charges and proceeded to place the alleged debt into collections.

Non-Disclosure of DPA Loan Information : The loan was represented as assistance under a state housing program, yet there is no proof of program-required borrower disclosures, eligibility certifications, or compliance with Florida Housing Finance Corporation guidelines . The company has also failed to demonstrate whether the DPA loan is subject to forgiveness, recapture, or resale provisions, raising concerns of program misuse and deceptive practices. 

Alleged Violations of Federal and State Laws FDCPA Violations : The ongoing collection efforts and threats of legal action by McCalla Raymer Leibert Pierce, XXXX, despite the active dispute of the debt, potentially violate the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), which prohibits the collection of debts that are disputed or unsubstantiated. 

RESPA Violations : The companys failure to provide critical disclosures, such as the closing documents and full accounting of both loans, likely violates the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act ( RESPA ), which mandates that lenders provide accurate and timely information regarding loan servicing and associated fees. 

CFPB Servicing Rules Violations : The company 's handling of the loan servicing and lack of required documentation appears to violate Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) servicing rules, which require accurate recordkeeping, transparency, and responsiveness to consumer inquiries. 

Misuse of Government Assistance : The improper treatment of the DPA loan as a collectible debt, without providing required documentation, raises concerns about the improper conversion of government assistance into private enforceable debt. This could violate federal and state guidelines governing the disbursement and management of housing assistance programs. 

Harm Caused Due to these actions, I have faced the following harm : Collection Activity on a Disputed Debt : The company has initiated collection activities on a loan that is disputed, potentially unenforceable, and improperly documented. 

Inaccurate Debt Amount : The amount of debt claimed by the company is inaccurate and inflated, which further exacerbates my financial distress.

I am disputing a fraudulent collection account reported by Sunrise Credit Services ( XXXX ) related to XXXX XXXX for {$400.00}. I have never conducted business with this company, never authorized any account, and never provided permission for my personal information to be accessed, used, or furnished. 

This account is not mine. 

Despite this, Sunrise Credit Services has reported and verified this account without providing any evidence of a valid contract, signature, or permissible purpose. The reporting also contains material inconsistencies, including conflicting balances ( {$190.00} vs. {$400.00} ), which further demonstrates a lack of reasonable investigation and accuracy. 

Their actions constitute clear violations of : * FCRA ( 15 U.S.C. 1681 ) failure to ensure maximum possible accuracy and reporting unverifiable information * FDCPA ( 15 U.S.C. 1692 ) attempting to collect a debt not owed and failure to properly validate * GLBA ( 15 U.S.C. 6801-6809 ) unauthorized access, use, and disclosure of my nonpublic personal information * Privacy Act of 1974 unlawful maintenance and dissemination of personal data without proper authority There is no permissible purpose for this inquiry or reporting, and no valid authorization exists. 

I demand : * Immediate deletion of this account from all credit reporting agencies * Written proof of authorization bearing my signature ( which does not exist ) * Full disclosure of how my personal information was obtained and shared * Immediate cease and desist of all reporting and collection activity Failure to correct this matter promptly will result in escalation for willful noncompliance and damages.

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Cash App concerning unauthorized transactions resulting from fraud. 

On XX/XX/year>, I was targeted by a scammer who falsely represented themselves as an employee of my bank, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. The individual claimed that my identity had been stolen and that my accounts were compromised. 

Based on these false claims, I was instructed to transfer my funds to what I believed was my own Cash App account to secure them. I followed these instructions under the belief that I was protecting my money. However, once the funds were transferred, the scammer immediately accessed and stole the money from my Cash App account without my authorization. I did not intentionally provide the scammer with any details such as pin number or login information, my funds were accessed and stolen by them without my consent. The account that the scammer used to steal the money is labelled as the XXXX XXXX  with a card number ending in XXXX, and the transaction number is XXXX. 

The total amount stolen was {$3700.00}. 

I immediately took every possible action to report the theft and have my funds returned to me. I filed a police report with local law enforcement. I reported the scam to my bank, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. I opened a fraud claim with Cash App. 

I provided Cash App with the police report documenting the theft, filed on the same day as the incident. 

In addition, I have made numerous attempts to resolve this issue through Cash App customer service, including multiple phone calls and use of their live chat feature. Despite these efforts, I have only received generic responses and have not been provided with any meaningful details regarding the investigation or reasoning behind the denial of my claims. 

Cash App has stated that the transactions appear to be authorized ; however, I explicitly dispute this characterization. The transfer was made under false pretenses as part of a scam, and I did not knowingly or willingly authorize the loss of my funds. 

This situation constitutes fraud and theft, and I believe Cash App has failed to adequately investigate the matter or provide appropriate consumer protections in response to a documented scam.

text:
I am filing a complaint against several debt collection agencies for what appears to be ongoing and deeply concerning violations of federal consumer protection laws. After formally disputing these alleged debts and requesting proper validation, I was left frustrated and discouraged when the companies failed to provide any meaningful proof that I am legally responsible for these accounts. They did not produce basic, essential documents such as original agreements, records of ownership, complete account histories, or any clear evidence of their authority to collect. 

Despite my repeated efforts to resolve this matter, collection attempts and credit reporting activity appear to have continued without proper verification. This has caused significant stress and uncertainty, as I am being held accountable for debts that have not been substantiated. It is both alarming and unfair to face ongoing financial and reputational harm without clear and lawful justification. 

These actions raise serious concerns about potential violations of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act, including the failure to properly validate debts and the use of misleading or unfair collection practices. Additionally, there appear to be violations of the Fair Credit Reporting Act, particularly regarding the accuracy of reported information and the obligation to conduct a reasonable investigation. 

Overall, the pattern of behavior suggests that these companies may be attempting to collect on unverified debts while reporting potentially inaccurate information. This situation has not only undermined my confidence in the fairness of the system but has also placed an unnecessary emotional and financial burden on me, pointing to what may be broader noncompliance with federal regulations.

text:
Following multiple fraud incidents, our office initiated a comprehensive internal review of vendor communications and payment procedures, with particular focus on requests to modify payment methods ( e.g., transitions from check or e-check to ACH ).

During this review, we identified that on XX/XX/year>, an email was received from a vendor, XXXX XXXX XXXX, requesting a change in their method of payment from e-checks to ACH. At the time, the request appeared legitimate and was processed in accordance with standard internal procedures. Subsequently, a payment in the amount of {$78000.00} was issued in XX/XX/year> based on the updated ACH information. This transaction was later determined to be fraudulent on XX/XX/year>. 

In a separate instance, we received a similar request from another vendor, XXXX XXXX XXXX, to switch their payment method to ACH. Based on the information provided, two payments were processed under this request : {$93000.00} on XX/XX/year> {$44000.00} on XX/XX/year> These transactions were later identified as fraudulent on XX/XX/year>. 

Further investigation into these incidents revealed multiple irregularities. In one case, a discrepancy was identified in the senders email domain. Prior communications from the vendor had originated from a XXXX XXXX, whereas the payment change request was sent from a XXXXXXXX XXXX. This inconsistency raised concerns regarding the authenticity of the request.

Additionally, we discovered that legitimate emails from at least one vendor were being diverted to the spam/junk folder, which prevented timely communication and verification. Concurrently, we began receiving emails and phone calls from subcontractors and vendors reporting that they had received messages appearing to originate from our company, which included a link that, when accessed, did not direct to any valid content.

Based on these findings, it appears that unauthorized third parties were impersonating both our company and our vendors, potentially through email spoofing or compromise. In at least one instance, the fraudulent party communicated with the vendor indicating that our company was delaying payment due to pending funds from the XXXX XXXX. This misinformation created confusion and disrupted normal communication channels, contributing to the success of the fraudulent scheme.

It is also important to note that in both cases, the vendors/scammers requested that ACH payments be directed to accounts held at Citi Bank , and that the funds were sent to two separate Citi Bank accounts , further indicating a coordinated fraudulent operation utilizing multiple receiving accounts.
label: fraud_scam
source_product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service

text:
COMPANY : Wise US Inc . 
PRODUCT : Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service ISSUE : Problem with a money transfer DESCRIPTION OF COMPLAINT : On XX/XX/year>, I used my Wise debit card to withdraw XXXX ( XXXX  ) from an ATM located at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX ( Transaction ID : XXXX ). The money was debited from my Wise account, but the ATM did not dispense any cash. I received nothing. 
I immediately reported this to Wise. Wise opened a dispute and initially provided me with provisional credit. However, Wise subsequently accepted an ATM audit log from the machine operator as sufficient evidence that the cash was dispensed, closed my dispute, and clawed back my provisional credit. 
The ATM audit log Wise relied on contains the notation 'XXXX  ( XXXX ) " meaning two notes were rejected during the dispense cycle which is direct evidence of a mechanical malfunction. Additionally, the 'BILLS TAKEN ' sensor event in the log does not constitute proof that I physically received cash ; ATM sensors can trigger even when bills are retracted by the machine. 
Most critically, Wise closed my case WITHOUT requesting or obtaining the ATM 's security camera footage, which would be the definitive evidence of whether cash was dispensed to me. This represents a failure to conduct a proper investigation. 
I have formally escalated within Wise and requested they obtain security footage and reconsider. I am filing this CFPB complaint because Wise has so far refused to reverse their decision and restore my $ XXXX USD loss. 
I am requesting the CFPB require Wise to : ( 1 ) fully investigate by obtaining ATM security footage, ( 2 ) reverse the provisional credit clawback, and ( 3 ) reimburse my XXXX loss.

source_product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service

text:

text:
Back on XX/XX/XXXX the company was named XXXX. Now today its AR services. I am filing this formal complaint against a debt collection agency, identified as AR services, for engaging in illegal and deceptive debt collection practices in violation of federal and California state laws. 

Details of the XXXX XXXX XX/XX/XXXX, I received a phone call from AR services regarding an alleged payday loan debt linked to a XXXX bank account. The agent, XXXX, informed me that the state was attempting to collect a {$520.00} debt and that I must make payment immediately or face a lawsuit, wage garnishment, and additional legal action. She also said she had put this on my credit report for {$4000.00} since I didnt want to pay the {$520.00}. 

I informed the agent that I never took out a payday loan in XXXX and that I never had a XXXX account in XXXX. I also pointed out that California 's statute of limitations on debt collection lawsuits is four ( 4 ) years ( California Code of Civil Procedure 337 ), meaning any legal action would be unenforceable. Instead of addressing my concerns, the agent became hostile, yelled over me, and continued to threaten legal action and wage garnishment if I did not pay that same day. This behavior is unethical, deceptive, and illegal under both the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) ( 15 U.S.C. 1692 et seq. ) and the Rosenthal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( Cal. Civ. Code 1788.10 et seq. ). Violations Committed by AR.

Threatening legal action on a time-barred debt It is unlawful to misrepresent a debts legal status or threaten legal action when the statute of limitations has expired ( FDCPA 1692e ( 2 ) ( A ) ). Engaging in harassment and intimidation The agent yelled, interrupted, and refused to allow me to speak, violating my consumer rights ( FDCPA 1692d ). Threatening wage garnishment without legal grounds False threats of garnishment or seizing bank accounts violate FDCPA 1692e ( 4 ). Failing to provide proper debt validation The agent refused to provide any written proof of the debt when I questioned its legitimacy, which violates FDCPA 1692g.

Requested Action I request that your office : Investigate AR services for violating state and federal debt collection laws Take appropriate legal action to stop their unlawful collection practices Impose penalties or fines if violations are confirmed Require AR services to cease all contact with me immediately

text:
Complaint Habitability Failure, Forced Heat Displacement, and Retaliatory Charges COMPLAINT Service Provider : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( AMH ) / XXXX Management GA XXXX XXXX XXXX : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, Georgia Relevant XXXX : XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX # What happened I was a tenant at the above-referenced property managed by American Homes XXXX Rent XXXX For an extended and continuous period exceeding XXXX year, spanning from the prior year through the XXXX XXXX, the property was maintained with a non-functioning and unresolved air-conditioning ( HVAC ) system. Despite repeated notice to management and multiple requests for repair, the HVAC system was never permanently corrected. 

As a result of the landlords failure to maintain this essential system, the residence experienced extreme indoor heat conditions, rendering the premises uninhabitable and posing serious health and safety risks. The severity of the condition prompted XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX to inspect the property, formally document the HVAC failure, and issue an inspection docket confirming a habitability violation. 

Due to the extreme heat conditions inside the home, my household was compelled to leave the residence for extended periods in order to avoid heat-related harm. To protect health and safety, we were forced to travel to cooler locations, including Florida and XXXX, solely to escape the uninhabitable heat caused by the landlords failure to maintain the air-conditioning system. These forced displacements resulted in substantial out-of-pocket expenses, significant disruption to daily life, and emotional distress. 

Despite these measures, the HVAC condition remained unresolved. Continued occupancy was unsafe and unreasonable. Consequently, my household was forced to vacate physical occupancy of the property on XX/XX/XXXX. 

I thereafter submitted a formal Notice to Vacate on XX/XX/XXXX, through the XXXX system. All keys and access devices were returned via FedEx Overnight, including XXXX garage remote controls ( XXXX provided by me and XXXX located in the kitchen closet above the microwave at move-in ). The return of keys constituted legal surrender of possession effective XX/XX/XXXX, consistent with lease requirements. 

Notwithstanding the documented habitability failure and lawful surrender of possession, the landlords billing system and local management attempted to mischaracterize the move-out as a voluntary early termination, including the posting or threatening of rent and fees beyond the surrender date. 



# Why I believe this violates fair housing law This matter reflects a systemic failure to provide and maintain habitable housing, followed by retaliatory and adverse actions taken after the tenant asserted rights related to unsafe housing conditions. 

Specifically : - The landlord failed to maintain a critical system necessary for safe, habitable occupancy. 
- The HVAC failure resulted in extreme heat exposure, forcing involuntary displacement and travel to avoid health risks. 
- The condition persisted despite repeated notice and XXXX XXXX code enforcement documentation. 
- After the tenant vacated due to the landlords failure, the landlord attempted to impose financial penalties as if the termination were voluntary. 

Attempting to collect rent or fees after a tenant vacates due to documented habitability violations constitutes retaliation and unlawful interference with housing rights under the XXXX XXXX XXXX. 



# Harm suffered As a direct and foreseeable result of the landlords actions and omissions, I suffered : - Forced displacement due to extreme heat conditions - Out-of-pocket expenses incurred to avoid uninhabitable housing - Emotional distress, stress, and disruption of family stability - Continued financial and administrative pressure through improper post-surrender charges # What I am seeking I seek : - Formal recognition that the vacating and termination resulted from the landlords failure to maintain habitable premises, not voluntary lease termination - Immediate reversal of any improper rent or fee charges beyond XX/XX/XXXX - Written assurance that no adverse rental reporting, collection activity, or negative reference will occur - Appropriate relief for the harm caused, including emotional distress and costs incurred as a result of the habitability failure - Enforcement action ensuring the housing providers future compliance with fair housing and habitability obligations

I am submitting this complaint regarding my mortgage servicer, Mr. Cooper ( servicing on behalf of XXXX Loan Servicing ) .

I was actively engaged in a short sale agreement with Mr. Cooper/Lakeview. We were still within the agreed-upon process timeframe and were working in good faith to complete the short sale. During this period, I remained in communication with their representatives and relied on their guidance. 

While the short sale was still in progress, the company initiated foreclosure proceedings against us. This action directly contradicted the ongoing loss mitigation process and caused significant confusion, stress, and financial harm. 

Additionally, I spoke with XXXX of their agents about the possibility of a deed in lieu of foreclosure. The agent encouraged us to consider this option and specifically stated that we had sufficient time to complete the deed-in-lieu process. However, the very next day after that conversation, we were served with foreclosure paperwork by their attorneys. This was unexpected and inconsistent with the information provided to us by their representative. 

I believe the servicer engaged in dual tracking by pursuing foreclosure while a loss mitigation option ( short sale and potential deed in lieu ) was actively under review. I relied on their representations regarding timelines and available options, and their actions have undermined my ability to resolve this matter through an alternative to foreclosure. 

I am requesting that the CFPB investigate this matter and require the servicer to : Immediately halt foreclosure proceedings while loss mitigation options are under review. 

Provide a clear written explanation of why foreclosure was initiated during an active short sale process. 

Confirm whether proper procedures were followed under applicable mortgage servicing regulations. 

Review all communications and ensure that borrowers are not misled regarding timelines or available alternatives. 

I am seeking a fair opportunity to complete the agreed-upon loss mitigation process without the threat of foreclosure proceeding simultaneously.

text:
I am filing a complaint regarding unresolved servicing issues related to my auto loan following a repossession that occurred after I was told a payment would bring my account current. 

On XX/XX/year>, I logged into my online account with Bellco Credit Union to determine the amount needed to bring my loan current. My account showed a past-due balance of {$2700.00}. Although the loan had matured and this amount did not represent the full payoff balance, it was the amount displayed as due in my online account.

Before making the payment, I called Bellco to confirm the amount. During that call I asked the representative to confirm the past-due balance and whether paying that amount would bring my account current. The representative confirmed that the amount shown online was correct and confirmed that making that payment would bring the account current. Based on that representation, I made a payment of {$2700.00} on XX/XX/XXXX at approximately XXXX a.m. I have a receipt confirming this payment. 

Despite making that payment, my vehicle was repossessed. On XX/XX/year>, after realizing my vehicle had been repossessed, I contacted Bellco to ask why the repossession had occurred given the payment made on XX/XX/XXXX. 

During that call, the representative stated that Bellco would review the XX/XX/XXXX call to determine whether an error had occurred. She explictly stated the previous rep " should never have taken that payment. '' She futher explained that if the earlier representative had provided incorrect information, Bellco would waive the repossession-related fees and reimburse the pickup and storage fees I would need to pay to the repossession facility in order to recover my vehicle.

She stated that in order for Bellco to authorize the repossession company to release the vehicle, I would need to pay the remaining balance on the loan itself, which she quoted as {$1300.00}. I was told this payment needed to be made that day in order for the vehicle to be released. I made that payment on XX/XX/XXXX and received confirmation that Bellco would authorize the vehicles release.

I subsequently paid pickup and storage fees directly to the repossession facility in order to retrieve my vehicle. 

Shortly after making this payment, the transaction history changed and the XX/XX/XXXX payment of {$2700.00} disappeared from the account history, leaving only the approximately {$1300.00} payment visible. 

After the first review of the XX/XX/XXXX call, the representative contacted me and stated that Bellcos position was that the earlier representative had not explicitly stated that the vehicle would not be subject to repossession. At that time, my account ledger was updated again and repossession-related fees were added to the balance. The account now showed repossession fees and the {$1300.00} payment, but still did not reflect the {$2700.00} payment made on XX/XX/XXXX. 

I explained that during the XX/XX/XXXX call I had specifically asked whether the payment would bring the account current and whether the past-due balance would be satisfied, and both were confirmed. I requested that my statement be formally documented and asked that the call be reviewed again. 

After a second review, the representative again stated that Bellco was maintaining its position based on the wording used during the call. At that point she arranged for me to speak with her manager.

During my conversation with the manager, I explained that I had relied on Bellcos confirmation that the payment would bring my account current and that a reasonable consumer would not expect their vehicle to be repossessed after confirming both that the past-due balance had been satisfied and that the account was current. The manager stated she understood that " from a consumer standpoint '' and said she would review the situation further.

The manager later called me back and stated that Bellco would waive the repossession-related fees as a one-time courtesy and that the account would reflect that no further balance was owed. She also stated that she would send written confirmation of this resolution. 

Following that call, the repossession fees that had appeared on the account were removed. However, the ledger still reflected only the approximately {$1300.00} payment and continued to omit the {$2700.00} payment made on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Shortly afterward, the entire loan account disappeared from my online portal and is no longer visible. The account does not appear as a closed loan, it simply does not appear at all, and no documentation explaining the final disposition of the loan has been provided.

Additionally, messages within Bellcos online message center are labeled as being associated with an account not found. If the message system still references the loan account, the account record itself should still exist with a complete transaction history. Instead, the loan account and portions of the payment history disappeared from my portal entirely.

I have sent multiple messages through Bellcos online portal requesting clarification, an updated account statement reflecting both payments, and confirmation of the final resolution. I have also left voicemail messages for the representative who handled my case. I have received no meaningful response. 

I have also not received any lien release, title documentation, or other paperwork confirming that the loan has been properly closed. 

The repeated changes to the account ledger, the disappearance of the XX/XX/XXXX payment from the transaction history, and the removal of the loan account from my online portal without any written accounting or XXXX release documentation have left me without a clear record of how my payments were applied or confirmation that the loan was properly closed. 

I am requesting written confirmation of the final status of the loan, a full accounting of the XX/XX/XXXX payment of {$2700.00}, reimbursement of the pickup and storage fees paid to the repossession facility as previously promised, and the appropriate XXXX release or title documentation confirming that the loan has been properly closed.

source_sub_issue: Account reinstatement or redemption after repossession

The Anatomy oXX/XX/XXXXXXXX The primary financial anchor of the document is XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. A REMIC is a specialized vehicle used to aggregate mortgage-backed securities and redistribute their cash flows into various classes, or tranches, which offer different risk and return profiles to investors. The XXXX series was a significant issuance during the XXXX, a period marked by the rapid expansion of the secondary mortgage market and the refinement of structured finance products. 
Technical Specifications and XXXX PA The document highlights a specific security within this trust : XXXX XXXX, identified as XXXX PA. In the nomenclature of structured finance, the " PA '' designation typically refers to a Planned Amortization Class ( PAC ). PAC tranches are engineered to provide investors with a highly predictable payment schedule, protected against prepayment risk by " companion '' or " support '' tranches that absorb the volatility of the underlying mortgage pool. This predictability is governed by a Prepayment Speed Assumption ( PSA ) model, which forecasts the rate at which homeowners will pay off their mortgages.

The Role of Myerberg & Company and XXXX XXXX A secondary but crucial focal point in the document is the involvement of Myerberg & Company L.P., specifically its founder, XXXX XXXX. The significance of her inclusion can not be overstated in the context of financial history. The Invention of the Collateralized Mortgage Obligation During her tenure at Freddie Mac, XXXX XXXX was directly responsible for the development of the first Collateralized Mortgage Obligation ( CMO ), the precursor to the modern REMIC. She also led the team that implemented the Book Entry program for mortgage securities with theXX/XX/XXXX New York. text:
I am filing this complaint regarding the handling of a recent loan application with XXXX XXXX 

Before allowing a hard credit inquiry to proceed, I had a detailed conversation with a representative who contacted me, regarding the type of loan I was willing to consider. She mentioned that it was for a secured loan, and I immediately told her I do not want to do a secured loan and if theres any small chance that I would be approved for only a secured loan then lets not proceed. During that conversation, And prior to finishing the application, I clearly stated the following : XXXX. I did not want a loan secured by a vehicle, XXXX. My current vehicle is in the process of being bought back by the manufacturer, so it can not be used as collateral XXXX. I would not place a replacement vehicle as collateral for a loan. 
XXXX. I was concerned about allowing a hard inquiry on my credit report unless there was a very realistic possibility of approval for the type of loan I was requesting. 

The representative acknowledged these concerns and stated that the loan could be structured as a personal property secured loan, instead of a vehicle-secured loan. She expressed confidence that this type of structure could be possible and indicated she had seen situations where the final approval amount differed, or even increased, from the initial pre-approval. On XX/XX/XXXX, when I answered her call I told her that I did not want to go through with it because I did not feel like the pre-approval amount was worth it. At that time, is when she then told me that she has seen scenarios where people get approved for more than the pre-approval amount, and I wouldnt not have to put a vehicle up as collateral. The confidence that she displayed in getting me a loan that was not tied to a vehicle is what convinced me to move forward. At the end of our phone call, she said I will have an approval for you by tomorrow morning. And this was after we discussed my concerns and that I did not want to move forward if there was even a small chance of any of my concerns being valid. 

Because of those assurances she gave me, after XXXX days of hesitance, I agreed to proceed with the application and allowed a hard inquiry to be placed on my credit report. 

The following day, when I had not heard anything from her after she stated she would be calling back in the morning, I contacted the bank for an update, at XXXX. I was informed by another XXXX representative that I was only approved for a loan secured by a vehicle, which would pay my vehicle off, which is the exact type of loan I had clearly declined from the beginning. At that point I had told him that I had already discussed with the previous representative that I did not want to do that type of loan and this car is being bought back by the manufacturer. He then started to come up with several excuses as to why their sales tactic was not unethical. 

During that conversation, the representative focused on the fact that I had received a pre-approval, and stated that underwriting ultimately determines the final decision. However, this response does not address the actual issue. 

My complaint is not about the underwriting decision itself. I understand that lenders ultimately determine approval terms. 

The issue is that I communicated very clearly that I would not accept a vehicle-secured loan, and the representative still encouraged me to proceed with a full application and hard credit inquiry based on the suggestion that a different loan structure was likely possible. It is their responsibility as the professionals in this industry to advise against applying, especially when someone has concerns as I did. I also brought up that I was ready to leave it because the initial preapproval was for a secured loan. It wasnt until she said, it doesnt have to be a vehicle that I even continued to entertain the conversation. That shouldve never happened. She shouldve advised against me moving forward with this loan because of my concerns and the circumstances. The XXXX representative I spoke to continue to argue with me that there was no problem with what happened. He also cut me off initially while I was speaking ; and several times after. When I asked him to let me finish speaking, so he could hear what I was saying, he said so its your way or the highway?. Lets be clear ; this man cut me off when I was in the middle of speaking. There was no reason to start cutting me off. I continued to try and explain to him why Im frustrated and he continued to defend their actions. He kept focusing on the pre-approval, and not the fact that I was convinced into completing this application. At that point, I hung up. 

Given the information I provided up front about my credit concerns, credit report history, collateral limitations, and loan preferences, I truly believe a reasonable and responsible course of action would have been to advise me that the pre-approval appeared to be tied specifically to a vehicle-secured loan and that proceeding with the application could result in exactly the outcome that occurred. 

Instead, I relied on the representatives assurances that a different loan structure was possible, which led me to authorize the application and incur a hard inquiry on my credit report. This has nothing to do with underwriting and everything to do with the fact that they are responsible for providing guidance in these situations, and they provided me with guidance that completely changed the course of this application, as I did not want to pursue it. 

As someone working to rebuild my credit, I was very transparent about my concerns regarding unnecessary credit inquiries. Had I been advised that a vehicle-secured loan was the most likely outcome, I would not have proceeded with the application. 

I believe consumers should be able to rely on clear and responsible guidance before authorizing a credit inquiry that can impact their credit profile.

text:
CFPB : Beginning in XXXX  XXXX, I engaged in documented, repeated good faith efforts to resolve my mortgage through a deed in lieu of foreclosure. Over nearly two years I made multiple written and verbal contacts with RoundPoint Mortgage Servicing LLC, spoke with numerous representatives, and submitted formal written requests. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, a RoundPoint representative named XXXX verbally communicated that my deed in lieu application had been approved subject to specific conditions including a cash contribution of {$11000.00}, immediate vacancy, broom sweep condition, and execution of a personal property release. I asked for a detailed breakdown of the {$11000.00} figure and specifically requested a callback before any denial was issued. RoundPoint never called back. The approval was subsequently withdrawn without written notice, without stating any reason, and without providing appeal rights in violation of 12 CFR 1024.41 ( d ). 
In XX/XX/XXXX I presented RoundPoint with a purchase offer of {$410000.00} on the property, which exceeded the outstanding loan balance. RoundPoint failed to respond substantively. The buyer was lost. RoundPoint 's documented response to multiple formal written requests was to require reapplication rather than evaluate pending requests on their merits. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, foreclosure was filed against the property. On XX/XX/XXXX while foreclosure was actively pending RoundPoint ordered a XXXX  XXXX  XXXX property valuation. On XX/XX/XXXX, RoundPoint sent written correspondence confirming that my borrower assistance application was still under active evaluation. This constitutes dual tracking in direct violation of Regulation X, 12 CFR 1024.41, which prohibits a servicer from proceeding with foreclosure while loss mitigation is under active review.

I have submitted a RESPA Qualified Written Request to RoundPoint dated XX/XX/XXXX requesting all call recordings, loss mitigation records, internal notes, and communications regarding this loan

On XX/XX/XXXX, I was involved in an accident with my XXXX XXXX XXXX. I reported the incident to XXXX XXXX on the same day. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, my XXXX claims agent determined the vehicle to be a total loss. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, my XXXX agent, XXXX XXXX, contacted me and stated she had received a payoff guarantee from XXXX XXXX XXXX in the amount of approximately {$240.00}. I immediately informed her that this figure appeared incorrect. XXXX XXXX then verified with XXXX, at which point it was discovered that Hyundai Motor Finance had made XXXX direct payments to my loan account : XXXX in the approximate amount of {$500.00} and another in the approximate amount of {$23000.00}. 
XXXX XXXX subsequently contacted Hyundai Motor Finance. Representatives explained that the payments resulted from an error committed by a former employee who had since been terminated. XXXX stated they would initiate the process to request a refund of the excess funds from XXXX. XXXX XXXX informed me of this explanation and advised that she intended to submit the {$240.00} payment to XXXX to secure release of the title to XXXX. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX followed up with Hyundai Motor Finance to clarify handling of the matter. During this call, the XXXX representative ended the conversation abruptly by hanging up. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted XXXX XXXX again. She informed me that she had ceased further direct engagement with XXXX until they resolved the refund issue internally. She stated that XXXX would release the remaining settlement proceeds to me once XXXX and XXXX clarified the loan status and coordinated appropriately. 
Believing the matter would be resolved promptly by the involved parties, I waited for updates. By XX/XX/XXXX, with no progress reported, I contacted both Hyundai Motor Finance and XXXX XXXX XXXX directly. 
I first spoke with XXXX representative XXXX XXXX, who refused to provide any proof or documentation of a refund request having been submitted to XXXX. After I insisted on evidence, he recorded my email address and assured me he would send the requested information promptly. I received nothing from XXXX. 
Immediately afterward, I contacted XXXX XXXX XXXX. When I requested confirmation or proof of any refund request from XXXX, the representative stated no such communication or funds had been received, and therefore no documentation could be provided. 
The following day ( XX/XX/XXXX ), I spoke with a different XXXX XXXX. After explaining the full situation, the manager promised to call me back within XXXX minutes with the requested information or updates. No callback was received. 
The day after that ( XX/XX/XXXX ), my subsequent attempts to contact Hyundai Motor Finance were unsuccessfulcalls did not connect, suggesting my number may have been blocked. 
I have audio recordings of my XX/XX/XXXX, conversation with XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ) and the immediate follow-up call to XXXX, which document the conflicting information, unfulfilled promises, and lack of resolution. 
After these interactions, I again paused direct outreach in the hope that XXXX and XXXX would address the known error without further intervention. However, as of XX/XX/XXXX, neither party has contacted me or XXXX XXXX with any updates, proof of actions taken, or resolution of the overpayment/refund issue. 
This ongoing delaynow more than XXXX weeks since Hyundais initial admission of the error on or about XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX prevented XXXX from finalizing the total loss settlement and releasing the full insurance proceeds to me. 
I respectfully request CFPB assistance in compelling Hyundai Motor Finance and XXXX XXXX XXXX to investigate promptly, provide written confirmation of the refund status and any actions taken, process the necessary corrections to settle the loan account accurately, coordinate with Progressive to enable claim resolution, and address any resulting impacts ( such as fees, interest, or credit reporting issues ) arising from the delay.

text:
My mortgage servicer, LoanCare, approved a mortgage deferment for my loan after I specifically confirmed with their representatives that participating in the program would not negatively affect my credit and that repayment options would be available at the end of the deferment period. 

Despite these assurances, the deferment appears to have never been properly implemented on my account. 

After approval of the deferment : * Payments continued to be withdrawn even though the deferment was supposedly active. 
* I was told the continued withdrawn payment would be refunded, but the refund never occurred. 
* I continued receiving delinquency letters and was repeatedly told by representatives to ignore them because the deferment had already been approved. 
* The start date of the deferment was changed or unclear. 

When the deferment period ended, I called prior to the XX/XX/XXXX payment date and was unexpectedly informed that the entire deferred balanceapproximately over XXXX XXXXwas due immediately in full. I had never been told this would happen. I was originally told there would be repayment options such as restructuring or repayment plans. 

After explaining that we could not pay {$16000.00} at once, LoanCare asked us to submit financial documentation showing why we could not come up with the money. We submitted documentation clearly showing that we do not have anywhere near that amount across our bank accounts. 

Despite providing the requested documentation, our request was denied. We were told we would have until XX/XX/XXXX to appeal, yet it appears the decision may have been finalized before that timeframe expired. 

Additionally, LoanCare reported the account as late or delinquent to the credit bureaus, despite my being told multiple times that participating in the deferment would not negatively affect our credit. This reporting caused our credit scores to drop more than XXXX points and has significantly impacted our financial situation. 

All of the calls referenced above were recorded by LoanCare, and I request that these recordings be reviewed. 

I am requesting that LoanCare : Conduct a full investigation into the handling of this deferment Provide written confirmation of when and how the deferment was applied to the account Correct any inaccurate reporting to all credit bureaus immediately and quickly Remove any late or delinquent reporting related to this deferment Provide a clear explanation of why we were told one set of terms but treated differently We entered the deferment in good faith based on the information provided by LoanCare representatives. The handling of this situation has caused significant financial harm and distress.

text: XXXX XXXX XXXX is knowingly furnishing, collecting, and defending a debt that its own records prove is not capable of verification. This is no longer a billing dispute. It is a demonstrated failure of furnishing accuracy, accounting integrity, and UCC compliance. Toyotas response to this complaint confirms the violations. 1. Toyota submitted two official documents to the CFPB that state different principal balances for the same loan and time period : {$30000.00} ( Redemption Notice XX/XX/year> ) {$30000.00} ( Deficiency Calculation XX/XX/year> ) Toyota offers no reconciliation, no explanation, and no correction. A furnisher that can not state the correct principal balance can not legally verify, report, or collect the debt. Continued reporting under these conditions is per se inaccurate. 2. Toyotas own ledger shows multiple loan balance increases after payments, after repossession, and after charge-off. A simple-interest retail installment contract does not permit principal increases absent a new advance. Toyota identifies no contractual authority, no consumer authorization, and no lawful basis for re-inflating principal. This is not an accounting error it is balance manipulation. Any balance derived from these entries is void and unreliable. 3. Toyota reversed multiple consumer payments ( {$400.00}, {$500.00}, {$770.00}, {$1000.00} ) without producing proof of returned funds, NSF notices, or bank documentation. Reporting reversals without evidence constitutes false payment history. A furnisher can not create delinquency or deficiency by reversing payments it can not prove were returned. This violates the core accuracy requirements of the FCRA. 4. Toyota claims a private repossession sale but failed to produce a single document demonstrating commercial reasonableness, including : Advertising or sale notice Bid logs or auction data Buyer identity Vehicle condition or valuation Sale methodology Under UCC 9-626, Toyota bears the burden of proof. It has failed. As a result, the alleged deficiency is legally unenforceable. 5. Despite clear internal contradictions and missing proof, Toyota continues to : Furnish derogatory credit data Demand payment on an unverified balance Represent the deficiency as valid This conduct is willful, not accidental. Toyota has been given notice of the defects and has chosen to continue reporting anyway.
label: loan_mortgage

Subject : Complaint Regarding Misleading Financing Terms, Mishandling of Personal Information, and Unprofessional Conduct I am submitting this complaint regarding my recent experience with the finance department at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

I initially contacted the dealership regarding a used XXXX  vehicle advertised with financing terms of {$0.00} down and 2.9 % APR, with an estimated loan amount of approximately {$36000.00}. During my conversation with a finance representative identified as XXXX, I was informed that I qualified for financing around 2.9 % APR for the vehicle in question.

Shortly afterward, I contacted another representative identified as XXXX, who informed me that the vehicle had already been sold. However, when my fiance contacted the dealership shortly after, she was told the same vehicle was still available. This conflicting information raises serious concerns about the accuracy and honesty of the information being provided to customers.

After this, my call was routed to another manager who attempted to redirect the conversation away from the previously discussed financing terms and instead tried to push a loan offer with an interest rate of approximately 29.9 % APR, which is dramatically different from the originally discussed 2.9 % APR financing. This felt like a bait-and-switch situation, where the initial financing terms were used to draw interest, only to later pressure me into an extremely high-interest loan.

Additionally, during this process I was asked to send a photo of my drivers license via text message, which I did in good faith believing I was proceeding with a legitimate loan approval process. However, given the way this situation was handled and the inconsistencies in communication, I am now extremely concerned about how my personal information is being stored, handled, or potentially shared. My drivers license contains sensitive identifying information, and the lack of professionalism during this transaction raises concerns about possible misuse of my identity information.

Overall, this experience was highly unprofessional and misleading. The dealership representatives : Provided inconsistent information about the availability of the vehicle Represented financing terms that were not honored or seriously considered Attempted to pressure me into a drastically higher interest rate loan Requested sensitive personal identification without clear transparency or proper process Consumers should be able to trust that advertised financing offers and statements made by finance managers are accurate and made in good faith. This situation wasted a significant amount of my time and created legitimate concerns about the handling of my personal data.

I am requesting the following : 1. A formal explanation regarding the conflicting statements about vehicle availability.

2. Clarification regarding why 2.9 % APR financing was represented but never actually offered.

3. Written confirmation of how my drivers license information is being stored and protected, and confirmation that it has not been shared, sold, or improperly retained.

4. A review of the conduct of the staff involved in this situation.

I hope this matter will be taken seriously so that other consumers do not experience the same misleading and frustrating situation.

text:
My name is XXXX XXXX XXXX, and I am submitting this complaint regarding ongoing issues with my mortgage loan, which is currently serviced by CalCon Mutual Mortgage LLC, doing business as XX/XX/XXXX, and involves Schumacher Mortgage XXXX and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

This matter is currently the subject of an active federal court case in the United States XXXX XXXX for XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Pennsylvania ( Case No. XXXX ). Despite the existence of this active dispute, the loan servicer continues to engage in improper servicing practices, inaccurate accounting, and actions that exceed the scope of the mortgage agreement. 

Most recently, in or around XX/XX/scrub> 2026, the servicer confirmed in writing that XXXX XXXX. XXXX was added to my escrow account and that tax disbursement was scheduled for that parcel. This action is improper and unauthorized. 

XXXX XXXX is solely owned by me and is not included in the mortgage instrument or its legal description. The mortgage encumbers XXXX XXXX only. Despite this, the servicer has taken steps to treat XXXX XXXX as if it were collateral securing the loan. 

Importantly, this conduct directly contradicts the servicers prior written statement. In an email dated XX/XX/year>2026, at XXXX XX/XX/XXXX, the servicer clearly acknowledged that only XXXX parcel is encumbered by the mortgage. This admission is consistent with the mortgage documents, which identify XXXX XXXX only. 

Despite this clear acknowledgment, the servicer has taken inconsistent and unauthorized actions by adding XXXX XXXX to escrow and attempting to disburse taxes for that parcel. This contradiction demonstrates a lack of accuracy and reliability in the servicers handling of the loan. 

I immediately disputed this action in writing, clearly stating that XXXX XXXX is not part of the mortgage and should not be included in escrow. To date, the servicer has not provided any documentation demonstrating authority to include this parcel or justification for its actions. 

This issue is not isolated. It is part of a broader pattern of conduct that includes : Misrepresentation of loan terms and structure Failure to apply credits and payments properly Inflation of loan amounts and payoff figures Improper inclusion of property not subject to the mortgage Ongoing inaccurate servicing and escrow practices Despite repeated disputes and requests for clarification, the servicer continues to act in a manner that increases my financial burden and creates confusion regarding what property secures the loan. 

This ongoing conduct is causing harm and uncertainty, particularly as it relates to the scope of the mortgage lien, the accuracy of the loan balance, and the handling of escrow funds. 

Given that this matter is already under active litigation, I am requesting that your agency : XXXX. Conduct a formal review of the loan servicing practices in this matter XXXX. Require the servicer to provide full documentation supporting their actions XXXX. Ensure that no property is treated as collateral unless properly disclosed and legally included in the mortgage XXXX. Address the ongoing inaccuracies and improper handling of escrow and loan accounting Applicable Pennsylvania Law and Legal Basis for Complaint : The issues described above are not only factual disputes but also raise serious concerns under Pennsylvania law governing real property, mortgage liens, and consumer protection. 

XXXX. XXXXXX/XX/XXXXXXXX Pennsylvania Recording Statute Under Pennsylvania law, any interest in real property, including a mortgage, must be in writing, properly recorded, and applies only to the property specifically described in the recorded instrument. 

If XXXX XXXX is not included in the mortgages legal description, it is not legally encumbered, and the servicer can not treat it as collateral securing the loan. 

XXXX. XX/XX/XXXX Statute of Frauds ( Pennsylvania ) The Statute of Frauds requires that any agreement involving real estate must be in writing and clearly defined. 

Accordingly, a mortgage can not be expanded verbally or administratively to include additional property. A servicer can not treat a parcel as collateral unless it is expressly included in the written mortgage agreement. 

XXXX. Established Pennsylvania Mortgage Law It is a fundamental principle of mortgage law that a lien attaches only to the property specifically described in the mortgage. 

If a parcel is not included in the legal description, it is not collateral for the loan and can not be treated as such. 

XXXX. Pennsylvania Unfair Trade Practices and Consumer Protection Law (XX/XX/XXXX) ( XXXXXX/XX/XXXXet seq. ) This law prohibits deceptive practices, misrepresentation, and unfair conduct in business. 

Where a servicer represents that only XXXX parcel is encumbered but later treats an additional parcel as collateral, such conduct raises concerns of misrepresentation and deceptive servicing practices. 

XXXX. Improper Loan Servicing Practices While mortgage servicing is also governed by federal law, Pennsylvania law recognizes that improper handling of escrow accounts, payments, and property designation may constitute unfair or deceptive practices. 

The inclusion of property not subject to the mortgage, along with inaccurate escrow handling, raises serious concerns regarding the accuracy and legality of the servicers actions.
label: loan_mortgage
source_sub_issue: An existing modification, forbearance plan, short sale, or other loss mitigation relief

text:
I am submitting a complaint regarding the servicing of my mortgage loan by Community Bank, N.A., specifically relating to an escrow adjustment, credit reporting during an active dispute, and the bank 's failure to provide clear, accurate, and actionable information required to bring my account current. 

Prior to this matter, I maintained a perfect payment history on this loan and had never been delinquent. My family and I have banked with Community Bank for decades, and I expected reasonable treatment, clear communication, and appropriate duty of care. 

Key timeline : - XX/XX/XXXX : Escrow-driven payment increase and initial attempts to understand and dispute the change - XX/XX/XXXX : Formal Notice of Error submitted - XX/XX/XXXX : Bank response XXXX ( not received until XX/XX/XXXX ) - Week of XX/XX/XXXX : Materially incorrect payoff amount provided - XX/XX/XXXX : Correct amount finally provided and paid The issue began in XX/XX/XXXX, when my monthly payment increased significantly due to an escrow recalculation, rising from approximately {$1500.00} to {$2800.00}. 

The nature of the increase itself made it extraordinarily difficult to understand without outside assistance. The underlying cause, a tenfold increase in property taxes resulting from a change in ownership structure and the loss of associated income-based deductions, was never explained to me by the bank. I came to understand it only after extensive independent research and direct conversations with town and state tax representatives. Until that point, the dramatic increase appeared to be a clear error, consistent with missing homestead credits. At no point did the bank provide an explanation that allowed me to understand what had actually driven the payment change. This further reinforced my belief that the account was subject to a legitimate error requiring correction, and that the dispute was valid. 

There are also unresolved issues regarding missing homestead credits that should reduce the underlying tax bills. I have been actively working with the XXXX and XXXX to correct these items, a process that overlapped with the holiday period and introduced unavoidable administrative delays. 

From XX/XX/XXXX onward, I made sustained, good-faith efforts to understand and resolve the adjustment. However, I encountered repeated servicing and communication failures that materially impaired my ability to do so, including : - Being repeatedly routed between departments without a single accountable point of contact. Over the course of this matter, I was directed to contact no fewer than XXXX different representatives, including XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX, across multiple departments, none of whom had continuity of knowledge about the account. 

- Inconsistent and conflicting information regarding submitted documents - Inability to obtain confirmation of document receipt - Incorrect internal contact information preventing callbacks- Delays in receiving formal correspondence- Being told that emails may not be responded to as a matter of policy an unacceptable position in a matter involving delinquency status and active credit reporting In addition, I was unable to obtain a clear and reliable statement of the amount required to bring the account current. 

When I contacted the bank to request the amount required to cure the account, I was not provided with a consistent or accurate answer. I was directed to loan activity statements that do not identify the outstanding amount required to bring the loan current, and which representatives themselves acknowledged are difficult to interpret. 

Further, I was provided with inconsistent and materially incorrect figures by different representatives. As recently as the week of XX/XX/XXXX, I was told an amount exceeding {$11000.00} was owed, when the correct amount was just over {$7000.00}. 

Throughout this period, I continued making payments in line with the original monthly obligation, including principal, interest, and the prior escrow amount, reflecting my ongoing good-faith effort to remain current while seeking clarity. 

I was also advised to rely on online banking to view my loan, but the loan is not visible or accessible in my account at all. It is not a case of limited functionality, the loan is simply not displayed in any form, leaving me without any online method to verify balances or amounts due. 

As a result, despite actively attempting to resolve the situation since XX/XX/XXXX, I was unable to determine a reliable and accurate amount required to bring the loan current. 
At the same time, the escrow shortage was being recovered over 12 months, significantly increasing the required monthly payment. Requests for a more manageable recovery structure were declined. This created a situation where the required monthly payment increased sharply at the same time that servicing and communication failures were preventing me from obtaining clear and reliable information about the account. The combination of a materially increased payment and the inability to determine accurate amounts owed made it significantly more difficult to bring the account current despite my good-faith efforts. 

During this period, adverse credit reporting was furnished. The bank has stated this reflects contractual payment status, but this does not reflect that I was actively challenging the account from XX/XX/XXXX onward and was unable to resolve it due to servicing failures. 

The bank has also taken the position that the account was not " in dispute '' until I submitted a formal Notice of XXXX. However, I had been actively challenging the escrow adjustment and requesting clarification since XX/XX/XXXX. At no point was I informed that a formal Notice of XXXX was required for my concerns to be treated as a dispute or for credit reporting to be affected. Had I been informed, I would have submitted XXXX immediately. Instead, I believed I was engaging in good faith through normal servicing channels. This resulted in a situation where I was actively attempting to resolve the account for months, but was treated as though no dispute existed while adverse credit reporting continued. 

This has caused measurable harm. My credit score has declined during this period, and I have been denied a phone plan that I would otherwise expect to qualify for. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I was finally provided with a clear and reliable amount required to bring the account current. Upon receiving this figure, I immediately paid the outstanding balance. I was contacted by individuals impersonating XXXX XXXX and Bank of America fraud representatives, who instructed me to purchase these gift cards to cancel an unauthorized transaction they claimed was occurring on my account. I am seeking a full refund of the {$8200.00}, as I followed all instructions from official channels to report the fraud promptly and provided all evidence to support my claim.
