text:
REBUTTAL TO COMPANY RESPONSE CFPB Complaint No. : XXXX Submitted by : XXXX : XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX : XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, Texas ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX I. PURPOSE OF THIS REBUTTAL This submission responds to the companys XX/XX/year>XXXX  correspondence and is made to document that the response provided does not address the material issues raised, and therefore does not constitute a reasonable investigation or substantive response within the meaning of applicable federal law. 

II. FAILURE TO ADDRESS MATERIAL DISPUTES The companys response relies primarily on generalized statements regarding credit reporting obligations and does not address the specific and material facts presented in my complaint and supporting affidavit.

The following issues remain unanswered and unrebutted : Contract Formation / Consummation Vehicle delivery occurred prior to finalized lender approval. 
A Notice of Adverse Action was issued after delivery. 
No revised agreement or disclosures were executed after the adverse action. 

These facts directly implicate whether a lawful and enforceable obligation was ever formed under : 15 U.S.C. 1638 ( Truth in Lending Act ) 12 C.F.R. 1026.17 and 1026.2 ( a ) ( 13 ) ( Regulation Z consummation ) Failure to Address Disclosure Timing and Accuracy No evidence has been provided demonstrating that required TILA disclosures were accurate and provided prior to consummation.

No explanation has been provided regarding how APR, finance charge, and payment terms remained compliant despite post-delivery changes and delayed approval.

Failure to Address Security Interest Attachment The response does not address whether a valid and enforceable obligation exists sufficient to support attachment under : UCC 9-203 Texas Business & Commerce Code 9.203 A security interest can not attach absent : a valid obligation, debtor rights in the collateral, and satisfaction of attachment requirements.

No documentation has been provided demonstrating lawful attachment. 

Failure to Address Add-On Products and Finance Charges The inclusion of add-on products ( service contract, maintenance plan, etc. ) was not addressed. 
No documentation was provided demonstrating : that such products were optional, or that their inclusion complied with TILA finance charge disclosure requirements ( 15 U.S.C. 1605 ).

Failure to Address Credit Reporting Accuracy The company asserts accuracy but does not address : disputes concerning the existence and enforceability of the underlying obligation, inconsistencies related to vehicle return, account restructuring, and reporting status. 

Under 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ), a furnisher must conduct a reasonable investigation, not merely assert accuracy.

III. DEFICIENCY OF THE COMPANYS RESPONSE The companys response : avoids the core issue of whether a legally enforceable obligation exists, relies on generalized legal statements unrelated to the disputed facts, fails to provide documentary evidence supporting its conclusions, and does not reflect a reasonable investigation of the specific issues raised.

As such, the response should be considered non-responsive to the material disputes presented. 

IV. REQUEST FOR REGULATORY CONSIDERATION This matter presents potential violations of : Truth in Lending Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq. ) Regulation Z ( 12 C.F.R. Part 1026 ) Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681 et seq. ) Consumer Financial Protection Act ( 12 U.S.C. 55315536, UDAAP ) The pattern reflected in the companys response raises concerns regarding : failure to conduct a reasonable investigation, reliance on post hoc justifications instead of addressing formation defects, and continued furnishing of disputed information without resolving underlying validity.

V. CONCLUSION The company has not addressed the material facts presented and has failed to provide a substantive response.

This rebuttal is submitted to ensure the record accurately reflects that : the dispute remains unresolved, the material issues remain unrebutted, and the company has not demonstrated that a lawful and enforceable obligation exists. 

All rights are expressly reserved. 

Submitted by : XXXX XXXX XXXX

text:
I am submitting this statement to formally document the ongoing failure of Land Home Financial Services and XXXX XXXX to provide adequate customer service, communication, and loss mitigation assistance regarding my mortgage loan. 

Due to documented financial hardship, I made multiple good-faith efforts to seek assistance and pursue resolution options, including a short sale or foreclosure alternative. Despite these repeated attempts, both the mortgage servicer and its agents have failed to respond in a professional, timely, consistent, or meaningful manner. 

I have made numerous requests for updates on the status of my property, clarification of available options, and guidance on how to proceed. These requests have been largely ignored, leaving me without direction during a critical financial hardship. This lack of communication has effectively prevented me from taking timely action to mitigate further damage. 

During the course of this hardshipand while my requests for assistance were ongoingthe financial burden associated with my loan increased significantly. My monthly mortgage payment XXXX from approximately {$3900.00} to {$4700.00}, further compounding my inability to maintain the loan under these circumstances. Additionally, more than {$100000.00} in fees, penalties, interest, and related charges have been added to the balance. These increases occurred without adequate explanation, transparency, or meaningful assistance, despite my repeated attempts to engage the servicer in loss mitigation efforts. 

As a direct result of this inaction, additional fees, penalties, and accrued interest have continued to accumulate on my account. Rather than working toward a resolution, the continued escalation of the loan balance has unnecessarily increased my financial burden. 

Additionally, this situation has had a severe and damaging impact on my credit profile. The failure of Land Home Financial Services and XXXX XXXX to engage in loss mitigation efforts or provide timely assistance has resulted in negative reporting and significant harm to my XXXX XXXX. Despite my proactive attempts to resolve the matter, their lack of cooperation has contributed to ongoing derogatory reporting. 

The pattern of non-responsiveness, combined with the substantial increase in my loan balance, escalating monthly payment, excessive fee accumulation, and continued negative credit reporting, reflects a disregard for fair servicing practices and has effectively resulted in the destruction of my credit standing, preventing any option for refinancing. This outcome could have been mitigated or avoided entirely had appropriate assistance and communication been provided. 

Their failure to act in good faith, provide reasonable assistance, or offer viable solutions has left me with no reasonable alternative but to default on the loan. 

Notice of Potential Regulatory Violations In addition to the concerns outlined above, the actions and inactions of Land Home Financial Services and XXXX  XXXX  may constitute violations of several federal consumer protection laws and mortgage servicing regulations. There are also various violations under the XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX, Contract for Deed and the Seller 's obligations. XXXX  XXXX XXXX ( XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ). 

Based on the pattern of non-responsiveness, failure to provide timely assistance, continued accrual of excessive fees, and negative credit reporting despite my documented hardship and repeated requests for loss mitigation, the following potential violations are of concern : Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act ( RESPA ) Regulation X The failure to respond to borrower inquiries, exercise reasonable diligence in processing loss mitigation requests, and provide timely and meaningful communication may constitute violations of RESPA requirements. 

Failure to Engage in Good Faith Loss Mitigation Efforts Despite repeated outreach, there has been a consistent failure to evaluate or provide viable options, contributing directly to the escalation of my financial hardship. 
Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ( XXXX ). 

The accumulation of over {$100000.00} in fees, the increase in my mortgage payment from {$3900.00} to {$4700.00} during an active hardship, and the lack of transparency or assistance may constitute unfair or abusive servicing practices. 
Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) Concerns. 

The reporting of negative credit information under these circumstances raises concerns regarding the accuracy and fairness of such reporting, particularly where the servicer failed to provide appropriate assistance. 

Lack of Transparency in Account XXXX The failure to provide a clear and accurate accounting of charges and loan balance increases raises additional concerns regarding proper servicing standards. 

This correspondence serves as my second formal complaint regarding the ongoing issues with my mortgage loan servicing. 

Following my initial complaint, the servicer did make contact ; however, that outreach did not result in any meaningful action, resolution, or follow-through. Despite the initial acknowledgment, my concerns remain unaddressed, and no substantive assistance has been provided. The lack of continued communication and failure to act after establishing contact has only prolonged the matter and contributed to further financial and credit-related harm. 

Again, this pattern of incomplete engagementinitiating contact without resolutiondemonstrates a continued failure to adequately service the account and respond in good faith to a borrower experiencing documented financial hardship. 

I am requesting that this matter now be handled with the urgency and diligence it requires, including consistent follow-through, clear communication, and a definitive path toward resolution.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
On XX/XX/XXXX, the company issued IRS Form 1099-C reporting cancellation of debt related to my auto loan account. On that same date, a principal adjustment was posted to the account.

The Form 1099-C does not state that the cancellation was partial.

Despite issuing the 1099-C and reducing the principal balance, the company continued to collect payments and maintained the original monthly payment amount of {$450.00}. I did not receive a revised amortization schedule, billing disclosure, or written explanation recalculating the payment amount after the principal reduction. 

After XX/XX/XXXX, I continued making payments in good faith. The total amount of payments made after the reported cancellation date is {$3400.00}. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the company issued a letter stating that it had forgiven part of the balance and that a remaining balance of {$540.00} was still owed. This letter was issued approximately XXXX months after the XXXX effective date. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, during a recorded call with a representative from the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, I was advised that no prior waiver letter had been sent before XX/XX/XXXX. The company maintains that it had and still has the right to continue collecting under what it describes as a maturity plan. 

However, my original signed retail installment contract does not contain any maturity plan clause authorizing post-term restructuring or continued collection following debt cancellation reporting. 

To prevent repossession while this dispute remained unresolved, I scheduled a protective payment of {$540.00} under protest in XX/XX/XXXX. This payment was made without admission of liability and while preserving my rights. 

My concerns are : The 1099-C was issued without specifying partial cancellation.

A principal reduction was applied while the payment amount remained unchanged.

No revised amortization schedule was provided after the account was materially modified.

Collection activity continued despite cancellation being reported to the IRS.

A partial waiver explanation was issued months later.

I am requesting that the company reconcile its IRS reporting, servicing actions, and collection position, and refund post-cancellation payments if the cancellation stands as reported.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
Complaint : I am filing a complaint regarding a private student loan issued by Ascent Funding for enrollment at Devslopes, an online education provider that permanently closed during my enrollment period. 

I enrolled in XXXX in XX/XX/year> and took out a loan through Ascent Funding with the expectation of completing the program and improving my earning potential. In XX/XX/year>, XXXX ceased operations before the program was completed. As a result, I was unable to complete the education or receive the full value of the services for which the loan was issued. 

Despite the schools closure and the incomplete delivery of educational services, I remain responsible for repayment of this loan.

I have acted in good faith throughout this process : I have remained current on all payments I promptly notified Ascent of the school closure I formally disputed the loan and requested relief I cooperated fully with their review process Ascent acknowledged the school closure and placed my account in temporary forbearance at 0 % interest. They also confirmed that their executive and legal teams reviewed the situation.

However, Ascent has denied full relief and instead offered a 50 % balance reduction as a courtesy, while maintaining that they are not responsible for the schools closure. They have since stated that no further relief, adjustments, or discharge options are available.

This outcome leaves me responsible for a significant remaining balance tied to an education that I was unable to complete due to circumstances entirely outside of my control.

Additionally, this situation has created financial hardship for my household. I am supporting a young child, and my partners ability to work is limited due to childcare responsibilities and health constraints. While I have continued to make payments to protect my credit, the remaining balance represents a substantial burden given the lack of educational value received.

I am requesting that Ascent Funding : Reconsider full discharge of the remaining balance due to failure of consideration Alternatively, provide additional balance reduction beyond the current 50 % offer Remove any interest accrued after the date of the schools closure Confirm that my account will not be negatively reported while this matter is under review I believe it is unfair to require repayment of a loan for educational services that were not fully delivered due to the schools closure. I am seeking a fair and reasonable resolution.
label: loan_mortgage
source_sub_issue: Can't get other flexible options for repaying your loan

I am submitting this complaint regarding the conduct of Wells Fargo Bank , N.A . in connection with a foreclosure action concerning my property. 

This foreclosure action was commenced in or about XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, Wells Fargo obtained an Order of Reference, which allowed it to proceed toward a judgment of foreclosure and sale.

However, after obtaining the Order of Reference, Wells Fargo failed to take any substantive action to prosecute the case for approximately XXXX years. The next significant action did not occur until XX/XX/XXXX, when Wells Fargo moved for a XXXX of Foreclosure and Sale. 

During this XXXX  period of inactivity, interest continued to accrue on the loan at a rate of approximately 6.00 % per annum, or approximately {$110.00} per day. 

As a result of this delay, more than {$240000.00} in additional interest accrued. The total amount claimed by Wells Fargo increased to approximately {>= $1,000,000}, which is nearly double the original principal balance of approximately {$670000.00}. 

Wells Fargo has attributed delays to XXXX moratoriums and XXXX. However, XXXX moratoriums are temporary in nature and can not justify years of inactivity, and XXXX does not explain delays occurring between XXXX and XXXX. 

This conduct appears to be unfair and abusive, as Wells Fargo benefited financially from its own failure to prosecute the action in a timely manner. 

I have filed a motion in court seeking to toll or reduce the interest accrued during this period of delay. 

This issue has caused significant financial harm and has impaired my ability to resolve the foreclosure and sell the property. 

I respectfully request that this matter be investigated.

I have fulfilled all requirements on my end, including maintaining insurance, completing smog requirements, and paying all DMV fees. The request for proof of DMV payment is improper, as the DMV maintains its own official records.

The lender has not provided a clear explanation of the issue, nor a resolution timeline. A delay of over three years to process a title and perfect a lien is unreasonable and constitutes negligence.

text:
Subject : Formal Rebuttal to Dividend / Fifth Third Bank Response Request for Further Investigation I do not accept Dividend / Fifth Third Banks response as a resolution to my complaint. 

Their conclusion that the loan is valid is based on assumptions that are incomplete and unsupported by sufficient evidence. I am formally disputing their findings and requesting further investigation. 

1. Lack of Informed Consent At no point did I knowingly agree to a loan with an interest rate of 7.99 % or monthly payments of approximately {$600.00}. I was under the clear understanding that I was entering into financing through XXXX XXXX at significantly different terms ( approximately 3.99 % and much lower monthly payments ). 

A digital signature alone does not establish informed consent. I was not clearly presented with, nor did I knowingly agree to, a change in lender or materially different loan terms. 

2. Alleged Notification of Lender Change Dividend states that an email was sent on XX/XX/year>, notifying me of a change in financing providers. This does not constitute consent. There is no evidence that I received, reviewed, or agreed to this change. 

Additionally, Dividend claims the loan was signed on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXXprior to this alleged notification. This raises a serious inconsistency : how could I knowingly agree to a change in lender before being informed of it? 

XXXX. Disputed Authorization of Payment Terms I dispute that I authorized automatic payments in the amount of {$600.00}. I did not knowingly set up or approve these payment terms. 

I am requesting documented proof of : - The exact date and method by which this payment amount was established - The device, IP address, and authentication details tied to this authorization - Any communication clearly showing my acceptance of this specific payment obligation 4. Request for Verifiable Evidence To date, I have not been provided with sufficient evidence to support Dividends claims. I am requesting the following : - Full DocuSign audit trail, including IP address, timestamps, and device identification - A complete copy of the signed Loan and Security Agreement as presented at the time of signing - Any and all call recordings or transcripts from XX/XX/year>, including the alleged conversation clearing a fraud alert - Proof of explicit consent to change lenders from XXXX XXXX  to Dividend - Proof of explicit consent to the loan terms, including interest rate and monthly payment amount 5. Credit Reporting and Harm As a result of this disputed loan, my credit has been negatively impacted, and I have been placed into collections. This is causing ongoing financial and emotional harm. 

Given that this loan is under active dispute, I request : - Immediate suspension of all negative credit reporting related to this account - Removal of any derogatory marks already reported, pending the outcome of this investigation 6. Conclusion This matter is not resolved. Significant questions remain regarding how this loan was originated, how terms were presented, and whether proper consent was obtained. 

I respectfully request that the CFPB continue its investigation and require Dividend/ Fifth Third Bank to provide the documentation listed above. 

I am prepared to pursue this matter further if necessary. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
label: loan_mortgage

text:
This complaint concerns mortgage servicing issues involving escrow accounting errors, force-placed insurance charges, inaccurate payment application, and failure to adequately respond to requests for information. 

The loan originated on XX/XX/XXXX with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Servicing was later handled by Ditech Financial LLC and eventually transferred to NewRez LLC in XXXX. 

Beginning in XXXX, mortgage statements reflected irregular escrow activity and insurance charges that appeared inconsistent with actual disbursements. On XX/XX/XXXX, force-placed insurance associated with XXXX XXXX Insurance Company appeared on the account despite evidence that insurance coverage already existed. These charges increased the escrow balance and monthly payment. 

Mortgage statements during this period also reflected inconsistent payment amounts and unexplained changes to the total amount due. Payments appear to have been misapplied or not properly credited, which contributed to the account being reported as in default. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a servicer response to a borrower inquiry reflected inconsistent account balances and late fees. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, the servicer issued a disaster-related forbearance due to a XXXX event . After the forbearance period ended, the loan was still reported as in default despite payments being made during that time. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a written request was submitted to the servicer requesting verification of the loan account, including escrow charges, insurance premiums, and payment application. The responses received did not fully address the discrepancies. 

Although servicing transferred to NewRez in XXXX, the escrow and accounting issues remain unresolved. 

From XXXX to the present, foreclosure activity has continued while these servicing disputes remain unresolved. 

These issues appear to violate obligations under the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act related to escrow management, force-placed insurance procedures, and responding to borrower requests for information. 

Closing Because these servicing disputes remain unresolved and foreclosure activity has continued despite prior requests for clarification and correction, regulatory review is requested to ensure the mortgage account is properly audited, errors are corrected, and the servicer complies with federal mortgage servicing requirements.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
I am reporting AmeriHome Mortgage. On XX/XX/XXXX I sent an email to their customer service department letting them know that I had been making additional payments to my escrow account and my principal each month. On XX/XX/XXXX I received an automated message saying they had received my email and would be reaching out. On XXXX I received a response saying that they needed the extra amounts I had paid and that I needed to specify where they needed to be applied. I responded by sending a screenshot of their website where when you make a payment you have the option to add additional to your escrow and principal. I then asked how I should further specify, as this is their literal website. I didn't receive another response until I reached out again on XX/XX/XXXXXXXX  requesting further assistance. I was then assigned a case owner, XXXX. I sent this person an email detailing the previous information and inquiring to why the payments were not applied properly. This person responded on XX/XX/XXXX saying they would look into it and would get with their case department to apply the payments. I would told they would confirm with me once that was done. I have not heard back. 

Fast forward to today, XX/XX/XXXX. I received an email from AmeriHome Mortgage saying that I was behind on my payments for 2 months and I have been sent to collections. As this shocked me, because I have never missed a payment, nor have I ever been late on a payment, so I called them. I told the lady that I don't understand why I was sent to collections, as I have never missed a payment. She informed me that they had the money that I paid, but it was not applied to my mortgage. I asked why, and explained my frustration, as I am talking to the debt department. She did not have an answer. She said she can just apply the payments and my account will be up to date. I told her that would be great but I needed to speak with someone to explain why this happened and how to avoid it in the future. She just kept saying that my money had been applied and my next payment is not due until XX/XX/XXXX. I told her I understand that but I need an explanation of why this happened. Can you imagine the distrust I now have? Also, it is poor customer service to not have an answer, and also not transfer me to someone who does. If this is how you do business, I do not want to be a part of it. 

Obviously, as someone who pays on time and takes pride in that, I was very stressed out. I have never been sent to collections and to find out that the mortgage company is not doing what they are supposed to do, and then they sent me to collections is outrageous. I don't understand how I am expected to pay on time, by the correct means, but then THEY messed it up and and then penalize me. I am beyond frustrated. 

Not only are they not applying my additional payments correctly, they messed up my last 2 months payments completely. Now I have to wait 3-5 business days to see if the changes she made today actually show up. On top of that I never heard back from my case owner. I am just so stressed. This is unacceptable.

We ended the call with the agent telling me to just call each time I make a payment so that I can ensure it it dispersed properly. That does not sound like something the customer should have to do, if you have the option to pay online. It seems like this is something the mortgage company should handle on their end. 

I have spent 2 hours of my day on the phone trying to figure this out and I am irritated beyond belief. I would never recommend anyone doing business with them.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
I am writing to file a formal complaint against Sallie Mae Bank regarding their handling of XXXX private student loans for which I am a co-signer. Sallie Mae has refused to offer any meaningful relief options and is threatening to default all XXXX  loans on XX/XX/year>, less than 30 days before the primary borrower is scheduled to graduate in XX/XX/year>. I am requesting that the CFPB investigate this matter urgently given the imminent default date. 

Under the terms of these loans, I am required to make interest-only payments while the primary borrower remains enrolled in school. I have been the co-signer responsible for these payments throughout the student 's enrollment. 

Due to unemployment, I have been unable to make the required in-school interest payments. I previously utilized the full XXXX forbearance allowance permitted under these loans. I am currently XXXX  days past due on the in-school interest payments across all XXXX  loans. 

I have contacted Sallie Mae on multiple occasions requesting relief options, including but not limited to : -Rolling the past-due interest into the principal balance of each loan ; -An in-school deferment based on the student 's active enrollment status ; -A modified repayment plan or temporary reduced payment arrangement ; -Any other hardship accommodation available to co-signers or borrowers. 

Sallie Mae has refused all of these requests and has offered no viable alternative. 

I wish to bring to the CFPB 's attention a particularly troubling pattern in Sallie Mae 's communications. Sallie Mae has sent me written letters stating that options are available to me to resolve my past-due balance. However, when I contact Sallie Mae by phone to inquire about and pursue those options, their representatives inform me that no options are available or offered to me whatsoever. 

This contradiction between Sallie Mae 's written communications and what their phone representatives actually provide constitutes deceptive and misleading conduct. I am being told in writing that help exists, and then denied any such help when I attempt to access it. This pattern has caused significant confusion, delay, and harm, as I have been unable to obtain the relief that Sallie Mae 's own letters suggest is available. 

The primary borrower is actively enrolled in school and is scheduled to graduate in XX/XX/year>, approximately 30 days away. Sallie Mae is choosing to default XXXX loans and permanently damage the credit of both the primary borrower and myself at the very moment the borrower is on the verge of graduation and workforce entry. This outcome serves no legitimate purpose and causes severe and lasting financial harm to both parties. 

Furthermore, the past-due balance consists entirely of in-school interest payments, not principal, accrued during a period of documented unemployment. Sallie Mae 's refusal to capitalize a total of {$2100.00} in past-due interest or offer any interim solution reflects an unreasonable and predatory servicing practice given how close the borrower is to graduation. 

I respectfully request that the CFPB : 1.Investigate Sallie Mae 's refusal to offer any relief options to a co-signer facing unemployment while the primary borrower remains actively enrolled in school ; 2.Investigate the contradiction between Sallie Mae 's written communications , which reference available options, and their phone representatives ' consistent refusal to provide any such options ; 3.Require Sallie Mae to pause the threatened default while this complaint is under review ; 4.Require Sallie Mae to offer meaningful hardship accommodations, including capitalization of past-due interest, in-school deferment, or a modified repayment plan ; 5.Review Sallie Mae 's general practices regarding co-signers and in-school borrowers who exhaust forbearance options XXXX 

I am available to provide any additional documentation, account information, or statements needed to support this complaint, including copies of the written letters received from Sallie Mae referencing available options. Given the urgency of the impending default, I respectfully request that this matter be reviewed as quickly as possible. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.
label: loan_mortgage
source_sub_issue: Can't get other flexible options for repaying your loan

text:
Issue Summary : Bank of America failed to provide a complete loan servicing history from origination ( XXXX ) and responded to a formal RESPA Request for Information by reissuing the same incomplete loan history ( beginning XXXX ) without explanation or acknowledgment of the missing XXXX through XXXX period. 

Note : Loan originated XXXX ; Bank of America history begins XXXX ( gap : XXXX through XXXX ). 

Complaint Narrative : This complaint concerns Bank of America, N.A.s failure to provide a complete loan servicing history and its failure to provide a compliant response to a Request for Information under 12 C.F.R. 1024.36.

The mortgage loan originated in XX/XX/XXXX. A request was made for a complete life-of-loan transaction history. Bank of America provided a detailed account history beginning in or around XX/XX/XXXX, omitting the period from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX. 
After identifying this gap, I contacted Bank of America by phone. Representatives stated that the missing history related to a prior servicer and directed me to contact XXXX XXXX. However, Bank of America did not identify the prior servicer of record, did not provide servicing transfer details, and did not provide any documentation regarding the transfer of servicing records. During a follow-up call, a manager call-back was requested ; no manager returned that call. Due to the lack of resolution through normal servicing channels, a formal written Request for Information was submitted on XXXX pursuant to 12 C.F.R. 1024.36. 
The XXXX Request for Information sought the following : A complete life-of-loan transaction history from origination Identification of prior servicers and servicing transfer dates Servicing transfer and boarding documentation A written explanation if any records were not available On or about XXXX, Bank of America issued a response consisting of an Account Detail / Loan History statement. This document is substantively identical to the prior statement and continues to begin in XX/XX/XXXX. No additional information or explanation was provided. 
Bank of Americas response did not : Address the missing XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX servicing period Provide any explanation that the requested records are unavailable Identify prior servicers or servicing transfer details Provide servicing transfer or boarding documentation Respond to the specific categories of information requested Reissuing the same incomplete dataset without explanation does not constitute a compliant response under 12 C.F.R. 1024.36.

Based on the foregoing, Bank of America has either failed to conduct a reasonable search for responsive information or failed to provide the required written explanation regarding the unavailability of such information.

A second Request for Information and Notice of Error has been submitted. As of this complaint, Bank of America has not provided a complete servicing history or a compliant written explanation regarding the missing records.

Requested Resolution : I request that Bank of America : Provide a complete life-of-loan transaction history from XX/XX/XXXX through present, including the missing XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX period, or Provide a written, specific explanation identifying : The exact date ranges and categories of missing records The reason those records are not available The efforts undertaken to obtain those records The identity of the prior servicer and the effective servicing transfer date Documentation of what loan data was received upon servicing transfer I also request that Bank of America provide a response that complies with its obligations under 12 C.F.R. 1024.36.

Documents Attached ( in chronological order ) : 1. XXXX Bank of America Account Detail / Loan History ( initial incomplete history ; begins XXXX ) XXXX. XXXX RESPA Request for Information 3. XXXX Bank of America Response ( reissued identical incomplete history ; no substantive response ) 4. XXXX Second Request for Information / Notice of Error DVW Loan No. ( redacted )

text:
I am filing a complaint regarding a personal installment loan that was approved and reoriginated but never funded. 

Timeline : - XX/XX/year>XXXX  : Loan approved - XX/XX/year>XXXX  : Initial origination date - XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX I received notifications that funds were not delivered. I contacted customer service and was instructed to submit a voided check. I submitted the voided check on XX/XX/XXXX. 
- XX/XX/year>XXXX  : My bank account was verified, and the loan was reoriginated. I was informed that funds would be delivered within 13 business days. 
- XX/XX/year>XXXX  : Funds were still not received. I contacted the company and a funding investigation was opened.

text:
What happened : I will turn XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. As my retirement comes close, this hit to my plans is significant. In order to ready my condo for sale, I had to take a XXXX loan and liquidate a XXXX IRA to fund it. Had I not done those upgrades the market price of my condo would have barely covered the XXXXXXXX XXXX settlement request. 
In XXXX, I entered into a Home Equity Investment agreement with Hometap Equity Partners , LLC after multiple conversations with their agent, XXXX XXXX. I was explicit from the outset that my sole objective was to protect my only significant asset my condominium and that I needed this to be a sound long-term financial decision. XXXX XXXX was fully aware that my property had experienced above-average equity appreciation during the pandemic-era sellers ' market. Rather than disclosing how that appreciation would dramatically increase my ultimate repayment obligation, he used it as a selling point. He specifically asked whether I planned to remain in my home for at least ten years, and assured me that my equity position would only grow stronger over time. At no point was I given a plain-language explanation of how the payoff calculation actually works, or that rising home values would directly and dramatically increase Hometap 's claim against my equity at settlement. 
When I sold my property on XX/XX/XXXX, Hometap demanded a payoff of approximately double the amount they originally advanced to me. This figure was presented through an opaque alphanumeric formula with no clear breakdown. Throughout XXXX and XXXX, I made repeated good-faith efforts to obtain a transparent explanation of the calculation from my assigned representative, XXXX. These requests were met with rigid, non-responsive replies and no meaningful attempt to negotiate or clarify. 
In XX/XX/XXXX, I filed a formal complaint with the Massachusetts Attorney General 's Office ( Complaint No. XXXX ). The AG 's office retained my complaint but advised me to seek private relief due to jurisdictional limitations. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent Hometap a formal certified demand letter addressed to General Counsel XXXX XXXX at their headquarters, XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX, MA XXXX. I requested a transparent recalculation, a written explanation of all components of the payoff formula, and a fair settlement. As of XX/XX/XXXX 14 days after delivery Hometap has not responded in any form.

I am a Virginia resident. The property was located in North Carolina. Hometap is headquartered in Massachusetts. I am seeking federal intervention and a full accounting of the payoff calculation, along with a fair resolution. 
What would be a fair resolution : A transparent, itemized recalculation of the payoff amount based strictly on the terms of the XXXX agreement, and a reasonable settlement that reflects the actual appreciation of the property rather than an inflated formula never disclosed to me at signing.

text:
I'm submitting this complaint regarding the servicing and origination of my private student loan, which has resulted in significant and ongoing financial harm. 

In XX/XX/year>, I refinanced approximately {$150000.00} in federal student loans into a private loan. At the time, this decision was made based on guidance and positioning that refinancing would be a beneficial financial move. However, this resulted in the loss of critical federal protections, including income-driven repayment options and loan forgiveness programs.

Following this refinance, my loan was serviced in a manner that materially worsened my financial position.

Over the past several years, I made substantial payments toward my loan. To date, I have paid approximately {$49000.00} in total. However, despite these payments, only {$9600.00} has been applied to principal, while approximately {$39000.00} has gone toward interest. My current balance is now approximately {$150000.00}, which exceeds my original loan amount.

This outcome is deeply concerning. Despite consistent efforts to manage my debt responsibly, my principal balance has barely decreased, and in fact has increased over time.

A key driver of this outcome was my repeated placement into forbearance programs. During these periods : - Interest continued to accrue - Accrued interest was capitalized into my principal- My balance grew significantly despite prior payments At no point was I clearly guided toward a structured repayment strategy that would meaningfully reduce my principal. Instead, forbearance was used in a way that caused avoidable balance growth.

I believe this reflects a pattern of harmful servicing decisions. Specifically : - I was not adequately guided on the long-term consequences of repeated forbearance - I was not presented with viable alternatives that could have reduced principal - Servicing actions prioritized short-term payment relief over long-term financial impact ( I have evidence of all of the above ) Additionally, when viewed in combination with the initial refinance, this situation is even more concerning. I was first placed into a private loan product that removed key borrower protections, and then subsequently serviced in a way that amplified my financial burden.

The result has been not only financial harm, but also significant XXXX  and XXXX XXXX which has led to serious XXXX XXXX XXXX ( evidence of hospitalization and XXXX XXXX XXXX damage ). Making tens of XXXX XXXX XXXX in payments while watching my balance stagnate or grow has been extremely discouraging and destabilizing. It has impacted my financial planning, sense of progress, and overall well-being.

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XX/XX/year> Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) Subject : Urgent Complaint Wrongful Foreclosure, Servicing Errors, and Loss Mitigation Violations Loan Servicer : Onity Mortgage Corporation ( formerly PHH Mortgage Corporation ) Property Address : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX To Whom It May Concern, We are submitting this complaint to report urgent and serious errors in the servicing of our mortgage loan, which have resulted in our home being placed into foreclosure despite our active efforts to comply with all requirements.

text:
This complaint is a formal follow-up to XXXX XXXX XXXX. I am filing a new complaint because Chase Banks previous response was non-responsive to my specific technical concerns and provided an inaccurate assessment of the fraudulent activity on my account. 
I am requesting a secondary review of the following points which the bank failed to address : Failure to Address Data Compromise : The card in question has never been physically or digitally used. It has been secured in a drawer since it was issued. Chase failed to explain how this card information was compromised. I am specifically requesting Chase to disclose if this account was impacted by any known data breach such as the documented technical bugs and internal data exposures reported by JPMorgan Chase in early XXXX, which allowed customer data to be accessed by unauthorized parties. 
Inaccurate Assessment of Unusual Activity : In their response to my previous case, Chase claimed there was no unusual activity because there were no failed PIN attempts. This is a non-responsive answer. Chase states that their research did not identify any unusual activity because there were no failed PIN attempts or high dollar transactions. This is an admission that their fraud detection is fundamentally flawed. Unusual activity is not defined solely by PIN attempts ; it is defined by impossible consumer behavior. The unusual activity is the " velocity '' of the charges especially on an account with XXXX activity from that card. For XXXX consecutive months, this account was hit with XXXX identical transactions of {$17.00} at the exact same second on the XXXX of the month. This is the industry-standard definition of automated fraud. The fact that Chases systems allowed XXXX identical transactions to process on a zero-use card without a single alert is a failure of their fraud monitoring tools, not a lack of consumer reporting. 
Failure of Monitoring Tools : Chase states they use fraud monitoring tools but that " some transactions may not require review. '' I am contesting that 6 simultaneous, identical charges on an unused card should always require review. I am seeking a technical explanation as to why their " identical transaction '' triggers were not active on this account between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. 

I have included the reply that I received from chase. Their reply was focused on the steps that were taken after I discovered the fraud. It does not address the questions stated above. 


" Thank you for expressing concerns to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. We appreciate the opportunity to address concerns. Our review is complete, and the details of our response are below. Our records show we received claim XXXX totaling {$330.00} for transactions with XXXX * XXXX XXXX, which was debited from your account ending in XXXX between XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. We issued a temporary credit of {$330.00} to your account on XX/XX/XXXX, while we complete our research. The claim remains in process and we are awaiting the merchants response. We expect to resolve the dispute by XX/XX/XXXX. We partially denied the claim and reversed a portion of the provisional credit because the merchant issued a credit of {$17.00} to you. This credit was also disputed under claim XXXX. You filed claim XXXX for {$17.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, for a merchant credit that posted to your account on XX/XX/XXXX. We processed a final debit on XX/XX/XXXX, as you requested. You refiled claim XXXX under claim XXXX for related transaction activity. We issued a final credit of {$17.00} to your account on XX/XX/XXXX, because the merchant credit was debited and the dispute was not possible with the merchant issuing that credit. We have enclosed a copy of the claim approval letter we sent on XX/XX/XXXX, for your records. 

Our research did not identify any unusual online activity or alerts in the system for the transactions in question. We did not generate any alerts because there were no failed PIN attempts, or high dollar transactions. We respectfully decline your allegations about our fraud monitoring practices. We use fraud monitoring tools to review account transactions ; however, some transactions XXXX not require review. Our policy is to recommend that customers protect their personal and account information, monitor account statements, and promptly report any unusual activity. 

The compromise of personal or account information XXXX occur to anyone, anywhere, at any time. We can not guarantee you will not be a victim of fraudulent activity. 

If you would like to discuss additional alerts or limitations for your account ending in XXXX, please contact our customer service team for available resources ''
label: loan_mortgage
source_issue: Problem with a lender or other company charging your account

text:
I am submitting this complaint regarding issues I have experienced with my mortgage servicer during an ongoing foreclosure situation. 

I have made multiple good faith efforts to resolve my mortgage delinquency and avoid foreclosure, including applying for loan modifications prior to entering foreclosure. My initial application for a modification was denied because I was told I was not far enough behind on my mortgage. Following that, I continued to apply for additional modifications ; however, each request was denied. 

As I continued to pursue assistance, I was eventually informed that there were too many modification applications in the system. I was working with a free mortgage counselor at the time, and based on guidance I received, it was suggested that all prior modification applications be removed so that I could reapply properly. 

At the beginning of the year, I was told that the prior applications would be deleted to allow for a fresh submission. However, after that occurred, I was then informed that no additional modification applications would be accepted. As a result, I was left without any opportunity to pursue a modification and have now been placed into foreclosure. 

In addition, I submitted a request for foreclosure mediation ; however, my application was denied due to allegedly missing or incomplete information. I was not clearly informed of what specific documents were missing, which has made it difficult to correct the issue and move forward. 

I attempted to contact the appropriate office for clarification and received multiple responses that did not contain any useful information or guidance. This lack of communication has delayed my ability to properly resubmit my application and pursue mediation in good faith. 

When attempting to correct and resubmit my application, I also encountered technical issues with the system. I received repeated error messages stating that I must select a valid transaction ID from the list using the down arrow, despite entering multiple valid transaction numbers related to my payment. This prevented me from completing the submission process. 

During this time, I have also experienced significant financial hardship, including unexpected property damage caused by a fallen tree, which resulted in major roof damage and required insurance involvement and emergency repairs. 

Throughout this entire process, I have made consistent good faith efforts to resolve my financial situation, apply for assistance, and avoid foreclosure. However, I have been denied opportunities to obtain a modification, provided with inconsistent and unclear information, and blocked by technical and administrative issues. 

I am requesting the following : A full review of my loan modification history and an explanation as to why my applications were not accepted or processed appropriately. 
A fair opportunity to submit a new loan modification application for proper review.

Assistance in resolving the technical issues preventing submission of my mediation application.

Clear identification of any missing or incomplete documents required for my mediation application.

Consideration of alternatives to foreclosure based on my demonstrated good faith efforts to resolve this matter.

I am committed to resolving this situation and avoiding foreclosure if possible, and I am requesting timely and clear assistance so that I may proceed.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
b"I am filing against Capital One, N.A. for failure to conduct a substantive investigation of a Regulation E billing error notice. My position is not that Capital One failed to respond within the regulatory window. It is that Capital One's response reveals the absence of the investigation the regulation requires.

On XX/XX/2026, I delivered a formal written Reg E billing error notice to Capital One regarding Account ending XXXX. It was received XX/XX/2026 via XXXX tracking XXXX XXXX XXXX. The notice identified a net disputed amount of $XXXX (XXXX unauthorized XXXX XXXX  debits totaling $XXXX, plus XXXX unauthorized XXXX XXXX-network transactions totaling $XXXX, less $XXXX in XXXX-initiated credits). It included the full transaction inventory, legal basis (XXXX XXXX data breach of XX/XX/2026; XXXX  unauthorized charges; XXXX XXXX structural deficiencies), and requested remedies.

On XX/XX/2026, twelve calendar days after receipt, Capital One opened four fragmentary claims, each demanding a separate Written Statement of Unauthorized Debit form within ten business days:

Claim XXXX: $XXXX Claim XXXX: $XXXX  Claim XXXX: $XXXX Claim XXXX: $XXXX

These four claims sum to $XXXX. My letter disputed $XXXX. The variance of $XXXX is numerically identical to the $XXXX in XXXX-initiated credits disclosed in my letter. Capital One's scoping extracted that detail but did not arrive at my stated total. The letter was in the file but not read for substance.

Capital One also issued one partial provisional credit of $XXXX (XXXX line items, $XXXX to $XXXX). That is approximately 12% of the disputed amount. No reconciliation, claim cross-reference, or  1005.11(d) completion notice accompanied it. It does not satisfy 12 C.F.R.  1005.11(c)(2).

The four-claim split cannot be reconciled to my letter. The demand that I now re-produce on four parallel forms the information the letter already contained is not an investigation step. It is a mechanism by which Capital One's own failure to read the notice becomes an additional consumer burden.

I am also concerned this pattern  Reg E notice ignored, phone call required, phone call redirected to additional written submission duplicating the original  is consistent across multiple institutions (XXXX, XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX). It warrants Bureau attention as a potential industry practice of regulatory posture without substantive review.

Related CFPB IDs: XXXX, XXXX, XXXX. FDIC cases: XXXX, XXXX."
label: loan_mortgage
source_issue: Problem with a lender or other company charging your account

text:
PenFed Credit Union received a XXXX XXXX check in the amount of {$2500.00} submitted for application toward a PenFed auto loan. The check was submitted with written instructions for application of the funds toward the loan.

PenFed stated in writing that it considered the check stale dated, intended to void it, and would forward it for shredding. Less than an hour later, PenFed representative XXXX stated by phone that the check had already been shredded and that PenFed would not correct the issue or take steps to obtain a replacement check. 

This complaint concerns PenFeds destruction of a negotiable instrument received for payment processing. It does not concern any demand that PenFed guarantee payment before the check cleared. The proper payment-processing issue was presentment to the appropriate payor or drawee institution for a payment decision, not unilateral destruction by PenFed. 

XXXX XXXX advised that the check remained valid and did not expire. Under Texas XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, a check more than XXXX months old is not automatically void. The statute provides that a bank is not obligated to pay a stale-dated check, but may still pay it in good faith. PenFed therefore appears to have treated a stale-dated instrument as void when the governing rule does not make the instrument void. 

Texas XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX addresses presentment as a demand for payment made to the drawee or party obligated to pay the instrument. By destroying the check instead of routing or presenting it for a payment decision, PenFed prevented ordinary presentment and deprived the payor or drawee side of the opportunity to decide whether the instrument would be paid or dishonored. 

Texas XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX recognizes that conversion principles apply to negotiable instruments. PenFeds destruction of the check raises a compliance concern because PenFed took possession of a negotiable instrument submitted for payment processing and then destroyed it rather than preserving, returning, routing, or presenting it.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
I am filing a complaint regarding the servicing of my student loans by XXXX XXXX XXXX servicing related to unauthorized repayment plan changes, involuntary forbearances, autopay failures, lack of communication, and resulting financial harm. 

I had an active autopay arrangement established for the XXXX of each month, and my payments had consistently been approximately {$710.00} per month. I did not request any repayment plan changes, pauses, or forbearances. 

In XXXX, the servicer experienced what they described as a system error that changed repayment plans for multiple borrowers. During my communications with the servicer, representatives acknowledged : my repayment plan was changed due to a system/system processing error, my XXXX payment was skipped because the account was placed into forbearance, my repayment plan was recalculated multiple times, my XXXX payment was also skipped due to additional corrective actions, and these actions were taken without prior communication or authorization from me. 

The servicer further acknowledged that they failed to notify me of these issues before my payments were interrupted and repayment terms altered. 

As a result of these servicing errors : accrued interest increased significantly, my payment allocation changed so that more of my monthly payment is now being applied toward accrued interest instead of principal, my repayment progress was negatively impacted, and my monthly payment amount changed multiple times without my authorization. 

Although the servicer admits the repayment disruptions and forbearances were caused by their own system error, they have refused to provide any remediation, interest adjustment, or account correction. Their position is that interest continued accruing during the involuntary forbearance periods and that no relief will be provided, despite the fact that the interruptions were entirely outside of my control. 

I believe I have been financially harmed by the servicers errors and lack of communication. Had the autopay process and repayment plan remained unchanged as originally agreed, my payments would have continued being applied normally and my accrued interest/principal balance trajectory would not have been adversely affected. 

I am requesting : A full review of my account servicing history ; A written accounting of all repayment plan changes, forbearances, skipped payments, and accrued interest during the affected time period ; Review for reversal or adjustment of interest/accrual impacts caused by the servicing errors and involuntary payment interruptions ; Confirmation that my repayment plan and autopay settings are restored correctly ; Assurance that negative servicing consequences resulting from this error will not continue to affect my account. 

I have screenshots/documentation of my communications with the servicer acknowledging the system error, repayment plan changes, skipped payments, and lack of prior communication.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
I am filing a complaint regarding unauthorized transactions on my Wells Fargo debit card account and the banks refusal to properly investigate or reopen my fraud claim. 

I requested a replacement debit card with a new card number. While waiting for the physical card to arrive in the mail, I was able to access the card digitally through my phone wallet. However, the physical debit card was stolen from my mailbox before I ever received it. 

The individual who stole the card was somehow able to activate the physical card and use it to make unauthorized contactless ( tap ) purchases at a mall near my area. These transactions were not made by me, and I never had possession of the physical card. 

The unauthorized transactions posted to my account on XX/XX/year> included : {$210.00} {$25.00} {$100.00} There was also an attempted transaction that showed as {$0.00}, which triggered a fraud alert email from the bank, as well as another attempted purchase for {$370.00}. 

I immediately reported the fraud to Wells Fargo and explained that I never received the physical card. Despite this, my claim was denied. I was later instructed to file a police report in order to have the claim reconsidered. After submitting the police report, I was informed that there was not enough information to reopen the investigation. 

I believe Wells Fargo failed to conduct a reasonable investigation into this matter. The transactions were clearly unauthorized and occurred after my replacement card was stolen in transit. The fact that the transactions were contactless purchases confirms that someone else had physical possession of the card. 

I am requesting that Wells Fargo : Reopen and properly investigate this fraud claim Provide documentation explaining how the card was activated and why the claim was denied Reimburse the stolen funds associated with the unauthorized transactions Review any available merchant, activation, or device information connected to the fraudulent activity I am also requesting assistance from the CFPB in ensuring that my rights under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act are properly upheld.
label: loan_mortgage
source_issue: Problem with a lender or other company charging your account

text:
I am filing this complaint regarding Sloan Servicings failure to properly process my Income-Based Repayment recertification despite my timely compliance with all document requests and direct confirmation from a Sloan representative that my application was complete. 
-- On XX/XX/XXXX, I submitted my IBR recertification application and tax documents. 
-- On XX/XX/XXXX, I received an email from Sloan Servicing requesting a prior tax document that included my name, Social Security Number and Sloan account number written on each page. That same day, I promptly uploaded the updated documents exactly as instructed. 
-- On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Sloan Servicing to confirm that the updated documents satisfied their requirements. During that call, the Sloan representative specifically confirmed that they had everything needed to process my IBR recertification and stated that nothing else is required. The representative also calculated and discussed my anticipated new payment amount under IBR. Based on these representations, I reasonably believed my IBR recertification had been successfully completed and was being processed appropriately.

-- However, the next communication I received from Sloan Servicing was dated XX/XX/XXXX, informing me that my IBR had expired. Sloan then placed me into the Standard Repayment Plan with a monthly payment of {$1100.00} beginning XX/XX/XXXX. 
Additionally, Sloan capitalized {$8600.00} in accrued interest as a direct result of their failure to properly process my recertification despite my timely compliance and their explicit confirmation that no further action was required from me. 
-- On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Sloan Servicing and explained that I had been explicitly told my documents were sufficient and therefore had no reason to believe my IBR recertification would expire. During that call, I was informed that additional pages from my tax document were allegedly needed. I uploaded those documents while still on the phone with the representative. A complaint was then escalated to higher management for review of my case about retroactively applying the IBR payment amount for XXXX as well as reversal of interest capitalization. 
-- On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a letter from Sloan Servicing with my new IBR payment amount, which is scheduled to begin XX/XX/XXXX. This confirms that I was in fact eligible for continued IBR and that the issue stemmed from Sloan Servicings handling and processing of my recertification documentation. 
-- On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Sloan Servicing again because the Standard Repayment amount of {$1100.00} was still showing due for XX/XX/XXXX. I also requested an update regarding the complaint previously submitted to higher management. An account specialist later returned my call and stated that Sloan Servicing would not retroactively apply my approved IBR payment amount and would not reverse the {$8600.00} interest capitalization, despite the fact that I timely submitted all requested documentation and relied on Sloans explicit assurances that nothing further was required from me. No explanation was provided for why these corrections could not be made, despite the servicing error originating with Sloan.

Instead, Sloan Servicing placed my account into a one-month hardship forbearance for XX/XX/XXXX. This action creates additional financial harm because interest will continue accruing and may further capitalize as a result of a situation caused entirely by Sloan Servicings failure to properly process my recertification.

I fulfilled all requested requirements on time and relied on Sloan Servicings representatives that my documentation was complete. Sloan Servicing ultimately approved my IBR application, further confirming that the issue was not borrower ineligibility, but rather Sloans mishandling and delayed processing of my recertification.
label: loan_mortgage
source_issue: Dealing with your lender or servicer
source_sub_issue: Trouble with how payments are being handled

text:
My federal student loans were originally serviced by Great Lakes. At some point, Nelnet took over as my loan servicer. After the transfer, I discovered that Nelnet had split what was previously a single consolidated loan into multiple smaller loans.

This restructuring caused my account to accrue significantly more interest than it should have. Instead of one loan balance with one interest calculation, my account now shows several smaller loans each accumulating separate interest. I did not request this restructuring, and I do not agree with the excess interest charges that resulted from it.

When I contacted Nelnet by phone to ask why my loan was divided and why interest increased, the representative could not provide a clear explanation. No corrective action was taken.

I requested a complete payment history, a breakdown of interest calculations on each split loan, my original promissory note, and documentation justifying the restructuring. Nelnet did not provide the information needed to resolve this issue.

I want the excess interest caused by the improper splitting removed from my account. I want my loan restored to its correct status as it was before Nelnet restructured it. I also want a complete accounting of how every payment has been applied since Nelnet took over servicing.

I am aware that Nelnet has been subject to lawsuits and federal audits regarding improper loan servicing practices, including manipulating loan structures to generate excess interest and subsidies. My experience appears consistent with this pattern of conduct.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
I am filing a formal complaint regarding SoFi Bank, N.A. 's continued delivery of mail and packages to an address I do not reside at, despite having provided my correct address on file.

The individual who resides at the incorrect address has contacted me no fewer than five times this week alone regarding mail and packages sent by SoFi being delivered to their home. This person has taken it upon themselves to open several pieces of mail in order to locate a contact number and has independently reached out to multiple senders and carriers to stop the misdeliveries, with success in several cases. SoFi is among the senders who have failed to correct this.

This situation is unacceptable for the following reasons : 1. I provided SoFi with my correct address. There is no reason my correspondence should be routed elsewhere.

2. A third party, someone I do not know, is being repeatedly contacted and burdened by mail and packages addressed to me that SoFi continues to send to the wrong address. This constitutes harassment of an uninvolved individual.

3. I am being repeatedly contacted by this person, and they most likely have personal information as a direct result of SoFi 's failure to maintain accurate address records and act on prior notice.

I have informed the individual at that address that they are within their rights to file complaints with every carrier and the State Attorney General 's office regarding SoFi Bank, N.A. 's continued delivery of unwanted correspondence to their home. I intend to do the same, so there is documentation of all incidents related to this matter, including dates and the number of contacts I have received from the third party.

I want to make the full scope of this situation clear. The individual residing at the incorrect address made repeated good-faith efforts to stop this returning mail, notifying SoFi directly and contacting carriers. Despite those efforts, SoFi continued sending correspondence to that address. It was only after being repeatedly ignored that this individual began opening the mail in an attempt to find contact information to resolve the issue themselves. text:
I am a long-term customer of Lower, XXXX with XXXX history of on-time payments. My account was set up for XXXX. In XX/XX/year>, the XXXX failed due to an oversight in my bank balance. 

Lower, XXXX failed to provide 'clear and conspicuous ' notice of this delinquency as required by Regulation Z ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) and RESPA XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ). While the company claims an email was sent, the communication had a generic headline that's identical to what Lower sends to me every month, not stating clearly a payment failure had occurred, so I did not open it. Nor did I receive a formal letter via mail. 

As a result, I was unaware of the failure until XXXX, at which point Lower attempted to pull a 'double payment ' of over {$5000.00}, and reporting me to the Credit bureaus, dropping my XXXX XXXX a full XXXX points. This credit drop notification was what finally alerted me to the overdue payment. I immediately remedied the full balance. Despite my immediate correction and perfect prior history, Lower reported a XXXX delinquency to the credit bureaus, and is placing the fault fully on me. This is causing significant financial harm as I am currently in the process of selling my home, as well as moving out of the country. 

I am asserting a Notice of XXXX. I request that Lower, XXXX retract this derogatory reporting to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX immediately, as the delinquency was prolonged only by the servicer 's failure to provide adequate, noticeable disclosure of the failed transaction. 

Furthermore, I demand that Lower, XXXX overhaul their notification procedures for failed payments. Using generic, marketing-style headers for critical financial defaults is a deceptive practice that prevents consumers from taking timely action. I request that Lower implement clear, urgent subject lines ( e.g., 'URGENT : Payment Failure Notice ' ) and ensure that physical mail notifications are sent via a verifiable method. A XXXX credit drop is a life-altering event ; a lender has a professional and ethical obligation to ensure the consumer is actually notified before reporting such devastating data to the bureaus.

text:
In XX/XX/XXXX I received written confirmation from USAA Federal Savings Bank stating explicitly : 'No interest will accrue during the extension period ' on my recreational vehicle consumer loan. My complete payment history obtained directly from USAA proves interest accrued anyway. 
The following payments were entirely consumed by interest with XXXX dollars applied to principal : XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} XX/XX/XXXX {$780.00} Total payments entirely consumed by interest : {$10000.00} Over 6.5 years I have paid approximately {$41000.00} in total interest on a {$75000.00} loan. Despite making 78 payments totaling approximately {$53000.00} my current balance remains {$62000.00} only {$12000.00} less than the original loan amount. 
I consistently make payments 19-21 days before the due date. I have never made a late payment in 6.5 years. 
Each deferment was presented as moving a payment to the end of the loan. I was never clearly disclosed that entire subsequent payments would be consumed by accrued interest. USAA 's own second deferment email contradicts the first by suddenly including detailed interest accrual language that was completely absent from the first deferment promise. 
When I escalated to USAA 's Executive Resolution office they reviewed the account and stated everything was correct because it is a daily simple interest loan. USAA 's original written promise of no interest accrual contained no such exception or qualification. 
I am a 100 % service connected XXXX veteran on fixed VA XXXX income. This vehicle is my primary asset. I am requesting a full investigation into USAA 's deferment practices, the application of payments on this account, and whether USAA 's written promise of no interest accrual constitutes a binding commitment that was violated. 
USAA claims their disclosure states one or more subsequent payments may be consumed by interest. However their first deferment confirmation dated XX/XX/XXXX contains no such disclosure whatsoever. It states only 'No interest will accrue during the extension period. ' The disclosure language USAA references appears for the first time in their second deferment email dated XX/XX/XXXX after the first deferment breach had already occurred. The absence of this disclosure in the first deferment email while present in all subsequent ones demonstrates USAA was aware the first email 's promise created legal exposure and modified their language accordingly. 
USAA 's first deferment email contains one disclosure regarding future payment impact relating exclusively to Debt Protection fees. USAA has confirmed I am not enrolled in Debt Protection. Therefore that disclosure did not apply to my loan. The first deferment email contains no applicable disclosure whatsoever regarding subsequent payments being consumed by interest. The only statement applicable to my loan regarding interest was the unqualified promise that no interest would accrue. 
I have call recordings of conversations with USAA representatives that are relevant to this complaint. The files are too large to upload through this portal. I am prepared to provide these recordings upon request.
label: loan_mortgage

text:
I am filing a formal complaint against my loan servicer, Solar Servicing LLC, for unfair billing practices and a direct violation of the FTCs " Holder in Due Course '' Rule ( XXXX CFR XXXX ). The servicer continues to collect full payments for a residential solar system that is defective, while the original installer has gone out of business, leaving me with a broken system and an abandoned XXXX warranty. 

Schedule of events : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX installed by XXXX XXXX, financed via Mosaic. XXXX included a XXXX production and workmanship warranty. 
Early XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ) solar panels became completely non-operational. System output dropped significantly below contracted levels. 
XX/XX/XXXX Submitted formal warranty service request to XXXX XXXX for the failed panels. 
XX/XX/XXXX Scheduled technician visit. The technician failed to arrive. 
XX/XX/XXXX Discovered XXXX XXXX ceased operations and abandoned all consumer warranties. XXXX Solar Servicing LLC of the dispute. 
XXXX Solar Servicing LLC continues to bill the full loan amount without offering hardship holds, principal adjustments, or dispute resolution. 

Under the XXXX XXXX XXXX " Holder in Due Course '' Rule, any holder of a consumer credit contract is subject to all claims and defenses that the debtor could assert against the original seller. 

Because XXXX XXXX sold me a system with a warranty they are no longer honoring, and because the system is partially inoperable, I have a valid legal defense against the enforcement of the full debt. Solar Servicing LLC is legally responsible for the failures of the seller up to the amount of the loan. By ignoring the system 's failure and the loss of the warranty, Solar Servicing LLC is engaging in unfair loan servicing practices and enforcing a debt that is formally in dispute.

Company : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Account Number : XXXX XXXX  Reference Number : XXXX Prior Collection Reference ( XXXX XXXX ) : XXXX I am responding to the companys prior submission because it did not address the substance of my dispute.

I am submitting this as an update to my existing complaint regarding Bridgecrest. Since my initial filing, the company has issued a written response ( dated XX/XX/year> ) that introduces new claims but continues to fail to provide any supporting documentation or resolve the underlying issues. 

In their response, Bridgecrest now asserts that they had a contractual right to recover collateral because the vehicle was labeled as abandoned. However, they have not identified a single provision in my contract that authorizes this action, nor have they provided any documentation to support the claim that the vehicle was legally classified as abandoned. 

Specifically, Bridgecrest has failed to provide : - The identity of the entity that allegedly designated the vehicle as abandoned- The legal or statutory basis for such a designation - The date of that determination - Any notice provided to me, as required under applicable law Additionally, Bridgecrest continues to mischaracterize the situation by referencing a reinstatement of the account, despite the fact that I was not in default due to nonpayment. At the time of these events, my vehicle had been stolen and was located in an impound facility. I was actively working with my insurance provider to assess the damage and determine next steps. I did not abandon the vehicle, nor did I authorize Bridgecrest to take possession of it. 

Bridgecrest also claims that a sales hold was placed to allow time for my insurance company to inspect the vehicle. This statement is directly contradicted by my insurer, who has confirmed that : - They have not been contacted by Bridgecrest, and- They were never informed of any sales hold to facilitate inspection This raises serious concerns that Bridgecrest has interfered with and potentially obstructed the insurance claims process, while misrepresenting their actions in written correspondence. 

To date, Bridgecrest has also failed to address my requested resolutions, including : - Release of the vehicle to my insurance company - Termination of my contractual obligations given their actions - Cessation of any further payments or interest accrual - Assurance that there will be no negative credit reporting related to this matter - Full written documentation substantiating their authority and actions Despite multiple requests, the company continues to rely on unsupported assertions rather than providing verifiable documentation.

On XX/XX/year>, while I was at work, I checked my bank account before going to lunch and discovered XXXX unauthorized transactions in the amount of {$2700.00} each. The transactions were posted on XX/XX/year> ( Saturday ) without my knowledge, permission, or authorization. 

Immediately after discovering the transactions, I contacted the Bank of America XXXX Department to report the transactions as unauthorized and to report my account/card as compromised. I fully cooperated with the fraud investigation process. 

However, Bank of America later denied my claim, stating that the transactions were authorized. I strongly dispute this conclusion. I never authorized these transactions in any form and did not provide my debit card, PIN, online banking credentials, verification code, or permission to any person or third party. 

The bank has not provided sufficient evidence showing how these transactions were determined to be authorized. I am requesting : XXXX. Documentation and evidence supporting the banks determination that the transactions were authorized. 
XXXX. XXXX under XXXX XXXX for unauthorized electronic fund transfers. 
XXXX. Reimbursement for the unauthorized transactions if the investigation confirms fraud. 

These transactions caused significant financial hardship and concern. I acted promptly upon discovering the fraud and reported it immediately. 

I respectfully request assistance in ensuring that Bank of America complies with its obligations under Regulation XXXX and conducts a reasonable investigation into these unauthorized transactions.
label: loan_mortgage
source_issue: Problem with a lender or other company charging your account
