text:
My loan was transferred from XXXX XXXX to FCC Finance around XX/XX/year>. I received a letter from FCC Finance explaining the transfer and stating that there was nothing I needed to do on my end, and that if my account was set up for automatic payments ( direct debit ), everything would roll over automatically. However, this did not occur.

After the transfer, my account information became unclear and inconsistent between mailed statements, the new online portal, and communications from the company. I later reviewed my bank statements and discovered that payments were not being processed as expected, despite the information provided in the transfer notice. At that point, I began researching the issue to understand what had happened.

As a result of the transfer, my account now shows approximately {$690.00} in past due payments. However, I was not clearly informed that I would need to take additional action or that payments would not successfully transfer as indicated.

I contacted the company to resolve the issue and requested a repayment arrangement that would allow me to continue making my standard monthly payment of {$230.00}. I also requested review and waiver of late fees and additional interest that may have resulted from the servicing transfer period.

The company responded by acknowledging the issue and offering to accept some payments and waive certain fees, but did not provide a clear resolution that allows me to remain current while continuing my regular monthly payment only. No final structured repayment plan or corrected account status was provided.

I am on a fixed income ( XXXX ) and can only afford my regular monthly payment. I am requesting that my account be corrected and managed in a way that reflects a sustainable and accurate payment arrangement.
label: loan_mortgage
source_product: Payday loan, title loan, personal loan, or advance loan

text:
I am requesting review of LoanCares mortgage servicing communication practices related to my ongoing mortgage assistance application. My concern is not a single isolated error, but a pattern of inconsistent, delayed, and unclear communication regarding required documentation and application status, which has made it difficult to understand and comply with LoanCares requirements.

I am seeking improved consistency, clarity, and timely communication regarding my mortgage assistance review process.

Since initiating mortgage assistance review due to my forbearance period nearing its end, I have experienced repeated issues with LoanCares communication process, including : Delayed visibility of critical notices in the borrower portal Inconsistent and sometimes conflicting document requirements Requests for documents already submitted Lack of clear, consolidated instructions regarding what is required to complete my application No consistent single point of contact for my case Difficulty obtaining a clear and stable list of outstanding requirements despite multiple attempts to clarify Specific Examples : Delayed Notice Visibility A document dated XX/XX/year> was not made available to me in the portal until XX/XX/year>. This notice included a response deadline of XX/XX/year>, effectively reducing the time I had to review and respond after becoming aware of it.

Conflicting Document Requirements Portal notices and communications have requested tax returns ; however, I was later informed by a LoanCare representative that tax returns were not required because IRS XXXX XXXX had already been submitted.

Inconsistent Guidance from Representatives During multiple phone conversations, I have received differing explanations regarding : What documents are still required Whether previously submitted documents were received and accepted Whether a complete application had already been submitted In one instance, I was informed that only paystubs and correction of XXXX  XXXX were needed, which contradicted earlier portal notices listing additional missing documents.

Inconsistent Document Availability Across Communication Channels During the initial mortgage assistance and forbearance process, LoanCare sent at least one required document via physical mail. However, this document is not available or reflected in my online borrower portal.

This inconsistency between mailed and portal documents contributes to confusion regarding what materials are part of my active mortgage assistance file and prevents me from having a single, complete record of required documentation.

Lack of Clear Case Ownership I have not been assigned a consistent point of contact or case manager. As a result, I have interacted with multiple representatives who provided inconsistent or incomplete information regarding my application status.

These issues have resulted in : Confusion surrounding post-forbearance options Significant confusion regarding required documentation Increased time burden and stress in attempting to comply with unclear requirements Difficulty determining whether my application is complete or still under review Reduced confidence in the accuracy and timeliness of servicing communications
label: loan_mortgage
source_sub_issue: Applying for or obtaining a modification, forbearance plan, short sale, or deed-in-lieu

text:
Complaint against Guild Mortgage Company Improper Credit Reporting Due to Servicing Delays and Inconsistent Loss Mitigation Handling I am submitting this complaint regarding Guild Mortgage Companys handling of my VA-backed mortgage following a forbearance period, and the resulting negative credit reporting for XX/XX/XXXX. 

My concern is not simply whether the delinquency was technically accurate, but that it was directly caused by servicer delays, inconsistent guidance, and failure to act when I actively sought assistance, despite my good-faith efforts to resolve my situation. 

Background - My forbearance period ended on XX/XX/XXXX. 
- I engaged immediately with Guild Mortgage to determine appropriate post-forbearance options. 
- On XX/XX/XXXX, I completed an attestation and was placed into review for a Formal Payment Plan ( FPP ). 
- On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Guild to discuss the status and affordability of the proposed plan. 

During the XX/XX/XXXX call : - I raised concerns that the FPP was not affordable. 
- I discussed alternative options, including a loan modification. 
- Despite this, no loan modification review was initiated, and I was instead directed to contact the VA. 

It was not until XXXX I independently obtained guidance and explicitly requested itthat a loan modification review was finally initiated. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX. Failure to Act When Assistance Was Requested ( XX/XX/XXXX ) During the XX/XX/XXXX call, I clearly communicated that the proposed repayment plan was not affordable and discussed alternatives. This was a critical intervention point where Guild Mortgage could have initiated a loan modification review or taken steps to evaluate all available loss mitigation options. 

Instead, no action was taken, and I was directed externally to the VA, delaying resolution. 

-- - XXXX. Inconsistent and Conflicting Information Regarding Loan Modification Eligibility Guild Mortgage has provided conflicting explanations regarding loan modification availability : - I was initially told that a loan modification was an available option ( with conditions ). 
- In later correspondence, Guild stated that I was not eligible at all due to a XXXX seasoning requirement. 

These contradictory statements demonstrate that I was not provided with clear, consistent, or reliable guidance, which directly impacted my ability to pursue the appropriate resolution in a timely manner. 

-- - XXXX. Servicer-Caused Delay Resulting in Negative Credit Reporting Because Guild Mortgage did not initiate a loan modification review on XXXX instead required additional steps such as opting out of the FPPthe process was delayed. 

As a result : - The loan remained in a delinquent status through the XX/XX/XXXX reporting cycle. 
- This delinquency was not the result of borrower inaction, but rather the result of delays and gaps in the servicers loss mitigation process. 

Had the review been initiated on XX/XX/XXXX, the timeline would have been materially different and XXXX have prevented or mitigated the negative credit reporting. 

-- - XXXX. Good Faith Efforts by Borrower Throughout this period, I acted in good faith : - I engaged promptly after forbearance ended.

- I communicated proactively with Guild Mortgage. 
- I raised affordability concerns and sought alternatives. 
- I pursued guidance from the VA when directed to do so. 

At no point did I ignore my obligations. I relied on the servicer to guide me through the process appropriately. 

-- - Additional Context This loan is backed by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, and this complaint involves the handling of loss mitigation under a VA-guaranteed loan. 

I believe this matter warrants review not only for technical compliance, but for whether the servicer met its obligation to provide timely, accurate, and effective assistance to a borrower actively seeking resolution. 

-- - I respectfully request that Guild Mortgage provide a substantive response addressing the issues of delay, inconsistent guidance, and causation of the delinquencynot solely whether the reporting was technically accurate.
label: loan_mortgage
source_sub_issue: An existing modification, forbearance plan, short sale, or other loss mitigation relief

text:
I have an installment loan with Upgrade with a fixed monthly payment of {$200.00} and a set repayment term. I have consistently made payments, including recent payments of {$200.00} ; however, my balance has not decreased as expected and instead appears inconsistent and inflated.

Upgrade informed me that I was placed into a six-month hardship program in XXXX  where payments were reduced to approximately 30 % of the regular amount. I was told this agreement was verbal, and Upgrade has refused to provide any written documentation, disclosures, or agreement terms related to this hardship program.

I requested a full payment history, amortization schedule, and a breakdown showing how each payment was applied between principal and interest. I also requested documentation explaining how deferred amounts were calculated and added back to my balance. Upgrade has refused to provide this information, stating that records and call recordings are internal and not available to customers.

Additionally, Upgrade stated that the remaining 70 % of payments during the hardship period was deferred and added back to the loan balance, and that payments made during that time were applied primarily to interest rather than principal. This has resulted in my balance not decreasing properly despite consistent payments.

At no point was I provided clear written disclosures explaining that interest would continue to accrue in this manner, that my balance would increase, or that my loan term or final payment would be affected. Without this transparency, I can not verify the accuracy of the balance or how my payments have been applied.

I believe this reflects a lack of proper disclosure, unclear loan servicing practices, and failure to provide requested account documentation. I am requesting a full accounting of my loan, including how all payments were applied, and verification that the balance is accurate.
label: loan_mortgage
source_product: Payday loan, title loan, personal loan, or advance loan

text:
I am submitting a complaint regarding my mortgage servicers failure to provide a clear and complete accounting of my loan balance and their repeated refusal to provide requested loan information.

My mortgage originated in XXXX with an original principal balance of apprximately {$160000.00}. After 21 years of payments, the payoff balance remained approximately {$140000.00} in XX/XX/XXXX, a principal reduction of only around {$20000.00} after 21 years. This raised concerns regarding the application of payments, capitalization of fees, and overall loan servicing accuracy.

I submitted a written Request for Information requesting a complete life-of-loan accounting, payment history, explanation of my current balance, and documentation supporting fees assessed to my account. The servicer acknowledged receipt of my request but responded by providing large volumes of raw transaction data and documents without a clear, reconciled explanation of how my balance was calculated.

The response also indicated that certain information could be withheld due to undue burden or not in their possession. They instead provided large amounts of raw data and not a meaningful explanation respond to my request or an explanation.

These responses and repeated refusal to discuss my account directly with me and provide the requested information are inconsistent with my understanding of my rights as a borrower to obtain loan servicing information.

text:
Summary of Issue Since initiating my refinance application on XX/XX/XXXX, my loan has been subject to prolonged and unexplained delays, repeated failures in communication, and missed processing expectations. Despite being financially well-qualified, my application has not been completed after more than three months, and I have incurred direct financial harm as a result. 

Detailed Timeline XX/XX/XXXX : Application initiated. 
XX/XX/XXXX : Initial contact made ; call scheduled. 
XX/XX/XXXX : Requested documentation submitted promptly ( same day, with one exception ). 
XX/XX/513 : Additional verification steps completed, including employment verification. I proactively assisted in ensuring my former employer responded. 
XXXX to early XXXX : No updates were provided despite multiple outreach attempts via email and phone. Communications went unanswered. 
XX/XX/XXXX : I was informed that my loan file had been submitted to underwriting and that I would be contacted within approximately one week. 
XXXX : After no follow-up occurred, I escalated the issue. I was then informed the file was under review and a decision was expected shortly. 
XX/XX/XXXX : My loan was reassigned to a new representative, and I was told it was conditionally approved. 
XXXX : I was led to expect closing during this timeframe ; however, no closing occurred and no meaningful updates were provided. 
As of XX/XX/XXXX : I have not received a final decision, closing date, or consistent status updates. 
Key Concerns Failure to Provide Timely and Adequate Communication Navy Federal failed to respond to repeated inquiries for extended periods. This lack of responsiveness prevented me from understanding the status of my loan or taking alternative action. 
Unreasonable and Unexplained Processing Delays The loan has remained in process for over three months despite my prompt submission of all required documentation and strong financial qualifications. No clear justification for these delays has been provided.

Inconsistent and Misleading Expectations I was given multiple anticipated timelines ( including expected contact from underwriting and an anticipated XXXX closing ) that were not met, without explanation or updated guidance. 
Potential Process and Oversight Failures My file was reassigned mid-process, and there appeared to be gaps in continuity, communication, and accountability between representatives and departments. 
Documented Financial Harm As a direct result of these delays, I was required to make two additional mortgage payments of approximately {$1500.00} eachcosts that exceed my anticipated refinanced payment. 
Strong Borrower Profile Ignored I have excellent credit, substantial assets, and a low loan-to-value ratio ( approximately 32 % ). There is no clear underwriting or risk-based justification for the extended delays.

Requested Resolution I respectfully request that the CFPB : Require Navy Federal Credit Union to provide a clear explanation for the delays and communication failures Ensure immediate action is taken to resolve and complete my loan process Review whether Navy Federals practices comply with applicable consumer protection standards regarding mortgage processing and communication Consider appropriate remediation for the financial harm I have incurred Additional Context Throughout this process, I have acted in good faith, responded promptly to all requests, and made repeated attempts to obtain updates. The lack of transparency and accountability has caused significant frustration and unnecessary financial burden.

text:
My vehicle was involved in an accident on XX/XX/XXXX. The vehicle was officially declared a total loss on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Following the accident, a claim was opened with my insurance company, XXXX. During the claim process, there were delays involving inspection, valuation, and communication between Westlake Financial and XXXX. These delays were not caused by me. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a GAP payment was applied to my account. However, the GAP coverage did not pay the full remaining balance, and no clear explanation or breakdown was provided to me regarding why the full balance was not covered. 

Despite the vehicle being declared a total loss on XX/XX/XXXX, and partial GAP payment being applied on XX/XX/XXXX, Westlake Financial has continued to add monthly payments, late fees, and increase my past-due balance. 

As of XX/XX/XXXX, my account reflects the following : - Total amount due : {$1700.00} - Past due amount : {$1300.00} - Upcoming payment due on XX/XX/XXXX : {$440.00} - Late fee : {$26.00} I have attempted to address this issue directly with Westlake Financial, but the balance continues to increase while the claim remains unresolved. 

The delays in resolving this matter were due to processing issues and disputes between the company and the insurance provider, not due to any failure on my part. 

I am requesting that Westlake Financial : - Immediately freeze my account and stop adding payments, interest, and fees - Remove all late fees and penalties added during the claim delay period - Provide a full explanation and itemized breakdown of the GAP payment, including why the full balance was not covered - Ensure that no negative credit reporting occurs as a result of this situation - Provide a final and accurate payoff amount after applying all insurance and GAP payments I believe this is an unfair billing practice. I should not be financially penalized for delays outside of my control or lack of transparency regarding the GAP coverage. I am requesting correction of my account balance and protection of my credit.
label: loan_mortgage

text: I am submitting a complaint regarding a denied debit card dispute in the amount of XXXX XXXX.On XX/XX/XXXX, I was involved in a minor vehicle accident in which I lightly scratched another car. We exchanged phone numbers, and the other driver informed me the vehicle was a rental. Later, I got a phone call from the other driver along with another person on the line claiming to represent a company called XXXX XXXX XXXX, stating they handled rental car damage claims. During the call, I was told I owed {$12000.00} for damages and that insurance would not be accepted. Both the other driver and I questioned this, stating we both had insurance but the individual continued to apply pressure for immediate payment, claiming that our insurance was invalid. Under these circumstances, and believing the situation was legitimate, I agreed to pay a reduced amount of {$890.00}. I provided my debit card information over the phone. The transaction was initially declined multiple times through NFCU, but I later approved it through a verification prompt sent to my phone on the mobile app. Shortly after completing the transaction, I reviewed the emails sent to me, stating i booked a car with the company, and the communication over the phone with the company and realized that this was a scam. The company became completely unresponsive. I received no documentation, or legitimate follow-up regarding the alleged damage claim. The business appears to be illegitimate. I reported this to my bank, but my claim was denied on the basis that the transaction was authorized. However, the payment was made under false pretenses due to misrepresentation and pressure tactics. I would not have approved the charge if I had known the true nature of the transaction. This situation qualifies as services not rendered and fraud by deception. I am requesting that this claim be reopened, properly investigated, and that I receive a full refund of {$890.00}.

text:
Executive Summary : I am filing this complaint because NewRez has failed to accurately process a Private Mortgage Insurance ( PMI ) removal request and has subsequently failed to respond to a formal dispute ( Notice of Error ) delivered via Certified Mail. Despite providing a professional Comparative Market Analysis ( CMA ) and documentation of over {$90000.00} in capital improvements, the company claims to have " no record '' of the dispute more than 30 days after documented delivery. 

Timeline and Actions Taken : XX/XX/XXXX : I contacted the servicer to confirm PMI removal requirements. An agent confirmed that an LTV of less than 80 % was required. I submitted the written application and the required appraisal/BPO fee. At this time, the loan balance was {$390000.00}. 

XX/XX/XXXX : A broker performed a Broker Price Opinion ( BPO ). My spouse provided the broker with a list of significant improvements, including three new HVAC systems, a new roof, and a renovated laundry room. 

XX/XX/XXXX : The servicer denied the request, claiming the LTV was 83 %. Contradicting the information provided on XX/XX/XXXX, the servicer now claimed a 75 % LTV was required. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I spoke with a representative who provided the BPO report. The BPO was fundamentally flawed, utilizing a primary comparable sale from XX/XX/XXXX ( nearly a year old ) and a property significantly smaller ( under 2,600 sq. ft. ) than the subject property ( over 3,300 sq. ft. ). 

XX/XX/XXXX : I mailed a formal dispute package via Certified Mail with Return Receipt Requested ( Tracking # XXXX ). This package included a Professional CMA from a local broker valuing the home at {$530000.00} ( 74.5 % LTV ) and receipts for {$91000.00} in capital improvements. USPS tracking confirms the item was delivered and picked up by an agent in XXXX, XXXX, on this date. 

XX/XX/XXXX : After receiving no response for over 30 days, I called the servicer. The representative stated there was " no record '' of the appeal being received. I provided the specific tracking and return receipt numbers. The agent promised a callback from the appeals department within 24 hours. 

XX/XX/XXXX : The promised 24-hour window passed with no contact from the servicer. 

Desired Resolution : I am requesting that the servicer honor the 80 % LTV threshold for PMI termination. Given the {$91000.00} in documented capital improvements ( including three HVAC systems and a new roof ), this request qualifies for the valuation standards set forth in secondary mortgage market guidelines ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) regarding property improvements. I am requesting the immediate termination of PMI and a refund of all PMI premiums paid since the original request in XX/XX/XXXX.
label: loan_mortgage

text: On XX/XX/year>, I submitted a complete Loan Modification Request to Select Portfolio Servicing. The documents were sent via certified mail with return receipt to XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX, UT XXXX. The return receipt confirms delivery on XX/XX/year>, signed for by XXXX XXXX. However, these documents were not uploaded or acknowledged in the system until XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX.In parallel, I attempted to upload documents through the SPS online portal beginning XX/XX/year>. The website repeatedly rejected uploads, requiring multiple attempts over several hours to successfully submit documents. This created unnecessary delays and barriers to timely completion of my application. Communication with SPS has been extremely poor, updates are not accurate or clear. Relationship managers do not respond to emails, despite advising borrowers to communicate through that channel. Application updates provided through the system are often unclear, inaccurate, or contradictory. Additionally, SPS repeatedly requested documentation that was either already submitted or not relevant. For example, a profit and loss statement was requested from entities that pay my husband ( an XXXX  XXXX ), even after his own profit and loss statement had already been reviewed and accepted. I submitted multiple Letters of Explanation and spoke with several relationship managers, including XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. During my most recent conversation on XX/XX/year>, a XXXX XXXX XXXX confirmed that a prior XXXX had already clarified the XXXX requests and indicated that my file should be marked complete for processing. I was instructed to allow XXXX days for the system to update and reflect that the application was complete and under review. Instead, I received a foreclosure notice indicating that foreclosure proceedings would begin in XXXX days due to missing documents, despite prior confirmation that my file was complete. Based on this pattern of delays, inconsistent communication, and shifting document requests, I am concerned that SPS is intentionally delaying the processing of my loan modification application. Specifically, it appears these delays may be intended to allow time for a forbearance-related balloon payment to become due, thereby increasing the likelihood of foreclosure rather than properly evaluating my application. Additionally, SPS prematurely reported the balloon payment amount to credit reporting agencies while simultaneously reporting the existing mortgage balance. This resulted in the appearance of double the actual debt obligation, negatively impacting both my credit score and my husbands credit score. I believe these actions may constitute unfair, deceptive, or abusive practices, particularly given the significant equity in our homestead property and the apparent incentive to proceed toward foreclosure.
label: loan_mortgage
source_issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage

I informed Onity Mortgage ( formerly PHH Mortgage ) on XX/XX/XXXX that the 78 % Loan-to-Value ( LTV ) threshold on my mortgage was reached on XX/XX/XXXX. text:
To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting a formal complaint regarding a residential solar installation and associated financing agreement that I entered into in XX/XX/2020. This complaint involves material misrepresentations made during the sales process that directly influenced my decision to proceed. 

Consumer Information : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Companies Involved : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  : Loanpal ( now GoodLeap ) -- - Summary of Complaint During the sales process, I was explicitly informed by the salesperson that I would receive approximately {$15000.00} as a check from the IRS after filing my taxes. I was further instructed that this money should be applied toward the loan balance to prevent an increase in my monthly payments. 

This representation was false and misleading. 

After consulting with a CPA and reviewing IRS guidance, I learned that the federal solar incentive is a tax credit, not a guaranteed payment. It does not function as a direct refund check in the manner it was presented, and eligibility depends on individual tax liability. 

-- - Material Impact This misrepresentation was central to the financial structure of the agreement : - The loan was designed to re-amortize after approximately 18 months, increasing monthly payments if a lump-sum payment was not made - I was led to believe the expected IRS funds would cover this payment - Because this representation was inaccurate, the financial obligations were not as presented at the time of sale Had accurate information been provided, I would not have agreed to the terms. 

-- - Additional Concerns - I have continued to receive utility bills in addition to solar loan payments, contrary to expectations set during the sales process - Attempts to resolve these issues have resulted in being redirected between parties without meaningful assistance- The salesperson who made the original representations is no longer responsive -- - Supporting Documentation I have retained documentation supporting these claims, including : - Text messages from the salesperson stating I would receive a government-issued check - Loan agreement documents outlining the actual terms and disclaimers - Utility billing records demonstrating ongoing financial impact A detailed timeline of events is attached as Exhibit A. 

-- - Request for Review I respectfully request that this matter be reviewed for potential deceptive or misleading sales practices. I am seeking appropriate remedies, which may include : - Review of the loan and sales practices involved - Consideration of financial adjustment, restructuring, or other relief - Any corrective actions deemed appropriate -- - Closing Statement This situation reflects a significant discrepancy between what was represented during the sales process and the actual financial terms of the agreement. The representations made were specific, repeated, and materially influenced my decision. This complaint relates to the sales practices associated with a licensed contractor operating in Nevada I appreciate your time and attention to this matter and request a written response regarding any findings or next steps. 

Sincerely, XXXX & XXXX XXXX

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a phone call that appeared to come from Navy Federal Credit Union. The caller ID displayed Navy Federals phone number, and the caller identified themselves as a member of Navy Federals fraud department. 

The caller informed me that my account had been compromised and that there were fraudulent transactions occurring. They stated that in order to secure my account and prevent further fraud, I needed to move my funds to a secure location temporarily while the issue was being investigated. 

Because the call appeared to come from Navy Federal and the person represented themselves as a bank fraud specialist, I believed I was speaking with my bank. Following the instructions provided, I transferred {$3900.00} from my Navy Federal account to XXXX XXXX, and from there the funds were converted to Bitcoin and sent to a recipient code provided by the caller. 

I later discovered that the caller was not associated with Navy Federal and that this was a bank impersonation scam. The caller had spoofed Navy Federals phone number and used social engineering to convince me that my funds were at risk and needed to be moved immediately. 

I immediately reported the incident to Navy Federal and filed a fraud claim. However, the claim was denied even though the transaction occurred as a result of deception by someone impersonating my bank.

This transaction was authorized only because I was misled by a person pretending to be a Navy Federal representative. I did not knowingly send money to a scammer or intend to transfer funds to an unauthorized party.

I am requesting that Navy Federal reopen and properly investigate this case as a bank impersonation and social engineering fraud. I am also requesting that they attempt to recover the funds or provide reimbursement, as the transaction occurred under deception and impersonation of the financial institution.

text:
Subject : Dispute Claim - Missing Bill Pay XXXX XXXX In late XXXX, we were invited to go on a XXXX trip with a group of friends. One person in the group made reservations at a XXXX and offered to pay for it upfront, with the understanding that we would reimburse him. 
In XXXX, he sent a message confirming the lodging was set and that each person 's share was {$1500.00}, along with his mailing address. We all paid him around that time. 
I have been a Wells Fargo and XXXX user for decades ( originally XXXX XXXX  before transitioning to XXXX when XXXX exited that business ). I entered the payee information as I would for any payment and scheduled a bill pay for XX/XX/XXXX. 
Shortly after the XXXX, I noticed the money had left my account and thought nothing more of it. 
While we were on vacation in XXXX, XXXX ( the person who paid for the lodging ) asked me, " Hey, I never received your payment for the XXXX. '' I was surprised. I opened my phone app, searched his name, showed him the payment from XX/XX/XXXX, and sent him a screenshot. 
He said he would check with his bank. He called them, and the next day he told me he had provided them with the information but they had not received the payment. 
I then contacted Wells Fargo. They advised me to provide the recipient with the entry from my bank statement, which I did in the form of a screenshot. Wells Fargo also offered to initiate a claim/dispute, but I asked to wait and see if his bank could locate the payment first. This seemed like a simple matter to resolve. 
I spoke with XXXX a few days later ( after we had all returned home ). He said his bank was unable to locate the payment using the tracker from my statement, or any other means. I asked if he could accept XXXX or another payment method. He confirmed he had XXXX and provided the necessary information. I then sent him the money via XXXX. 
I also called Wells Fargo back and asked them to start the process to locate and recover the funds. The representative initiated the claim and set everything up, informing me that it could take up to 10 days for a response. 
Today marks 10 business days. I called to check on the status, and Wells Fargo informed me that, per their policy, they are denying my claim on the grounds that I authorized the withdrawal and that more than 60 days had passed. 
I asked to escalate the issue and was transferred to someone who essentially told me the same thing. When I asked if there was an appeals process, I was told there is not. 
I did ask specifically if there was anything I could do or if I was to just accept this loss.. 
His response was that there was nothing more for them to do. 
I have used this same payment process ( and bank ) for decades and have never had an issue like this. I did nothing out of the ordinary. I have verified the recipient 's address directly with him. 
There was nothing weird about this payment, yet I am out {$1500.00}, which is significant. As a bank customer, I would expect my bank to have the ability to initiate some sort of request with the recipient 's bank to help locate the funds. Over and over, they tell me to contact the vendorbut this is not a vendor ; it is a person, and he has already reached out to his bank on multiple occasions. 
I do not know what other recourse I might have. 
Please advise on next steps. 
Thank you,
label: payment_transfer
source_product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service

I have been overseas for a while.

I was really shocked by her extreme reaction since I was thinking she would just dispute the payment to this merchant and blocked them. I asked why she did it and she argue that this is only measure to take because my card was already compromised, and they had to reissue a new card. I told her I was overseas and if she measures the consequences of her actions because I need my money to take care of my daily needs here overseas. On that day I called 8 times and spent 1h09 minutes over the phone. You can already imagine how much it cost when calling the US from overseas for over 1h. 

The next day, XX/XX/year> I called back and asked to speak with a supervisor. I explained to her the whole situation, and stated the emergency. I gave her the address to send my new card to and made sure that she got everything right by spelling the address and had her repeat. She talked about phone verification but I told I don't have my US phone number anymore since I have been overseas for a while. She went ahead and processed everything. I asked her who will be the mail carrier, she said she didn't know. I explained that for faster mailing, they should use XXXX as the mail carrier since they go worldwide and they are safe because they provide a tracking number and they will call me once the mail arrives. She said she had no control over this but I will get my card no worries. I said OK. This call lasted 35 min 32 s. 

Seeing nothing, I called back on XX/XX/year>, nearly a month later, to inquire about the status of my card. I tried to make sure that they sent my card to the right address, and Truist customer service assured me that everything was fine and it was sent to the address I provided. I called twice and they lasted 20 min 36 s. 

On April 2d, 2026, I called again three times trying to know who was the mail carrier and getting the tracking number. I stayed over the phone for 1h30, and to my surprise, after verification I realized that part of the address was missing, and they even failed to include the country. I was really devasted. I spelled my address again and made sure they have everything correct. I reminded the lady that I was calling from overseas and didn't have enough credit, not money to stay longer than that on the phone. Meanwhile I mentioned that even if I had to pay for expediate shipping through XXXX I would do it. The conversation was cut off, but she already had everything to process the shipment. 

On XX/XX/year>, I called back asking about the mail carrier and the tracking number, but a lady supervisor kept me that it was not sent because verification was not done amd the address was just changed in the system. I reminded her that I never asked to be with Truist, I was forced to become a Truist customer after my bank decided to close their business in the state. Third, I gave the right address back on XX/XX/year> but someone made a mistake and did not include the whole information so this is not my problem, otherwise I would have had my card. text:
On Saturday, XX/XX/year>, between XXXXXXXX XXXX, I deposited Check # XXXX for {$20000.00} into my JPMorgan Chase checking account via Chase 's mobile deposit feature. 
On Monday, XX/XX/year>, Chase placed a hold on the entire {$20000.00}. The reason Chase displayed on my account reads : " We have not yet collected the money from the paying bank. '' Hold expiration : XX/XX/year>. 
On the SAME day Monday, XX/XX/year> the paying bank, XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX, debited the {$20000.00} from the payer 's account. I have XXXXXXXX XXXX transaction statement and the cleared check image to prove it. The money left XXXX XXXX days ago. 
Today is Monday, XX/XX/year>. Chase is still holding my money. The reason Chase shows on my account is factually false. XXXX released the funds XXXX days ago. Chase either already has the funds or they cleared through the Federal Reserve either way, Chase has no lawful basis to continue withholding them from me. 
This is a violation of Regulation CC ( 12 CFR 229.13 ). Chase is required to provide a truthful written hold notice with accurate reasoning. The reason Chase is showing me is provably wrong. 
I invoked the XXXX XXXX hardship provision by phone and was refused. I went in person to my local Chase branch for help and was greeted with " do you have an appointment? '' before anyone asked what I needed while staff socialized with each other. I was treated as an inconvenience. 
The harm : My bills are unpaid. 
My home security monitoring is unpaid. 
Vendor payments for my work has been disrupted. 
I have spent XXXX days being bounced between Chase and XXXX, each blaming the other. 
I am a mother of young children trying to manage life and my work while fighting a XXXX  bank holding my own money on a false pretext. 

The hypocrisy : when my account runs {$20.00} short, Chase charges me {$34.00} in overdraft fees within hours. When Chase wrongfully holds {$20000.00} of MY money, Chase offers no interest, no urgency, no corrected notice, and no accountability. 
I have filed in parallel with the XXXX and the U.S. Department of the Treasury. I am asking the CFPB to investigate Chase 's practice of displaying false hold reasons and take appropriate enforcement action. 

immediate release of the full {$20000.00}. 
Statutory damages under the XXXX Funds Availability Act ( 12 USC 4010 ) up to {$8000.00} plus actual damages. 
Reimbursement of every fee, late charge, returned-payment fee, or penalty I have incurred or will incur from any creditor because of this hold. 
Interest on the {$20000.00} at Chase 's own prevailing overdraft penalty rate for every day the funds were wrongfully held. 
XXXX compensation of XXXX days of financial harm, distress, disruption to my work operations, and hours of wasted phone calls and branch visits. 
A written, corrected XXXX XXXX hold notice acknowledging the original reason displayed was inaccurate. 
Correction of any internal risk flag, XXXX notation, or credit reporting impact caused by this wrongful hold. 
also want the CFPB to know how Chase treated me when I went in person to my local branch seeking help resolving this wrongful hold. Despite being a customer in visible distress with a {$20000.00} issue, I was immediately asked " do you have an appointment? '' before anyone bothered to ask what I needed. Meanwhile, Chase staff were socially engaged with XXXX another and with other visitors laughing, chatting, comfortable clearly NOT too busy to help a walk-in. I was made to feel like an inconvenience, turned away at the door, and denied the in-person help every banking customer is entitled to. 
This is the same Chase that charges me a {$34.00} overdraft fee within hours when my account is {$20.00} short no appointment required for THAT. But when a customer walks in needing help because Chase is wrongfully holding {$20000.00} of her own money, suddenly there's a gatekeeping " appointment '' requirement and staff have more important social matters to attend to. 
The appointment-only walk-in policy for distressed customers, while staff are visibly available and unoccupied, is a deflection tactic not a service standard. It is designed to push customers away from in-person resolution and force them back into phone queues where they can be bounced indefinitely. It is exactly how Chase ensures complaints like mine never get resolved at the branch level. 
I am asking the CFPB to look at Chase 's branch service practices as part of this complaint. Branch address : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX FYI : account has joint name with myself ( XXXX XXXX ) & My husband XXXX XXXX

I am reporting PayPals refusal to refund an unauthorized {$500.00} transaction that occurred on my PayPal account on XX/XX/year>. The payment was sent to an individual I do not know ( name : XXXX XXXX. XXXX, email : XXXX ) and was funded from my XXXX  XXXX XXXX checking account ending in XXXX. I did not authorize this transaction. 

I received no login alerts, no twofactor authentication prompts, no device verification, and no notification of unusual activity from PayPal. The first time I became aware of the transaction was on XX/XX/year> at XXXX XXXX, when PayPal emailed me stating that the XXXX payment had cleared. 

Immediately after discovering the unauthorized payment, I contacted XXXX  XXXX XXXX and filed a fraud claim. Later that same day, my bank account began showing unauthorized XXXX accountverify microdebits ( {$0.00}, {$0.00}, {$0.00} ) from an unknown individual through XXXX XXXX. These microtransactions are commonly used by fraudsters to test an account before larger withdrawals. This indicates that my bank account information was compromised as part of the same incident. 

Despite these clear signs of account takeover, PayPal denied my unauthorized access claim within minutes. When I appealed, PayPal denied it again. Their only justification was that the IP address used for the transaction was consistent. This does not prove authorization. IP addresses can be shared, spoofed, approximated, or reassigned by ISPs. PayPal did not review device logs, login history, or any other indicators of unauthorized access.

I attempted to resolve this through PayPals Resolution Center and customer service, but I was not allowed to upload documentation or have the case properly reviewed. PayPal has refused to reopen the case or conduct a meaningful investigation. 

I am requesting CFPB assistance because PayPal has denied my claim without properly investigating the unauthorized access or the subsequent fraudulent activity on my bank account.
label: payment_transfer
source_product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
source_issue: Unauthorized transactions or other transaction problem

text:
This complaint is submitted as a follow-up to CFPB Case # XXXX, which was closed after the company provided a response that did not address the core issues.

The central issue remains unresolved : despite repeated requests over a period exceeding nine months, Chase has failed to provide any documentation demonstrating what trust-related documents were submitted, when they were submitted, to whom they were submitted, or what determinations were made.

Prior to the closing of a property sale on XX/XX/year>, Chase requested and received specific documentation, including an attorney opinion letter prepared at their request. I was explicitly informed by a Chase representative that the documentation was sufficient and that the transaction could proceed. Based on these representations, the closing occurred and funds in excess of {$500000.00} were wired into a Chase account.

Immediately thereafter, Chase reversed its position and denied access to the funds, asserting that additional requirementsspecifically probatewere necessary. This directly contradicts prior representations and occurred without any documented explanation or notice of deficiency.

Since that time, I have made more than ten requests, including through the Executive Office, for the following : Documentation confirming what was submitted and processed Dates and timestamps of submission and review Identification of the department or individuals responsible for review Written explanation of any rejection or deficiency To date, none of this information has been provided. I was explicitly told by a branch manager that such documentation is not retained, which raises serious concerns regarding compliance, internal controls, and transparency.

I have also submitted a formal written demand letter requesting this information and a substantive explanation. That demand has not been meaningfully addressed.

This complaint is not simply about access to fundsit concerns the companys failure to provide basic documentation, failure to explain contradictory actions, and failure to respond to repeated, reasonable requests for information necessary for me to fulfill my fiduciary duties as Trustee.

Requested Resolution : I am requesting that Chase provide : Written documentation of all trust-related submissions, including dates, recipients, and internal routing Documentation of any review, approval, or rejection, including the basis for such determinations A written explanation reconciling the prior approval to proceed with closing and the subsequent denial of access to funds Confirmation of whether required documentation was ever submitted internally, and if not, why the closing was permitted to proceed Immediate steps toward resolution of access to the funds I am also requesting that this matter be reviewed for compliance with applicable regulatory requirements regarding documentation retention, transparency, and fair handling of fiduciary accounts.
label: payment_transfer

text:
To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this complaint after repeated and unsuccessful attempts to resolve a fraud incident directly with Regions Bank. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent a formal written request to Regions Bank detailing a fraud induced teller withdrawal that occurred on XX/XX/XXXX. As of today, more than three weeks later, I have received no response, no acknowledgment, and no written determination of any kind. 

This complaint represents my escalation after exhausting reasonable efforts to resolve the matter directly. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I was the victim of a law enforcement impersonation scam. The individuals involved claimed to be affiliated with the XXXX Police Department and possessed my full Social Security number, date of birth, and home address prior to my confirming any information. Based on that, I had every reason to believe the situation was legitimate. 

I was kept on the phone continuously under what was described as a mobile escort, with instructions that compliance would affect legal consequences. The individuals tracked my movements in real time and demonstrated knowledge of my location, including referencing my wifes location before I arrived to meet her. 

While under this coercion, I completed two teller withdrawals at a Regions branch on XXXX XXXX XXXX totaling XXXX dollars, one for XXXX dollars and one for XXXX dollars. During the transaction, I told the teller that I was in trouble with the police. I was still on the phone with the individual directing me at the time, although the phone was in my pocket. 

The funds were immediately converted to cryptocurrency under direction from the impersonators. I did not receive any goods or services. This was not an investment or voluntary transaction, but one made under deception and pressure.

Following the incident, I reported the fraud in person at the Regions branch located at XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, FL XXXX. I have returned to this branch at least six times seeking updates. During those visits, I was initially told the claim had been denied, but then told it would be escalated further because staff clearly recognized the fraud involved. The branch manager who was reportedly handling that escalation later left their position, and I have not received any confirmation that the escalation was ever completed. 

After months without communication, I began contacting Regions abuse department by phone to request documentation and updates. One report was submitted, followed by another after two weeks of silence. It has now been approximately four months since the incident, and despite consistently following up, I have not received a written determination, supporting documentation, or any meaningful communication regarding the status of my case. 

At this point, the issue is not only the fraud itself, but the complete lack of response, documentation, and transparency from Regions Bank. I have received no written determination, no explanation of policy, no documentation of teller interaction or internal review, and no meaningful communication. 

I am requesting a formal investigation into Regions Banks handling of this matter, a written response outlining their position and supporting policy, confirmation of whether proper fraud review and escalation procedures were followed, and accountability for the lack of response after my formal written request. 

The email referenced above, sent on XX/XX/XXXX, was my last direct communication to Regions Bank and remains unanswered. Due to the lack of response, I am now escalating this matter for regulatory review
label: payment_transfer

text:
I am writing to formally appeal the permanent suspension of my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX accounts which occurred on XX/XX/year> caused by a falsely reported Zelle transaction. I respectfully request a thorough review of the circumstances surrounding this incident, as I believe I was mistakenly implicated in a situation I had no knowledge of, nor any intent to participate in fraud or unethical activity. I have spoken with XXXX representatives about the situation via telephone call and all have given me different answers and suggestions, which has been extremely confusing in attempting to solve the following incidents. I also have been into XXXX different branches where I have been told that there is nothing that can be done on a branch level and there is no one that could be contacted to appeal or hear out the incident that has greatly affected my life and finances. Instead of XXXX XXXX protecting me as a customer, protecting my funds and acting on behalf of a consistent and trustworthy consumer, they did the opposite. The did not protect my finances, did not protect my name and further jeopardized my life after handing me a check for all of my savings and kicking me out of the institution after XXXX years of banking. I have undergone extreme stress while trying to gain access to my money, find a new financial institution and secure my finances over XXXX Zelle transaction discrepancy. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I was informed by a XXXX XXXX representative that a fraud report had been filed related to a transaction that took place on XX/XX/year>. The XXXX XXXX representative would not provide further details, except that the report involved a woman. I never received a phone call, email or letter in the mail from XXXX, Zelle or any other individual about said report, until I noticed money removed from my checking account on XXXX XXXX. Through my own investigation, I identified XXXX Zelle transactions sent to my phone number ( XXXX ) on XX/XX/year> : XXXX for {$5.00} and another for {$1400.00}, both from XXXX XXXX. After speaking directly with XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX, my then boyfriend, I pieced together the following : XXXX XXXX had contacted XXXX XXXX to arrange transportation for her child to a XXXX facility in XXXX. XXXX, who resides in XXXX and is an XXXX XXXX, asked me -- his then-girlfriend, XXXX XXXX XXXX for a XXXX TIME favor : to receive the payment from XXXX on XXXX behalf via Zelle. At the time, I was unaware of the transaction 's purpose but trusted XXXX, who regularly assists treatment centers with transportation services. I have personally volunteered at such facilities and am passionate about helping others, so did not have any negative thoughts or concerns about receiving money on his behalf and agreed to do so, but followed up by telling him, " You need to get your own account for the future ''. 

I received the funds believing the transaction was legal, ethical, and moral. I had no reason to suspect otherwise. I have never received money on behalf of XXXX before, and this was the only time I did so. I was alarmed to learn that XXXX later reported the transaction as fraudulent. I did ask XXXX for XXXX 's number to gain more information on what occurred. According to her, she changed her mind about the service and sought a refund via XXXX. I asked XXXX to provide any text message or other proof showing he denied her a refund, but she did not provide. I asked XXXX to provide any text or email proof of any further information about said conversations and has yet to provide anything further. I also asked XXXX if she received her money back. She confirmed to me that she received her money back ( via bank refund ), which was debited from my account. XXXX XXXX did not incur any financial loss, but I did. 

Despite my long-standing relationship with XXXX XXXX XXXX I was never contacted, questioned, or informed of any investigation. I was not given the opportunity to explain my side or clarify my involvement. This lack of communication has been deeply distressing, especially given my impeccable banking history : -I have maintained multiple accounts with XXXX XXXX XXXX including XXXX checking and XXXX savings accounts, a credit card, and a car loan -- all in excellent standing. 
XXXX had approximately {$39000.00} in my checking/ savings account at the time of suspension, earned through hard work across XXXX jobs with direct deposits into my account .-I have never bounced a check, cashed a bad check, or had any prior issues with Zelle. 
-I have consistently demonstrated responsible financial behavior and have relied on XXXX XXXX to support my family 's military journey and personal milestones. 

I understand that XXXX XXXX has policies regarding Zelle discrepancies, and I was told that the XXXX transactions triggered a restriction. However, I respectfully ask that you consider the context : I was unknowingly caught in a dispute between XXXX individuals whose agreement I was not privy to. XXXX has provided a statement confirming my lack of involvement, and XXXX has acknowledged that her actions affected me unfairly via telephone conversation. The XXXX transactions that occurred were of the same incident. I have never had any other incidents in my ten-year period with XXXX or XXXX with any institution. The money was returned to sender from my account. 

I deeply regret accepting the favor without full understanding and assure you that I will never again receive funds on behalf of another individual. I acted in good faith and had no intention to facilitate fraud or cause harm. I am asking XXXX XXXX to clear my name and reinstate my accounts, as I have always been a loyal and ethical member. 

Thank you for taking the time to review my appeal and the supporting documentation. I am available to provide any further information or clarification needed. I am attaching documents to support the above information.

text:
Supplemental Evidence Impact of the FCRA Section 605A Extended Fraud Alert on the Identity Theft Claim To whom it may concern at the FDIC Consumer Response Center : This statement serves to clarify the legal and technical implications of the newly submitted XXXX  Credit Report evidence, which confirms a pre-existing Extended Fraud Alert active until XX/XX/XXXX. 

XXXX. Statutory Mandate under FCRA Section 605A Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically Section 605A, an " Extended Fraud Alert '' is a high-level federal protection for confirmed victims of identity theft. This alert places a mandatory " Duty of Care '' on any financial institution, such as FirstBank Puerto Rico, before authorizing high-risk transactions or changes to an account.

2. Failure of Identity Verification Protocols The banks initial denial letter, dated XX/XX/XXXX, claims the transactions were " processed correctly '' because the name and account number matched. However, for a consumer with an Extended Fraud Alert, matching basic data is legally insufficient.

The Violation : The bank failed to follow the heightened verification requirements mandated by my credit profile. By authorizing the transfer of {$24000.00} to multiple third parties ( including XXXX XXXX and XXXX ) without a rigorous, manual identity confirmation, the bank directly bypassed federal safeguards.

3. Proof of Systemic Negligence The credit report shows a VantageScore of XXXX and a history of fraud protection dating back to XXXX. This documentation proves that the consumer was proactive in securing his identity. 

FirstBank 's Omission : Despite having access to this information via standard credit monitoring tools, the bank allowed an unauthorized third party ( whose voice did not match the account holder 's, as admitted by bank personnel ) to bypass an active XXXX federal alert. 

XXXX. Compounding Damages to Academic and Financial Standing The unauthorized withdrawal of these fundswhich include {$7900.00} in Federal Title IV fundshas caused a financial catastrophe. Currently, the account balance remains at {$0.00}, directly impacting the consumers ability to continue his Masters degree studies at XXXX University and maintain his primary transportation. 

Conclusion The existence of the Extended Fraud Alert serves as " XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX '' in this case. It proves that the bank did not just suffer a simple error, but rather a compliance failure. The bank was formally warned by the credit bureaus to verify identity with extreme caution, yet they allowed the account to be drained by a stranger. 

We request that the FDIC compel FirstBank to issue an immediate Provisional Credit for the full amount of {$24000.00}, as the banks failure to acknowledge the federal fraud alert constitutes gross negligence under the FCRA and Regulation E.

Company : SoFi Bank, N.A.

Account Type : Checking Account Number ( last 4 ) : XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX  On XX/XX/year>, I deposited a personal check via mobile deposit into my SoFi checking account ( ending XXXX ). The check was written by XXXX XXXX and drawn on XXXX XXXX in the amount of {$500.00}. Reference number : XXXX. 

On XX/XX/year>, I received email notice that the funds would be held until XX/XX/year> XXXX business days after deposit. The notice stated SoFi was " still working to verify information about the item or the sender '' but did not identify any specific regulatory exception.

This hold appears to violate Regulation CC ( 12 CFR Part 229 ) in the following ways : 1. 229.12 ( b ) requires funds from a local check to be available no later than the 2nd business day after deposit ( by XX/XX/year> ). SoFi is holding the funds until XX/XX/XXXX the XXXX business day. 

2. 229.10 ( c ) ( 1 ) ( vii ) requires the first {$270.00} to be made available by the next business day ( XX/XX/year> ) with no exceptions permitted. The full {$500.00} has been withheld, including this amount.

3. 229.13 ( g ) requires written notice identifying the specific exception being invoked. No such notice was provided. The email I received describes an internal verification process, which is not a recognized exception under 229.13.

When I raised these concerns via SoFi 's online chat, a representative stated this was " the standard process for check deposits '' and subsequently demanded contact information for the remitter ( my grandfather, XXXX XXXX XXXX as a condition of releasing the funds. Conditioning fund availability on third-party contact verification is not authorized under any exception in 229.13.

XX/XX/year>XXXX  - I deposited a check that I intended to use for tax payment by XX/XX/XXXX. I received and have in my possession a copy of a statement from Bank Of America indicating that the funds would be available by XX/XX/XXXX. I was not going to be able to pay my amount of tax due without these funds. I have a picture of the document from Bank Of America indicating that the funds would be available by XX/XX/XXXX.

I spent many hours on recorded phone call lines, in person at my local branch, and through the app for the company attempting to verify availability of funds by XX/XX/XXXX as initially documented. 

I would estimate a total of approximately 15 hours worth of effort were made during business hours, attempting to resolve this. I received a little to no tangible assistance. I am an educated consumer, and I found it nearly impossible to get in touch with anyone who could actually make any resolution to this. No one was able to lift this hold that was arbitrarily lengthened beyond a reasonable and promised timeline.

I contacted the bank that issued the check. I had an official from this bank call Bank Of America to verify to the veracity of the check, the funds, etc. such that Bank Of America was at no risk in this process. text:
On XX/XX/year>, I deposited a certified check in the amount of {$25000.00} into my account at Truist Bank. The check was physically presented in person, reviewed by the teller, accepted without objection, and processed by the bank. Funds were subsequently made available in my account, and I relied on that availability in good faith to conduct normal business operations and satisfy ongoing financial obligations. 

On or about XX/XX/year>, the bank reversed the {$25000.00} deposit, stating that the endorsement on the check did not comply with an internal policy allegedly requiring stamped endorsements rather than signed endorsements. I was informed this policy had been changed prior to my deposit ; however, the requirement was never communicated to me at the time of deposit, and the teller accepted the check without issue. The banks actions created a situation in which I reasonably relied upon the acceptance and availability of funds after the institution reviewed and processed the instrument. Under principles recognized by the Uniform Commercial Code ( UCC ), including UCC Articles 3 and 4 governing negotiable instruments and bank deposits/collections, a banks acceptance, processing, and release of funds creates foreseeable reliance by the customer. The subsequent reversal after funds were made available caused substantial financial harm and disruption. Additionally, pursuant to UCC 3-411 regarding refusal to pay cashiers checks, tellers checks, and certified checks, certified checks are generally treated as accepted obligations of the issuing institution and are intended to function as reliable instruments in commerce. The handling of this transaction undermined that reliance and caused significant financial disruption after the bank accepted and processed the instrument. The reversal caused my account to reflect a negative balance exceeding {$20000.00} and immediately disrupted my business cash flow. Because the funds had already been released and relied upon, the reversal directly impacted my ability to meet financial obligations, maintain ongoing projects, and preserve normal business operations and client relationships. After the reversal, I was informed that the original certified check would be mailed back to me so that corrective action could be taken. However, the original instrument was never returned, and no tracking information or documentation confirming its status was provided despite repeated requests. Due to the banks failure to timely return the original certified check and resolve the issue, I was ultimately forced to obtain an additional certified check from my client in order to continue business operations. This was not simply a replacement of the original funds. Rather, my client was forced to provide additional funds while the original {$25000.00} remained unresolved due to the banks handling of the transaction. This created compounded financial pressure, strained client relationships, and caused substantial operational and reputational harm to my business. Further, approximately one month prior to this incident, I deposited another check at the same institution in a similar manner, including a corrected endorsement, and that check was accepted and processed without issue. This indicates that the bank did not consistently enforce the policy it is now citing as justification for the reversal. Had the policy been enforced at the time of
presentment on XX/XX/year>, this situation and resulting damages could have been entirely avoided.

This matter has now remained unresolved for nearly two months despite repeated good-faith efforts on my part to resolve the issue directly with the bank.

In summary, the bank accepted the certified check, processed the transaction, released funds, and allowed me to reasonably rely upon the availability of those funds, only to later reverse the transaction based upon an internal policy that was not enforced at the time of deposit. The banks subsequent failure to return the original instrument further compounded the issue and prolonged the financial and operational harm caused to me and my business.
label: payment_transfer

text:
claim number : # XXXX date of incident : XX/XX/year> date of report to bank : XX/XX/year> police report made : XX/XX/year> XXXX On XX/XX/year>, at approximately XXXX XXXX, I was contacted by this cell number ( XXXX ) XXXX. This number sent a message stating : Bank of America Security Alert : Did you attempt a transaction for {$670.00} at XXXX in XXXX, Florida, on ( XX/XX/year> ). 

The sender informed me to respond with Y to confirm or N to deny. I then replied no, as I did not authorize the charge. The number then replied that a specialist from Bank of America 's fraud department would be contacting me with a reference number of # XXXX. 

Shortly after, I received a phone call from this number, identifying themselves as Bank of America 's fraud department. I can not recall the individual 's name at this time, but his name is listed on the police report. This individual began verifying my personal information, like home address, first name, and last name. After reading off my personal information, He began explaining that he was a representative for Bank of America 's fraud department and that there was suspicious activity taking place within my account.

When asked to clarify, He stated that someone was attempting to steal money from my account. He then provided instructions for me to secure all of my funds through a wired transfer through " XXXX '' I then followed his instructions, believing him to be a representative. He stated that the funds would then be returned to my account within 24 hours. The total amount lost was {$6300.00}. XXXX XXXX support was also contacted, but was informed to file the report through Bank of America.

However, after the transfer was completed, I was informed that I had fallen victim to a scam under Regulation E of the Electronic Funds Transfer Act. I informed Bank of America on XX/XX/year> and opened a claim under this regulation. Bank of America did not take any evidence from me and stated that the claim would be reviewed. 

During this process, Bank of America restored the funds to my account, but did not inform me that the claim was still under review. On XXXX XX/XX/year>, Bank of America informed me by email that they would be removing the funds of {$6300.00} on XX/XX/year>. The reason for their denial was " the charge was authorized by you, or made by someone who has permission to use the card or account. '' I was the victim of a bank impersonation scam in which the caller identified themselves as spoofing Bank of America 's identity. According to CFPB guidance, authorization obtained through impersonation of the financial institution is considered " unauthorized '' under Regulation E. The Bank is refusing to follow federal error resolution requirements.
label: payment_transfer

text:
I opened my Wise account in XXXX, 2026. 

It was suggested to me to use Wise to pay for a virtual assistant who is assisting me with running business reports. 

Once my account was opened and verified by Wise, I subsequently sent two payments without issue. Those payments were as follows : XX/XX/year> for XXXX  {$240.00} XX/XX/year> for XXXX  {$350.00} I was sending a third payment on XX/XX/XXXX and received the message Your transfer of XXXX USD to XXXX  is set up. Your money will get to us in 3 working days. 
Later in the day on XX/XX/XXXX, I received the following message : Weve paused your transfer until we can verify your bank account. We need to make sure that this account is yours before we can complete your transfer. Please upload a copy of a bank statement that clearly shows your full name and account number your bank should be able to provide you with a PDF of this. I complied with the request and uploaded an XXXX bank statement. 

On XX/XX/year>, I received the following email : Weve restricted your account and will close it on XXXX XX/XX/year>. This is because we dont support the way you use or plan to use Wise, and there are strict limits to what we can support as a regulated financial institution. Section 1.2 of our Acceptable Use Policy states that we reserve our right to deny our services to any customers who exceed our risk tolerance. 

I was then given the opportunity to appeal the decision by submitting a written explanation on why my account should be reopened. I wrote the following : Thank you for the opportunity to appeal our recent account closing. The email we received stated the reason for closure was because we dont support the way you use or plan to use Wise. We disagree with this closure because we have no illegal or malicious intent. We are an honest company simply looking for a secure way to pay about XXXX {$500.00} a month to a person helping me run business reports for my company. If we are doing anything wrong, Im happy to address it directly and provide any additional information or evidence you may need. We have nothing to hide. My name is XXXX XXXX XXXX, I am the founder and owner of my company and have been since I began it over 30 years ago. I own the company 100 %. We are a XXXX  ; USA based company and have been incorporated as a XXXX  USA company since our beginning. Thank you. 

On XX/XX/year>, I received a new email with the following : Weve decided to keep your account closed. We checked the info that you sent us, and we wont be able to reopen your account. Were unable to share the specific reason why were closing your account. As a regulated financial institution, there's limited information we can share when we close an account. But we can choose to close an account if we think : * Its a duplicate of another ( you can only have one account with us, unless we agree otherwise ) * Its security has been compromised * There are transactions on your account that we cant complete These are just examples, though. You can find more reasons in our Terms of Use, where we explain how were allowed to close accounts. 

We dont feel the three reasons given above for closure apply to us. However, we are not being given the opportunity to address the specific reason for closure so that we can address it directly. 

As an honest, XXXXyear-old, USA company just trying to pay a few XXXX dollars a month to a person who is in another country helping us run reports, I cant figure what Wise could be considering reason for account closure. 

Thank you.
label: payment_transfer

text:
1. Description of the Issue I am filing this complaint against Capital One for their failure to protect my account from unauthorized access, their failure to notify me of security breaches, and their refusal to process disputes for fraudulent transactions totaling {$100000.00}.

2. Statement of Facts Discovery of Fraud : On XX/XX/year> I discovered that my account had been compromised. There were unauthorized transactions totaling {$100000.00}.

Unauthorized Access : Upon contacting the bank, I was informed that multiple unauthorized devices had been linked to my account. When I reset my password I saw that another individual identified as " XXXX '' had gained access to my account and using that username to access my account. I was also informed that multiple unauthorized representative or third parties were linked to my account without my consent. 

Communication Failure : The bank failed to send me any sort of notifications regarding this activity, but they admitted the emails were sent to the wrong email address because the hacker had changed my contact information. The bank did not use secondary verification ( like a phone call ) to confirm these high-value changes or transactions.

Account Restriction : Capital One has restricted my account and stated they intend to close it. Crucially, they informed me that I can not dispute these fraudulent items until the account is closed, which is a violation of my rights under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( Regulation E ).

3. Actions Already Taken I have been proactive in reporting this identity theft and financial fraud : Filed a report with the Sheriffs Department Filed a complaint with the FBIs Internet Crime Complaint Center ( IC3 ).

Emailed the Capital One Investigations Department directly with all supporting documentation.
label: payment_transfer

text:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I was robbed in XXXX XXXX, Louisiana. My phone was stolen. Immediately following the theft, unauthorized individuals accessed my Cash App account and executed approximately 28 fraudulent transactions totaling over {$2500.00}. All transactions occurred on the morning of XX/XX/XXXX. Recipients of the unauthorized transfers include " XXXX XXXX XXXX '' ( {$1200.00}, {$500.00}, {$300.00}, and {$170.00} ), " XXXX XXXX '' ( {$1.00} ), " XXXX XXXX '' ( {$200.00} and {$85.00} ), " XXXX XXXX XXXX '' ( {$8.00} ), and others. I did not authorize any of these transactions and do not recognize any of the recipients.

I filed a police report with local law enforcement and submitted an identity theft report with the Federal Trade Commission documenting the robbery and resulting unauthorized account activity.

I reported the fraud to Cash App and filed disputes for all unauthorized transactions. Cash App denied my disputes on approximately XX/XX/XXXX, marking them as " Dispute completed '' without providing any written explanation of its findings or the evidence it relied on, as required under Regulation E ( 12 C.F.R. 1005.11 ( d ) ).

When I contacted Cash App Support and requested the evidence supporting the denial of my disputes, Cash App did not provide the requested documentation. Instead, Cash App informed me that my account had been found in violation of its Terms of Service and/or Acceptable Use Policy and closed my account, disabling all features including the Cash App Card. The only remaining functionality is the ability to withdraw remaining funds.

Cash App 's conduct raises several concerns under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act and Regulation E. First, Cash App failed to conduct a reasonable investigation into the reported unauthorized transactions. Second, Cash App failed to provide provisional credit during its investigation. Third, Cash App failed to provide a written explanation of its findings or notify me of my right to request supporting documents. Fourth, rather than fulfilling these regulatory obligations, Cash App retaliated by closing my account when I requested evidence of its decision.

I note that in XX/XX/XXXX, the CFPB issued a consent order ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) against Block, Inc. for these same failures conducting incomplete investigations, improperly denying fraud claims, and violating Regulation E. The treatment I have received appears to reflect a continuation of the practices that prompted that enforcement action.

I am requesting that the CFPB direct Cash App to conduct a proper investigation, provide a written explanation of its findings with supporting documentation, reverse all unauthorized transactions, and restore my account in good standing. I have a police report and FTC identity theft report available as supporting documentation.
